text.Rotherham:
27:1 Yahweh, is my light and my salvation, Of whom shall I be afraid? Yahweh, is the refuge of my life, Of whom shall I be in dread?
27:2 When there drew near against me evil-doers to devour my flesh, mine adversaries, and mine enemies—mine!—they, stumbled and fell!
27:3 Though there pitch against me—a camp, my heart shall not fear,—Though there come up against me—a battle, still, am I confident.
27:4 One thing, have I asked of Yahweh, That, will I seek to secure,—That I may dwell in the house of Yahweh, all the days of my life, To view the delightfulness of Yahweh, And to contemplate in his temple.
27:5 For he will hide me in his pavilion, in the day of calamity,—He will conceal me, in the secrecy of his tent, Within a rock, will he set me on high.
27:6 Now, therefore, shall my head be raised high above my foes round about me, and I will sacrifice in his tent the sacrifices of triumphant joy, I will sing and touch the strings, to Yahweh.
27:7 Hear, O Yahweh, With my voice, do I cry, Oh then show me favour and answer me.
27:8 To thee, said my heart—[When thou saidst] Seek ye my face—Thy face, O Yahweh, will I seek!
27:9 Do not hide thy face from me, Do not repulse, in thine anger, thine own servant,—My help, thou hast been, Do not send me away nor forsake me, O my saving God!
27:10 When, my own father and mother, had forsaken me, Then, Yahweh, took me up!
27:11 Point out to me, O Yahweh, thy way,—And guide me in a level path, because of mine adversaries.
27:12 Do not give me up unto the desire of mine adversaries, for there have risen up against me witnesses that are false, and he that fanneth up violence.
27:13 I believe that I shall see—The goodness of Yahweh in the land of the living,
27:14 Wait thou for Yahweh,—Be strong, and let thy heart be bold, Wait, then, for Yahweh!
text.NABRE:
27:1 Of David. I The Lord is my light and my salvation; whom should I fear? The Lord is my life’s refuge; of whom should I be afraid?
27:2 When evildoers come at me to devour my flesh, These my enemies and foes themselves stumble and fall.
27:3 Though an army encamp against me, my heart does not fear; Though war be waged against me, even then do I trust.
27:4 II One thing I ask of the Lord; this I seek: To dwell in the Lord ’s house all the days of my life, To gaze on the Lord ’s beauty, to visit his temple.
27:5 For God will hide me in his shelter in time of trouble, He will conceal me in the cover of his tent; and set me high upon a rock.
27:6 Even now my head is held high above my enemies on every side! I will offer in his tent sacrifices with shouts of joy; I will sing and chant praise to the Lord.
27:7 B I Hear my voice, Lord, when I call; have mercy on me and answer me.
27:8 “Come,” says my heart, “seek his face”; your face, Lord, do I seek!
27:9 Do not hide your face from me; do not repel your servant in anger. You are my salvation; do not cast me off; do not forsake me, God my savior!
27:10 Even if my father and mother forsake me, the Lord will take me in.
27:11 II Lord, show me your way; lead me on a level path because of my enemies.
27:12 Do not abandon me to the desire of my foes; malicious and lying witnesses have risen against me.
27:13 I believe I shall see the Lord ’s goodness in the land of the living.
27:14 Wait for the Lord, take courage; be stouthearted, wait for the Lord!
text.DRB:
27:1 Apsalm to David himself. To thee, o Lord, I will cry, my God keep not silence from me: lest at any time thou hold thy peace from me, & I shall be like to them that go down into the lake.
27:2 Hear, o Lord, the voice of my petition whilst I pray to thee: whilst I lift up my hands to thy holy temple.
27:3 Draw me not together with sinners: and with them that work iniquity destroy me not. Which speak peace with their neighbour, but evils in their hearts.
27:4 Give them according to their works, & according to the wickedness of their inventions. According to the works of their hands give unto them: render them their retribution.
27:5 Because they have not understood the works of our Lord, and in the works of his hands thou shalt destroy them, & not build them up.
27:6 blessed be our Lord: because he hath heard the voice of my petition.
27:7 Our Lord is my helper, and my protector: in him my heart hath hoped, and I was holpen. And my flesh flourished again: & with my will I will confess to him.
27:8 Our Lord is the strength of his people: and he is the protector of the salvations of his anointed.
27:9 Save thy people, o Lord, and bless thine inheritance: and rule them, and extol them for ever.

text.NRSV:
28:1 To you, O Lord, I call; my rock, do not refuse to hear me, for if you are silent to me, I shall be like those who go down to the Pit.
28:2 Hear the voice of my supplication, as I cry to you for help, as I lift up my hands toward your most holy sanctuary.
28:3 Do not drag me away with the wicked, with those who are workers of evil, who speak peace with their neighbors, while mischief is in their hearts.
28:4 Repay them according to their work, and according to the evil of their deeds; repay them according to the work of their hands; render them their due reward.
28:5 Because they do not regard the works of the Lord, or the work of his hands, he will break them down and build them up no more.
28:6 Blessed be the Lord, for he has heard the sound of my pleadings.
28:7 The Lord is my strength and my shield; in him my heart trusts; so I am helped, and my heart exults, and with my song I give thanks to him.
28:8 The Lord is the strength of his people; he is the saving refuge of his anointed.
28:9 O save your people, and bless your heritage; be their shepherd, and carry them forever.
text.Coverdale:
28:1 O LORD, I call to you; my Rock, do not be deaf to my cry; * lest, if you do not hear me, I become like those who go down to the Pit.
28:2 Hear the voice of my prayer when I cry out to you, * when I lift up my hands to your holy of holies.
28:3 Do not snatch me away with the wicked or with the evildoers, * who speak peaceably with their neighbors, while strife is in their hearts.
28:4 Repay them according to their deeds, * and according to the wickedness of their actions.
28:5 According to the work of their hands repay them, * and give them their just deserts.
28:6 They have no understanding of the LORD'S doings, nor of the works of his hands; * therefore he will break them down and not build them up.
28:7 Blessed is the LORD! * for he has heard the voice of my prayer.
28:8 The LORD is my strength and my shield; * my heart trusts in him, and I have been helped;
28:9 Therefore my heart dances for joy, * and in my song will I praise him.
28:10 The LORD is the strength of his people, * a safe refuge for his anointed.
28:11 Save your people and bless your inheritance; * shepherd them and carry them for ever.
text.KJV:
28:1 A Psalm of David. Unto thee will I cry, O Lord my rock; be not silent to me: lest, if thou be silent to me, I become like them that go down into the pit.
28:2 Hear the voice of my supplications, when I cry unto thee, when I lift up my hands toward thy holy oracle.
28:3 Draw me not away with the wicked, and with the workers of iniquity, which speak peace to their neighbours, but mischief is in their hearts.
28:4 Give them according to their deeds, and according to the wickedness of their endeavours: give them after the work of their hands; render to them their desert.
28:5 Because they regard not the works of the Lord, nor the operation of his hands, he shall destroy them, and not build them up.
28:6 Blessed be the Lord, because he hath heard the voice of my supplications.
28:7 The Lord is my strength and my shield; my heart trusted in him, and I am helped: therefore my heart greatly rejoiceth; and with my song will I praise him.
28:8 The Lord is their strength, and he is the saving strength of his anointed.
28:9 Save thy people, and bless thine inheritance: feed them also, and lift them up for ever.
text.JPS1985:
28:1 Of David. O LORD, I call to You; my rock, do not disregard me, for if You hold aloof from me, I shall be like those gone down into the Pit.
28:2 Listen to my plea for mercy when I cry out to You, when I lift my hands toward Your inner sanctuary.
28:3 Do not count me with the wicked and evildoers who profess goodwill toward their fellows while malice is in their heart.
28:4 Pay them according to their deeds, their malicious acts; according to their handiwork pay them, give them their deserts.
28:5 For they do not consider the LORD’s deeds, the work of His hands. May He tear them down, never to rebuild them!
28:6 Blessed is the LORD, for He listens to my plea for mercy.
28:7 The LORD is my strength and my shield; my heart trusts in Him. I was helped, and my heart exulted, so I will glorify Him with my song.
28:8 The LORD is their strength; He is a stronghold for the deliverance of His anointed.
28:9 Deliver and bless Your very own people; tend them and sustain them forever.

text.Rotherham:
28:1 Unto thee, O Yahweh, do I cry, O my Rock! do not turn in silence from me,—lest, if thou turn from me in silence, I be like them who go down into the pit.
28:2 Hear the voice of my supplication, when I cry to thee for help, when I lift up my hands toward thy holy shrine.
28:3 Do not drag me away—with the lawless, or with the workers of iniquity,—who speak peaceably with their neighbours, but, wrong, is in their heart.
28:4 Give them, according to their deed, and, according to the wrong of their practices,—According to the work of their own hands, give thou unto them, Bring back their own dealing to themselves.
28:5 Because they heed not The Deeds of Yahweh, or the Work of his hands, he will pull them down, and not build them.
28:6 Blessed be Yahweh, Because he hath heard the voice of my supplication.
28:7 Yahweh, is my strength and my shield, In him, hath trusted my heart, and I have found help,—Therefore hath my heart danced for joy, and, with my song, do I praise him.
28:8 Yahweh, is strength to his people,—And, the all-saving refuge of his Anointed One, is he!
28:9 Oh save thy people, and bless thine inheritance, Tend them also, and carry them, Unto times age-abiding.
text.NABRE:
28:1 Of David. To you, Lord, I call; my Rock, do not be deaf to me, Do not be silent toward me, so that I join those who go down to the pit.
28:2 Hear the sound of my pleading when I cry to you for help when I lift up my hands toward your holy place.
28:3 Do not drag me off with the wicked, with those who do wrong, Who speak peace to their neighbors though evil is in their hearts.
28:4 Repay them for their deeds, for the evil that they do. For the work of their hands repay them; give them what they deserve.
28:5 Because they do not understand the Lord ’s works, the work of his hands, He will tear them down, never to rebuild them.
28:6 II Blessed be the Lord, who has heard the sound of my pleading.
28:7 The Lord is my strength and my shield, in whom my heart trusts. I am helped, so my heart rejoices; with my song I praise him.
28:8 III Lord, you are a strength for your people, the saving refuge of your anointed.
28:9 Save your people, bless your inheritance; pasture and carry them forever!
text.DRB:
28:1 The Psalm of David, in the consummation of the tabernacle. Bring to our Lord ye children of God: bring to our Lord the sons of rams.
28:2 Bring to our Lord glory and honour, bring to our Lord glory unto his name: adore ye our Lord in his holy court.
28:3 The voice of our Lord upon waters, the God of majesty hath thundered: Our Lord upon many waters.
28:4 The voice of our Lord in power: the voice of our Lord in magnificence.
28:5 The voice of our Lord breaking cedars: and our Lord shall break the cedars of Libanus:
28:6 And he shall break them in pieces as a calf of Libanus. And the beloved as the son of unicorns.
28:7 The voice of our Lord dividing the flame of fire.
28:8 The voice of our Lord shaking the desert, and our Lord shall move the desert of Cades.
28:9 The voice of our Lord preparing harts, and he shall discover thick woods : and in his temple all shall say glory.
28:10 Our Lord maketh to inhabit the flood: and our Lord shall sit King for ever.
28:11 Our Lord will give strength to his people: our Lord will bless his people in peace.

text.NRSV:
29:1 Ascribe to the Lord, O heavenly beings, ascribe to the Lord glory and strength.
29:2 Ascribe to the Lord the glory of his name; worship the Lord in holy splendor.
29:3 The voice of the Lord is over the waters; the God of glory thunders, the Lord, over mighty waters.
29:4 The voice of the Lord is powerful; the voice of the Lord is full of majesty.
29:5 The voice of the Lord breaks the cedars; the Lord breaks the cedars of Lebanon.
29:6 He makes Lebanon skip like a calf, and Sirion like a young wild ox.
29:7 The voice of the Lord flashes forth flames of fire.
29:8 The voice of the Lord shakes the wilderness; the Lord shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.
29:9 The voice of the Lord causes the oaks to whirl, and strips the forest bare; and in his temple all say,"Glory!"
29:10 The Lord sits enthroned over the flood; the Lord sits enthroned as king forever.
29:11 May the Lord give strength to his people! May the Lord bless his people with peace!
text.Coverdale:
29:1 Ascribe to the LORD, you gods, * ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.
29:2 Ascribe to the LORD the glory due his Name; * worship the LORD in the beauty of holiness.
29:3 The voice of the LORD is upon the waters; the God of glory thunders; * the LORD is upon the mighty waters.
29:4 The voice of the LORD is a powerful voice; * the voice of the LORD is a voice of splendor.
29:5 The voice of the LORD breaks the cedar trees; * the LORD breaks the cedars of Lebanon;
29:6 He makes Lebanon skip like a calf, * and Mount Hermon like a young wild ox.
29:7 The voice of the LORD splits the flames of fire; the voice of the LORD shakes the wilderness; * the LORD shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.
29:8 The voice of the LORD makes the oak trees writhe * and strips the forests bare.
29:9 And in the temple of the LORD * all are crying,"Glory!"
29:10 The LORD sits enthroned above the flood; * the LORD sits enthroned as King for evermore.
29:11 The LORD shall give strength to his people; * the LORD shall give his people the blessing of peace.
text.KJV:
29:1 A Psalm of David. Give unto the Lord, O ye mighty, give unto the Lord glory and strength.
29:2 Give unto the Lord the glory due unto his name; worship the Lord in the beauty of holiness.
29:3 The voice of the Lord is upon the waters: the God of glory thundereth: the Lord is upon many waters.
29:4 The voice of the Lord is powerful; the voice of the Lord is full of majesty.
29:5 The voice of the Lord breaketh the cedars; yea, the Lord breaketh the cedars of Lebanon.
29:6 He maketh them also to skip like a calf; Lebanon and Sirion like a young unicorn.
29:7 The voice of the Lord divideth the flames of fire.
29:8 The voice of the Lord shaketh the wilderness; the Lord shaketh the wilderness of Kadesh.
29:9 The voice of the Lord maketh the hinds to calve, and discovereth the forests: and in his temple doth every one speak of his glory.
29:10 The Lord sitteth upon the flood; yea, the Lord sitteth King for ever.
29:11 The Lord will give strength unto his people; the Lord will bless his people with peace.
text.JPS1985:
29:1 A psalm of David. Ascribe to the LORD, O divine beings, ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.
29:2 Ascribe to the LORD the glory of His name; bow down to the LORD, majestic in holiness.
29:3 The voice of the LORD is over the waters; the God of glory thunders, the LORD, over the mighty waters.
29:4 The voice of the LORD is power; the voice of the LORD is majesty;
29:5 the voice of the LORD breaks cedars; the LORD shatters the cedars of Lebanon.
29:6 He makes Lebanon skip like a calf, Sirion, like a young wild ox.
29:7 The voice of the LORD kindles flames of fire;
29:8 the voice of the LORD convulses the wilderness; the LORD convulses the wilderness of Kadesh;
29:9 the voice of the LORD causes hinds to calve, and strips forests bare; while in His temple all say “Glory! ”
29:10 The LORD sat enthroned at the Flood; the LORD sits enthroned, king forever.
29:11 May the LORD grant strength to His people; may the LORD bestow on His people wellbeing.

text.NABRE:
29:1 A psalm of David. Give to the Lord, you sons of God, give to the Lord glory and might;
29:2 Give to the Lord the glory due his name. Bow down before the Lord ’s holy splendor!
29:3 II The voice of the Lord is over the waters; the God of glory thunders, the Lord, over the mighty waters.
29:4 The voice of the Lord is power; the voice of the Lord is splendor.
29:5 The voice of the Lord cracks the cedars; the Lord splinters the cedars of Lebanon,
29:6 Makes Lebanon leap like a calf, and Sirion like a young bull.
29:7 The voice of the Lord strikes with fiery flame;
29:8 the voice of the Lord shakes the desert; the Lord shakes the desert of Kadesh.
29:9 The voice of the Lord makes the deer dance and strips the forests bare. All in his Temple say, “Glory!”
29:10 III The Lord sits enthroned above the flood! The Lord reigns as king forever!
29:11 May the Lord give might to his people; may the Lord bless his people with peace!
text.DRB:
29:1 A Psalm of Canticle, in the dedication of David's house.
29:2 I will exalt thee, o Lord because thou hast received me: neither hast delighted mine enemies over me.
29:3 O Lord my God I have cried to thee, and thou hast healed me.
29:4 Lord thou hast brought forth my soul out of hell: thou hast saved me from them that go down into the lake.
29:5 Sing to our Lord ye his Saints: and confess to the memory of his holiness.
29:6 Because wrath is in his indignation: and life in his will. At evening shall weeping abide: and in the morning gladness.
29:7 And I said in my aboundance: I will not be moved for ever.
29:8 O Lord in thy will thou hast given strength to my beauty. Thou hast turned away thy face from me, and I became troubled.
29:9 To thee o Lord I will cry: and I will pray to my God.
29:10 What profit is in my blood, whilst I descend into corruption? Shall dust confess to thee, or declare thy truth?
29:11 Our Lord hath heard, and had mercy on me: our Lord is become my helper.
29:12 Thou hast turned my mourning into joy unto me: thou hast cut my sackcloth, and hast compassed me with gladness.
29:13 That my glory may sing to thee, and I be not compunct: Lord my God for ever will I confess to thee.

text.NRSV:
30:1 I will extol you, O Lord, for you have drawn me up, and did not let my foes rejoice over me.
30:2 O Lord my God, I cried to you for help, and you have healed me.
30:3 O Lord, you brought up my soul from Sheol, restored me to life from among those gone down to the Pit.
30:4 Sing praises to the Lord, O you his faithful ones, and give thanks to his holy name.
30:5 For his anger is but for a moment; his favor is for a lifetime. Weeping may linger for the night, but joy comes with the morning.
30:6 As for me, I said in my prosperity,"I shall never be moved."
30:7 By your favor, O Lord, you had established me as a strong mountain; you hid your face; I was dismayed.
30:8 To you, O Lord, I cried, and to the Lord I made supplication:
30:9 "What profit is there in my death, if I go down to the Pit? Will the dust praise you? Will it tell of your faithfulness?
30:10 Hear, O Lord, and be gracious to me! O Lord, be my helper!"
30:11 You have turned my mourning into dancing; you have taken off my sackcloth and clothed me with joy,
30:12 so that my soul may praise you and not be silent. O Lord my God, I will give thanks to you forever.
text.Coverdale:
30:1 I will exalt you, O LORD, because you have lifted me up * and have not let my enemies triumph over me.
30:2 O LORD my God, I cried out to you, * and you restored me to health.
30:3 You brought me up, O LORD, from the dead; * you restored my life as I was going down to the grave.
30:4 Sing to the LORD, you servants of his; * give thanks for the remembrance of his holiness.
30:5 For his wrath endures but the twinkling of an eye, * his favor for a lifetime.
30:6 Weeping may spend the night, * but joy comes in the morning.
30:7 While I felt secure, I said,"I shall never be disturbed. * You, LORD, with your favor, made me as strong as the mountains."
30:8 Then you hid your face, * and I was filled with fear.
30:9 I cried to you, O LORD; * I pleaded with the Lord, saying,
30:10 "What profit is there in my blood, if I go down to the Pit? * will the dust praise you or declare your faithfulness?
30:11 Hear, O LORD, and have mercy upon me; * O LORD, be my helper."
30:12 You have turned my wailing into dancing; * you have put off my sack-cloth and clothed me with joy.
30:13 Therefore my heart sings to you without ceasing; * O LORD my God, I will give you thanks for ever.
text.KJV:
30:1 A Psalm and Song at the dedication of the house of David. I will extol thee, O Lord; for thou hast lifted me up, and hast not made my foes to rejoice over me.
30:2 O Lord my God, I cried unto thee, and thou hast healed me.
30:3 O Lord, thou hast brought up my soul from the grave: thou hast kept me alive, that I should not go down to the pit.
30:4 Sing unto the Lord, O ye saints of his, and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness.
30:5 For his anger endureth but a moment; in his favour is life: weeping may endure for a night, but joy cometh in the morning.
30:6 And in my prosperity I said, I shall never be moved.
30:7 Lord, by thy favour thou hast made my mountain to stand strong: thou didst hide thy face, and I was troubled.
30:8 I cried to thee, O Lord; and unto the Lord I made supplication.
30:9 What profit is there in my blood, when I go down to the pit? Shall the dust praise thee? shall it declare thy truth?
30:10 Hear, O Lord, and have mercy upon me: Lord, be thou my helper.
30:11 Thou hast turned for me my mourning into dancing: thou hast put off my sackcloth, and girded me with gladness;
30:12 To the end that my glory may sing praise to thee, and not be silent. O Lord my God, I will give thanks unto thee for ever.
text.JPS1985:
30:1 A psalm of David. A song for the dedication of the House.
30:2 I extol You, O LORD, for You have lifted me up, and not let my enemies rejoice over me.
30:3 O LORD, my God, I cried out to You, and You healed me.
30:4 O LORD, You brought me up from Sheol, preserved me from going down into the Pit.
30:5 O you faithful of the LORD, sing to Him, and praise His holy name.
30:6 For He is angry but a moment, and when He is pleased there is life. One may lie down weeping at nightfall; but at dawn there are shouts of joy.
30:7 When I was untroubled, I thought, “I shall never be shaken, ”
30:8 for You, O LORD, when You were pleased, made [me] firm as a mighty mountain. When You hid Your face, I was terrified.
30:9 I called to You, O LORD; to my Lord I made appeal,
30:10 “What is to be gained from my death, from my descent into the Pit? Can dust praise You? Can it declare Your faithfulness?
30:11 Hear, O LORD, and have mercy on me; O LORD, be my help! ”
30:12 You turned my lament into dancing, you undid my sackcloth and girded me with joy,
30:13 that [my] whole being might sing hymns to You endlessly; O LORD my God, I will praise You forever.
text.Grail1963:
.2 I will praise you, Lord, you have rescued me
and have not let my enemies rejoice over me.
* * *
3 O Lord, I cried to you for help
and you, my God, have healed me.
4 O Lord, you have raised my soul from the dead,
restored me to life from those who sink into the grave.
5 Sing psalms to the Lord, you who love him,
give thanks to his holy name.
6 His anger lasts a moment; his favor all through life.
At night there are tears, but joy comes with dawn.

Can dust give you praise or proclaim your truth?

text.Rotherham:
30:1 I will extol thee, O Yahweh, for thou hast drawn me up,—and not suffered my foes to rejoice over me.
30:2 O Yahweh, my God, I cried for help unto thee, and thou hast healed me.
30:3 O Yahweh! thou hast lifted, out of hades, my soul, Thou hast brought me back to life, from among those who were going down to the pit.
30:4 Sweep the strings to Yahweh, ye his men of lovingkindness, and give ye praise at the mention of his holiness.
30:5 For there is, a Moment, in his anger, a Life-time, in his good-pleasure, In the evening, cometh Weeping to lodge, But, by the morning, ’tis a Shout of Triumph!
30:6 But, I, said, in my tranquility, I shall not be shaken to times age-abiding!
30:7 O Yahweh, in thy good-pleasure, hadst thou caused my mountain to stand, strong,—Thou didst hide thy face—I was dismayed!
30:8 Unto thee, O Yahweh, do I cry,—and, unto My Lord, do I make supplication:
30:9 What profit in my blood? in my going down into the pit? Can dust praise thee? Can it declare thy faithfulness?
30:10 Hear, O Yahweh, and show me favour, O Yahweh! become thou a helper unto me.
30:11 Thou hast turned my lamentation, into a dance for me,—Thou hast torn off my sackcloth, and girded me with gladness:
30:12 To the end that mine honour should make melody unto thee, and not be silent. O Yahweh, my God! Unto times age-abiding, will I praise thee.
text.NABRE:
30:1 A psalm. A song for the dedication of the Temple. Of David.
30:2 I I praise you, Lord, for you raised me up and did not let my enemies rejoice over me.
30:3 O Lord, my God, I cried out to you for help and you healed me.
30:4 Lord, you brought my soul up from Sheol; you let me live, from going down to the pit.
30:5 II Sing praise to the Lord, you faithful; give thanks to his holy memory.
30:6 For his anger lasts but a moment; his favor a lifetime. At dusk weeping comes for the night; but at dawn there is rejoicing.
30:7 III Complacent, I once said, “I shall never be shaken.”
30:8 Lord, you showed me favor, established for me mountains of virtue. But when you hid your face I was struck with terror.
30:9 To you, Lord, I cried out; with the Lord I pleaded for mercy:
30:10 “What gain is there from my lifeblood, from my going down to the grave? Does dust give you thanks or declare your faithfulness?
30:11 Hear, O Lord, have mercy on me; Lord, be my helper.”
30:12 IV You changed my mourning into dancing; you took off my sackcloth and clothed me with gladness.
30:13 So that my glory may praise you and not be silent. O Lord, my God, forever will I give you thanks.
text.DRB:
30:1 Unto the end, the Psalm of David, for excess of mind.
30:2 In thee o Lord have I hoped, let me not be confounded for ever: in thy justice deliver me.
30:3 Incline thine ear to me, make haste to deliver me. Be unto me for a God protector: and for a house of refuge, that thou mayst save me.
30:4 Because thou art my strength, and my refuge: and for thy name thou wilt conduct me, and wilt nourish me.
30:5 Thou wilt bring me out of this snare, which they have hid for me: because thou art my protector.
30:6 Into thy hands I commend my spirit: thou hast redeemed me, o Lord God of truth.
30:7 Thou hast hated them that observe vanities, unprofitably. But I have hoped in our Lord:
30:8 Ishall rejoice and be joyful in thy mercy. Because thou hast respected my humility, thou hast saved my soul out of necessities.
30:9 Neither hast thou shut me up in the hands of the enemy: thou hast set my feet in a large place.
30:10 Have mercy upon me, o Lord, because I am in tribulation: mine eye is troubled for wrath, my soul, and my belly:
30:11 Because my life is decayed for sorrow: and my years for groanings. My strength is weakened for poverty; and my bones are troubled.
30:12 Above all mine enemies I am made a reproach, both to my neighbours exceedingly: and a fear to my acquaintance. They that saw me, fled forth from me:
30:13 I am forgotten, from the heart as one dead. I am made as a vessel destroyed:
30:14 because I have heard the reprehension of many that abide round about: In that whilst, they assembled together against me, they consulted to take my soul.
30:15 But I have hoped in thee. O Lord: I said, Thou art my God:
30:16 my lots are in thy hands. Deliver me out of the hands of my enemies, and from them that persecute me.
30:17 Illustrate thy face upon thy servant, save me in thy mercy:
30:18 Lord let me not be confounded, because I have invocated thee. Let the impious be ashamed, and brought down into hell:
30:19 let the deceitful lips be made mute. Which speak iniquity against the just, in pride and abuse.
30:20 How great is the multitude of thy sweetness, o Lord which thou hast hid for them that fear thee. Thou hast perfited it for them that hope in thee, in the sight of the children of men.
30:21 Thou shalt hide them in the secret of thy face from the disturbance of men. Thou shall protect them in thy tabernacle from the contradiction of tongues.
30:22 Blessed be our Lord: because he hath made his mercy marvelous to me in the fenced city.
30:23 But I have said in the excess of my mind: I am cast away from the sight of thine eyes. Therefore thou hast heard the voice of my prayer, whilst I cried to thee.
30:24 Love our Lord all ye his Saints: because our Lord will require truth, & will repay them aboundantly that do proudly.
30:25 Do ye manfully, and let your heart take courage, all ye that hope in our Lord.

text.NRSV:
31:1 In you, O Lord, I seek refuge; do not let me ever be put to shame; in your righteousness deliver me.
31:2 Incline your ear to me; rescue me speedily. Be a rock of refuge for me, a strong fortress to save me.
31:3 You are indeed my rock and my fortress; for your name's sake lead me and guide me,
31:4 take me out of the net that is hidden for me, for you are my refuge.
31:5 Into your hand I commit my spirit; you have redeemed me, O Lord, faithful God.
31:6 You hate those who pay regard to worthless idols, but I trust in the Lord.
31:7 I will exult and rejoice in your steadfast love, because you have seen my affliction; you have taken heed of my adversities,
31:8 and have not delivered me into the hand of the enemy; you have set my feet in a broad place.
31:9 Be gracious to me, O Lord, for I am in distress; my eye wastes away from grief, my soul and body also.
31:10 For my life is spent with sorrow, and my years with sighing; my strength fails because of my misery, and my bones waste away.
31:11 I am the scorn of all my adversaries, a horror to my neighbors, an object of dread to my acquaintances; those who see me in the street flee from me.
31:12 I have passed out of mind like one who is dead; I have become like a broken vessel.
31:13 For I hear the whispering of many— terror all around!— as they scheme together against me, as they plot to take my life.
31:14 But I trust in you, O Lord; I say,"You are my God."
31:15 My times are in your hand; deliver me from the hand of my enemies and persecutors.
31:16 Let your face shine upon your servant; save me in your steadfast love.
31:17 Do not let me be put to shame, O Lord, for I call on you; let the wicked be put to shame; let them go dumbfounded to Sheol.
31:18 Let the lying lips be stilled that speak insolently against the righteous with pride and contempt.
31:19 O how abundant is your goodness that you have laid up for those who fear you, and accomplished for those who take refuge in you, in the sight of everyone!
31:20 In the shelter of your presence you hide them from human plots; you hold them safe under your shelter from contentious tongues.
31:21 Blessed be the Lord, for he has wondrously shown his steadfast love to me when I was beset as a city under siege.
31:22 I had said in my alarm,"I am driven far from your sight."But you heard my supplications when I cried out to you for help.
31:23 Love the Lord, all you his saints. The Lord preserves the faithful, but abundantly repays the one who acts haughtily.
31:24 Be strong, and let your heart take courage, all you who wait for the Lord.
text.Coverdale:
31:1 In you, O LORD, have I taken refuge; let me never be put to shame; * deliver me in your righteousness.
31:2 Incline your ear to me; * make haste to deliver me.
31:3 Be my strong rock, a castle to keep me safe, for you are my crag and my stronghold; * for the sake of your Name, lead me and guide me.
31:4 Take me out of the net that they have secretly set for me, * for you are my tower of strength.
31:5 Into your hands I commend my spirit, * for you have redeemed me, O LORD, O God of truth.
31:6 I hate those who cling to worthless idols, * and I put my trust in the LORD.
31:7 I will rejoice and be glad because of your mercy; * for you have seen my affliction; you know my distress.
31:8 You have not shut me up in the power of the enemy; * you have set my feet in an open place.
31:9 Have mercy on me, O LORD, for I am in trouble; * my eye is consumed with sorrow, and also my throat and my belly.
31:10 For my life is wasted with grief, and my years with sighing; * my strength fails me because of affliction, and my bones are consumed.
31:11 I have become a reproach to all my enemies and even to my neighbors, a dismay to those of my acquaintance; * when they see me in the street they avoid me.
31:12 I am forgotten like a dead man, out of mind; * I am as useless as a broken pot.
31:13 For I have heard the whispering of the crowd; fear is all around; * they put their heads together against me; they plot to take my life.
31:14 But as for me, I have trusted in you, O LORD. * I have said,"You are my God.
31:15 My times are in your hand; * rescue me from the hand of my enemies, and from those who persecute me.
31:16 Make your face to shine upon your servant, * and in your loving-kindness save me."
31:17 LORD, let me not be ashamed for having called upon you; * rather, let the wicked be put to shame; let them be silent in the grave.
31:18 Let the lying lips be silenced which speak against the righteous, * haughtily, disdainfully, and with contempt.
31:19 How great is your goodness, O LORD! which you have laid up for those who fear you; * which you have done in the sight of all for those who put their trust in you.
31:20 You hide them in the covert of your presence from those who slander them; * you keep them in your shelter from the strife of tongues.
31:21 Blessed be the LORD! * for he has shown me the wonders of his love in a besieged city.
31:22 Yet I said in my alarm, I have been cut off from the sight of your eyes."* Nevertheless, you heard the sound of my entreaty when I cried out to you.
31:23 Love the LORD, all you who worship him; * the LORD protects the faithful, but repays to the full those who act haughtily.
31:24 Be strong and let your heart take courage, * all you who wait for the LORD.
text.KJV:
31:1 To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. In thee, O Lord, do I put my trust; let me never be ashamed: deliver me in thy righteousness.
31:2 Bow down thine ear to me; deliver me speedily: be thou my strong rock, for an house of defence to save me.
31:3 For thou art my rock and my fortress; therefore for thy name’s sake lead me, and guide me.
31:4 Pull me out of the net that they have laid privily for me: for thou art my strength.
31:5 Into thine hand I commit my spirit: thou hast redeemed me, O Lord God of truth.
31:6 I have hated them that regard lying vanities: but I trust in the Lord.
31:7 I will be glad and rejoice in thy mercy: for thou hast considered my trouble; thou hast known my soul in adversities;
31:8 And hast not shut me up into the hand of the enemy: thou hast set my feet in a large room.
31:9 Have mercy upon me, O Lord, for I am in trouble: mine eye is consumed with grief, yea, my soul and my belly.
31:10 For my life is spent with grief, and my years with sighing: my strength faileth because of mine iniquity, and my bones are consumed.
31:11 I was a reproach among all mine enemies, but especially among my neighbours, and a fear to mine acquaintance: they that did see me without fled from me.
31:12 I am forgotten as a dead man out of mind: I am like a broken vessel.
31:13 For I have heard the slander of many: fear was on every side: while they took counsel together against me, they devised to take away my life.
31:14 But I trusted in thee, O Lord: I said, Thou art my God.
31:15 My times are in thy hand: deliver me from the hand of mine enemies, and from them that persecute me.
31:16 Make thy face to shine upon thy servant: save me for thy mercies’ sake.
31:17 Let me not be ashamed, O Lord; for I have called upon thee: let the wicked be ashamed, and let them be silent in the grave.
31:18 Let the lying lips be put to silence; which speak grievous things proudly and contemptuously against the righteous.
31:19 Oh how great is thy goodness, which thou hast laid up for them that fear thee; which thou hast wrought for them that trust in thee before the sons of men!
31:20 Thou shalt hide them in the secret of thy presence from the pride of man: thou shalt keep them secretly in a pavilion from the strife of tongues.
31:21 Blessed be the Lord: for he hath shewed me his marvellous kindness in a strong city.
31:22 For I said in my haste, I am cut off from before thine eyes: nevertheless thou heardest the voice of my supplications when I cried unto thee.
31:23 O love the Lord, all ye his saints: for the Lord preserveth the faithful, and plentifully rewardeth the proud doer.
31:24 Be of good courage, and he shall strengthen your heart, all ye that hope in the Lord.
text.JPS1985:
31:1 For the leader. A psalm of David.
31:2 I seek refuge in You, O LORD; may I never be disappointed; as You are righteous, rescue me.
31:3 Incline Your ear to me; be quick to save me; be a rock, a stronghold for me, a citadel, for my deliverance.
31:4 For You are my rock and my fortress; You lead me and guide me as befits Your name.
31:5 You free me from the net laid for me, for You are my stronghold.
31:6 Into Your hand I entrust my spirit; You redeem me, O LORD, faithful God.
31:7 I detest those who rely on empty folly, but I trust in the LORD.
31:8 Let me exult and rejoice in Your faithfulness when You notice my affliction, are mindful of my deep distress,
31:9 and do not hand me over to my enemy, but grant me relief.
31:10 Have mercy on me, O LORD, for I am in distress; my eyes are wasted by vexation, my substance and body too.
31:11 My life is spent in sorrow, my years in groaning; my strength fails because of my iniquity, my limbs waste away.
31:12 Because of all my foes I am the particular butt of my neighbors, a horror to my friends; those who see me on the street avoid me.
31:13 I am put out of mind like the dead; I am like an object given up for lost.
31:14 I hear the whisperings of many, intrigue on every side, as they scheme together against me, plotting to take my life.
31:15 But I trust in You, O LORD; I say, “You are my God! ”
31:16 My fate is in Your hand; save me from the hand of my enemies and pursuers.
31:17 Show favor to Your servant; as You are faithful, deliver me.
31:18 O LORD, let me not be disappointed when I call You; let the wicked be disappointed; let them be silenced in Sheol;
31:19 let lying lips be stilled that speak haughtily against the righteous with arrogance and contempt.
31:20 How abundant is the good that You have in store for those who fear You, that You do in the full view of men for those who take refuge in You.
31:21 You grant them the protection of Your presence against scheming men; You shelter them in Your pavilion from contentious tongues.
31:22 Blessed is the LORD, for He has been wondrously faithful to me, a veritable bastion.
31:23 Alarmed, I had thought, “I am thrust out of Your sight”; yet You listened to my plea for mercy when I cried out to You.
31:24 So love the LORD, all you faithful; the LORD guards the loyal, and more than requites him who acts arrogantly.
31:25 Be strong and of good courage, all you who wait for the LORD.

text.Rotherham:
31:1 In thee, O Yahweh, have I sought refuge, Let me not be ashamed to times age-abiding, In thy righteousness, deliver me:
31:2 Incline unto me, thine ear, Speedily rescue me,—Become to me a Rock of refuge, a Place of security, For saving me.
31:3 Because, my mountain crag and my stronghold, thou art, Therefore, for the sake of thine own Name, wilt thou lead me and guide me?
31:4 Wilt thou bring me forth, out of the net which they have hidden for me? For, thou, art my refuge.
31:5 Into thy hand, do I commit my spirit—Thou hast ransomed me, O Yahweh, GOD most faithful.
31:6 I hate such as give heed to false vanities, I, then, in Yahweh, have set my hope.
31:7 I will indeed exult and rejoice, in thy lovingkindness,—In that thou hast looked upon my humiliation, thou hast taken note that in distresses was my life;
31:8 And hast not shut me up in the hand of the foe, Thou hast given standing, in a roomy place, unto my feet.
31:9 Show me favour, O Yahweh, for in distress am I,—Wasted with vexation, is mine eye—my soul and my body;
31:10 For, consumed with sorrow, is my life, and my years with sighing,—My strength hath staggered with my humiliation, and, my bones, are without marrow.
31:11 By reason of all mine adversaries, have I become a reproach. Even to my neighbours, altogether, and a dread to mine acquaintance,—They who have seen me abroad, have fled from me:
31:12 I have been forgotten, like one dead—out of mind, I have been as a missing vessel.
31:13 For I have heard the whispering of many—A terror round about! When they have sat in conclave together against me, to take away my life, have they intrigued.
31:14 I, therefore. in thee, have put my trust, O Yahweh, I have said, My God, thou art!
31:15 In thy hand, are my times, Rescue me from the hand of my foes and from my pursuers!
31:16 Cause thy face to shine upon thy servant, Save me in thy lovingkindness.
31:17 O Yahweh! let me not be ashamed, For I have called upon thee, Let the lawless be ashamed, Go down in silence to hades!
31:18 Let false lips be made dumb,—which are speaking—against a righteous one—arrogantly, with pride and contempt.
31:19 How great is thy goodness, which thou hast hidden away for them who revere thee,—Thou hast wrought for them who seek refuge in thee, in sight of the sons of men.
31:20 Thou wilt conceal them, in the secrecy of thine own presence, from the conspiracies of men,—Thou wilt hide them in a pavilion, from the strife of tongues.
31:21 Blessed be Yahweh, for he hath made wonderful his lovingkindness for me, in a fortified city.
31:22 But, I, had said in mine alarm, I am cut off from before thine eyes,—But, indeed, thou didst hear the voice of my supplication, when I cried for help unto thee.
31:23 Love Yahweh, all ye his men of lovingkindness,—Faithfulness, doth Yahweh observe, but repayeth abundantly him that worketh proudly.
31:24 Be strong, and let your heart be bold, all ye who are waiting for Yahweh.
text.NABRE:
31:1 For the leader. A psalm of David.
31:2 I In you, Lord, I take refuge; let me never be put to shame. In your righteousness deliver me;
31:3 incline your ear to me; make haste to rescue me! Be my rock of refuge, a stronghold to save me.
31:4 For you are my rock and my fortress; for your name’s sake lead me and guide me.
31:5 Free me from the net they have set for me, for you are my refuge.
31:6 Into your hands I commend my spirit; you will redeem me, Lord, God of truth.
31:7 You hate those who serve worthless idols, but I trust in the Lord.
31:8 I will rejoice and be glad in your mercy, once you have seen my misery, [and] gotten to know the distress of my soul.
31:9 You will not abandon me into enemy hands, but will set my feet in a free and open space.
31:10 II Be gracious to me, Lord, for I am in distress; affliction is wearing down my eyes, my throat and my insides.
31:11 My life is worn out by sorrow, and my years by sighing. My strength fails in my affliction; my bones are wearing down.
31:12 To all my foes I am a thing of scorn, and especially to my neighbors a horror to my friends. When they see me in public, they quickly shy away.
31:13 I am forgotten, out of mind like the dead; I am like a worn-out tool.
31:14 I hear the whispers of the crowd; terrors are all around me. They conspire together against me; they plot to take my life.
31:15 But I trust in you, Lord; I say, “You are my God.”
31:16 My destiny is in your hands; rescue me from my enemies, from the hands of my pursuers.
31:17 Let your face shine on your servant; save me in your mercy.
31:18 Do not let me be put to shame, for I have called to you, Lord. Put the wicked to shame; reduce them to silence in Sheol.
31:19 Strike dumb their lying lips, which speak arrogantly against the righteous in contempt and scorn.
31:20 III How great is your goodness, Lord, stored up for those who fear you. You display it for those who trust you, in the sight of the children of Adam.
31:21 You hide them in the shelter of your presence, safe from scheming enemies. You conceal them in your tent, away from the strife of tongues.
31:22 Blessed be the Lord, marvelously he showed to me his mercy in a fortified city.
31:23 Though I had said in my alarm, “I am cut off from your eyes.” Yet you heard my voice, my cry for mercy, when I pleaded with you for help.
31:24 Love the Lord, all you who are faithful to him. The Lord protects the loyal, but repays the arrogant in full.
31:25 Be strong and take heart, all who hope in the Lord.
text.DRB:
31:1 To David himself understanding. Blessed are they, whose iniquities are forgiven: and whose sins be covered.
31:2 Blessed is the man, to whom our Lord hath not imputed sin, neither is there guile in his spirit.
31:3 Because I held my peace, my bones are inveterated, whilst I cried all the day.
31:4 Because day & night thy hand is made heavy upon me: I am turned in my anguish, whilst the thorn is fastened.
31:5 I have made my sin known to thee: and my injustice I have not hid. I said: I will confess against me my injustice to our Lord: and thou hast forgiven the impiety of my sin.
31:6 For this shall every holy one pray to thee, in time convenient. But yet in the flood of many waters, they shall not approach to him.
31:7 Thou art my refuge from tribulation, which hath compassed me: my exultation, deliver me from them that compass me.
31:8 I will give thee understanding, and will instruct thee in the way, that thou shalt go: I will fasten mine eyes upon thee.
31:9 Do not become as horse and mule, which have no understanding. In bit and bridle bind fast their cheeks that approach not to thee.
31:10 Many are the scourges of a sinner, but him that hopeth in our Lord mercy shall compass.
31:11 Be joyful in our Lord and rejoice ye just, and glory all ye right of heart.

text.NRSV:
32:1 Happy are those whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered.
32:2 Happy are those to whom the Lord imputes no iniquity, and in whose spirit there is no deceit.
32:3 While I kept silence, my body wasted away through my groaning all day long.
32:4 For day and night your hand was heavy upon me; my strength was dried up as by the heat of summer. Selah
32:5 Then I acknowledged my sin to you, and I did not hide my iniquity; I said,"I will confess my transgressions to the Lord,"and you forgave the guilt of my sin. Selah
32:6 Therefore let all who are faithful offer prayer to you; at a time of distress, the rush of mighty waters shall not reach them.
32:7 You are a hiding place for me; you preserve me from trouble; you surround me with glad cries of deliverance. Selah
32:8 I will instruct you and teach you the way you should go; I will counsel you with my eye upon you.
32:9 Do not be like a horse or a mule, without understanding, whose temper must be curbed with bit and bridle, else it will not stay near you.
32:10 Many are the torments of the wicked, but steadfast love surrounds those who trust in the Lord.
32:11 Be glad in the Lord and rejoice, O righteous, and shout for joy, all you upright in heart.
text.Coverdale:
32:1 Happy are they whose transgressions are forgiven, * and whose sin is put away!
32:2 Happy are they to whom the LORD imputes no guilt, * and in whose spirit there is no guile!
32:3 While I held my tongue, my bones withered away, * because of my groaning all day long.
32:4 For your hand was heavy upon me day and night; * my moisture was dried up as in the heat of summer.
32:5 Then I acknowledged my sin to you, * and did not conceal my guilt.
32:6 I said,"I will confess my transgressions to the LORD."* Then you forgave me the guilt of my sin.
32:7 Therefore all the faithful will make their prayers to you in time of trouble; * when the great waters overflow, they shall not reach them.
32:8 You are my hiding-place; you preserve me from trouble; * you surround me with shouts of deliverance.
32:9 "I will instruct you and teach you in the way that you should go; * I will guide you with my eye.
32:10 Do not be like horse or mule, which have no understanding; * who must be fitted with bit and bridle, or else they will not stay near you."
32:11 Great are the tribulations of the wicked; * but mercy embraces those who trust in the LORD.
32:12 Be glad, you righteous, and rejoice in the LORD; * shout for joy, all who are true of heart.
text.KJV:
32:1 A Psalm of David, Maschil. Blessed is he whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered.
32:2 Blessed is the man unto whom the Lord imputeth not iniquity, and in whose spirit there is no guile.
32:3 When I kept silence, my bones waxed old through my roaring all the day long.
32:4 For day and night thy hand was heavy upon me: my moisture is turned into the drought of summer. Selah.
32:5 I acknowledged my sin unto thee, and mine iniquity have I not hid. I said, I will confess my transgressions unto the Lord; and thou forgavest the iniquity of my sin. Selah.
32:6 For this shall every one that is godly pray unto thee in a time when thou mayest be found: surely in the floods of great waters they shall not come nigh unto him.
32:7 Thou art my hiding place; thou shalt preserve me from trouble; thou shalt compass me about with songs of deliverance. Selah.
32:8 I will instruct thee and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will guide thee with mine eye.
32:9 Be ye not as the horse, or as the mule, which have no understanding: whose mouth must be held in with bit and bridle, lest they come near unto thee.
32:10 Many sorrows shall be to the wicked: but he that trusteth in the Lord, mercy shall compass him about.
32:11 Be glad in the Lord, and rejoice, ye righteous: and shout for joy, all ye that are upright in heart.
text.JPS1985:
32:1 Of David. A maskil. Happy is he whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered over.
32:2 Happy the man whom the LORD does not hold guilty, and in whose spirit there is no deceit.
32:3 As long as I said nothing, my limbs wasted away from my anguished roaring all day long.
32:4 For night and day Your hand lay heavy on me; my vigor waned as in the summer drought. Selah.
32:5 Then I acknowledged my sin to You; I did not cover up my guilt; I resolved, “I will confess my transgressions to the LORD, ” and You forgave the guilt of my sin. Selah.
32:6 Therefore let every faithful man pray to You upon discovering [his sin], that the rushing mighty waters not overtake him.
32:7 You are my shelter; You preserve me from distress; You surround me with the joyous shouts of deliverance. Selah.
32:8 Let me enlighten you and show you which way to go; let me offer counsel; my eye is on you.
32:9 Be not like a senseless horse or mule whose movement must be curbed by bit and bridle; far be it from you!
32:10 Many are the torments of the wicked, but he who trusts in the LORD shall be surrounded with favor.
32:11 Rejoice in the LORD and exult, O you righteous; shout for joy, all upright men!
text.Grail1963:

whose sin is remitted.
2 O happy the man to whom the Lord
imputes no guilt,
in whose spirit is no guile.
3 I kept it secret and my frame was wasted.
I groaned all day long,
4 for night and day your hand was heavy upon me.
Indeed my strength was dried up
as by the summer's heat.
5 But now I have acknowledged my sins;
my guilt I did not hide.
I said: "I will confess
my offense to the Lord."
And you, Lord, have forgiven
the guilt of my sin.
6 So let every good man pray to you
in the time of need.
The floods of water may reach high
but him they shall not reach.
7 You are my hiding place, O Lord;
you save me from distress.
(You surround me with cries of deliverance.)
* * *
8 I will instruct you and teach you

the way you should go;
I will give you counsel
with my eye upon you.
9 Be not like horse and mule, unintelligent,
needing bridle and bit
else they will not approach you.
10 Many sorrows has the wicked
but he who trusts in the Lord,
loving mercy surrounds him.

11 Rejoice, rejoice in the Lord,
exult, you just!

text.Rotherham:
32:1 How happy is he whose transgression is forgiven! whose sin is pardoned!
32:2 How happy the son of earth, to whom Yahweh will not reckon iniquity! and in whose spirit is no guile!
32:3 When I kept silence, my bones became worn out, Through my groaning all the day;
32:4 For, day and night, heavy upon me, was thy hand,—Changed was my life-sap into the drought of summer. [Selah.]
32:5 My sin, would I own unto thee, and, mine iniquity, not hide, I said, I will confess my transgressions unto Yahweh, And, thou, didst forgive the iniquity of my sin. [Selah.]
32:6 For this cause, will every man of lovingkindness pray unto thee, in time to obtain,—Surely, in the overflow of many waters, unto him, shall they not reach.
32:7 Thou, art a hiding-place for me, From distress, wilt thou preserve me,—With shouts of deliverance, wilt thou compass me about. [Selah.]
32:8 I will make thee discreet, I will point out to thee the way which thou must go, I will fix upon thee mine eye.
32:9 Do not ye become like a horse, like a mule, without discernment,—With the bit and bridle of his mouth, [hast thou] to restrain him,—He will not come near unto thee.
32:10 Many pains, hath the lawless one,—But, he that trusteth in Yahweh, Lovingkindness, shall compass him about.
32:11 Rejoice in Yahweh and exult, O ye righteous, Yea, shout in triumph, all ye upright in heart!
text.NABRE:
32:1 Of David. A maskil. Blessed is the one whose fault is removed, whose sin is forgiven.
32:2 Blessed is the man to whom the Lord imputes no guilt, in whose spirit is no deceit.
32:3 II Because I kept silent, my bones wasted away; I groaned all day long.
32:4 For day and night your hand was heavy upon me; my strength withered as in dry summer heat. Selah
32:5 Then I declared my sin to you; my guilt I did not hide. I said, “I confess my transgression to the Lord,” and you took away the guilt of my sin. Selah
32:6 Therefore every loyal person should pray to you in time of distress. Though flood waters threaten, they will never reach him.
32:7 You are my shelter; you guard me from distress; with joyful shouts of deliverance you surround me. Selah
32:8 III I will instruct you and show you the way you should walk, give you counsel with my eye upon you.
32:9 Do not be like a horse or mule, without understanding; with bit and bridle their temper is curbed, else they will not come to you.
32:10 IV Many are the sorrows of the wicked one, but mercy surrounds the one who trusts in the Lord.
32:11 Be glad in the Lord and rejoice, you righteous; exult, all you upright of heart.
text.DRB:
32:1 The Psalm of David. Rejoice ye just in our Lord; praising becometh the righteous.
32:2 Confess ye to our Lord on the harp: on a psalter of ten strings sing to him.
32:3 Sing ye to him a new song: sing well to him in jubilation.
32:4 Because the word of our Lord is right, and all his works are in faith.
32:5 He loveth mercy and judgment: the earth is full of the mercy of our Lord.
32:6 By the word of our Lord the heavens are established: and by the spirit of his mouth all the power of them,
32:7 Gathering together the waters of the sea, as it were in a bottle: putting the depths in treasures.
32:8 Let all the earth fear our Lord: and let all the inhabitants of the world be moved at him.
32:9 Because he said, and they were made: he commanded and they were created,
32:10 Our Lord dissipateth the counsels of nations: and he reproveth the cogitations of people, and he reproveth the counsels of princes.
32:11 But the counsel of our Lord abideth for ever, the cogitations of his heart in generation and generation.
32:12 Blessed is the nation, whose God is our Lord: the people whom he hath chosen for his inheritance.
32:13 Our Lord hath looked from heaven: he hath seen all the children of men.
32:14 From his prepared habitation he hath looked upon all that inhabit the earth
32:15 Who made their hearts severally: who understandeth all their works.
32:16 The king is not saved by much power: and the giant shall not be saved in the multitude of his strength.
32:17 The horse faileth to safety, and in the aboundance of his force he shall not be saved.
32:18 Behold the eyes of our Lord be upon them that fear him: and on them, that hope upon his mercy.
32:19 That he may deliver their souls from death: and nourish them in famine.
32:20 Our soul expecteth our Lord: because he is our helper and protector.
32:21 Because in him our heart shall rejoice: and we have trusted in his holy name.
32:22 Let thy mercy, o Lord, be made upon us, as we have hoped in thee.

text.NRSV:
33:1 Rejoice in the Lord, O you righteous. Praise befits the upright.
33:2 Praise the Lord with the lyre; make melody to him with the harp of ten strings.
33:3 Sing to him a new song; play skillfully on the strings, with loud shouts.
33:4 For the word of the Lord is upright, and all his work is done in faithfulness.
33:5 He loves righteousness and justice; the earth is full of the steadfast love of the Lord.
33:6 By the word of the Lord the heavens were made, and all their host by the breath of his mouth.
33:7 He gathered the waters of the sea as in a bottle; he put the deeps in storehouses.
33:8 Let all the earth fear the Lord; let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him.
33:9 For he spoke, and it came to be; he commanded, and it stood firm.
33:10 The Lord brings the counsel of the nations to nothing; he frustrates the plans of the peoples.
33:11 The counsel of the Lord stands forever, the thoughts of his heart to all generations.
33:12 Happy is the nation whose God is the Lord, the people whom he has chosen as his heritage.
33:13 The Lord looks down from heaven; he sees all humankind.
33:14 From where he sits enthroned he watches all the inhabitants of the earth—
33:15 he who fashions the hearts of them all, and observes all their deeds.
33:16 A king is not saved by his great army; a warrior is not delivered by his great strength.
33:17 The war horse is a vain hope for victory, and by its great might it cannot save.
33:18 Truly the eye of the Lord is on those who fear him, on those who hope in his steadfast love,
33:19 to deliver their soul from death, and to keep them alive in famine.
33:20 Our soul waits for the Lord; he is our help and shield.
33:21 Our heart is glad in him, because we trust in his holy name.
33:22 Let your steadfast love, O Lord, be upon us, even as we hope in you.
text.Coverdale:
33:1 Rejoice in the LORD, you righteous; * it is good for the just to sing praises.
33:2 Praise the LORD with the harp; * play to him upon the psaltery and lyre.
33:3 Sing for him a new song; * sound a fanfare with all your skill upon the trumpet.
33:4 For the word of the LORD is right, * and all his works are sure.
33:5 He loves righteousness and justice; * the loving-kindness of the LORD fills the whole earth.
33:6 By the word of the LORD were the heavens made, * by the breath of his mouth all the heavenly hosts.
33:7 He gathers up the waters of the ocean as in a water-skin * and stores up the depths of the sea.
33:8 Let all the earth fear the LORD; * let all who dwell in the world stand in awe of him.
33:9 For he spoke, and it came to pass; * he commanded, and it stood fast.
33:10 The LORD brings the will of the nations to naught; * he thwarts the designs of the peoples.
33:11 But the LORD'S will stands fast for ever, * and the designs of his heart from age to age.
33:12 Happy is the nation whose God is the LORD! * happy the people he has chosen to be his own!
33:13 The LORD looks down from heaven, * and beholds all the people in the world.
33:14 From where he sits enthroned he turns his gaze * on all who dwell on the earth.
33:15 He fashions all the hearts of them * and understands all their works.
33:16 There is no king that can be saved by a mighty army; * a strong man is not delivered by his great strength.
33:17 The horse is a vain hope for deliverance; * for all its strength it cannot save.
33:18 Behold, the eye of the LORD is upon those who fear him, * on those who wait upon his love,
33:19 To pluck their lives from death, * and to feed them in time of famine.
33:20 Our soul waits for the LORD; * he is our help and our shield.
33:21 Indeed, our heart rejoices in him, * for in his holy Name we put our trust.
33:22 Let your loving-kindness, O LORD, be upon us, * as we have put our trust in you.
text.KJV:
33:1 Rejoice in the Lord, O ye righteous: for praise is comely for the upright.
33:2 Praise the Lord with harp: sing unto him with the psaltery and an instrument of ten strings.
33:3 Sing unto him a new song; play skilfully with a loud noise.
33:4 For the word of the Lord is right; and all his works are done in truth.
33:5 He loveth righteousness and judgment: the earth is full of the goodness of the Lord.
33:6 By the word of the Lord were the heavens made; and all the host of them by the breath of his mouth.
33:7 He gathereth the waters of the sea together as an heap: he layeth up the depth in storehouses.
33:8 Let all the earth fear the Lord: let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him.
33:9 For he spake, and it was done; he commanded, and it stood fast.
33:10 The Lord bringeth the counsel of the heathen to nought: he maketh the devices of the people of none effect.
33:11 The counsel of the Lord standeth for ever, the thoughts of his heart to all generations.
33:12 Blessed is the nation whose God is the Lord; and the people whom he hath chosen for his own inheritance.
33:13 The Lord looketh from heaven; he beholdeth all the sons of men.
33:14 From the place of his habitation he looketh upon all the inhabitants of the earth.
33:15 He fashioneth their hearts alike; he considereth all their works.
33:16 There is no king saved by the multitude of an host: a mighty man is not delivered by much strength.
33:17 An horse is a vain thing for safety: neither shall he deliver any by his great strength.
33:18 Behold, the eye of the Lord is upon them that fear him, upon them that hope in his mercy;
33:19 To deliver their soul from death, and to keep them alive in famine.
33:20 Our soul waiteth for the Lord: he is our help and our shield.
33:21 For our heart shall rejoice in him, because we have trusted in his holy name.
33:22 Let thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us, according as we hope in thee.
text.JPS1985:
33:1 Sing forth, O you righteous, to the LORD; it is fit that the upright acclaim Him.
33:2 Praise the LORD with the lyre; with the ten-stringed harp sing to Him;
33:3 sing Him a new song; play sweetly with shouts of joy.
33:4 For the word of the LORD is right; His every deed is faithful.
33:5 He loves what is right and just; the earth is full of the LORD’s faithful care.
33:6 By the word of the LORD the heavens were made, by the breath of His mouth, all their host.
33:7 He heaps up the ocean waters like a mound, stores the deep in vaults.
33:8 Let all the earth fear the LORD; let all the inhabitants of the world dread Him.
33:9 For He spoke, and it was; He commanded, and it endured.
33:10 The LORD frustrates the plans of nations, brings to naught the designs of peoples.
33:11 What the LORD plans endures forever, what He designs, for ages on end.
33:12 Happy the nation whose God is the LORD, the people He has chosen to be His own.
33:13 The LORD looks down from heaven; He sees all mankind.
33:14 From His dwelling-place He gazes on all the inhabitants of the earth—
33:15 He who fashions the hearts of them all, who discerns all their doings.
33:16 Kings are not delivered by a large force; warriors are not saved by great strength;
33:17 horses are a false hope for deliverance; for all their great power they provide no escape.
33:18 Truly the eye of the LORD is on those who fear Him, who wait for His faithful care
33:19 to save them from death, to sustain them in famine.
33:20 We set our hope on the LORD, He is our help and shield;
33:21 in Him our hearts rejoice, for in His holy name we trust.
33:22 May we enjoy, O LORD, Your faithful care, as we have put our hope in You.
text.Grail1963:
1 Ring out your joy to the Lord, O you just;
for praise is fitting for loyal hearts.
2 Give thanks to the Lord upon the harp,
with a tenstringed lute sing him songs.
3 O sing him a song that is new,
play loudly, with all your skill.
4 For the word of the Lord is faithful
and all his works to be trusted.
5 The Lord loves justice and right
and fills the earth with his love.
6 By his word the heavens were made,
by the breath of his mouth all the stars.
7 He collects the waves of the ocean;
he stores up the depths of the sea.
8 Let all the earth fear the Lord
all who live in the world revere him.
9 He spoke; and it came to be.
He commanded; it sprang into being.
10 He frustrates the designs of the nations,
he defeats the plans of the peoples.
11 His own designs shall stand for ever,
the plans of his heart from age to age.
12 They are happy, whose God is the Lord,
the people he has chosen as his own.
13 From the heavens the Lord looks forth,
he sees all the children of men.
14 From the place where he dwells he gazes
on all the dwellers on the earth;

15 he who shapes the hearts of them all;
and considers all their deeds.
16 A king is not saved by his army,
nor a warrior preserved by his strength.
17 A vain hope for safety is the horse;
despite its power it cannot save.
18 The Lord looks on those who revere him,
on those who hope in his love,
19 to rescue their souls from death,
to keep them alive in famine.
20 Our soul is waiting for the Lord.
The Lord is our help and our shield.
21 In him do our hearts find joy.
We trust in his holy name.
22 May your love be upon us, O Lord,
as we place all our hope in you.
text.Rotherham:
33:1 Shout for joy, ye righteous, in Yahweh, To the upright, seemly is praise.
33:2 Give ye thanks unto Yahweh with the lyre, With a harp of ten strings, make ye music unto him.
33:3 Sing unto him, a song that is new, With skill, sweep the strings, with loud noise.
33:4 For right is the word of Yahweh, and, all his work, is in faithfulness:
33:5 Who loveth righteousness and justice, With the lovingkindness of Yahweh, the earth is full.
33:6 By the word of Yahweh, the heavens were made, and, by the spirit of his mouth, all their host:
33:7 Who gathered as into a skin-bottle the waters of the sea, Delivering, into treasuries, the roaring deeps.
33:8 Let all the earth, stand in awe of Yahweh, Of him, be in dread, all ye inhabitants of the world;
33:9 For, he, spake, and it was, He, commanded, and it stood forth.
33:10 Yahweh, hath frustrated the counsel of nations, hath brought to nothing the devices of peoples.
33:11 The counsel of Yahweh, to times age-abiding, shall stand, The devices of his heart, from generation to generation.
33:12 How happy the nation whose God is, Yahweh, The people he hath chosen as his own inheritance!
33:13 Out of the heavens, hath Yahweh looked, He hath seen all the sons of men:
33:14 Out of his settled place of abode, hath he fixed his gaze on all the inhabitants of the earth:
33:15 Who fashioneth their heart all together, Who understandeth all their doings.
33:16 Not a king, can be saved by greatness of force, nor, hero, deliver himself by greatness of strength:
33:17 A deception, is the horse, for victory, and, by his great strength, shall he not deliver.
33:18 Lo! the eye of Yahweh, is toward them who revere him, unto such as are waiting for his lovingkindness:
33:19 To rescue, from death, their soul, and to keep them alive in famine.
33:20 Our own soul, hath waited for Yahweh, Our help and our shield, is he!
33:21 For, in him, shall our heart rejoice, For, in his holy Name, have we trusted.
33:22 Be thy lovingkindness, O Yahweh, upon us, According as we have waited for thee.
text.NABRE:
33:1 Praise of God’s Power and Providence I Rejoice, you righteous, in the Lord; praise from the upright is fitting.
33:2 Give thanks to the Lord on the harp; on the ten-stringed lyre offer praise.
33:3 Sing to him a new song; skillfully play with joyful chant.
33:4 For the Lord ’s word is upright; all his works are trustworthy.
33:5 He loves justice and right. The earth is full of the mercy of the Lord.
33:6 II By the Lord ’s word the heavens were made; by the breath of his mouth all their host.
33:7 He gathered the waters of the sea as a mound; he sets the deep into storage vaults.
33:8 III Let all the earth fear the Lord; let all who dwell in the world show him reverence.
33:9 For he spoke, and it came to be, commanded, and it stood in place.
33:10 The Lord foils the plan of nations, frustrates the designs of peoples.
33:11 But the plan of the Lord stands forever, the designs of his heart through all generations.
33:12 Blessed is the nation whose God is the Lord, the people chosen as his inheritance.
33:13 IV From heaven the Lord looks down and observes the children of Adam,
33:14 From his dwelling place he surveys all who dwell on earth.
33:15 The One who fashioned together their hearts is the One who knows all their works.
33:16 V A king is not saved by a great army, nor a warrior delivered by great strength.
33:17 Useless is the horse for safety; despite its great strength, it cannot be saved.
33:18 Behold, the eye of the Lord is upon those who fear him, upon those who count on his mercy,
33:19 To deliver their soul from death, and to keep them alive through famine.
33:20 VI Our soul waits for the Lord, he is our help and shield.
33:21 For in him our hearts rejoice; in his holy name we trust.
33:22 May your mercy, Lord, be upon us; as we put our hope in you.
text.DRB:
33:1 To David, when he changed his countenance before Abimelech, and he dismist him, and he went away
33:2 I will bless our Lord at all time: his praise always in my mouth.
33:3 In our Lord my soul shall be praised: let the mild hear, and rejoice.
33:4 Magnify ye our Lord with me: and let us exalt his name for ever.
33:5 I have sought out our Lord, and he hath heard me: and from all my tribulations he hath delivered me.
33:6 Come ye to him, and be illuminated: and your faces shall not be confounded.
33:7 This poor man hath cried, and our Lord hath heard him: and from all his tribulations he hath saved him.
33:8 The Angel of our Lord shall put in himself about them that fear him: and shall deliver them.
33:9 Taste ye, and see that our Lord is sweet: blessed is the man, that hopeth in him.
33:10 Fear ye our Lord all ye his Saints: because there is no lack to them that fear him.
33:11 The rich have wanted, and have been hungry: but they that seek after our Lord shall not be diminished of any good.
33:12 Come children, hear me: I will teach you the fear of our Lord.
33:13 who is the man that will have life: loveth to see good days?
33:14 Stay thy tongue from evil: and thy lips that they speak not guile.
33:15 Turn away from evil, and do good: seek after peace and pursue it.
33:16 The eyes of our Lord upon the just: and his ears unto their prayers.
33:17 But the countenance of our Lord is upon them that do evil things: to destroy their memory out of the earth.
33:18 The just have cried, and our Lord hath heard them: and out of all their tribulations he hath delivered them.
33:19 Our Lord is nigh to them, that are of a contrite heart: and the humble of spirit he will save.
33:20 Many are the tribulations of the just: and out of all these our Lord will deliver them.
33:21 Our Lord keepeth all their bones: there shall not one of them be broken.
33:22 The death of sinners is very ill: and they that hate the just shall offend.
33:23 Our Lord will redeem the souls of his servants: and all that hope in him shall not offend.

text.NRSV:
34:1 I will bless the Lord at all times; his praise shall continually be in my mouth.
34:2 My soul makes its boast in the Lord; let the humble hear and be glad.
34:3 O magnify the Lord with me, and let us exalt his name together.
34:4 I sought the Lord, and he answered me, and delivered me from all my fears.
34:5 Look to him, and be radiant; so your faces shall never be ashamed.
34:6 This poor soul cried, and was heard by the Lord, and was saved from every trouble.
34:7 The angel of the Lord encamps around those who fear him, and delivers them.
34:8 O taste and see that the Lord is good; happy are those who take refuge in him.
34:9 O fear the Lord, you his holy ones, for those who fear him have no want.
34:10 The young lions suffer want and hunger, but those who seek the Lord lack no good thing.
34:11 Come, O children, listen to me; I will teach you the fear of the Lord.
34:12 Which of you desires life, and covets many days to enjoy good?
34:13 Keep your tongue from evil, and your lips from speaking deceit.
34:14 Depart from evil, and do good; seek peace, and pursue it.
34:15 The eyes of the Lord are on the righteous, and his ears are open to their cry.
34:16 The face of the Lord is against evildoers, to cut off the remembrance of them from the earth.
34:17 When the righteous cry for help, the Lord hears, and rescues them from all their troubles.
34:18 The Lord is near to the brokenhearted, and saves the crushed in spirit.
34:19 Many are the afflictions of the righteous, but the Lord rescues them from them all.
34:20 He keeps all their bones; not one of them will be broken.
34:21 Evil brings death to the wicked, and those who hate the righteous will be condemned.
34:22 The Lord redeems the life of his servants; none of those who take refuge in him will be condemned.
text.Coverdale:
34:1 I will bless the LORD at all times; * his praise shall ever be in my mouth.
34:2 I will glory in the LORD; * let the humble hear and rejoice.
34:3 Proclaim with me the greatness of the LORD; * let us exalt his Name together.
34:4 I sought the LORD, and he answered me * and delivered me out of all my terror.
34:5 Look upon him and be radiant, * and let not your faces be ashamed.
34:6 I called in my affliction and the LORD heard me * and saved me from all my troubles.
34:7 The angel of the LORD encompasses those who fear him, * and he will deliver them.
34:8 Taste and see that the LORD is good; * happy are they who trust in him!
34:9 Fear the LORD, you that are his saints, * for those who fear him lack nothing.
34:10 The young lions lack and suffer hunger, * but those who seek the LORD lack nothing that is good.
34:11 Come, children, and listen to me; * I will teach you the fear of the LORD.
34:12 Who among you loves life * and desires long life to enjoy prosperity?
34:13 Keep your tongue from evil-speaking * and your lips from lying words.
34:14 Turn from evil and do good; * seek peace and pursue it.
34:15 The eyes of the LORD are upon the righteous, * and his ears are open to their cry.
34:16 The face of the LORD is against those who do evil, * to root out the remembrance of them from the earth.
34:17 The righteous cry, and the LORD hears them * and delivers them from all their troubles.
34:18 The LORD is near to the brokenhearted * and will save those whose spirits are crushed.
34:19 Many are the troubles of the righteous, * but the LORD will deliver him out of them all.
34:20 He will keep safe all his bones; * not one of them shall be broken.
34:21 Evil shall slay the wicked, * and those who hate the righteous will be punished.
34:22 The LORD ransoms the life of his servants, * and none will be punished who trust in him.
text.KJV:
34:1 A Psalm of David, when he changed his behaviour before Abimelech; who drove him away, and he departed. I will bless the Lord at all times: his praise shall continually be in my mouth.
34:2 My soul shall make her boast in the Lord: the humble shall hear thereof, and be glad.
34:3 O magnify the Lord with me, and let us exalt his name together.
34:4 I sought the Lord, and he heard me, and delivered me from all my fears.
34:5 They looked unto him, and were lightened: and their faces were not ashamed.
34:6 This poor man cried, and the Lord heard him, and saved him out of all his troubles.
34:7 The angel of the Lord encampeth round about them that fear him, and delivereth them.
34:8 O taste and see that the Lord is good: blessed is the man that trusteth in him.
34:9 O fear the Lord, ye his saints: for there is no want to them that fear him.
34:10 The young lions do lack, and suffer hunger: but they that seek the Lord shall not want any good thing.
34:11 Come, ye children, hearken unto me: I will teach you the fear of the Lord.
34:12 What man is he that desireth life, and loveth many days, that he may see good?
34:13 Keep thy tongue from evil, and thy lips from speaking guile.
34:14 Depart from evil, and do good; seek peace, and pursue it.
34:15 The eyes of the Lord are upon the righteous, and his ears are open unto their cry.
34:16 The face of the Lord is against them that do evil, to cut off the remembrance of them from the earth.
34:17 The righteous cry, and the Lord heareth, and delivereth them out of all their troubles.
34:18 The Lord is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit.
34:19 Many are the afflictions of the righteous: but the Lord delivereth him out of them all.
34:20 He keepeth all his bones: not one of them is broken.
34:21 Evil shall slay the wicked: and they that hate the righteous shall be desolate.
34:22 The Lord redeemeth the soul of his servants: and none of them that trust in him shall be desolate.
text.JPS1985:
34:1 Of David, when he feigned madness in the presence of Abimelech, who turned him out, and he left.
34:2 I bless the LORD at all times; praise of Him is ever in my mouth.
34:3 I glory in the LORD; let the lowly hear it and rejoice.
34:4 Exalt the LORD with me; let us extol His name together.
34:5 I turned to the LORD, and He answered me; He saved me from all my terrors.
34:6 Men look to Him and are radiant; let their faces not be downcast.
34:7 Here was a lowly man who called, and the LORD listened, and delivered him from all his troubles.
34:8 The angel of the LORD camps around those who fear Him and rescues them.
34:9 Taste and see how good the LORD is; happy the man who takes refuge in Him!
34:10 Fear the LORD, you His consecrated ones, for those who fear Him lack nothing.
34:11 Lions have been reduced to starvation, but those who turn to the LORD shall not lack any good.
34:12 Come, my sons, listen to me; I will teach you what it is to fear the LORD.
34:13 Who is the man who is eager for life, who desires years of good fortune?
34:14 Guard your tongue from evil, your lips from deceitful speech.
34:15 Shun evil and do good, seek amity and pursue it.
34:16 The eyes of the LORD are on the righteous, His ears attentive to their cry.
34:17 The face of the LORD is set against evildoers, to erase their names from the earth.
34:18 They cry out, and the LORD hears, and saves them from all their troubles.
34:19 The LORD is close to the brokenhearted; those crushed in spirit He delivers.
34:20 Though the misfortunes of the righteous be many, the LORD will save him from them all,
34:21 Keeping all his bones intact, not one of them being broken.
34:22 One misfortune is the deathblow of the wicked; the foes of the righteous shall be ruined.
34:23 The LORD redeems the life of His servants; all who take refuge in Him shall not be ruined.
