text.Rotherham:
36:1 Declareth the transgression of the lawless one, within my heart, There is, no dread of God, before his eyes;
36:2 For he flattereth himself [too much] in his own eyes, to find his iniquity—to hate [it] .
36:3 The words of his mouth, are iniquity and deceit, he hath left off to show discretion by doing well:
36:4 Iniquity, deviseth he upon his bed,—he taketh his stand in a way, not good, Wrong, doth he not abhor!
36:5 O Yahweh! in the heavens, is thy lovingkindness, Thy faithfulness, as far as the fleecy clouds:
36:6 Thy righteousness, is like mighty mountains, and, thy just decrees, are a great resounding deep,—Man and beast, thou savest, O Yahweh!
36:7 How precious thy lovingkindness, O God,—Therefore, the sons of men, under the shadow of thy wings, seek refuge:
36:8 They abundantly relish the fatness of thy house,—And out of the full stream of thine own pleasures, thou givest them to drink.
36:9 For, with thee, is the fountain of life, In thy light, we see light.
36:10 Prolong thy lovingkindness unto them who know thee,—and thy righteousness, to the upright in heart.
36:11 Let not the foot of pride reach me, nor, the hand of the lawless, scare me away.
36:12 There did the workers of iniquity fall,—thrust down and not able to rise!
text.NABRE:
36:1 For the leader. Of David, the servant of the Lord.
36:2 I Sin directs the heart of the wicked man; his eyes are closed to the fear of God.
36:3 For he lives with the delusion: his guilt will not be known and hated.
36:4 Empty and false are the words of his mouth; he has ceased to be wise and do good.
36:5 On his bed he hatches plots; he sets out on a wicked way; he does not reject evil.
36:6 II Lord, your mercy reaches to heaven; your fidelity, to the clouds.
36:7 Your justice is like the highest mountains; your judgments, like the mighty deep; human being and beast you sustain, Lord.
36:8 How precious is your mercy, O God! The children of Adam take refuge in the shadow of your wings.
36:9 They feast on the rich food of your house; from your delightful stream you give them drink.
36:10 For with you is the fountain of life, and in your light we see light.
36:11 Show mercy on those who know you, your just defense to the upright of heart.
36:12 Do not let the foot of the proud overtake me, nor the hand of the wicked disturb me.
36:13 There make the evildoers fall; thrust them down, unable to rise.
text.DRB:
36:1 A Psalm to David himself. Have no emulation toward the malignant: neither envy them that do iniquity.
36:2 Because they shall quickly wither as grass: and as the blossoms of herbs they shall soon fall.
36:3 Hope in our Lord and do good: and inhabit the land, and thou shalt be fed in the riches thereof.
36:4 Be delighted in our Lord: & he will give thee the petitions of thy heart.
36:5 Reveal thy way to our Lord, and hope in him: and he will do it.
36:6 And he will bring forth thy justice as light: and thy judgment as midday:
36:7 Be subject to our Lord, and pray him. Have no emulation in him, that prospereth in his way: in a man that doth injustices.
36:8 Cease from wrath, and leave fury: have not emulation that thou be malignant.
36:9 Because they that are malignant, shall be cast out: but they that expect our Lord, the same shall inherit the land.
36:10 And yet a little while, and the sinner shall not be: and thou shalt seek his place, and shall not find it.
36:11 But the meek shall inherit the land, and shall be delighted in multitude of peace.
36:12 The sinner shall observe the just: & shall gnash upon him with his teeth.
36:13 But our Lord shall scorn him: because he foreseeth that his day shall come.
36:14 Sinners have drawn out the sword: they have bent their bow; That they may deceive the poor and needy: that they may murder the right of heart.
36:15 Let their own swords enter into their hearts: and let their bow be broken.
36:16 Better is a little to the just, above much riches of sinners.
36:17 Because the arms of sinners shall be broken in pieces: but our Lord confirmeth the just.
36:18 Our Lord knoweth the days of the immaculate: and their inheritance shall be for ever.
36:19 They shall not be confounded in the evil time, and in the days of famine they shall be filled:
36:20 because the sinners shall perish. But the enemies of our Lord forthwith as they shall be honoured and exalted, vanishing shall vanish as smoke.
36:21 The sinner shall borrow, and not pay: but the just is merciful and will give.
36:22 Because they that bless him shall inherit the land: but they that curse him shall perish.
36:23 With our Lord the step of man shall be directed: and he shall like well of his way.
36:24 When he shall fall, he shall not be bruised: because our Lord putteth his hand under.
36:25 I have been young, for I am old: and I have not seen the just forsaken, nor his seed seeking bread.
36:26 All the day he is merciful and lendeth: & his seed shall be in blessing.
36:27 Decline from evil, & do good: and inhabit for ever & ever.
36:28 Because our Lord loveth judgment, and he will not forsake his saints: they shall be preserved for ever. The unjust shall be punished: and the seed of the impious shall perish.
36:29 But the just shall inherit the land: and shall inhabit for ever and ever upon it.
36:30 The mouth of the just shall meditate wisdom, and his tongue shall speak judgment.
36:31 The law of his God in his heart: and his steps shall not be supplanted.
36:32 The sinner considereth the just: and seeketh to murder him.
36:33 But our Lord will not leave him in his hands: neither will he condemn him, when judgment shall be given of him.
36:34 Expect our Lord, and keep his way: and he will exalt thee, that thou mayst inherit the land: when the sinners shall perish thou shalt see.
36:35 I have seen the impious highly exalted, and advance as the cedars of Libanus.
36:36 And I passed by, and behold he was not: and I sought him, and his place was not found.
36:37 Keep innocency, and see equity: because there are remains for the peaceable man.
36:38 But the unjust shall perish together: the remains of the impious shall perish.
36:39 But the salvation of the just is of our Lord: and he is their protector in the time of tribulation.
36:40 And our Lord will help them, and deliver them: and he will take them away from sinners, and save them, because they have hoped in him.

text.NRSV:
37:1 Do not fret because of the wicked; do not be envious of wrongdoers,
37:2 for they will soon fade like the grass, and wither like the green herb.
37:3 Trust in the Lord, and do good; so you will live in the land, and enjoy security.
37:4 Take delight in the Lord, and he will give you the desires of your heart.
37:5 Commit your way to the Lord; trust in him, and he will act.
37:6 He will make your vindication shine like the light, and the justice of your cause like the noonday.
37:7 Be still before the Lord, and wait patiently for him; do not fret over those who prosper in their way, over those who carry out evil devices.
37:8 Refrain from anger, and forsake wrath. Do not fret—it leads only to evil.
37:9 For the wicked shall be cut off, but those who wait for the Lord shall inherit the land.
37:10 Yet a little while, and the wicked will be no more; though you look diligently for their place, they will not be there.
37:11 But the meek shall inherit the land, and delight themselves in abundant prosperity.
37:12 The wicked plot against the righteous, and gnash their teeth at them;
37:13 but the Lord laughs at the wicked, for he sees that their day is coming.
37:14 The wicked draw the sword and bend their bows to bring down the poor and needy, to kill those who walk uprightly;
37:15 their sword shall enter their own heart, and their bows shall be broken.
37:16 Better is a little that the righteous person has than the abundance of many wicked.
37:17 For the arms of the wicked shall be broken, but the Lord upholds the righteous.
37:18 The Lord knows the days of the blameless, and their heritage will abide forever;
37:19 they are not put to shame in evil times, in the days of famine they have abundance.
37:20 But the wicked perish, and the enemies of the Lord are like the glory of the pastures; they vanish—like smoke they vanish away.
37:21 The wicked borrow, and do not pay back, but the righteous are generous and keep giving;
37:22 for those blessed by the Lord shall inherit the land, but those cursed by him shall be cut off.
37:23 Our steps are made firm by the Lord, when he delights in our way;
37:24 though we stumble, we shall not fall headlong, for the Lord holds us by the hand.
37:25 I have been young, and now am old, yet I have not seen the righteous forsaken or their children begging bread.
37:26 They are ever giving liberally and lending, and their children become a blessing.
37:27 Depart from evil, and do good; so you shall abide forever.
37:28 For the Lord loves justice; he will not forsake his faithful ones. The righteous shall be kept safe forever, but the children of the wicked shall be cut off.
37:29 The righteous shall inherit the land, and live in it forever.
37:30 The mouths of the righteous utter wisdom, and their tongues speak justice.
37:31 The law of their God is in their hearts; their steps do not slip.
37:32 The wicked watch for the righteous, and seek to kill them.
37:33 The Lord will not abandon them to their power, or let them be condemned when they are brought to trial.
37:34 Wait for the Lord, and keep to his way, and he will exalt you to inherit the land; you will look on the destruction of the wicked.
37:35 I have seen the wicked oppressing, and towering like a cedar of Lebanon.
37:36 Again I passed by, and they were no more; though I sought them, they could not be found.
37:37 Mark the blameless, and behold the upright, for there is posterity for the peaceable.
37:38 But transgressors shall be altogether destroyed; the posterity of the wicked shall be cut off.
37:39 The salvation of the righteous is from the Lord; he is their refuge in the time of trouble.
37:40 The Lord helps them and rescues them; he rescues them from the wicked, and saves them, because they take refuge in him.
text.Coverdale:
37:1 Do not fret yourself because of evildoers; * do not be jealous of those who do wrong.
37:2 For they shall soon wither like the grass, * and like the green grass fade away.
37:3 Put your trust in the LORD and do good; * dwell in the land and feed on its riches.
37:4 Take delight in the LORD, * and he shall give you your heart's desire.
37:5 Commit your way to the LORD and put your trust in him, * and he will bring it to pass.
37:6 He will make your righteousness as clear as the light * and your just dealing as the noonday.
37:7 Be still before the LORD * and wait patiently for him.
37:8 Do not fret yourself over the one who prospers, * the one who succeeds in evil schemes.
37:9 Refrain from anger, leave rage alone; * do not fret yourself; it leads only to evil.
37:10 For evildoers shall be cut off, * but those who wait upon the LORD shall possess the land.
37:11 In a little while the wicked shall be no more; * you shall search out their place, but they will not be there.
37:12 But the lowly shall possess the land; * they will delight in abundance of peace.
37:13 The wicked plot against the righteous * and gnash at them with their teeth.
37:14 The Lord laughs at the wicked, * because he sees that their day will come.
37:15 The wicked draw their sword and bend their bow to strike down the poor and needy, * to slaughter those who are upright in their ways.
37:16 Their sword shall go through their own heart, * and their bow shall be broken.
37:17 The little that the righteous has * is better than great riches of the wicked.
37:18 For the power of the wicked shall be broken, * but the LORD upholds the righteous. Psalm 37: Part II Novit Dominus
37:19 The LORD cares for the lives of the godly, * and their inheritance shall last for ever.
37:20 They shall not be ashamed in bad times, * and in days of famine they shall have enough.
37:21 As for the wicked, they shall perish, * and the enemies of the LORD, like the glory of the meadows, shall vanish; they shall vanish like smoke.
37:22 The wicked borrow and do not repay, * but the righteous are generous in giving.
37:23 Those who are blessed by God shall possess the land, * but those who are cursed by him shall be destroyed.
37:24 Our steps are directed by the LORD; * he strengthens those in whose way he delights.
37:25 If they stumble, they shall not fall headlong, * for the LORD holds them by the hand.
37:26 I have been young and now I am old, * but never have I seen the righteous forsaken, or their children begging bread.
37:27 The righteous are always generous in their lending, * and their children shall be a blessing.
37:28 Turn from evil, and do good, * and dwell in the land for ever.
37:29 For the LORD loves justice; * he does not forsake his faithful ones.
37:30 They shall be kept safe for ever, * but the offspring of the wicked shall be destroyed.
37:31 The righteous shall possess the land * and dwell in it for ever.
37:32 The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom, * and their tongue speaks what is right.
37:33 The law of their God is in their heart, * and their footsteps shall not falter.
37:34 The wicked spy on the righteous * and seek occasion to kill them.
37:35 The LORD will not abandon them to their hand, * nor let them be found guilty when brought to trial.
37:36 Wait upon the LORD and keep his way; * he will raise you up to possess the land, and when the wicked are cut off, you will see it.
37:37 I have seen the wicked in their arrogance, * flourishing like a tree in full leaf.
37:38 I went by, and behold, they were not there; * I searched for them, but they could not be found.
37:39 Mark those who are honest; observe the upright; * for there is a future for the peaceable.
37:40 Transgressors shall be destroyed, one and all; * the future of the wicked is cut off.
37:41 But the deliverance of the righteous comes from the LORD; * he is their stronghold in time of trouble.
37:42 The LORD will help them and rescue them; * he will rescue them from the wicked and deliver them, because they seek refuge in him.
text.KJV:
37:1 A Psalm of David. Fret not thyself because of evildoers, neither be thou envious against the workers of iniquity.
37:2 For they shall soon be cut down like the grass, and wither as the green herb.
37:3 Trust in the Lord, and do good; so shalt thou dwell in the land, and verily thou shalt be fed.
37:4 Delight thyself also in the Lord; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart.
37:5 Commit thy way unto the Lord; trust also in him; and he shall bring it to pass.
37:6 And he shall bring forth thy righteousness as the light, and thy judgment as the noonday.
37:7 Rest in the Lord, and wait patiently for him: fret not thyself because of him who prospereth in his way, because of the man who bringeth wicked devices to pass.
37:8 Cease from anger, and forsake wrath: fret not thyself in any wise to do evil.
37:9 For evildoers shall be cut off: but those that wait upon the Lord, they shall inherit the earth.
37:10 For yet a little while, and the wicked shall not be: yea, thou shalt diligently consider his place, and it shall not be.
37:11 But the meek shall inherit the earth; and shall delight themselves in the abundance of peace.
37:12 The wicked plotteth against the just, and gnasheth upon him with his teeth.
37:13 The Lord shall laugh at him: for he seeth that his day is coming.
37:14 The wicked have drawn out the sword, and have bent their bow, to cast down the poor and needy, and to slay such as be of upright conversation.
37:15 Their sword shall enter into their own heart, and their bows shall be broken.
37:16 A little that a righteous man hath is better than the riches of many wicked.
37:17 For the arms of the wicked shall be broken: but the Lord upholdeth the righteous.
37:18 The Lord knoweth the days of the upright: and their inheritance shall be for ever.
37:19 They shall not be ashamed in the evil time: and in the days of famine they shall be satisfied.
37:20 But the wicked shall perish, and the enemies of the Lord shall be as the fat of lambs: they shall consume; into smoke shall they consume away.
37:21 The wicked borroweth, and payeth not again: but the righteous sheweth mercy, and giveth.
37:22 For such as be blessed of him shall inherit the earth; and they that be cursed of him shall be cut off.
37:23 The steps of a good man are ordered by the Lord: and he delighteth in his way.
37:24 Though he fall, he shall not be utterly cast down: for the Lord upholdeth him with his hand.
37:25 I have been young, and now am old; yet have I not seen the righteous forsaken, nor his seed begging bread.
37:26 He is ever merciful, and lendeth; and his seed is blessed.
37:27 Depart from evil, and do good; and dwell for evermore.
37:28 For the Lord loveth judgment, and forsaketh not his saints; they are preserved for ever: but the seed of the wicked shall be cut off.
37:29 The righteous shall inherit the land, and dwell therein for ever.
37:30 The mouth of the righteous speaketh wisdom, and his tongue talketh of judgment.
37:31 The law of his God is in his heart; none of his steps shall slide.
37:32 The wicked watcheth the righteous, and seeketh to slay him.
37:33 The Lord will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.
37:34 Wait on the Lord, and keep his way, and he shall exalt thee to inherit the land: when the wicked are cut off, thou shalt see it.
37:35 I have seen the wicked in great power, and spreading himself like a green bay tree.
37:36 Yet he passed away, and, lo, he was not: yea, I sought him, but he could not be found.
37:37 Mark the perfect man, and behold the upright: for the end of that man is peace.
37:38 But the transgressors shall be destroyed together: the end of the wicked shall be cut off.
37:39 But the salvation of the righteous is of the Lord: he is their strength in the time of trouble.
37:40 And the Lord shall help them, and deliver them: he shall deliver them from the wicked, and save them, because they trust in him.
text.JPS1985:
37:1 Of David. Do not be vexed by evil men; do not be incensed by wrongdoers;
37:2 for they soon wither like grass, like verdure fade away.
37:3 Trust in the LORD and do good, abide in the land and remain loyal.
37:4 Seek the favor of the LORD, and He will grant you the desires of your heart.
37:5 Leave all to the LORD; trust in Him; He will do it.
37:6 He will cause your vindication to shine forth like the light, the justice of your case, like the noonday sun.
37:7 Be patient and wait for the LORD, do not be vexed by the prospering man who carries out his schemes.
37:8 Give up anger, abandon fury, do not be vexed; it can only do harm.
37:9 For evil men will be cut off, but those who look to the LORD — they shall inherit the land.
37:10 A little longer and there will be no wicked man; you will look at where he was— he will be gone.
37:11 But the lowly shall inherit the land, and delight in abundant well-being.
37:12 The wicked man schemes against the righteous, and gnashes his teeth at him.
37:13 The Lord laughs at him, for He knows that his day will come.
37:14 The wicked draw their swords, bend their bows, to bring down the lowly and needy, to slaughter upright men.
37:15 Their swords shall pierce their own hearts, and their bows shall be broken.
37:16 Better the little that the righteous man has than the great abundance of the wicked.
37:17 For the arms of the wicked shall be broken, but the LORD is the support of the righteous.
37:18 The LORD is concerned for the needs of the blameless; their portion lasts forever;
37:19 they shall not come to grief in bad times; in famine, they shall eat their fill.
37:20 But the wicked shall perish, and the enemies of the LORD shall be consumed, like meadow grass consumed in smoke.
37:21 The wicked man borrows and does not repay; the righteous is generous and keeps giving.
37:22 Those blessed by Him shall inherit the land, but those cursed by Him shall be cut off.
37:23 The steps of a man are made firm by the LORD, when He delights in his way.
37:24 Though he stumbles, he does not fall down, for the LORD gives him support.
37:25 I have been young and am now old, but I have never seen a righteous man abandoned, or his children seeking bread.
37:26 He is always generous, and lends, and his children are held blessed.
37:27 Shun evil and do good, and you shall abide forever.
37:28 For the LORD loves what is right, He does not abandon His faithful ones. They are preserved forever, while the children of the wicked will be cut off.
37:29 The righteous shall inherit the land, and abide forever in it.
37:30 The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom, and his tongue speaks what is right.
37:31 The teaching of his God is in his heart; his feet do not slip.
37:32 The wicked watches for the righteous, seeking to put him to death;
37:33 the LORD will not abandon him to his power; He will not let him be condemned in judgment.
37:34 Look to the LORD and keep to His way, and He will raise you high that you may inherit the land; when the wicked are cut off, you shall see it.
37:35 I saw a wicked man, powerful, well-rooted like a robust native tree.
37:36 Suddenly he vanished and was gone; I sought him, but he was not to be found.
37:37 Mark the blameless, note the upright, for there is a future for the man of integrity.
37:38 But transgressors shall be utterly destroyed, the future of the wicked shall be cut off.
37:39 The deliverance of the righteous comes from the LORD, their stronghold in time of trouble.
37:40 The LORD helps them and rescues them, rescues them from the wicked and delivers them, for they seek refuge in Him.
text.Grail1963:
1 Do not fret because of the wicked;
do not envy those who do evil,

2 for they wither quickly like grass
and fade like the green of the fields.
3 If you trust in the Lord and do good,
then you will live in the land and be secure.
4 If you find your delight in the Lord,
he will grant your heart's desire.
5 Commit your life to the Lord,
trust in him and he will act,
6 so that your justice breaks forth like the light,
your cause like the noonday sun.
7 Be still before the Lord and wait in patience;
do not fret at the man who prospers;
a man who makes evil plots
14c to bring down the needy and the poor.
8 Calm your anger and forget your rage;
do not fret, it only leads to evil.
9 For those who do evil shall perish;
the patient shall inherit the land.
10 A little longer--and the wicked shall have gone.
Look at his place, he is not there.
11 But the humble shall own the land
and enjoy the fullness of peace.
12 The wicked man plots against the just
and gnashes his teeth against him;
13 but the Lord laughs at the wicked
for he sees that his day is at hand.
14 The sword of the wicked is drawn,
his bow is bent to slaughter the upright.
15 Their sword shall pierce their own hearts
and their bows shall be broken to pieces.
16 The just man's few possessions
are better than the wicked man's wealth;
17 for the power of the wicked shall be broken
and the Lord will support the just.
18 He protects the lives of the upright,
their heritage will last for ever.
19 They shall not be put to shame in evil days,
in time of famine their food shall not fail.
20 But all the wicked shall perish
and all the enemies of the Lord.
They are like the beauty of the meadows,
they shall vanish, they shall vanish like smoke.
21 The wicked man borrows without repaying,
but the just man is generous and gives.
22 Those blessed by the Lord shall own the land,
but those he has cursed shall be destroyed.

23 The Lord guides the steps of a man
and makes safe the path of one he loves.
24 Though he stumble he shall never fall
for the Lord holds him by the hand.
25 I was young and now I am old,
but I have never seen the just man forsaken
nor his children begging for bread.
26 All the day he is generous and lends
and his children become a blessing.
27 Then turn away from evil and do good
and you shall have a home for ever;
28 for the Lord loves justice
and will never forsake his friends.
The unjust shall be wiped out for ever
and the children of the wicked destroyed.
29 The just shall inherit the land;
there they shall live for ever.
30 The just man's mouth utters wisdom
and his lips speak what is right;
31 the law of his God is in his heart,
his steps shall be saved from stumbling.
32 The wicked man watches for the just
and seeks occasion to kill him.
33 The Lord will not leave him in his power
nor let him be condemned when he is judged.
34 Then wait for the Lord, keep to his way.
It is he who will free you from the wicked,
raise you up to possess the land
and see the wicked destroyed.
35 I have seen the wicked triumphant,
towering like a cedar of Lebanon.
36 I passed by again; he was gone.
I searched; he was nowhere to be found.
37 See the just man, mark the upright,
for the peaceful man a future lies in store,
38 but sinners shall all be destroyed.
No future lies in store for the wicked.
39 The salvation of the just comes from the Lord,
their stronghold in time of distress.
40 The Lord helps them and delivers them
and saves them: for their refuge is in him.
text.Rotherham:
37:1 Burn not with vexation because of evil-doers, Be not envious of the workers of perversity;
37:2 For, like grass, soon shall they wither, and, like green herbage, shall they fade.
37:3 Trust in Yahweh, and do good, Dwell in the land, and feed on fidelity;
37:4 Yea, rest thy delight on Yahweh, that he may give thee the requests of thy heart.
37:5 Roll on Yahweh thy way, Trust also in him, and, he, will effectually work:
37:6 So will he bring forth, as the light, thy righteousness, and thy vindication as the noonday.
37:7 Be resigned to Yahweh, yea wait with longing for him; Burn not with vexation at him who prospereth in his way,—at the man who doeth wickedness.
37:8 Cease from anger, and forsake wrath, Burn not with vexation—[it would be] only to do evil;
37:9 For, evil doers, shall be cut off, but, as for them who wait for Yahweh, they, shall inherit the earth.
37:10 Yet a little, therefore, and the lawless one shall not be, Yea thou shalt look about, over his place—and he shall have vanished!
37:11 But, the patient oppressed-ones, shall inherit the earth, and shall delight themselves over the abundance of prosperity.
37:12 Plotting is the lawless one, against the just, and gnashing upon him with his teeth.
37:13 My Lord, shall laugh at him, for he seeth, that his day, will come.
37:14 A sword, have the lawless, drawn out, and have trodden their bow,—To bring down the oppressed and the needy, To slaughter the upright in life:
37:15 Their sword, shall enter into their own heart, and, their bow, shall be broken.
37:16 Better the little of the righteous man, than the abundance of the lawless who are mighty;
37:17 For, the arms of the lawless, shall be broken, But Yahweh is upholding the righteous.
37:18 Yahweh knoweth the days of the blameless, that, their inheritance, unto times age-abiding, shall continue.
37:19 They shall not be ashamed in the time of calamity, and, in the days of famine, shall they be filled.
37:20 For, the lawless, shall perish, and, the foes of Yahweh, be like the glory of the meadows, They have vanished! In smoke, have they vanished!
37:21 A lawless man borroweth, and will not repay, But, a righteous man, showeth favour and giveth;
37:22 For, such as are blessed of him, shall inherit the earth, But, the cursed of him, shall be cut off.
37:23 From Yahweh, are the steps of a man made firm, When, with his way, he is well pleased:
37:24 Though he fall, he shall not be hurled headlong, For, Yahweh, is holding his hand.
37:25 Young, have I been, moreover am old,—Yet have I not seen, A righteous man forsaken, Nor his seed begging bread:
37:26 All day long, is he showing favour and lending, his seed, therefore, shall have a blessing.
37:27 Turn from evil, and do good, and so settle down, unto times age-abiding.
37:28 For, Yahweh, loveth justice, and will not forsake his men of lovingkindness, Unto times age-abiding, have the perverse been destroyed,—and the seed of the lawless, been cut off.
37:29 The righteous, shall inherit the earth, that they may settle down, to futurity, thereupon.
37:30 The mouth of a righteous man, softly uttereth wisdom, and, his tongue, speaketh justice:
37:31 The law of his God, is in his heart, his steps shall not swerve.
37:32 The lawless man, lieth in wait, for the just, and seeketh to put him to death:
37:33 Yahweh, will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him, when he is judged.
37:34 Wait for Yahweh, and observe thou his path, that he may exalt thee, to inherit the earth, On the cutting off of the lawless, shalt thou look.
37:35 I have seen a lawless man, a tyrant, and spreading himself out, like a cedar in Lebanon;
37:36 Then I passed by, and lo! he had vanished! Yea I sought him, but he could not be found.
37:37 Mark the blameless man, and behold the upright, for there is a hereafter for the man of peace;
37:38 But, transgressors, are to be destroyed together, the hereafter of lawless men, is to be cut off.
37:39 But, the deliverance of the righteous, is from Yahweh, their refuge in a time of distress.
37:40 Thus hath Yahweh helped them, thus hath he delivered them,—He will deliver them from the lawless, and will save them, because they have sought refuge in him.
text.NABRE:
37:1 Of David. Do not be provoked by evildoers; do not envy those who do wrong.
37:2 Like grass they wither quickly; like green plants they wilt away.
37:3 Beth Trust in the Lord and do good that you may dwell in the land and live secure.
37:4 Find your delight in the Lord who will give you your heart’s desire.
37:5 Gimel Commit your way to the Lord; trust in him and he will act
37:6 And make your righteousness shine like the dawn, your justice like noonday.
37:7 Daleth Be still before the Lord; wait for him. Do not be provoked by the prosperous, nor by malicious schemers.
37:8 He Refrain from anger; abandon wrath; do not be provoked; it brings only harm.
37:9 Those who do evil will be cut off, but those who wait for the Lord will inherit the earth.
37:10 Waw Wait a little, and the wicked will be no more; look for them and they will not be there.
37:11 But the poor will inherit the earth, will delight in great prosperity.
37:12 Zayin The wicked plot against the righteous and gnash their teeth at them;
37:13 But my Lord laughs at them, because he sees that their day is coming.
37:14 Heth The wicked unsheath their swords; they string their bows To fell the poor and oppressed, to slaughter those whose way is upright.
37:15 Their swords will pierce their own hearts; their bows will be broken.
37:16 Teth Better the meagerness of the righteous one than the plenty of the wicked.
37:17 The arms of the wicked will be broken, while the Lord will sustain the righteous.
37:18 Yodh The Lord knows the days of the blameless; their heritage lasts forever.
37:19 They will not be ashamed when times are bad; in days of famine they will be satisfied.
37:20 Kaph The wicked perish, enemies of the Lord; They shall be consumed like fattened lambs; like smoke they disappear.
37:21 Lamedh The wicked one borrows but does not repay; the righteous one is generous and gives.
37:22 For those blessed by the Lord will inherit the earth, but those accursed will be cut off.
37:23 Mem The valiant one whose steps are guided by the Lord, who will delight in his way,
37:24 May stumble, but he will never fall, for the Lord holds his hand.
37:25 Nun Neither in my youth, nor now in old age have I seen the righteous one abandoned or his offspring begging for bread.
37:26 All day long he is gracious and lends, and his offspring become a blessing.
37:27 Samekh Turn from evil and do good, that you may be settled forever.
37:28 For the Lord loves justice and does not abandon the faithful. Ayin When the unjust are destroyed, and the offspring of the wicked cut off,
37:29 The righteous will inherit the earth and dwell in it forever.
37:30 Pe The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom; his tongue speaks what is right.
37:31 God’s teaching is in his heart; his steps do not falter.
37:32 Sadhe The wicked spies on the righteous and seeks to kill him.
37:33 But the Lord does not abandon him in his power, nor let him be condemned when tried.
37:34 Qoph Wait eagerly for the Lord, and keep his way; He will raise you up to inherit the earth; you will see when the wicked are cut off.
37:35 Resh I have seen a ruthless scoundrel, spreading out like a green cedar.
37:36 When I passed by again, he was gone; though I searched, he could not be found.
37:37 Shin Observe the person of integrity and mark the upright; Because there is a future for a man of peace.
37:38 Sinners will be destroyed together; the future of the wicked will be cut off.
37:39 Taw The salvation of the righteous is from the Lord, their refuge in a time of distress.
37:40 The Lord helps and rescues them, rescues and saves them from the wicked, because they take refuge in him.
text.DRB:
37:1 A Psalm of David, in recordation of the sabbath.
37:2 Lord rebuke me not in thy fury: nor chastise me in wrath.
37:3 Because thy arrows are fast sticked in me: and thou hast fastened thy hand upon me.
37:4 There is no health in my flesh at the face of thy wrath: my bones have no peace at the face of my sins.
37:5 Because mine iniquities are gone over my head: and as a heavy burden are become heavy upon me.
37:6 My scars are putrefied and corrupted, because of my foolishness.
37:7 I am become miserable, and am made crooked even to the end: I went sorrowful all the day.
37:8 Because my loins are filled with illusions: and there is no health in my flesh.
37:9 I am afflicted and am humbled exceedingly: I roared for the groaning of my heart.
37:10 Lord before thee is all my desire: and my groaning is not hid from thee.
37:11 My heart is troubled, my strength hath forsaken me: and the light of mine eyes, and the same is not with me.
37:12 My friends, and my neighbours have approached, & stood against me. And they that were near me, stood far off:
37:13 and they did violence which sought my soul. And they that sought me evils spake vanities: and meditated guiles all the day.
37:14 But I as one deaf did not hear: and as one dumb not opening his mouth.
37:15 And I became as a man not hearing: and not having reproofs in his mouth.
37:16 Because in thee, o Lord have I hoped, thou wilt hear me, o Lord my God.
37:17 Because I said: Lest sometime mine enemies rejoice over me, and whilst my feet are moved, they spake great things upon me.
37:18 Because I am ready for scourges: and my sorrow is in my sight always:
37:19 Because I will declare my iniquity: and I will think for my sin.
37:20 But mine enemies live, and are confirmed over me: and they are multiplied that hate me unjustly.
37:21 They that repay evil things for good, detracted from me: because I followed goodness.
37:22 Forsake me not, o Lord my God, depart not from me.
37:23 Attend unto my help, o Lord the God of my salvation.

text.NRSV:
38:1 O Lord, do not rebuke me in your anger, or discipline me in your wrath.
38:2 For your arrows have sunk into me, and your hand has come down on me.
38:3 There is no soundness in my flesh because of your indignation; there is no health in my bones because of my sin.
38:4 For my iniquities have gone over my head; they weigh like a burden too heavy for me.
38:5 My wounds grow foul and fester because of my foolishness;
38:6 I am utterly bowed down and prostrate; all day long I go around mourning.
38:7 For my loins are filled with burning, and there is no soundness in my flesh.
38:8 I am utterly spent and crushed; I groan because of the tumult of my heart.
38:9 O Lord, all my longing is known to you; my sighing is not hidden from you.
38:10 My heart throbs, my strength fails me; as for the light of my eyes—it also has gone from me.
38:11 My friends and companions stand aloof from my affliction, and my neighbors stand far off.
38:12 Those who seek my life lay their snares; those who seek to hurt me speak of ruin, and meditate treachery all day long.
38:13 But I am like the deaf, I do not hear; like the mute, who cannot speak.
38:14 Truly, I am like one who does not hear, and in whose mouth is no retort.
38:15 But it is for you, O Lord, that I wait; it is you, O Lord my God, who will answer.
38:16 For I pray,"Only do not let them rejoice over me, those who boast against me when my foot slips."
38:17 For I am ready to fall, and my pain is ever with me.
38:18 I confess my iniquity; I am sorry for my sin.
38:19 Those who are my foes without cause are mighty, and many are those who hate me wrongfully.
38:20 Those who render me evil for good are my adversaries because I follow after good.
38:21 Do not forsake me, O Lord; O my God, do not be far from me;
38:22 make haste to help me, O Lord, my salvation.
text.Coverdale:
38:1 O LORD, do not rebuke me in your anger; * do not punish me in your wrath.
38:2 For your arrows have already pierced me, * and your hand presses hard upon me.
38:3 There is no health in my flesh, because of your indignation; * there is no soundness in my body, because of my sin.
38:4 For my iniquities overwhelm me; * like a heavy burden they are too much for me to bear.
38:5 My wounds stink and fester * by reason of my foolishness.
38:6 I am utterly bowed down and prostrate; * I go about in mourning all the day long.
38:7 My loins are filled with searing pain; * there is no health in my body.
38:8 I am utterly numb and crushed; * I wail, because of the groaning of my heart.
38:9 O Lord, you know all my desires, * and my sighing is not hidden from you.
38:10 My heart is pounding, my strength has failed me, * and the brightness of my eyes is gone from me.
38:11 My friends and companions draw back from my affliction; * my neighbors stand afar off.
38:12 Those who seek after my life lay snares for me; * those who strive to hurt me speak of my ruin and plot treachery all the day long.
38:13 But I am like the deaf who do not hear, * like those who are mute and who do not open their mouth.
38:14 I have become like one who does not hear * and from whose mouth comes no defense.
38:15 For in you, O LORD, have I fixed my hope; * you will answer me, O Lord my God.
38:16 For I said,"Do not let them rejoice at my expense, * those who gloat over me when my foot slips."
38:17 Truly, I am on the verge of falling, * and my pain is always with me.
38:18 I will confess my iniquity * and be sorry for my sin.
38:19 Those who are my enemies without cause are mighty, * and many in number are those who wrongfully hate me.
38:20 Those who repay evil for good slander me, * because I follow the course that is right.
38:21 O LORD, do not forsake me; * be not far from me, O my God.
38:22 Make haste to help me, * O Lord of my salvation.
text.KJV:
38:1 A Psalm of David, to bring to remembrance. O Lord, rebuke me not in thy wrath: neither chasten me in thy hot displeasure.
38:2 For thine arrows stick fast in me, and thy hand presseth me sore.
38:3 There is no soundness in my flesh because of thine anger; neither is there any rest in my bones because of my sin.
38:4 For mine iniquities are gone over mine head: as an heavy burden they are too heavy for me.
38:5 My wounds stink and are corrupt because of my foolishness.
38:6 I am troubled; I am bowed down greatly; I go mourning all the day long.
38:7 For my loins are filled with a loathsome disease: and there is no soundness in my flesh.
38:8 I am feeble and sore broken: I have roared by reason of the disquietness of my heart.
38:9 Lord, all my desire is before thee; and my groaning is not hid from thee.
38:10 My heart panteth, my strength faileth me: as for the light of mine eyes, it also is gone from me.
38:11 My lovers and my friends stand aloof from my sore; and my kinsmen stand afar off.
38:12 They also that seek after my life lay snares for me: and they that seek my hurt speak mischievous things, and imagine deceits all the day long.
38:13 But I, as a deaf man, heard not; and I was as a dumb man that openeth not his mouth.
38:14 Thus I was as a man that heareth not, and in whose mouth are no reproofs.
38:15 For in thee, O Lord, do I hope: thou wilt hear, O Lord my God.
38:16 For I said, Hear me, lest otherwise they should rejoice over me: when my foot slippeth, they magnify themselves against me.
38:17 For I am ready to halt, and my sorrow is continually before me.
38:18 For I will declare mine iniquity; I will be sorry for my sin.
38:19 But mine enemies are lively, and they are strong: and they that hate me wrongfully are multiplied.
38:20 They also that render evil for good are mine adversaries; because I follow the thing that good is.
38:21 Forsake me not, O Lord: O my God, be not far from me.
38:22 Make haste to help me, O Lord my salvation.
text.JPS1985:
38:1 A psalm of David. Lehazkir.
38:2 O LORD, do not punish me in wrath; do not chastise me in fury.
38:3 For Your arrows have struck me; Your blows have fallen upon me.
38:4 There is no soundness in my flesh because of Your rage, no wholeness in my bones because of my sin.
38:5 For my iniquities have overwhelmed me; they are like a heavy burden, more than I can bear.
38:6 My wounds stink and fester because of my folly.
38:7 I am all bent and bowed; I walk about in gloom all day long.
38:8 For my sinews are full of fever; there is no soundness in my flesh.
38:9 I am all benumbed and crushed; I roar because of the turmoil in my mind.
38:10 O Lord, You are aware of all my entreaties; my groaning is not hidden from You.
38:11 My mind reels; my strength fails me; my eyes too have lost their luster.
38:12 My friends and companions stand back from my affliction; my kinsmen stand far off.
38:13 Those who seek my life lay traps; those who wish me harm speak malice; they utter deceit all the time.
38:14 But I am like a deaf man, unhearing, like a dumb man who cannot speak up;
38:15 I am like one who does not hear, who has no retort on his lips.
38:16 But I wait for You, O LORD; You will answer, O Lord, my God.
38:17 For I fear they will rejoice over me; when my foot gives way they will vaunt themselves against me.
38:18 For I am on the verge of collapse; my pain is always with me.
38:19 I acknowledge my iniquity; I am fearful over my sin;
38:20 for my mortal enemies are numerous; my treacherous foes are many.
38:21 Those who repay evil for good harass me for pursuing good.
38:22 Do not abandon me, O LORD; my God, be not far from me;
38:23 hasten to my aid, O Lord, my deliverance.
text.Grail1963:
.2 O Lord, do not rebuke me in your anger;

do not punish me, Lord, in your rage.
3 Your arrows have sunk deep in me;
your hand has come down upon me.
4 Through your anger all my body is sick:
through my sin, there is no health in my limbs.
5 My guilt towers higher than my head;
it is a weight too heavy to bear.
6 My wounds are foul and festering,
the result of my own folly.
7 I am bowed and brought to my knees.
I go mourning all the day long.
8 All my frame burns with fever;
all my body is sick.
9 Spent and utterly crushed,
I cry aloud in anguish of heart.
10 O Lord, you know all my longing:
my groans are not hidden from you.
11 My heart throbs, my strength is spent;
the very light has gone from my eyes.
12 My friends avoid me like a leper;
those closest to me stand afar off.
13 Those who plot against my life lay snares;
those who seek my ruin speak of harm,
planning treachery all the day long.
14 But I am like the deaf who cannot hear,
like the dumb unable to speak.
15 I am like a man who hears nothing
in whose mouth is no defense.
16 I count on you, O Lord:
it is you, Lord God, who will answer.
17 I pray: "Do not let them mock me,
those who triumph if my foot should slip."
18 For I am on the point of falling
and my pain is always before me.
19 I confess that I am guilty
and my sin fills me with dismay.
20 My wanton enemies are numberless
and my lying foes are many.
21 They repay me evil for good
and attack me for seeking what is right.
22 O Lord, do not forsake me!
My God, do not stay afar off!
23 Make haste and come to my help,
O Lord, my God, my savior!
text.Rotherham:
38:1 O Yahweh, do not, in thine anger, correct me, nor, in thy wrath, chastise me;
38:2 For, thine arrows, have sunk down into me, and thy hand, presseth heavily upon me.
38:3 There is no soundness in my flesh, By reason of thine indignation, There is no peace in my bones, By reason of my sin;
38:4 For, mine iniquities, have passed over my head, Like a heavy burden, they are too heavy for me:
38:5 My wounds are of bad odour—they have festered, by reason of my folly:
38:6 I am bent, I am bowed down very low. All the day, have I gloomily walked;
38:7 For, my loins, are filled with inflammation, and there is no soundness in my flesh:
38:8 I am benumbed and crushed exceedingly,—I have cried aloud because of the groaning of my heart.
38:9 O My Lord! before thee, is all my longing, and, my sighing, from thee, hath not been hid:
38:10 My heart, fluttereth, my strength hath forsaken me, and, as for the light of mine eyes, even they, are not with me:
38:11 My lovers, and my friends, from before my stroke, stand aloof,—and, my near ones, far away, do stand:
38:12 Yea they who are seeking my life, have laid snares, and they who are asking my harm, have threatened engulfing ruin, And, deceitful things—all day long, do they mutter.
38:13 But, I, as one deaf, will not hear,—and as one dumb, who will not open his mouth:
38:14 Thus have I become as a man who cannot hear, in whose mouth are no arguments:
38:15 Because, for thee, O Yahweh, have I waited, Thou, wilt answer, O Adonay, my God!
38:16 For I said, Lest they rejoice over me! When my feet were tottering, against me, have they magnified themselves:
38:17 For, I, to halt, am ready, and, my pain, is before me continually;
38:18 For, mine iniquity, will I declare, I shall be anxious because of my sin;
38:19 And, my foes, are alive—have become strong,—and multiplied are they who hate me for false cause:
38:20 Even they who are repaying evil for good, accuse me because I pursue the good.
38:21 Do not forsake me, O Yahweh, My God! be not far from me:
38:22 Make haste to help me, My Lord, my deliverance!
text.NABRE:
38:1 A psalm of David. For remembrance.
38:2 I Lord, do not punish me in your anger; in your wrath do not chastise me!
38:3 Your arrows have sunk deep in me; your hand has come down upon me.
38:4 There is no wholesomeness in my flesh because of your anger; there is no health in my bones because of my sin.
38:5 My iniquities overwhelm me, a burden too heavy for me.
38:6 II Foul and festering are my sores because of my folly.
38:7 I am stooped and deeply bowed; every day I go about mourning.
38:8 My loins burn with fever; there is no wholesomeness in my flesh.
38:9 I am numb and utterly crushed; I wail with anguish of heart.
38:10 My Lord, my deepest yearning is before you; my groaning is not hidden from you.
38:11 My heart shudders, my strength forsakes me; the very light of my eyes has failed.
38:12 Friends and companions shun my disease; my neighbors stand far off.
38:13 Those who seek my life lay snares for me; they seek my misfortune, they speak of ruin; they plot treachery every day.
38:14 III But I am like the deaf, hearing nothing, like the mute, I do not open my mouth,
38:15 I am even like someone who does not hear, who has no answer ready.
38:16 Lord, it is for you that I wait; O Lord, my God, you respond.
38:17 For I have said that they would gloat over me, exult over me if I stumble.
38:18 IV I am very near to falling; my wounds are with me always.
38:19 I acknowledge my guilt and grieve over my sin.
38:20 My enemies live and grow strong, those who hate me grow numerous fraudulently,
38:21 Repaying me evil for good, accusing me for pursuing good.
38:22 Do not forsake me, O Lord; my God, be not far from me!
38:23 Come quickly to help me, my Lord and my salvation!
text.DRB:
38:1 Unto the end, to Idithun himself, a canticle of David.
38:2 I have said: I will keep my ways, that I offend not in my tongue. I have set a guard to my mouth, when the sinner stood against me.
38:3 I was dumb and humbled, and kept silence from good things: and my sorrow was renewed.
38:4 My heart waxed hot within me: & in my meditation a fire shall burn.
38:5 I have spoken in my tongue: Lord make mine end known to me. And the number of my days what it is: that I may know what is lacking to me.
38:6 Behold thou hast put my days measurable, and my substance is as nothing before thee. Doubtless all things are vanity, every man living.
38:7 Surely man passeth as an image, yea and he is troubled in vain. He gathereth treasure, and knoweth not to whom he shall gather them.
38:8 And now what is my expectation? Is not our Lord? And my substance is with thee.
38:9 From all mine iniquities deliver me: a reproach to the foolish thou hast given me.
38:10 I was dumb, and opened not my mouth, because thou didst it:
38:11 Remove thy scourges from me.
38:12 By the strength of thy hand I have fainted in reprehensions, for iniquity thou hast chastised man. And thou hast made his soul pine away as a spider: but vainly is every man troubled.
38:13 Hear my prayer, o Lord, and my petition; with thine ears receive my tears. Keep not silence: because I am a stranger with thee, and a pilgrim, as my fathers.
38:14 Forgive me, that I may be refreshed before I depart: and shall be no more.

text.NRSV:
39:1 I said,"I will guard my ways that I may not sin with my tongue; I will keep a muzzle on my mouth as long as the wicked are in my presence."
39:2 I was silent and still; I held my peace to no avail; my distress grew worse,
39:3 my heart became hot within me. While I mused, the fire burned; then I spoke with my tongue:
39:4 "Lord, let me know my end, and what is the measure of my days; let me know how fleeting my life is.
39:5 You have made my days a few handbreadths, and my lifetime is as nothing in your sight. Surely everyone stands as a mere breath. Selah
39:6 Surely everyone goes about like a shadow. Surely for nothing they are in turmoil; they heap up, and do not know who will gather.
39:7 "And now, O Lord, what do I wait for? My hope is in you.
39:8 Deliver me from all my transgressions. Do not make me the scorn of the fool.
39:9 I am silent; I do not open my mouth, for it is you who have done it.
39:10 Remove your stroke from me; I am worn down by the blows of your hand.
39:11 "You chastise mortals in punishment for sin, consuming like a moth what is dear to them; surely everyone is a mere breath. Selah
39:12 "Hear my prayer, O Lord, and give ear to my cry; do not hold your peace at my tears. For I am your passing guest, an alien, like all my forebears.
39:13 Turn your gaze away from me, that I may smile again, before I depart and am no more."
text.Coverdale:
39:1 I said,"I will keep watch upon my ways, * so that I do not offend with my tongue.
39:2 I will put a muzzle on my mouth * while the wicked are in my presence."
39:3 So I held my tongue and said nothing; * I refrained from rash words; but my pain became unbearable.
39:4 My heart was hot within me; while I pondered, the fire burst into flame; * I spoke out with my tongue:
39:5 LORD, let me know my end and the number of my days, * so that I may know how short my life is.
39:6 You have given me a mere handful of days, and my lifetime is as nothing in your sight; * truly, even those who stand erect are but a puff of wind.
39:7 We walk about like a shadow, and in vain we are in turmoil; * we heap up riches and cannot tell who will gather them.
39:8 And now, what is my hope? * O Lord, my hope is in you.
39:9 Deliver me from all my transgressions * and do not make me the taunt of the fool.
39:10 I fell silent and did not open my mouth, * for surely it was you that did it.
39:11 Take your affliction from me; * I am worn down by the blows of your hand.
39:12 With rebukes for sin you punish us; like a moth you eat away all that is dear to us; * truly, everyone is but a puff of wind.
39:13 Hear my prayer, O LORD, and give ear to my cry; * hold not your peace at my tears.
39:14 For I am but a sojourner with you, * a wayfarer, as all my forebears were.
39:15 Turn your gaze from me, that I may be glad again, * before I go my way and am no more.
text.KJV:
39:1 To the chief Musician, even to Jeduthun, A Psalm of David. I said, I will take heed to my ways, that I sin not with my tongue: I will keep my mouth with a bridle, while the wicked is before me.
39:2 I was dumb with silence, I held my peace, even from good; and my sorrow was stirred.
39:3 My heart was hot within me, while I was musing the fire burned: then spake I with my tongue,
39:4 Lord, make me to know mine end, and the measure of my days, what it is; that I may know how frail I am.
39:5 Behold, thou hast made my days as an handbreadth; and mine age is as nothing before thee: verily every man at his best state is altogether vanity. Selah.
39:6 Surely every man walketh in a vain shew: surely they are disquieted in vain: he heapeth up riches, and knoweth not who shall gather them.
39:7 And now, Lord, what wait I for? my hope is in thee.
39:8 Deliver me from all my transgressions: make me not the reproach of the foolish.
39:9 I was dumb, I opened not my mouth; because thou didst it.
39:10 Remove thy stroke away from me: I am consumed by the blow of thine hand.
39:11 When thou with rebukes dost correct man for iniquity, thou makest his beauty to consume away like a moth: surely every man is vanity. Selah.
39:12 Hear my prayer, O Lord, and give ear unto my cry; hold not thy peace at my tears: for I am a stranger with thee, and a sojourner, as all my fathers were.
39:13 O spare me, that I may recover strength, before I go hence, and be no more.
text.JPS1985:
39:1 For the leader; for Jeduthun. A psalm of David.
39:2 I resolved I would watch my step lest I offend by my speech; I would keep my mouth muzzled while the wicked man was in my presence.
39:3 I was dumb, silent; I was very still while my pain was intense.
39:4 My mind was in a rage, my thoughts were all aflame; I spoke out:
39:5 Tell me, O LORD, what my term is, what is the measure of my days; I would know how fleeting my life is.
39:6 You have made my life just handbreadths long; its span is as nothing in Your sight; no man endures any longer than a breath. Selah.
39:7 Man walks about as a mere shadow; mere futility is his hustle and bustle, amassing and not knowing who will gather in.
39:8 What, then, can I count on, O Lord? In You my hope lies.
39:9 Deliver me from all my transgressions; make me not the butt of the benighted.
39:10 I am dumb, I do not speak up, for it is Your doing.
39:11 Take away Your plague from me; I perish from Your blows.
39:12 You chastise a man in punishment for his sin, consuming like a moth what he treasures. No man is more than a breath. Selah.
39:13 Hear my prayer, O LORD; give ear to my cry; do not disregard my tears; for like all my forebears I am an alien, resident with You.
39:14 Look away from me, that I may recover, before I pass away and am gone.
text.Grail1963:
.2 I said: "I will be watchful of my ways
for fear I should sin with my tongue.
I will put a curb on my lips
when the wicked man stands before me."
3 I was dumb, silent and still.
His prosperity stirred my grief.
4 My heart was burning within me.
At the thought of it, the fire blazed up
and my tongue burst into speech:
5 "O Lord, you have shown me my end,
how short is the length of my days.
Now I know how fleeting is my life.
6 You have given me a short span of days;
my life is as nothing in your sight.
A mere breath, the man who stood so firm,
7 a mere shadow, the man passing by;
a mere breath the riches he hoards,
not knowing who will have them."
8 And now, Lord, what is there to wait for?
In you rests all my hope.
9 Set me free from all my sins,
do not make me the taunt of the fool.
10 I was silent, not opening my lips,
because this was all your doing.
11 Take away your scourge from me.
I am crushed by the blows of your hand.
12 You punish man's sins and correct him;
like the moth you devour all he treasures.
Mortal man is no more than a breath;
13 O Lord, hear my prayer.

a pilgrim, like all my fathers.

text.Rotherham:
39:1 I said, [to myself,] I will take heed to my ways, That I sin not with my tongue,—I will put on my mouth a muzzle, So long as the lawless is before me.
39:2 I was dumb with silence, I held my peace, afar from happiness, But, my pain had been stirred:
39:3 Hot was my heart within me, While I was musing, there was kindled a fire, I spake with my tongue!
39:4 Let me know, O Yahweh, mine end, And the measure of my days—what it is, I would know how short-lived I am.
39:5 Lo! as hand-breadths, hast thou granted my days, and my life-time, is as nothing before thee,—Surely, a mere breath, are all men, [even] such as stand firm. [Selah.]
39:6 Surely as a shadow, doth every man wander, Surely in vain, do they bustle about, he heapeth things up, and knoweth not who shall gather them in.
39:7 Now, therefore, for what have I waited, O My Lord? My hope, is, in thee.
39:8 From all my transgressions, rescue thou me,—The reproach of the base, oh do not make me!
39:9 I am dumb, I cannot open my mouth, for, thou, hast done it.
39:10 Remove from off me thy stroke, Because of the hostility of thy hand, am, I, consumed.
39:11 When, by rebukes for iniquity, thou hast corrected a man, Then hast thou consumed, as a moth, all that was delightful within him, Surely, a breath, are all men. [Selah.]
39:12 Hear my prayer, O Yahweh, And, unto my cry for help, give ear, At my tears, do not be silent,—For, a sojourner, am, I, with thee, A stranger, like all my fathers.
39:13 Look away from me that I may brighten up, Ere yet I depart and am no more.
text.NABRE:
39:1 For the leader, for Jeduthun. A psalm of David.
39:2 I I said, “I will watch my ways, lest I sin with my tongue; I will keep a muzzle on my mouth.”
39:3 Mute and silent before the wicked, I refrain from good things. But my sorrow increases;
39:4 my heart smolders within me. In my sighing a fire blazes up, and I break into speech:
39:5 II Lord, let me know my end, the number of my days, that I may learn how frail I am.
39:6 To be sure, you establish the expanse of my days; indeed, my life is as nothing before you. Every man is but a breath. Selah
39:7 III Man goes about as a mere phantom; they hurry about, although in vain; he heaps up stores without knowing for whom.
39:8 And now, Lord, for what do I wait? You are my only hope.
39:9 From all my sins deliver me; let me not be the taunt of fools.
39:10 I am silent and do not open my mouth because you are the one who did this.
39:11 Take your plague away from me; I am ravaged by the touch of your hand.
39:12 You chastise man with rebukes for sin; like a moth you consume his treasures. Every man is but a breath. Selah
39:13 Listen to my prayer, Lord, hear my cry; do not be deaf to my weeping! For I am with you like a foreigner, a refugee, like my ancestors.
39:14 Turn your gaze from me, that I may smile before I depart to be no more.
text.DRB:
39:1 Unto the end, a Psalm to David himself.
39:2 Expecting I expected our Lord, and he hath attended to me.
39:3 And he heard my prayers, and brought me out of the lake of misery, and from the mire of dregs. And hath set my feet upon a rock: and hath directed my steps.
39:4 And he hath put a new canticle into my mouth: a song to our God. Many shall see, and shall fear: and they shall hope in our Lord.
39:5 Blessed is the man, whose hope is in the name of our Lord: and hath not had regard to vanities and false madness.
39:6 Thou hast done many thy marvelous things, o Lord my God: & in thy cogitations there is none that may be like to thee. I have declared and have spoken: they multiplied above number.
39:7 Sacrifice and oblation thou wouldest not: but ears thou hast perfited to me. Holocaust and for sin thou didst not require:
39:8 then said I, Behold I come. In the head of the book it is written of me,
39:9 that I should do thy will: my God I would, and thy law in the midst of my heart.
39:10 I have declared thy justice in the great Church, lo I will not stay my lips: Lord thou hast known it.
39:11 Thy justice I have not hid in my heart: thy truth and thy salvation I have spoken. I have not hid thy mercy, and thy truth from the great Council.
39:12 But thou, o Lord, make not thy commiserations far from me: thy mercy, and thy truth have always received me.
39:13 Because evils have compassed me, which have no number: mine iniquities have overtaken me, & I was not able to see. They are multiplied above the hairs of my head: and my heart hath forsaken me.
39:14 It may please thee, o Lord, to deliver me: Lord have respect to help me.
39:15 Let them be confounded and ashamed together, that seek my soul to take it away. Let them be turned backward, and be ashamed that will me evils.
39:16 Let them forthwith receive their confusion, that say to me: Well, well.
39:17 Let all that seek thee rejoice and be glad upon thee: and let them that love thy salvation, say always: Our Lord be magnified:
39:18 But I am a beggar, and poor: Our Lord is careful to me. Thou art my helper, & my protector: my God be not slack.

text.NRSV:
40:1 I waited patiently for the Lord; he inclined to me and heard my cry.
40:2 He drew me up from the desolate pit, out of the miry bog, and set my feet upon a rock, making my steps secure.
40:3 He put a new song in my mouth, a song of praise to our God. Many will see and fear, and put their trust in the Lord.
40:4 Happy are those who make the Lord their trust, who do not turn to the proud, to those who go astray after false gods.
40:5 You have multiplied, O Lord my God, your wondrous deeds and your thoughts toward us; none can compare with you. Were I to proclaim and tell of them, they would be more than can be counted.
40:6 Sacrifice and offering you do not desire, but you have given me an open ear. Burnt offering and sin offering you have not required.
40:7 Then I said,"Here I am; in the scroll of the book it is written of me.
40:8 I delight to do your will, O my God; your law is within my heart."
40:9 I have told the glad news of deliverance in the great congregation; see, I have not restrained my lips, as you know, O Lord.
40:10 I have not hidden your saving help within my heart, I have spoken of your faithfulness and your salvation; I have not concealed your steadfast love and your faithfulness from the great congregation.
40:11 Do not, O Lord, withhold your mercy from me; let your steadfast love and your faithfulness keep me safe forever.
40:12 For evils have encompassed me without number; my iniquities have overtaken me, until I cannot see; they are more than the hairs of my head, and my heart fails me.
40:13 Be pleased, O Lord, to deliver me; O Lord, make haste to help me.
40:14 Let all those be put to shame and confusion who seek to snatch away my life; let those be turned back and brought to dishonor who desire my hurt.
40:15 Let those be appalled because of their shame who say to me,"Aha, Aha!"
40:16 But may all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you; may those who love your salvation say continually,"Great is the Lord!"
40:17 As for me, I am poor and needy, but the Lord takes thought for me. You are my help and my deliverer; do not delay, O my God.
text.Coverdale:
40:1 I waited patiently upon the LORD; * he stooped to me and heard my cry.
40:2 He lifted me out of the desolate pit, out of the mire and clay; * he set my feet upon a high cliff and made my footing sure.
40:3 He put a new song in my mouth, a song of praise to our God; * many shall see, and stand in awe, and put their trust in the LORD.
40:4 Happy are they who trust in the LORD! * they do not resort to evil spirits or turn to false gods.
40:5 Great things are they that you have done, O LORD my God! how great your wonders and your plans for us! * there is none who can be compared with you.
40:6 Oh, that I could make them known and tell them! * but they are more than I can count.
40:7 In sacrifice and offering you take no pleasure * (you have given me ears to hear you);
40:8 Burnt-offering and sin-offering you have not required, * and so I said,"Behold, I come.
40:9 In the roll of the book it is written concerning me: *'I love to do your will, O my God; your law is deep in my heart.'"
40:10 I proclaimed righteousness in the great congregation; * behold, I did not restrain my lips; and that, O LORD, you know.
40:11 Your righteousness have I not hidden in my heart; I have spoken of your faithfulness and your deliverance; * I have not concealed your love and faithfulness from the great congregation.
40:12 You are the LORD; do not withhold your compassion from me; * let your love and your faithfulness keep me safe for ever,
40:13 For innumerable troubles have crowded upon me; my sins have overtaken me, and I cannot see; * they are more in number than the hairs of my head, and my heart fails me.
40:14 Be pleased, O LORD, to deliver me; * O LORD, make haste to help me.
40:15 Let them be ashamed and altogether dismayed who seek after my life to destroy it; * let them draw back and be disgraced who take pleasure in my misfortune.
40:16 Let those who say"Aha!"and gloat over me be confounded, * because they are ashamed.
40:17 Let all who seek you rejoice in you and be glad; * let those who love your salvation continually say, Great is the LORD!"
40:18 Though I am poor and afflicted, * the Lord will have regard for me.
40:19 You are my helper and my deliverer; * do not tarry, O my God.
text.KJV:
40:1 To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. I waited patiently for the Lord; and he inclined unto me, and heard my cry.
40:2 He brought me up also out of an horrible pit, out of the miry clay, and set my feet upon a rock, and established my goings.
40:3 And he hath put a new song in my mouth, even praise unto our God: many shall see it, and fear, and shall trust in the Lord.
40:4 Blessed is that man that maketh the Lord his trust, and respecteth not the proud, nor such as turn aside to lies.
40:5 Many, O Lord my God, are thy wonderful works which thou hast done, and thy thoughts which are to us-ward: they cannot be reckoned up in order unto thee: if I would declare and speak of them, they are more than can be numbered.
40:6 Sacrifice and offering thou didst not desire; mine ears hast thou opened: burnt offering and sin offering hast thou not required.
40:7 Then said I, Lo, I come: in the volume of the book it is written of me,
40:8 I delight to do thy will, O my God: yea, thy law is within my heart.
40:9 I have preached righteousness in the great congregation: lo, I have not refrained my lips, O Lord, thou knowest.
40:10 I have not hid thy righteousness within my heart; I have declared thy faithfulness and thy salvation: I have not concealed thy lovingkindness and thy truth from the great congregation.
40:11 Withhold not thou thy tender mercies from me, O Lord: let thy lovingkindness and thy truth continually preserve me.
40:12 For innumerable evils have compassed me about: mine iniquities have taken hold upon me, so that I am not able to look up; they are more than the hairs of mine head: therefore my heart faileth me.
40:13 Be pleased, O Lord, to deliver me: O Lord, make haste to help me.
40:14 Let them be ashamed and confounded together that seek after my soul to destroy it; let them be driven backward and put to shame that wish me evil.
40:15 Let them be desolate for a reward of their shame that say unto me, Aha, aha.
40:16 Let all those that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee: let such as love thy salvation say continually, The Lord be magnified.
40:17 But I am poor and needy; yet the Lord thinketh upon me: thou art my help and my deliverer; make no tarrying, O my God.
text.JPS1985:
40:1 For the leader. A psalm of David.
40:2 I put my hope in the LORD; He inclined toward me, and heeded my cry.
40:3 He lifted me out of the miry pit, the slimy clay, and set my feet on a rock, steadied my legs.
40:4 He put a new song into my mouth, a hymn to our God. May many see it and stand in awe, and trust in the LORD.
40:5 Happy is the man who makes the LORD his trust, who turns not to the arrogant or to followers of falsehood.
40:6 You, O LORD my God, have done many things; the wonders You have devised for us cannot be set out before You; I would rehearse the tale of them, but they are more than can be told.
40:7 You gave me to understand that You do not desire sacrifice and meal offering; You do not ask for burnt offering and sin offering.
40:8 Then I said, “See, I will bring a scroll recounting what befell me.”
40:9 To do what pleases You, my God, is my desire; Your teaching is in my inmost parts.
40:10 I proclaimed [Your] righteousness in a great congregation; see, I did not withhold my words; O LORD, You must know it.
40:11 I did not keep Your beneficence to myself; I declared Your faithful deliverance; I did not fail to speak of Your steadfast love in a great congregation.
40:12 O LORD, You will not withhold from me Your compassion; Your steadfast love will protect me always.
40:13 For misfortunes without number envelop me; my iniquities have caught up with me; I cannot see; they are more than the hairs of my head; I am at my wits’ end.
40:14 O favor me, LORD, and save me; O LORD, hasten to my aid.
40:15 Let those who seek to destroy my life be frustrated and disgraced; let those who wish me harm fall back in shame.
40:16 Let those who say “Aha! Aha! ” over me be desolate because of their frustration.
40:17 But let all who seek You be glad and rejoice in You; let those who are eager for Your deliverance always say, “Extolled be the LORD! ”
40:18 But I am poor and needy; may the Lord devise [deliverance] for me. You are my help and my rescuer; my God, do not delay.
text.Grail1963:
.2 I waited, I waited for the Lord
and he stooped down to me;
he heard my cry.
3 He drew me from the deadly pit,
from the miry clay.
He set my feet upon a rock
and made my footsteps firm.
4 He put a new song into my mouth,

praise of our God.
Many shall see and fear
and shall trust in the Lord.
5 Happy the man who has placed
his trust in the Lord
and has not gone over to the rebels
who follow false gods.
6 How many, O Lord my God,
are the wonders and designs
that you have worked for us;
you have no equal.
Should I proclaim and speak of them,
they are more than I can tell!
7 You do not ask for sacrifice and offerings,
but an open ear.
You do not ask for holocaust and victim.
8 Instead, here am I.
In the scroll of the book it stands written
9 that I should do your will.
My God, I delight in your law
in the depth of my heart.
10 Your justice I have proclaimed
in the great assembly.
My lips I have not sealed;
you know it, O Lord.
11 I have not hidden your justice in my heart
but declared your faithful help.
I have not hidden your love and your truth
from the great assembly.
12 O Lord, you will not withhold
your compassion from me.
Your merciful love and your truth
will always guard me.
13 For I am beset with evils
too many to be counted.
My sins have fallen upon me
and my sight fails me.

and my heart sinks.
14 O Lord, come to my rescue;
Lord, come to my aid.
15 O let there be shame and confusion

who delight in my harm.
16 Let them be appalled, covered with shame,

17 O let there be rejoicing and gladness
