the God of Jacob is our stronghold.
text.Rotherham:
46:1 God—for us, is a refuge and strength, A help in distresses, soon found.
46:2 For this cause, will we not fear, Though the earth showeth change, Or the mountains slip into the heart of the seas:
46:3 The waters thereof roar and foam, The mountains tremble with the swelling thereof [Selah.]
46:4 A river! whose channels, shall gladden the city of God, The Most High, hath hallowed his habitation.
46:5 God, is in the midst of her, she shall not be shaken, God will help her, by the turnings of the morning.
46:6 Nations have roared, Kingdoms have tottered, He hath uttered his voice, Earth melteth.
46:7 Yahweh of hosts, is with us, A high tower for us, is the God of Jacob. [Selah.]
46:8 Come! view the doings of Yahweh,—Who hath set desolations in the earth;
46:9 Causing wars to cease unto the end of the earth,—The bow, he shivereth, And breaketh in pieces the spear, war-chariots, burneth he up with fire.
46:10 Let be! and know that, I, am God, I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted in the earth.
46:11 Yahweh of hosts, is with us, A high tower for us, is the God of Jacob. [Selah.]
text.NABRE:
46:1 For the leader. A song of the Korahites. According to alamoth.
46:2 I God is our refuge and our strength, an ever-present help in distress.
46:3 Thus we do not fear, though earth be shaken and mountains quake to the depths of the sea,
46:4 Though its waters rage and foam and mountains totter at its surging. Selah
46:5 II Streams of the river gladden the city of God, the holy dwelling of the Most High.
46:6 God is in its midst; it shall not be shaken; God will help it at break of day.
46:7 Though nations rage and kingdoms totter, he utters his voice and the earth melts.
46:8 The Lord of hosts is with us; our stronghold is the God of Jacob. Selah
46:9 III Come and see the works of the Lord, who has done fearsome deeds on earth;
46:10 Who stops wars to the ends of the earth, breaks the bow, splinters the spear, and burns the shields with fire;
46:11 “Be still and know that I am God! I am exalted among the nations, exalted on the earth.”
46:12 The Lord of hosts is with us; our stronghold is the God of Jacob. Selah
text.DRB:
46:1 Unto the end, for the sons of Core.
46:2 All ye Nations clap hands: make jubilation to God in the voice of exultation.
46:3 Because our Lord is high, terrible: a great King over all the earth.
46:4 He hath made peoples subject to us: & gentiles under our feet.
46:5 He hath chosen his inheritance in us: the beauty of Jacob which he loved.
46:6 God is ascended in jubilation: and our Lord in the voice of trumpet.
46:7 Sing ye to our God, sing ye: Sing ye to our King, sing ye.
46:8 Because God is King of all the earth: sing ye wisely.
46:9 God shall reign over the gentiles: God sitteth upon his holy seat.
46:10 Princes of peoples are gathered together with the God of Abraham: because the strong gods of the earth are exceedingly advanced.

text.NRSV:
47:1 Clap your hands, all you peoples; shout to God with loud songs of joy.
47:2 For the Lord, the Most High, is awesome, a great king over all the earth.
47:3 He subdued peoples under us, and nations under our feet.
47:4 He chose our heritage for us, the pride of Jacob whom he loves. Selah
47:5 God has gone up with a shout, the Lord with the sound of a trumpet.
47:6 Sing praises to God, sing praises; sing praises to our King, sing praises.
47:7 For God is the king of all the earth; sing praises with a psalm.
47:8 God is king over the nations; God sits on his holy throne.
47:9 The princes of the peoples gather as the people of the God of Abraham. For the shields of the earth belong to God; he is highly exalted.
text.Coverdale:
47:1 Clap your hands, all you peoples; * shout to God with a cry of joy.
47:2 For the LORD Most High is to be feared; * he is the great King over all the earth.
47:3 He subdues the peoples under us, * and the nations under our feet.
47:4 He chooses our inheritance for us, * the pride of Jacob whom he loves.
47:5 God has gone up with a shout, * the LORD with the sound of the ram's-horn.
47:6 Sing praises to God, sing praises; * sing praises to our King, sing praises.
47:7 For God is King of all the earth; * sing praises with all your skill.
47:8 God reigns over the nations; * God sits upon his holy throne.
47:9 The nobles of the peoples have gathered together * with the people of the God of Abraham.
47:10 The rulers of the earth belong to God, * and he is highly exalted.
text.KJV:
47:1 To the chief Musician, A Psalm for the sons of Korah. O clap your hands, all ye people; shout unto God with the voice of triumph.
47:2 For the Lord most high is terrible; he is a great King over all the earth.
47:3 He shall subdue the people under us, and the nations under our feet.
47:4 He shall choose our inheritance for us, the excellency of Jacob whom he loved. Selah.
47:5 God is gone up with a shout, the Lord with the sound of a trumpet.
47:6 Sing praises to God, sing praises: sing praises unto our King, sing praises.
47:7 For God is the King of all the earth: sing ye praises with understanding.
47:8 God reigneth over the heathen: God sitteth upon the throne of his holiness.
47:9 The princes of the people are gathered together, even the people of the God of Abraham: for the shields of the earth belong unto God: he is greatly exalted.
text.JPS1985:
47:1 For the leader. Of the Korahites. A psalm.
47:2 All you peoples, clap your hands, raise a joyous shout for God.
47:3 For the LORD Most High is awesome, great king over all the earth;
47:4 He subjects peoples to us, sets nations at our feet.
47:5 He chose our heritage for us, the pride of Jacob whom He loved. Selah.
47:6 God ascends midst acclamation; the LORD, to the blasts of the horn.
47:7 Sing, O sing to God; sing, O sing to our king;
47:8 for God is king over all the earth; sing a hymn.
47:9 God reigns over the nations; God is seated on His holy throne.
47:10 The great of the peoples are gathered together, the retinue of Abraham’s God; for the guardians of the earth belong to God; He is greatly exalted.
text.Grail1963:
.2 All peoples, clap your hands,
cry to God with shouts of joy!
3 For the Lord, the Most High, we must fear,
great king over all the earth.
4 He subdues peoples under us
and nations under our feet.
5 Our inheritance, our glory, is from him,
given to Jacob out of love.
6 God goes up with shouts of joy;
the Lord goes up with trumpet blast.
7 Sing praise for God, sing praise,
sing praise to our king, sing praise.
8 God is king of all the earth,
sing praise with all your skill.
9 God is king over the nations;
God reigns on his holy throne.
10 The princes of the people are assembled
with the people of Abraham's God.
The rulers of the earth belong to God,
to God who reigns over all.
text.Rotherham:
47:1 All ye peoples, clap your hands, Shout unto God, with the voice of triumph;
47:2 For, Yahweh—as Most High, is to be revered, A great king, over all the earth,
47:3 He will subjugate, Peoples under us, and, Tribes of men beneath our feet.
47:4 He chooseth for us our inheritance, The excellence of Jacob, which he hath loved. [Selah.]
47:5 God hath ascended with a shout, Yahweh, with the sound of a horn.
47:6 Sing praises unto God, sing praises, Sing praises to our King, sing praises;
47:7 For God is king of all the earth, Sing praises with understanding.
47:8 God, hath become king, over the nations, God, hath taken his seat upon his holy throne.
47:9 The willing-hearted of the peoples, have gathered themselves together, The people of the God of Abraham; For, to God, belong the shields of the earth, Greatly is he exalted.
text.NABRE:
47:1 For the leader. A psalm of the Korahites.
47:2 I All you peoples, clap your hands; shout to God with joyful cries.
47:3 For the Lord, the Most High, is to be feared, the great king over all the earth,
47:4 Who made people subject to us, nations under our feet,
47:5 Who chose our heritage for us, the glory of Jacob, whom he loves. Selah
47:6 II God has gone up with a shout; the Lord, amid trumpet blasts.
47:7 Sing praise to God, sing praise; sing praise to our king, sing praise.
47:8 III For God is king over all the earth; sing hymns of praise.
47:9 God rules over the nations; God sits upon his holy throne.
47:10 The princes of the peoples assemble with the people of the God of Abraham. For the shields of the earth belong to God, highly exalted.
text.DRB:
47:1 A Psalm of Canticle to the sons of Core, the second of the Sabbath.
47:2 Great is our Lord, and to be praised exceedingly, in the city of our God, in his holy mount.
47:3 Mount Sion is founded with the exultation of the whole earth, the sides of the North, the city of the great King.
47:4 God shall be known in the houses thereof, when he shall receive it.
47:5 For behold the kings of the earth were gathered together: they assembled in one.
47:6 They seeing it so were in admiration, were troubled, were moved:
47:7 Trembling took them. Their sorrows as a woman travailing.
47:8 In a vehement spirit thou shalt break the ships of Tharsis.
47:9 As we have heard, so have we seen in the city of the Lord of hosts, in the city of our God: God hath founded it for ever.
47:10 We have received thy mercy, o God, in the midst of thy temple.
47:11 According to thy name, o God, so also is thy praise unto the ends of the earth: thy right hand is full of justice.
47:12 Let mount Sion be glad, and the daughters of Juda rejoice, because of thy judgments, o Lord.
47:13 Compass Sion, and embrace ye her: tell ye in her towers.
47:14 Set your hearts on her strength: and distribute ye her houses, that you may declare it in another generation.
47:15 Because this is God, our God for ever, and for ever and ever: he shall rule us evermore.

text.NRSV:
48:1 Great is the Lord and greatly to be praised in the city of our God. His holy mountain,
48:2 beautiful in elevation, is the joy of all the earth, Mount Zion, in the far north, the city of the great King.
48:3 Within its citadels God has shown himself a sure defense.
48:4 Then the kings assembled, they came on together.
48:5 As soon as they saw it, they were astounded; they were in panic, they took to flight;
48:6 trembling took hold of them there, pains as of a woman in labor,
48:7 as when an east wind shatters the ships of Tarshish.
48:8 As we have heard, so have we seen in the city of the Lord of hosts, in the city of our God, which God establishes forever. Selah
48:9 We ponder your steadfast love, O God, in the midst of your temple.
48:10 Your name, O God, like your praise, reaches to the ends of the earth. Your right hand is filled with victory.
48:11 Let Mount Zion be glad, let the towns of Judah rejoice because of your judgments.
48:12 Walk about Zion, go all around it, count its towers,
48:13 consider well its ramparts; go through its citadels, that you may tell the next generation
48:14 that this is God, our God forever and ever. He will be our guide forever.
text.Coverdale:
48:1 Great is the LORD, and highly to be praised; * in the city of our God is his holy hill.
48:2 Beautiful and lofty, the joy of all the earth, is the hill of Zion, * the very center of the world and the city of the great King.
48:3 God is in her citadels; * he is known to be her sure refuge.
48:4 Behold, the kings of the earth assembled * and marched forward together.
48:5 They looked and were astounded; * they retreated and fled in terror.
48:6 Trembling seized them there; * they writhed like a woman in childbirth, like ships of the sea when the east wind shatters them.
48:7 As we have heard, so have we seen, in the city of the LORD of hosts, in the city of our God; * God has established her for ever.
48:8 We have waited in silence on your loving-kindness, O God, * in the midst of your temple.
48:9 Your praise, like your Name, O God, reaches to the world's end; * your right hand is full of justice.
48:10 Let Mount Zion be glad and the cities of Judah rejoice, * because of your judgments.
48:11 Make the circuit of Zion; walk round about her; * count the number of her towers.
48:12 Consider well her bulwarks; examine her strongholds; * that you may tell those who come after.
48:13 This God is our God for ever and ever; * he shall be our guide for evermore.
text.KJV:
48:1 A Song and Psalm for the sons of Korah. Great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised in the city of our God, in the mountain of his holiness.
48:2 Beautiful for situation, the joy of the whole earth, is mount Zion, on the sides of the north, the city of the great King.
48:3 God is known in her palaces for a refuge.
48:4 For, lo, the kings were assembled, they passed by together.
48:5 They saw it, and so they marvelled; they were troubled, and hasted away.
48:6 Fear took hold upon them there, and pain, as of a woman in travail.
48:7 Thou breakest the ships of Tarshish with an east wind.
48:8 As we have heard, so have we seen in the city of the Lord of hosts, in the city of our God: God will establish it for ever. Selah.
48:9 We have thought of thy lovingkindness, O God, in the midst of thy temple.
48:10 According to thy name, O God, so is thy praise unto the ends of the earth: thy right hand is full of righteousness.
48:11 Let mount Zion rejoice, let the daughters of Judah be glad, because of thy judgments.
48:12 Walk about Zion, and go round about her: tell the towers thereof.
48:13 Mark ye well her bulwarks, consider her palaces; that ye may tell it to the generation following.
48:14 For this God is our God for ever and ever: he will be our guide even unto death.
text.JPS1985:
48:1 A song. A psalm of the Korahites.
48:2 The LORD is great and much acclaimed in the city of our God, His holy mountain—
48:3 fair-crested, joy of all the earth, Mount Zion, summit of Zaphon, city of the great king.
48:4 Through its citadels, God has made Himself known as a haven.
48:5 See, the kings joined forces; they advanced together.
48:6 At the mere sight of it they were stunned, they were terrified, they panicked;
48:7 they were seized there with a trembling, like a woman in the throes of labor,
48:8 as the Tarshish fleet was wrecked in an easterly gale.
48:9 The likes of what we heard we have now witnessed in the city of the LORD of hosts, in the city of our God— may God preserve it forever! Selah.
48:10 In Your temple, God, we meditate upon Your faithful care.
48:11 The praise of You, God, like Your name, reaches to the ends of the earth; Your right hand is filled with beneficence.
48:12 Let Mount Zion rejoice! Let the towns of Judah exult, because of Your judgments.
48:13 Walk around Zion, circle it; count its towers,
48:14 take note of its ramparts; go through its citadels, that you may recount it to a future age.
48:15 For God—He is our God forever; He will lead us evermore.
text.Grail1963:
.2 The Lord is great and worthy to be praised
in the city of our God.
3 His holy mountain rises in beauty,
the joy of all the earth.
Mount Zion, true pole of the earth,
the Great King's city!

4 God, in the midst of its citadels,
has shown himself its stronghold.
5 For the kings assembled together,
together they advanced.
6 They saw; at once they were astounded;
dismayed, they fled in fear.
7 A trembling seized them there,
like the pangs of birth.
8 By the east wind you have destroyed
the ships of Tarshish.
9 As we have heard, so we have seen
in the city of our God,
in the city of the Lord of hosts
which God upholds for ever.
10 O God, we ponder your love
within your temple.
11 Your praise, O God, like your name
reaches the ends of the earth.
With justice your right hand is filled.
12 Mount Zion rejoices;
the people of Judah rejoice
at the sight of your judgments.
13 Walk through Zion, walk all round it;
count the number of its towers.
14 Review all its ramparts,
examine its castles,
that you may tell the next generation
15 that such is our God,
our God for ever and always.
It is he who leads us.
text.Rotherham:
48:1 Great is Yahweh, and worthy to be mightily praised, In the city of our God, His holy mountain.
48:2 Beautiful in elevation, the joy of all the land, Is Mount Zion, in the recesses of the north,—The city of a great king.
48:3 God, in her palaces, is to be known as a high tower.
48:4 For lo! Kings—Met as appointed, Passed by together;
48:5 They themselves, saw, So, were they amazed, Dismayed—they hurried way!
48:6 Trembling, seized them, there, Pangs. like hers who is in travail.
48:7 With an east wind, wilt thou shatter the ships of Tarshish.
48:8 Just as we have heard, So, have we seen, In the city of Yahweh of hosts, In the city of our God, God himself, will establish her, unto times age-abiding. [Selah.]
48:9 We have thought, O God, upon thy lovingkindness, In the midst of thy temple:
48:10 According to thy Name, O God, So, be thy praise, unto the ends of the earth, With righteousness, is, thy right hand, filled.
48:11 Let Mount Zion rejoice, Let the daughters of Judah exult, Because of thy judgments.
48:12 Go round Zion, and compass her about, Reckon up her towers;
48:13 Apply your mind to her rampart, Pass between her palaces, That ye may recount them to an after generation;
48:14 For, this God, is our God, to times age-abiding and beyond, He himself, will conduct us till death.
text.NABRE:
48:1 A psalm of the Korahites. A song.
48:2 I Great is the Lord and highly praised in the city of our God: His holy mountain,
48:3 fairest of heights, the joy of all the earth, Mount Zion, the heights of Zaphon, the city of the great king.
48:4 II God is in its citadel, renowned as a stronghold.
48:5 See! The kings assembled, together they advanced.
48:6 When they looked they were astounded; terrified, they were put to flight!
48:7 Trembling seized them there, anguish, like a woman’s labor,
48:8 As when the east wind wrecks the ships of Tarshish!
48:9 III What we had heard we have now seen in the city of the Lord of hosts, In the city of our God, which God establishes forever. Selah
48:10 We ponder, O God, your mercy within your temple
48:11 Like your name, O God, so is your praise to the ends of the earth. Your right hand is fully victorious.
48:12 Mount Zion is glad! The daughters of Judah rejoice because of your judgments!
48:13 IV Go about Zion, walk all around it, note the number of its towers.
48:14 Consider the ramparts, examine its citadels, that you may tell future generations:
48:15 That this is God, our God for ever and ever. He will lead us until death.
text.DRB:
48:1 Unto the end, to the sons of Core a Psalm.
48:2 Hear these things all ye Gentiles: receive with your ears all ye that inhabit the earth.
48:3 All ye earthly persons, and children of men: together in one the rich and the poor.
48:4 My mouth shall speak wisdom, and the meditation of my heart prudence.
48:5 I will incline mine ear unto a parable: I will open my proposition on a Psalter.
48:6 Why shall I fear in the evil day? The iniquity of my heel shall compass me.
48:7 They that trust in their strength: and glory in the multitude of their riches.
48:8 A brother doth not redeem, man shall redeem: he shall not give unto God his reconciliation.
48:9 And the price of the redemption of his own soul: and he shall labour for ever,
48:10 and shall live yet unto the end.
48:11 He shall not see death, when he shall see the wise dying, the unwise, and the fool shall perish together. And they shall leave their riches to strangers:
48:12 and their sepulchres their houses for ever. Their Tabernacles in generation and generation: they have renowned their names in their lands.
48:13 And man when he was in honour, did not understand: he was compared to beasts without understanding, and became like to them.
48:14 This their way is a scandal to them: and afterward in their mouth they shall take pleasure.
48:15 As sheep they are put in hell: death shall feed upon them. And the just shall rule over them in the morning: and their aid shall wax old in hell from their glory.
48:16 Nevertheless God will redeem my soul out of the hand of hell, when he shall take me,
48:17 Fear not when a man shall be made rich: and when the glory of his house shall be multiplied.
48:18 Because when he shall die, he shall not take all things: neither shall his glory go down with him.
48:19 Because his soul in his life shall be blessed: he will confess to thee when thou shalt do him good.
48:20 He shall enter in, even to the progenies of his fathers: and he shall not see light for ever.
48:21 Man, when he was in honour, did not understand: he was compared to beasts without understanding, and became like to them.

text.NRSV:
49:1 Hear this, all you peoples; give ear, all inhabitants of the world,
49:2 both low and high, rich and poor together.
49:3 My mouth shall speak wisdom; the meditation of my heart shall be understanding.
49:4 I will incline my ear to a proverb; I will solve my riddle to the music of the harp.
49:5 Why should I fear in times of trouble, when the iniquity of my persecutors surrounds me,
49:6 those who trust in their wealth and boast of the abundance of their riches?
49:7 Truly, no ransom avails for one's life, there is no price one can give to God for it.
49:8 For the ransom of life is costly, and can never suffice
49:9 that one should live on forever and never see the grave.
49:10 When we look at the wise, they die; fool and dolt perish together and leave their wealth to others.
49:11 Their graves are their homes forever, their dwelling places to all generations, though they named lands their own.
49:12 Mortals cannot abide in their pomp; they are like the animals that perish.
49:13 Such is the fate of the foolhardy, the end of those who are pleased with their lot. Selah
49:14 Like sheep they are appointed for Sheol; Death shall be their shepherd; straight to the grave they descend, and their form shall waste away; Sheol shall be their home.
49:15 But God will ransom my soul from the power of Sheol, for he will receive me. Selah
49:16 Do not be afraid when some become rich, when the wealth of their houses increases.
49:17 For when they die they will carry nothing away; their wealth will not go down after them.
49:18 Though in their lifetime they count themselves happy—for you are praised when you do well for yourself—
49:19 they will go to the company of their ancestors, who will never again see the light.
49:20 Mortals cannot abide in their pomp; they are like the animals that perish.
text.Coverdale:
49:1 Hear this, all you peoples; hearken, all you who dwell in the world, * you of high degree and low, rich and poor together.
49:2 My mouth shall speak of wisdom, * and my heart shall meditate on understanding.
49:3 I will incline my ear to a proverb * and set forth my riddle upon the harp.
49:4 Why should I be afraid in evil days, * when the wickedness of those at my heels surrounds me,
49:5 The wickedness of those who put their trust in their goods, * and boast of their great riches?
49:6 We can never ransom ourselves, * or deliver to God the price of our life;
49:7 For the ransom of our life is so great, * that we should never have enough to pay it,
49:8 In order to live for ever and ever, * and never see the grave.
49:9 For we see that the wise die also; like the dull and stupid they perish * and leave their wealth to those who come after them.
49:10 Their graves shall be their homes for ever, their dwelling places from generation to generation, * though they call the lands after their own names.
49:11 Even though honored, they cannot live for ever; * they are like the beasts that perish.
49:12 Such is the way of those who foolishly trust in themselves, * and the end of those who delight in their own words.
49:13 Like a flock of sheep they are destined to die; Death is their shepherd; * they go down straightway to the grave.
49:14 Their form shall waste away, * and the land of the dead shall be their home.
49:15 But God will ransom my life; * he will snatch me from the grasp of death.
49:16 Do not be envious when some become rich, * or when the grandeur of their house increases;
49:17 For they will carry nothing away at their death, * nor will their grandeur follow them.
49:18 Though they thought highly of themselves while they lived, * and were praised for their success,
49:19 They shall join the company of their forebears, * who will never see the light again.
49:20 Those who are honored, but have no understanding, * are like the beasts that perish.
text.KJV:
49:1 To the chief Musician, A Psalm for the sons of Korah. Hear this, all ye people; give ear, all ye inhabitants of the world:
49:2 Both low and high, rich and poor, together.
49:3 My mouth shall speak of wisdom; and the meditation of my heart shall be of understanding.
49:4 I will incline mine ear to a parable: I will open my dark saying upon the harp.
49:5 Wherefore should I fear in the days of evil, when the iniquity of my heels shall compass me about?
49:6 They that trust in their wealth, and boast themselves in the multitude of their riches;
49:7 None of them can by any means redeem his brother, nor give to God a ransom for him:
49:8 (For the redemption of their soul is precious, and it ceaseth for ever:)
49:9 That he should still live for ever, and not see corruption.
49:10 For he seeth that wise men die, likewise the fool and the brutish person perish, and leave their wealth to others.
49:11 Their inward thought is, that their houses shall continue for ever, and their dwelling places to all generations; they call their lands after their own names.
49:12 Nevertheless man being in honour abideth not: he is like the beasts that perish.
49:13 This their way is their folly: yet their posterity approve their sayings. Selah.
49:14 Like sheep they are laid in the grave; death shall feed on them; and the upright shall have dominion over them in the morning; and their beauty shall consume in the grave from their dwelling.
49:15 But God will redeem my soul from the power of the grave: for he shall receive me. Selah.
49:16 Be not thou afraid when one is made rich, when the glory of his house is increased;
49:17 For when he dieth he shall carry nothing away: his glory shall not descend after him.
49:18 Though while he lived he blessed his soul: and men will praise thee, when thou doest well to thyself.
49:19 He shall go to the generation of his fathers; they shall never see light.
49:20 Man that is in honour, and understandeth not, is like the beasts that perish.
text.JPS1985:
49:1 For the leader. Of the Korahites. A psalm.
49:2 Hear this, all you peoples; give ear, all inhabitants of the world,
49:3 men of all estates, rich and poor alike.
49:4 My mouth utters wisdom, my speech is full of insight.
49:5 I will turn my attention to a theme, set forth my lesson to the music of a lyre.
49:6 In time of trouble, why should I fear the encompassing evil of those who would supplant me—
49:7 men who trust in their riches, who glory in their great wealth?
49:8 Ah, it cannot redeem a man, or pay his ransom to God;
49:9 the price of life is too high; and so one ceases to be, forever.
49:10 Shall he live eternally, and never see the grave?
49:11 For one sees that the wise die, that the foolish and ignorant both perish, leaving their wealth to others.
49:12 Their grave is their eternal home, the dwelling-place for all generations of those once famous on earth.
49:13 Man does not abide in honor; he is like the beasts that perish.
49:14 Such is the fate of those who are self-confident, the end of those pleased with their own talk. Selah.
49:15 Sheeplike they head for Sheol, with Death as their shepherd. The upright shall rule over them at daybreak, and their form shall waste away in Sheol till its nobility be gone.
49:16 But God will redeem my life from the clutches of Sheol, for He will take me. Selah.
49:17 Do not be afraid when a man becomes rich, when his household goods increase;
49:18 for when he dies he can take none of it along; his goods cannot follow him down.
49:19 Though he congratulates himself in his lifetime — “They must admit that you did well by yourself” —
49:20 yet he must join the company of his ancestors, who will never see daylight again.
49:21 Man does not understand honor; he is like the beasts that perish.
text.Grail1963:
.2 Hear this, all you peoples,
give heed, all who dwell in the world,
3 men both high and low,
rich and poor alike!
4 My lips will speak words of wisdom.
My heart is full of insight.

with the harp I will solve my problem.

6 Why should I fear in evil days

and boast of the vastness of their riches?

nor avoid coming to the grave.

their dwelling place from age to age,

text.Rotherham:
49:1 Hear ye, this all ye peoples, Give ear, all ye inhabitants of this passing world;
49:2 Both sons of the low, And sons of the high,—Together both rich and needy:—
49:3 My mouth, shall speak forth Wisdom, And the soft utterance of my heart be Understanding:
49:4 I will bend, to a by-word, mine ear, I will open, on the lyre, mine enigma.
49:5 Wherefore should I fear in the days of calamity, Though the iniquity of them who lie in wait for me should enclose me?
49:6 As for them who are trusting in their wealth,—And, in the abundance of their riches, do boast themselves,
49:7 A brother, can none of them, redeem, he cannot give unto God a ransom for himself:
49:8 So costly, is the redemption of their soul, That it faileth unto times age-abiding;
49:9 That he should, yet, live on, continually, Should not see corruption.
49:10 For it is seen that, the wise, die, Together with the dullard, and the brutish, do they perish, And leave, to others, their wealth:
49:11 Their, inward thought, is that their houses are for times age-abiding, Their habitations, for generation after generation,—They give their own names unto lands!
49:12 But, a son of earth, though wealthy, cannot tarry, He hath made himself a by-word—Beasts, they resemble:
49:13 This, their way, is a folly to them, And yet, their followers, with their mouth, approve. [Selah.]
49:14 Like sheep—into hades, are they driven, Death shall shepherd them,—And the upright shall have dominion over them in the morning, Even their form, is to decay, Hades, is all that remaineth of a habitation for him.
49:15 But, God, will redeem my soul, out of the hand of hades, For he will take me. [Selah.]
49:16 Do not fear, When a man becometh rich, When the glory of his house increaseth;
49:17 For, when he dieth, he shall take, nothing, his glory shall not descend after him;
49:18 For, though, his own self—while he lived, he used to bless, And they will praise thee, when thou doest well to thyself,
49:19 He shall enter as far as the circle of his fathers, Nevermore, shall they see the light.
49:20 A son of earth though wealthy, who discerneth not, Hath made himself a by-word, Beasts, they resemble.
text.NABRE:
49:1 For the leader. A psalm of the Korahites.
49:2 Hear this, all you peoples! Give ear, all who inhabit the world,
49:3 You of lowly birth or high estate, rich and poor together.
49:4 My mouth shall speak words of wisdom, my heart shall offer insights.
49:5 I will turn my ear to a riddle, expound my question on a lyre.
49:6 I Why should I fear in evil days, with the iniquity of my assailants surrounding me,
49:7 Of those who trust in their wealth and boast of their abundant riches?
49:8 No man can ransom even a brother, or pay to God his own ransom.
49:9 The redemption of his soul is costly; and he will pass away forever.
49:10 Will he live on forever, then, and never see the Pit of Corruption?
49:11 Indeed, he will see that the wise die, and the fool will perish together with the senseless, and they leave their wealth to others.
49:12 Their tombs are their homes forever, their dwellings through all generations, “They named countries after themselves”
49:13 —but man does not abide in splendor. He is like the beasts—they perish.
49:14 II This is the way of those who trust in themselves, and the end of those who take pleasure in their own mouth. Selah
49:15 Like a herd of sheep they will be put into Sheol, and Death will shepherd them. Straight to the grave they descend, where their form will waste away, Sheol will be their palace.
49:16 But God will redeem my life, will take me from the hand of Sheol. Selah
49:17 Do not fear when a man becomes rich, when the wealth of his house grows great.
49:18 At his death he will not take along anything, his glory will not go down after him.
49:19 During his life his soul uttered blessings; “They will praise you, for you do well for yourself.”
49:20 But he will join the company of his fathers, never again to see the light.
49:21 In his prime, man does not understand. He is like the beasts—they perish.
text.DRB:
49:1 A Psalm to Asaph. The God of gods our Lord hath spoken: and he hath called the earth, from the rising of the sun even to the going down.
49:2 Out of Sion the beauty of his comeliness.
49:3 God will come manifestly: our God and he will not keep silence. Fire shall burn forth in his sight: and round about him a mighty tempest.
49:4 He shall call the heaven from above: and the earth to discern his people.
49:5 Gather ye together his Saints unto him: which ordain his testament above sacrifices.
49:6 And the heavens shall shew forth his justice: because God is Judge.
49:7 Hear, o my people, and I will speak: Israel, and I will testify to thee: God thy God am I.
49:8 I will not rebuke thee in thy sacrifices: and thy holocausts are in my sight always.
49:9 I will not take calves out of thy house: nor buck-goats out of thy flocks.
49:10 Because all the wild beasts of the woods be mine, the cattle in the mountains and oxen.
49:11 I have known all the fowls of the air: and the beauty of the field is with me.
49:12 If I shall be hungry, I will not tell thee: for the round earth is mine, and the fullness thereof.
49:13 Will I eat the flesh of oxen? Or will I drink the blood of buck-goats?
49:14 Immolate to God the sacrifice of praise, and pay thy vows to the Highest.
49:15 And invocate me in the day of tribulation: I will deliver thee, and thou shalt glorify me.
49:16 But to the sinner God hath said: Why doest thou declare my justices, and takest my testament by thy mouth?
49:17 But thou hast hated discipline & cast my words behind thee.
49:18 If thou didst see a thief, thou didst run with him: and with adulterers thou didst put thy portion.
49:19 Thy mouth hath abounded with malice: and thy tongue forged guiles.
49:20 Sitting thou spakest against thy brother, and against thy mother's son thou didst put a scandal:
49:21 these things hast thou done, and I have held my peace. Thou hast thought unjustly that I will be like thee: I will reprove thee, and set it against thy face.
49:22 Understand these things you that forget God: lest sometime he take you violently and there be none to deliver you.
49:23 The sacrifice of praise shall glorify me: and there is the way, by which I will shew him the salvation of God.

text.NRSV:
50:1 The mighty one, God the Lord, speaks and summons the earth from the rising of the sun to its setting.
50:2 Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty, God shines forth.
50:3 Our God comes and does not keep silence, before him is a devouring fire, and a mighty tempest all around him.
50:4 He calls to the heavens above and to the earth, that he may judge his people:
50:5 "Gather to me my faithful ones, who made a covenant with me by sacrifice!"
50:6 The heavens declare his righteousness, for God himself is judge. Selah
50:7 "Hear, O my people, and I will speak, O Israel, I will testify against you. I am God, your God.
50:8 Not for your sacrifices do I rebuke you; your burnt offerings are continually before me.
50:9 I will not accept a bull from your house, or goats from your folds.
50:10 For every wild animal of the forest is mine, the cattle on a thousand hills.
50:11 I know all the birds of the air, and all that moves in the field is mine.
50:12 "If I were hungry, I would not tell you, for the world and all that is in it is mine.
50:13 Do I eat the flesh of bulls, or drink the blood of goats?
50:14 Offer to God a sacrifice of thanksgiving, and pay your vows to the Most High.
50:15 Call on me in the day of trouble; I will deliver you, and you shall glorify me."
50:16 But to the wicked God says:"What right have you to recite my statutes, or take my covenant on your lips?
50:17 For you hate discipline, and you cast my words behind you.
50:18 You make friends with a thief when you see one, and you keep company with adulterers.
50:19 "You give your mouth free rein for evil, and your tongue frames deceit.
50:20 You sit and speak against your kin; you slander your own mother's child.
50:21 These things you have done and I have been silent; you thought that I was one just like yourself. But now I rebuke you, and lay the charge before you.
50:22 "Mark this, then, you who forget God, or I will tear you apart, and there will be no one to deliver.
50:23 Those who bring thanksgiving as their sacrifice honor me; to those who go the right way I will show the salvation of God."
text.Coverdale:
50:1 The LORD, the God of gods, has spoken; * he has called the earth from the rising of the sun to its setting.
50:2 Out of Zion, perfect in its beauty, * God reveals himself in glory.
50:3 Our God will come and will not keep silence; * before him there is a consuming flame, and round about him a raging storm.
50:4 He calls the heavens and the earth from above * to witness the judgment of his people.
50:5 "Gather before me my loyal followers, * those who have made a covenant with me and sealed it with sacrifice."
50:6 Let the heavens declare the rightness of his cause; * for God himself is judge.
50:7 Hear, O my people, and I will speak:"O Israel, I will bear witness against you; * for I am God, your God.
50:8 I do not accuse you because of your sacrifices; * your offerings are always before me.
50:9 I will take no bull-calf from your stalls, * nor he-goats out of your pens;
50:10 For all the beasts of the forest are mine, * the herds in their thousands upon the hills.
50:11 I know every bird in the sky, * and the creatures of the fields are in my sight.
50:12 If I were hungry, I would not tell you, * for the whole world is mine and all that is in it.
50:13 Do you think I eat the flesh of bulls, * or drink the blood of goats?
50:14 Offer to God a sacrifice of thanksgiving * and make good your vows to the Most High.
50:15 Call upon me in the day of trouble; * I will deliver you, and you shall honor me."
50:16 But to the wicked God says: * Why do you recite my statutes, and take my covenant upon your lips;
50:17 Since you refuse discipline, * and toss my words behind your back?
50:18 When you see a thief, you make him your friend, * and you cast in your lot with adulterers.
50:19 You have loosed your lips for evil, * and harnessed your tongue to a lie.
50:20 You are always speaking evil of your brother * and slandering your own mother's son.
50:21 These things you have done, and I kept still, * and you thought that I am like you."
50:22 "I have made my accusation; * I have put my case in order before your eyes.
50:23 Consider this well, you who forget God, * lest I rend you and there be none to deliver you.
50:24 Whoever offers me the sacrifice of thanksgiving honors me; * but to those who keep in my way will I show the salvation of God."
text.KJV:
50:1 A Psalm of Asaph. The mighty God, even the Lord, hath spoken, and called the earth from the rising of the sun unto the going down thereof.
50:2 Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty, God hath shined.
50:3 Our God shall come, and shall not keep silence: a fire shall devour before him, and it shall be very tempestuous round about him.
50:4 He shall call to the heavens from above, and to the earth, that he may judge his people.
50:5 Gather my saints together unto me; those that have made a covenant with me by sacrifice.
50:6 And the heavens shall declare his righteousness: for God is judge himself. Selah.
50:7 Hear, O my people, and I will speak; O Israel, and I will testify against thee: I am God, even thy God.
50:8 I will not reprove thee for thy sacrifices or thy burnt offerings, to have been continually before me.
50:9 I will take no bullock out of thy house, nor he goats out of thy folds.
50:10 For every beast of the forest is mine, and the cattle upon a thousand hills.
50:11 I know all the fowls of the mountains: and the wild beasts of the field are mine.
50:12 If I were hungry, I would not tell thee: for the world is mine, and the fulness thereof.
50:13 Will I eat the flesh of bulls, or drink the blood of goats?
50:14 Offer unto God thanksgiving; and pay thy vows unto the most High:
50:15 And call upon me in the day of trouble: I will deliver thee, and thou shalt glorify me.
50:16 But unto the wicked God saith, What hast thou to do to declare my statutes, or that thou shouldest take my covenant in thy mouth?
50:17 Seeing thou hatest instruction, and castest my words behind thee.
50:18 When thou sawest a thief, then thou consentedst with him, and hast been partaker with adulterers.
50:19 Thou givest thy mouth to evil, and thy tongue frameth deceit.
50:20 Thou sittest and speakest against thy brother; thou slanderest thine own mother’s son.
50:21 These things hast thou done, and I kept silence; thou thoughtest that I was altogether such an one as thyself: but I will reprove thee, and set them in order before thine eyes.
50:22 Now consider this, ye that forget God, lest I tear you in pieces, and there be none to deliver.
50:23 Whoso offereth praise glorifieth me: and to him that ordereth his conversation aright will I shew the salvation of God.
text.JPS1985:
50:1 A psalm of Asaph. God, the LORD God spoke and summoned the world from east to west.
50:2 From Zion, perfect in beauty, God appeared
50:3 —let our God come and not fail to act! Devouring fire preceded Him; it stormed around Him fiercely.
50:4 He summoned the heavens above, and the earth, for the trial of His people.
50:5 “Bring in My devotees, who made a covenant with Me over sacrifice! ”
50:6 Then the heavens proclaimed His righteousness, for He is a God who judges. Selah.
50:7 “Pay heed, My people, and I will speak, O Israel, and I will arraign you. I am God, your God.
50:8 I censure you not for your sacrifices, and your burnt offerings, made to Me daily;
50:9 I claim no bull from your estate, no he-goats from your pens.
50:10 For Mine is every animal of the forest, the beasts on a thousand mountains.
50:11 I know every bird of the mountains, the creatures of the field are subject to Me.
50:12 Were I hungry, I would not tell you, for Mine is the world and all it holds.
50:13 Do I eat the flesh of bulls, or drink the blood of he-goats?
50:14 Sacrifice a thank offering to God, and pay your vows to the Most High.
50:15 Call upon Me in time of trouble; I will rescue you, and you shall honor Me.”
50:16 And to the wicked, God said: “Who are you to recite My laws, and mouth the terms of My covenant,
50:17 seeing that you spurn My discipline, and brush My words aside?
50:18 When you see a thief, you fall in with him, and throw in your lot with adulterers;
50:19 you devote your mouth to evil, and yoke your tongue to deceit;
50:20 you are busy maligning your brother, defaming the son of your mother.
50:21 If I failed to act when you did these things, you would fancy that I was like you; so I censure you and confront you with charges.
50:22 Mark this, you who are unmindful of God, lest I tear you apart and no one save you.
50:23 He who sacrifices a thank offering honors Me, and to him who improves his way I will show the salvation of God.”
text.Grail1963:
1 The God of gods, the Lord,
has spoken and summoned the earth,
from the rising of the sun to its setting.
2 Out of Zion's perfect beauty he shines.
3 (Our God comes, he keeps silence no longer.)
Before him fire devours,
around him tempest rages.
4 He calls on the heavens and the earth

to witness his judgment of his people.
5 "Summon before me my people
who made covenant with me by sacrifice."
6 The heavens proclaim his justice,
for he, God, is the judge.
7 "Listen, my people, I will speak;
Israel, I will testify against you,
for I am God, your God.
21c I accuse you, lay the charge before you.
8 I find no fault with your sacrifices,
your offerings are always before me.
9 I do not ask more bullocks from your farms,
nor goats from among your herds.
10 For I own all the beasts of the forest,
beasts in their thousands on my hills.
11 I know all the birds in the sky,
all that moves in the field belongs to me.
12 Were I hungry, I would not tell you,
for I own the world and all it holds.
13 Do you think I eat the flesh of bulls,
or drink the blood of goats?
14 Pay your sacrifice of thanksgiving to God
and render him your votive offerings.
15 Call on me in the day of distress.
I will free and you shall honor me."
16 (But God says to the wicked:)
"But how can you recite my commandments
and take my covenant on your lips,
17 you who despise my law
and throw my words to the winds,
18 you who see a thief and go with him;
who throw in your lot with adulterers,
19 who unbridle your mouth for evil
and whose tongue is plotting crime,
20 you who sit and malign your brother
and slander your own mother's son.
21 You do this, and should I keep silence?
Do you think that I am like you?
22 Mark this, you who never think of God,
lest I seize you and you cannot escape;
23 a sacrifice of thanksgiving honors me
and I will show God's salvation to the upright."
text.Rotherham:
50:1 El, Elohim, Yahweh, hath spoken, and culled the earth, From the rising of the sun, unto the going in thereof:
50:2 Out of Zion the perfection of beauty, God, hath shone forth.
50:3 Let our God come, and let him not keep silence! A fire—before him, shall devour, And, around him, hath it become exceeding tempestuous:
50:4 He calleth, Unto the heavens above, And unto the earth, That he may judge his people.
50:5 Gather yourselves unto me—ye my men of lovingkindness, Who have solemnised my covenant over sacrifice.
50:6 Now have the heavens declared his righteousness, Because, God, is, about to judge. [Selah.]
50:7 Hear, O my people, and I will speak, O Israel, and I will adjure thee, God, thine own God, I am:—
50:8 Not, for thy sacrifices, will I reprove thee, Nor for thine ascending-offerings, before me continually:
50:9 I will not take out of thy house—a bullock, Nor out of thy folds—he-goats;
50:10 For, mine, is every wild-beast of the forest, The cattle on the mountains, in their thousands;
50:11 I know every bird of the mountains, And, the moving things of the plain, are with me:
50:12 If I were hungry, I would not tell thee, For, mine, is the world, and the fulness thereof.
50:13 Will I eat the flesh of mighty oxen? Or, the blood of he-goats, will I drink?
50:14 Sacrifice to God a thankoffering, And pay to the Most High thy vows;
50:15 Call upon me, then, in the day of distress, I will deliver thee, that thou mayest glorify me.
50:16 But, to the lawless one, God saith, What hast, thou, to do, to recount my statutes? Or that thou hast taken up my covenant upon thy mouth?
50:17 Seeing that, thou, hast hated correction, And hast cast my words behind thee;
50:18 If thou sawest a thief, then didst thou run with him,—And, with adulterers, hath been thy chosen life;
50:19 Thy mouth, hast thou thrust into wickedness, And, thy tongue, kept weaving deceit;
50:20 Thou wouldst sit down—Against thine own brother, wouldst thou speak, Against thine own mother’s son, wouldst thou expose a fault:—
50:21 These things, hast thou done, and I have kept silence, Thou thoughtest that I should really be like thyself, I will convict thee, yea I will set [thine offences] in order before thine eyes.
50:22 Understand this, I pray you, ye forgetters of GOD, Lest I tear in pieces, and there be none to deliver:—
50:23 He that sacrificeth a thankoffering, will glorify me,—And will prepare a way by which I may show him the salvation of God.
text.NABRE:
50:1 A psalm of Asaph. The God of gods, the Lord, has spoken and summoned the earth from the rising of the sun to its setting.
50:2 From Zion, the perfection of beauty, God shines forth.
50:3 Our God comes and will not be silent! Devouring fire precedes him, it rages strongly around him.
50:4 He calls to the heavens above and to the earth to judge his people:
50:5 “Gather my loyal ones to me, those who made a covenant with me by sacrifice.”
50:6 The heavens proclaim his righteousness, for God himself is the judge. Selah
50:7 II “Listen, my people, I will speak; Israel, I will testify against you; God, your God, am I.
50:8 Not for your sacrifices do I rebuke you, your burnt offerings are always before me.
50:9 I will not take a bullock from your house, or he-goats from your folds.
50:10 For every animal of the forest is mine, beasts by the thousands on my mountains.
50:11 I know every bird in the heights; whatever moves in the wild is mine.
50:12 Were I hungry, I would not tell you, for mine is the world and all that fills it.
50:13 Do I eat the flesh of bulls or drink the blood of he-goats?
50:14 Offer praise as your sacrifice to God; fulfill your vows to the Most High.
50:15 Then call on me on the day of distress; I will rescue you, and you shall honor me.”
50:16 III But to the wicked God says: “Why do you recite my commandments and profess my covenant with your mouth?
50:17 You hate discipline; you cast my words behind you!
50:18 If you see a thief, you run with him; with adulterers you throw in your lot.
50:19 You give your mouth free rein for evil; you yoke your tongue to deceit.
50:20 You sit and speak against your brother, slandering your mother’s son.
50:21 When you do these things should I be silent? Do you think that I am like you? I accuse you, I lay out the matter before your eyes.
50:22 IV “Now understand this, you who forget God, lest I start ripping apart and there be no rescuer.
50:23 Those who offer praise as a sacrifice honor me; I will let him whose way is steadfast look upon the salvation of God.”
text.DRB:
50:1 Unto the end, a Psalm of David,
50:2 when Nathan the Prophet came to him, after that he had sinned with Bethsabee.
50:3 Have mercy on me, o God, according to thy great mercy. And according to the multitude of thy commiserations, take away mine iniquity.
50:4 Wash me more amply from mine iniquity: & cleanse me from my sin.
50:5 Because I do know mine iniquity: and my sin is before me always.
50:6 To thee only have I sinned, and have done evil before thee: that thou mayst be justified in thy words, and mayst overcome when thou art judged.
50:7 For behold I was conceived in iniquities: & my mother conceived me in sins.
50:8 For behold thou hast loved truth: the uncertain and hidden things of thy wisdom thou hast made manifest to me.
50:9 Thou shalt sprinkle me with hyssop, and I shall be cleansed: thou shalt wash me, and I shall be made whiter than snow.
50:10 To my hearing thou shalt give joy and gladness, and the bones humbled shall rejoice.
50:11 Turn away thy face from my sins: and wipe away all mine iniquities.
50:12 Create a clean heart in me, o God: and renew a right spirit in my bowels.
50:13 Cast me not away from thy face: and thy Holy spirit take not from me.
50:14 Render unto me the joy of thy salvation? And confirm me with the principal spirit.
50:15 I will teach the unjust thy ways: and the impious shall be converted to thee.
50:16 Deliver me from bloods, o God, the God of my salvation: and my tongue shall exult thy justice.
50:17 Lord thou wilt open my lips: & my mouth shall shew forth thy praise.
50:18 Because if thou wouldest have had sacrifice, I had verily given it: with holocausts thou wilt not be delighted.
50:19 Asacrifice to God is an afflicted spirit: a contrite and humbled heart, o God, thou wilt not despise.
50:20 Deal favourably, o Lord, in thy good will with Sion: that the walls of Jerusalem may be built up.
50:21 Then shall thou accept sacrifice of justice, oblations, & holocausts: then shall they lay calves upon thine altar.

text.NRSV:
51:1 Have mercy on me, O God, according to your steadfast love; according to your abundant mercy blot out my transgressions.
51:2 Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity, and cleanse me from my sin.
51:3 For I know my transgressions, and my sin is ever before me.
51:4 Against you, you alone, have I sinned, and done what is evil in your sight, so that you are justified in your sentence and blameless when you pass judgment.
51:5 Indeed, I was born guilty, a sinner when my mother conceived me.
51:6 You desire truth in the inward being; therefore teach me wisdom in my secret heart.
51:7 Purge me with hyssop, and I shall be clean; wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.
51:8 Let me hear joy and gladness; let the bones that you have crushed rejoice.
51:9 Hide your face from my sins, and blot out all my iniquities.
51:10 Create in me a clean heart, O God, and put a new and right spirit within me.
51:11 Do not cast me away from your presence, and do not take your holy spirit from me.
51:12 Restore to me the joy of your salvation, and sustain in me a willing spirit.
51:13 Then I will teach transgressors your ways, and sinners will return to you.
51:14 Deliver me from bloodshed, O God, O God of my salvation, and my tongue will sing aloud of your deliverance.
51:15 O Lord, open my lips, and my mouth will declare your praise.
51:16 For you have no delight in sacrifice; if I were to give a burnt offering, you would not be pleased.
51:17 The sacrifice acceptable to God is a broken spirit; a broken and contrite heart, O God, you will not despise.
51:18 Do good to Zion in your good pleasure; rebuild the walls of Jerusalem,
51:19 then you will delight in right sacrifices, in burnt offerings and whole burnt offerings; then bulls will be offered on your altar.
text.Coverdale:
51:1 Have mercy on me, O God, according to your loving-kindness; * in your great compassion blot out my offenses.
51:2 Wash me through and through from my wickedness * and cleanse me from my sin.
51:3 For I know my transgressions, * and my sin is ever before me.
51:4 Against you only have I sinned * and done what is evil in your sight.
51:5 And so you are justified when you speak * and upright in your judgment.
51:6 Indeed, I have been wicked from my birth, * a sinner from my mother's womb.
51:7 For behold, you look for truth deep within me, * and will make me understand wisdom secretly.
51:8 Purge me from my sin, and I shall be pure; * wash me, and I shall be clean indeed.
51:9 Make me hear of joy and gladness, * that the body you have broken may rejoice.
51:10 Hide your face from my sins * and blot out all my iniquities.
51:11 Create in me a clean heart, O God, * and renew a right spirit within me.
51:12 Cast me not away from your presence * and take not your holy Spirit from me.
51:13 Give me the joy of your saving help again * and sustain me with your bountiful Spirit.
51:14 I shall teach your ways to the wicked, * and sinners shall return to you.
51:15 Deliver me from death, O God, * and my tongue shall sing of your righteousness, O God of my salvation.
51:16 Open my lips, O Lord, * and my mouth shall proclaim your praise.
51:17 Had you desired it, I would have offered sacrifice, * but you take no delight in burnt-offerings.
51:18 The sacrifice of God is a troubled spirit; * a broken and contrite heart, O God, you will not despise.
51:19 Be favorable and gracious to Zion, * and rebuild the walls of Jerusalem.
51:20 Then you will be pleased with the appointed sacrifices, with burnt-offerings and oblations; * then shall they offer young bullocks upon your altar.
text.KJV:
51:1 To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David, when Nathan the prophet came unto him, after he had gone in to Bath–sheba. Have mercy upon me, O God, according to thy lovingkindness: according unto the multitude of thy tender mercies blot out my transgressions.
51:2 Wash me throughly from mine iniquity, and cleanse me from my sin.
51:3 For I acknowledge my transgressions: and my sin is ever before me.
51:4 Against thee, thee only, have I sinned, and done this evil in thy sight: that thou mightest be justified when thou speakest, and be clear when thou judgest.
51:5 Behold, I was shapen in iniquity; and in sin did my mother conceive me.
51:6 Behold, thou desirest truth in the inward parts: and in the hidden part thou shalt make me to know wisdom.
51:7 Purge me with hyssop, and I shall be clean: wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.
51:8 Make me to hear joy and gladness; that the bones which thou hast broken may rejoice.
51:9 Hide thy face from my sins, and blot out all mine iniquities.
51:10 Create in me a clean heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within me.
51:11 Cast me not away from thy presence; and take not thy holy spirit from me.
51:12 Restore unto me the joy of thy salvation; and uphold me with thy free spirit.
51:13 Then will I teach transgressors thy ways; and sinners shall be converted unto thee.
51:14 Deliver me from bloodguiltiness, O God, thou God of my salvation: and my tongue shall sing aloud of thy righteousness.
51:15 O Lord, open thou my lips; and my mouth shall shew forth thy praise.
51:16 For thou desirest not sacrifice; else would I give it: thou delightest not in burnt offering.
51:17 The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit: a broken and a contrite heart, O God, thou wilt not despise.
51:18 Do good in thy good pleasure unto Zion: build thou the walls of Jerusalem.
51:19 Then shalt thou be pleased with the sacrifices of righteousness, with burnt offering and whole burnt offering: then shall they offer bullocks upon thine altar.
text.JPS1985:
51:1 For the leader. A psalm of David,
51:2 when Nathan the prophet came to him after he had come to Bathsheba.
51:3 Have mercy upon me, O God, as befits Your faithfulness; in keeping with Your abundant compassion, blot out my transgressions.
51:4 Wash me thoroughly of my iniquity, and purify me of my sin;
51:5 for I recognize my transgressions, and am ever conscious of my sin.
51:6 Against You alone have I sinned, and done what is evil in Your sight; so You are just in Your sentence, and right in Your judgment.
51:7 Indeed I was born with iniquity; with sin my mother conceived me.
51:8 Indeed You desire truth about that which is hidden; teach me wisdom about secret things.
51:9 Purge me with hyssop till I am pure; wash me till I am whiter than snow.
51:10 Let me hear tidings of joy and gladness; let the bones You have crushed exult.
51:11 Hide Your face from my sins; blot out all my iniquities.
51:12 Fashion a pure heart for me, O God; create in me a steadfast spirit.
51:13 Do not cast me out of Your presence, or take Your holy spirit away from me.
51:14 Let me again rejoice in Your help; let a vigorous spirit sustain me.
51:15 I will teach transgressors Your ways, that sinners may return to You.
51:16 Save me from bloodguilt, O God, God, my deliverer, that I may sing forth Your beneficence.
51:17 O Lord, open my lips, and let my mouth declare Your praise.
51:18 You do not want me to bring sacrifices; You do not desire burnt offerings;
51:19 True sacrifice to God is a contrite spirit; God, You will not despise a contrite and crushed heart.
51:20 May it please You to make Zion prosper; rebuild the walls of Jerusalem.
51:21 Then You will want sacrifices offered in righteousness, burnt and whole offerings; then bulls will be offered on Your altar.

text.Rotherham:
51:1 Be favorable unto me, O God, according to thy lovingkindness, And, in the multitude of thy compassions, blot out my transgressions;
51:2 Thoroughly wash me from mine iniquity, And, from my sin, make me pure;
51:3 For, my transgressions, do, I, acknowledge, And, my sin, is before me continually:
51:4 Against thee—against thee alone, have I sinned, And, wickedness—in thine eyes, have I done,—That thou mayest, Be justified when thou speakest,—Be clear when thou judgest.
51:5 Lo! in iniquity, was I brought forth, And, in sin, did my mother conceive me.
51:6 Lo! faithfulness, hast thou desired in the inward parts, Yea, in the hidden part, wilt thou cause me to know, wisdom.
51:7 Wilt thou cleanse me from sin with hyssop, That I may be pure? Wilt thou wash me, That I may be whiter, than snow?
51:8 Wilt thou cause me to hear joy and gladness? The bones thou hast crushed would exult.
51:9 Hide thy face from my sins,—And, all mine iniquities, blot out;
51:10 A pure heart, create for me, O God, And, a steadfast spirit, renew within me.
51:11 Do not cast me away from thy presence, And, thy Holy Spirit, do not take from me:
51:12 Restore unto me the joy of thy salvation, And, with a willing spirit, wilt thou uphold me:—
51:13 I would teach transgressors thy ways, And, sinners, unto thee, should return.
51:14 Rescue me from shed-blood, O God, the God of my salvation, My tongue should shout thy righteousness;
51:15 O My Lord! my lips, wilt thou open, And, my mouth, shall declare thy praise.
51:16 For thou wilt not desire sacrifice—that I should give it, Ascending-offering, will not please:
51:17 The sacrifices of God, are a spirit that is broken,—A heart—broken and crushed, O God, thou wilt not despise.
51:18 Do good, in thy good-pleasure, unto Zion, Wilt thou build the walls of Jerusalem!
51:19 Then, shalt thou desire the sacrifices of righteousness, Ascending-sacrifice and whole burnt-offering, Then, shall ascend upon thine altar, young bulls.
text.NABRE:
51:1 For the leader. A psalm of David,
51:2 when Nathan the prophet came to him after he had gone in to Bathsheba.
51:3 I Have mercy on me, God, in accord with your merciful love; in your abundant compassion blot out my transgressions.
51:4 Thoroughly wash away my guilt; and from my sin cleanse me.
51:5 For I know my transgressions; my sin is always before me.
51:6 Against you, you alone have I sinned; I have done what is evil in your eyes So that you are just in your word, and without reproach in your judgment.
51:7 Behold, I was born in guilt, in sin my mother conceived me.
51:8 Behold, you desire true sincerity; and secretly you teach me wisdom.
51:9 Cleanse me with hyssop, that I may be pure; wash me, and I will be whiter than snow.
51:10 You will let me hear gladness and joy; the bones you have crushed will rejoice.
51:11 II Turn away your face from my sins; blot out all my iniquities.
51:12 A clean heart create for me, God; renew within me a steadfast spirit.
51:13 Do not drive me from before your face, nor take from me your holy spirit.
51:14 Restore to me the gladness of your salvation; uphold me with a willing spirit.
51:15 I will teach the wicked your ways, that sinners may return to you.
51:16 Rescue me from violent bloodshed, God, my saving God, and my tongue will sing joyfully of your justice.
51:17 Lord, you will open my lips; and my mouth will proclaim your praise.
51:18 For you do not desire sacrifice or I would give it; a burnt offering you would not accept.
51:19 My sacrifice, O God, is a contrite spirit; a contrite, humbled heart, O God, you will not scorn.
51:20 III Treat Zion kindly according to your good will; build up the walls of Jerusalem.
51:21 Then you will desire the sacrifices of the just, burnt offering and whole offerings; then they will offer up young bulls on your altar.
text.DRB:
51:1 Unto the end, understanding to David,
51:2 When Doeg the Idumeite came and told Saul: David is come into the house of Achimelech.
51:3 Why doest thou glory in malice, which are mighty in iniquity?
51:4 All the day hath thy tongue thought injustice: as a sharp razor thou hast done guile.
51:5 Thou hast loved malice more than benignity: iniquity rather than to speak equity.
51:6 Thou hast loved all words of precipitation, a deceitful tongue.
51:7 Therefore will God destroy thee for ever, he will pluck thee out, & remove thee out of thy tabernacle: & thy root out of the land of the living.
51:8 The just shall see, and fear, and shall laugh at him, and they shall say:
51:9 Behold the man, that hath not put God for his helper. But hath hoped in the multitude of his riches, and hath prevailed in his vanity.
51:10 But I as a fruitful olive tree in the house of God, have hoped in the mercy of God for ever: and for ever and ever.
51:11 I will confess to thee for ever, because thou hast done it: and I will expect thy name, because it is good in the sight of thy Saints.

text.NRSV:
52:1 Why do you boast, O mighty one, of mischief done against the godly? All day long
52:2 you are plotting destruction. Your tongue is like a sharp razor, you worker of treachery.
52:3 You love evil more than good, and lying more than speaking the truth. Selah
52:4 You love all words that devour, O deceitful tongue.
52:5 But God will break you down forever; he will snatch and tear you from your tent; he will uproot you from the land of the living. Selah
52:6 The righteous will see, and fear, and will laugh at the evildoer, saying,
52:7 "See the one who would not take refuge in God, but trusted in abundant riches, and sought refuge in wealth!"
52:8 But I am like a green olive tree in the house of God. I trust in the steadfast love of God forever and ever.
52:9 I will thank you forever, because of what you have done. In the presence of the faithful I will proclaim your name, for it is good.
text.Coverdale:
52:1 You tyrant, why do you boast of wickedness * against the godly all day long?
52:2 You plot ruin; your tongue is like a sharpened razor, * O worker of deception.
52:3 You love evil more than good * and lying more than speaking the truth.
52:4 You love all words that hurt, * O you deceitful tongue.
52:5 Oh, that God would demolish you utterly, * topple you, and snatch you from your dwelling, and root you out of the land of the living!
52:6 The righteous shall see and tremble, * and they shall laugh at him, saying,
52:7 "This is the one who did not take God for a refuge, * but trusted in great wealth and relied upon wickedness."
52:8 But I am like a green olive tree in the house of God; * I trust in the mercy of God for ever and ever.
52:9 I will give you thanks for what you have done * and declare the goodness of your Name in the presence of the godly.
text.KJV:
52:1 To the chief Musician, Maschil, A Psalm of David, when Doeg the Edomite came and told Saul, and said unto him, David is come to the house of Ahimelech. Why boastest thou thyself in mischief, O mighty man? the goodness of God endureth continually.
52:2 Thy tongue deviseth mischiefs; like a sharp razor, working deceitfully.
52:3 Thou lovest evil more than good; and lying rather than to speak righteousness. Selah.
52:4 Thou lovest all devouring words, O thou deceitful tongue.
52:5 God shall likewise destroy thee for ever, he shall take thee away, and pluck thee out of thy dwelling place, and root thee out of the land of the living. Selah.
52:6 The righteous also shall see, and fear, and shall laugh at him:
52:7 Lo, this is the man that made not God his strength; but trusted in the abundance of his riches, and strengthened himself in his wickedness.
52:8 But I am like a green olive tree in the house of God: I trust in the mercy of God for ever and ever.
52:9 I will praise thee for ever, because thou hast done it: and I will wait on thy name; for it is good before thy saints.
text.JPS1985:
52:1 For the leader. A maskil of David,
52:2 when Doeg the Edomite came and informed Saul, telling him, “David came to Ahimelech’s house.”
52:3 Why do you boast of your evil, brave fellow? God’s faithfulness never ceases.
52:4 Your tongue devises mischief, like a sharpened razor that works treacherously.
52:5 You prefer evil to good, the lie, to speaking truthfully. Selah.
52:6 You love all pernicious words, treacherous speech.
52:7 So God will tear you down for good, will break you and pluck you from your tent, and root you out of the land of the living. Selah.
52:8 The righteous, seeing it, will be awestruck; they will jibe at him, saying,
52:9 “Here was a fellow who did not make God his refuge, but trusted in his great wealth, relied upon his mischief.”
52:10 But I am like a thriving olive tree in God’s house; I trust in the faithfulness of God forever and ever.
52:11 I praise You forever, for You have acted; I declare that Your name is good in the presence of Your faithful ones.
text.Grail1963:
.3 Why do you boast of your wickedness,
you champion of evil,

4 planning ruin all day long,

text.Rotherham:
52:1 Why dost thou boast thyself of wickedness, O mighty man? The lovingkindness of GOD, [lasteth] all the day.
52:2 Engulfing ruin, doth thy tongue devise, Like a whetted razor, working deceit;
52:3 Thou lovest Evil more than good, Falsehood, more than speaking righteousness. [Selah.]
52:4 Thou lovest all devouring words, O deceitful tongue!
52:5 GOD also, will break thee down utterly, He will snatch thee up and tear thee away tentless, And uproot thee, out of the land of the living. [Selah.]
52:6 The righteous shall both see and fear, And, over him, shall laugh:
52:7 Lo! the man who made not God his refuge,—But trusted in the abundance of his riches, Emboldened himself in his wealth!
52:8 But, I, am like a flourishing olive-tree, in the house of God, I have put confidence in the lovingkindness of God, for times age-abiding and beyond.
52:9 I will praise thee to times age-abiding, Because thou didst effectually work,—And I will wait on thy Name, Because it is good, In the presence of thy men of lovingkindness.
text.NABRE:
52:1 For the leader. A maskil of David,
52:2 when Doeg the Edomite entered and reported to Saul, saying to him: “David has entered the house of Ahimelech.”
52:3 I Why do you glory in what is evil, you who are mighty by the mercy of God? All day long
52:4 you are thinking up intrigues; your tongue is like a sharpened razor, you worker of deceit.
52:5 You love evil more than good, lying rather than saying what is right. Selah
52:6 You love all the words that create confusion, you deceitful tongue.
52:7 II God too will strike you down forever, he will lay hold of you and pluck you from your tent, uproot you from the land of the living. Selah
52:8 The righteous will see and they will fear; but they will laugh at him:
52:9 “Behold the man! He did not take God as his refuge, but he trusted in the abundance of his wealth, and grew powerful through his wickedness.”
52:10 III But I, like an olive tree flourishing in the house of God, I trust in God’s mercy forever and ever.
52:11 I will thank you forever for what you have done. I will put my hope in your name—for it is good, —in the presence of those devoted to you.
text.DRB:
52:1 Unto the end, for Mæleth, understandings of David. The fool hath said in his heart: There is no God.
52:2 They are corrupt, and become abominable in iniquities: there is not that doth good.
52:3 God hath looked forth from heaven, upon the children of men: to see if there be that understandeth, or seeketh after God.
52:4 All have declined, they are become unprofitable together: there is not that doth good, no there is not one.
52:5 Shall they not all know that work iniquity, that devour my people as food of bread?
52:6 God they have not invocated: there have they trembled for fear, where no fear was. Because God hath dissipated the bones of them that please men: they are confounded, because God hath despised them.
52:7 Who will give out of Sion the salvation of Israel? When God shall convert the captivity of his people: Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.

text.NRSV:
53:1 Fools say in their hearts,"There is no God."They are corrupt, they commit abominable acts; there is no one who does good.
53:2 God looks down from heaven on humankind to see if there are any who are wise, who seek after God.
53:3 They have all fallen away, they are all alike perverse; there is no one who does good, no, not one.
53:4 Have they no knowledge, those evildoers, who eat up my people as they eat bread, and do not call upon God?
53:5 There they shall be in great terror, in terror such as has not been. For God will scatter the bones of the ungodly; they will be put to shame, for God has rejected them.
53:6 O that deliverance for Israel would come from Zion! When God restores the fortunes of his people, Jacob will rejoice; Israel will be glad.
text.Coverdale:
53:1 The fool has said in his heart,"There is no God."* All are corrupt and commit abominable acts; there is none who does any good.
53:2 God looks down from heaven upon us all, * to see if there is any who is wise, if there is one who seeks after God.
53:3 Every one has proved faithless; all alike have turned bad; * there is none who does good; no, not one.
53:4 Have they no knowledge, those evildoers * who eat up my people like bread and do not call upon God?
53:5 See how greatly they tremble, such trembling as never was; * for God has scattered the bones of the enemy; they are put to shame, because God has rejected them.
53:6 Oh, that Israel's deliverance would come out of Zion! * when God restores the fortunes of his people Jacob will rejoice and Israel be glad.
text.KJV:
53:1 To the chief Musician upon Mahalath, Maschil, A Psalm of David. The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God. Corrupt are they, and have done abominable iniquity: there is none that doeth good.
53:2 God looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there were any that did understand, that did seek God.
53:3 Every one of them is gone back: they are altogether become filthy; there is none that doeth good, no, not one.
53:4 Have the workers of iniquity no knowledge? who eat up my people as they eat bread: they have not called upon God.
53:5 There were they in great fear, where no fear was: for God hath scattered the bones of him that encampeth against thee: thou hast put them to shame, because God hath despised them.
53:6 Oh that the salvation of Israel were come out of Zion! When God bringeth back the captivity of his people, Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.
text.JPS1985:
53:1 For the leader; on mahalath. A maskil of David.
53:2 The benighted man thinks, “God does not care.” Man’s wrongdoing is corrupt and loathsome; no one does good.
53:3 God looks down from heaven on mankind to find a man of understanding, a man mindful of God.
53:4 Everyone is dross, altogether foul; there is none who does good, not even one.
53:5 Are they so witless, those evildoers, who devour my people as they devour food, and do not invoke God?
53:6 There they will be seized with fright — never was there such a fright— for God has scattered the bones of your besiegers; you have put them to shame, for God has rejected them.
53:7 O that the deliverance of Israel might come from Zion! When God restores the fortunes of His people, Jacob will exult, Israel will rejoice.

text.Rotherham:
53:1 The impious hath said in his heart, There is no God! They have acted corruptly, They have wrought abominable perversity, There is none that doeth good:—
53:2 God, out of the heavens, looked down upon the sons of men,—To see whether there was one that showed wisdom, Enquiring after God.
53:3 They all, have turned back, together have they become tainted,—There is none that doeth good, Not so much as, one!
53:4 Are the workers of iniquity, without knowledge? Devouring my people, [as] they devour food? Upon God, have they not called.
53:5 There have they been in great dread where no dread was, Because, God, hath scattered the bones of thy besieger,—Thou hast put [him] to shame, Because, God, had, rejected, them.

text.NABRE:
53:1 For the leader; according to Mahalath. A maskil of David.
53:2 I The fool says in his heart, “There is no God.” They act corruptly and practice injustice; there is none that does good.
53:3 God looks out from the heavens upon the children of Adam, To see if there is a discerning person who is seeking God.
53:4 All have gone astray; each one is altogether perverse. There is not one who does what is good, not even one.
53:5 II Do they not know better, those who do evil, who feed upon my people as they feed upon bread? Have they not called upon God?
53:6 They are going to fear his name with great fear, though they had not feared it before. For God will scatter the bones of those encamped against you. They will surely be put to shame, for God has rejected them.

text.DRB:
53:1 Unto the end, in songs understanding for David
53:2 when the Zipheites were come, and said to Saul: Is not David hid with us?
53:3 O God save me in thy name: and in thy strength judge me.
53:4 O God hear my prayer with thine ears: receive the words of my mouth.
53:5 Because strangers have risen up against me, & the strong have sought my soul: and they have not set God before their eyes.
53:6 For behold God helpeth me: and our Lord is the receiver of my soul.
53:7 Turn away the evils of mine enemies: and in thy truth destroy them.
53:8 I will voluntarily sacrifice to thee, and will confess to thy name o Lord, because it is good:
53:9 Because thou hast delivered me out of all tribulation: and mine eye hath looked down upon mine enemies.

text.NRSV:
54:1 Save me, O God, by your name, and vindicate me by your might.
54:2 Hear my prayer, O God; give ear to the words of my mouth.
54:3 For the insolent have risen against me, the ruthless seek my life; they do not set God before them. Selah
54:4 But surely, God is my helper; the Lord is the upholder of my life.
54:5 He will repay my enemies for their evil. In your faithfulness, put an end to them.
54:6 With a freewill offering I will sacrifice to you; I will give thanks to your name, O Lord, for it is good.
54:7 For he has delivered me from every trouble, and my eye has looked in triumph on my enemies.
text.Coverdale:
54:1 Save me, O God, by your Name; * in your might, defend my cause.
54:2 Hear my prayer, O God; * give ear to the words of my mouth.
54:3 For the arrogant have risen up against me, and the ruthless have sought my life, * those who have no regard for God.
54:4 Behold, God is my helper; * it is the Lord who sustains my life.
54:5 Render evil to those who spy on me; * in your faithfulness, destroy them.
54:6 I will offer you a freewill sacrifice * and praise your Name, O LORD, for it is good.
54:7 For you have rescued me from every trouble, * and my eye has seen the ruin of my foes.
text.KJV:
54:1 To the chief Musician on Neginoth, Maschil, A Psalm of David, when the Ziphims came and said to Saul, Doth not David hide himself with us? Save me, O God, by thy name, and judge me by thy strength.
54:2 Hear my prayer, O God; give ear to the words of my mouth.
54:3 For strangers are risen up against me, and oppressors seek after my soul: they have not set God before them. Selah.
54:4 Behold, God is mine helper: the Lord is with them that uphold my soul.
54:5 He shall reward evil unto mine enemies: cut them off in thy truth.
54:6 I will freely sacrifice unto thee: I will praise thy name, O Lord; for it is good.
54:7 For he hath delivered me out of all trouble: and mine eye hath seen his desire upon mine enemies.
text.JPS1985:
54:1 For the leader; with instrumental music. A maskil of David,
54:2 when the Ziphites came and told Saul, “Know, David is in hiding among us.”
54:3 O God, deliver me by Your name; by Your power vindicate me.
54:4 O God, hear my prayer; give ear to the words of my mouth.
54:5 For strangers have risen against me, and ruthless men seek my life; they are unmindful of God. Selah.
54:6 See, God is my helper; the Lord is my support.
54:7 He will repay the evil of my watchful foes; by Your faithfulness, destroy them!
54:8 Then I will offer You a freewill sacrifice; I will praise Your name, LORD, for it is good,
54:9 for it has saved me from my foes, and let me gaze triumphant upon my enemies.

text.NABRE:
54:1 For the leader. On stringed instruments. A maskil of David,
54:2 when the Ziphites came and said to Saul, “David is hiding among us.”
54:3 I O God, by your name save me. By your strength defend my cause.
54:4 O God, hear my prayer. Listen to the words of my mouth.
