text.DRB:
54:1 Unto the end in songs, understanding to David.
54:2 Hear my prayer, o God, despise not my petition.
54:3 Attend to me and hear me.
54:4 I am made sorrowful in my exercise: and am troubled at the voice of the enemy: and at the tribulation of the sinner. Because they have wrested iniquities upon me: & in anger they were troublesome to me.
54:5 My heart is troubled in me: & the fear of death is fallen upon me.
54:6 Fear and trembling are come upon me: and darkness hath covered me.
54:7 And I said: Who will give me wings as of a dove, and I will fly: and rest?
54:8 Lo I have gone far flying away: and I abode in the wilderness.
54:9 I expected him, that saved me from pusillanimity of spirit, and tempest.
54:10 Precipitate, o Lord, and divide their tongues: because I have seen iniquity, and contradiction in the city.
54:11 Day and night shall iniquity compass it upon the walls thereof, and labour in the midst thereof, and injustice.
54:12 And there hath not ceased out of the streets thereof usury and guile.
54:13 For if mine enemy had spoken evil to me, I would verily have born it. And if he that hated me had spoken great things upon me: I would perhaps have hid myself from him.
54:14 But thou a man of the same mind: my guide, and my familiar.
54:15 Which didest take sweet meats together with me: in the house of God we walked with consent.
54:16 Let death come upon them: and let them go down quick into hell. Because there is wickedness in their habitations, in the midst of them.
54:17 But I have cried to God, and our Lord will save me.
54:18 In the evening, and morning, & at midday, I will speak, and declare, and he will hear my voice.
54:19 He will redeem my soul in peace from them, that approach to me: because among many they were with me.
54:20 God will hear, and he which is before the worlds will humble them.
54:21 For there is no change with them, & they feared not God: he hath stretched forth his hand in repaying.
54:22 They have contaminated his testament, they are divided by the wrath of his countenance; and his heart hath approached. His words are made softer than oil: and the same are darts.
54:23 Cast thy care upon our Lord: and he will nourish thee: he will not give fluctuation to the just for ever.
54:24 But thou, o God, wilt bring them down into the pit of destruction. Bloody and deceitful men shall not live half their days, but I will hope in thee, o Lord.

text.NRSV:
55:1 Give ear to my prayer, O God; do not hide yourself from my supplication.
55:2 Attend to me, and answer me; I am troubled in my complaint. I am distraught
55:3 by the noise of the enemy, because of the clamor of the wicked. For they bring trouble upon me, and in anger they cherish enmity against me.
55:4 My heart is in anguish within me, the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
55:5 Fear and trembling come upon me, and horror overwhelms me.
55:6 And I say,"O that I had wings like a dove! I would fly away and be at rest;
55:7 truly, I would flee far away; I would lodge in the wilderness; Selah
55:8 I would hurry to find a shelter for myself from the raging wind and tempest."
55:9 Confuse, O Lord, confound their speech; for I see violence and strife in the city.
55:10 Day and night they go around it on its walls, and iniquity and trouble are within it;
55:11 ruin is in its midst; oppression and fraud do not depart from its marketplace.
55:12 It is not enemies who taunt me— I could bear that; it is not adversaries who deal insolently with me— I could hide from them.
55:13 But it is you, my equal, my companion, my familiar friend,
55:14 with whom I kept pleasant company; we walked in the house of God with the throng.
55:15 Let death come upon them; let them go down alive to Sheol; for evil is in their homes and in their hearts.
55:16 But I call upon God, and the Lord will save me.
55:17 Evening and morning and at noon I utter my complaint and moan, and he will hear my voice.
55:18 He will redeem me unharmed from the battle that I wage, for many are arrayed against me.
55:19 God, who is enthroned from of old, Selah will hear, and will humble them— because they do not change, and do not fear God.
55:20 My companion laid hands on a friend and violated a covenant with me
55:21 with speech smoother than butter, but with a heart set on war; with words that were softer than oil, but in fact were drawn swords.
55:22 Cast your burden on the Lord, and he will sustain you; he will never permit the righteous to be moved.
55:23 But you, O God, will cast them down into the lowest pit; the bloodthirsty and treacherous shall not live out half their days. But I will trust in you.
text.Coverdale:
55:1 Hear my prayer, O God; * do not hide yourself from my petition.
55:2 Listen to me and answer me; * I have no peace, because of my cares.
55:3 I am shaken by the noise of the enemy * and by the pressure of the wicked;
55:4 For they have cast an evil spell upon me * and are set against me in fury.
55:5 My heart quakes within me, * and the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
55:6 Fear and trembling have come over me, * and horror overwhelms me.
55:7 And I said,"Oh, that I had wings like a dove! * I would fly away and be at rest.
55:8 I would flee to a far-off place * and make my lodging in the wilderness.
55:9 I would hasten to escape * from the stormy wind and tempest."
55:10 Swallow them up, O Lord; confound their speech; * for I have seen violence and strife in the city.
55:11 Day and night the watchmen make their rounds upon her walls, * but trouble and misery are in the midst of her.
55:12 There is corruption at her heart; * her streets are never free of oppression and deceit.
55:13 For had it been an adversary who taunted me, then I could have borne it; * or had it been an enemy who vaunted himself against me, then I could have hidden from him.
55:14 But it was you, a man after my own heart, * my companion, my own familiar friend.
55:15 We took sweet counsel together, * and walked with the throng in the house of God.
55:16 Let death come upon them suddenly; let them go down alive to the grave; * for wickedness is in their dwellings, in their very midst.
55:17 But I will call upon God, * and the LORD will deliver me.
55:18 In the evening, in the morning, and at noonday, I will complain and lament, * and he will hear my voice.
55:19 He will bring me safely back from the battle waged against me; * for there are many who fight me.
55:20 God, who is enthroned of old, will hear me and bring them down; * they never change; they do not fear God.
55:21 My companion stretched forth his hand against his comrade; * he has broken his covenant.
55:22 His speech is softer than butter, * but war is in his heart.
55:23 His words are smoother than oil, * but they are drawn swords.
55:24 Cast your burden upon the LORD, and he will sustain you; * he will never let the righteous stumble.
55:25 For you will bring the bloodthirsty and deceitful * down to the pit of destruction, O God.
55:26 They shall not live out half their days, * but I will put my trust in you.
text.KJV:
55:1 To the chief Musician on Neginoth, Maschil, A Psalm of David. Give ear to my prayer, O God; and hide not thyself from my supplication.
55:2 Attend unto me, and hear me: I mourn in my complaint, and make a noise;
55:3 Because of the voice of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked: for they cast iniquity upon me, and in wrath they hate me.
55:4 My heart is sore pained within me: and the terrors of death are fallen upon me.
55:5 Fearfulness and trembling are come upon me, and horror hath overwhelmed me.
55:6 And I said, Oh that I had wings like a dove! for then would I fly away, and be at rest.
55:7 Lo, then would I wander far off, and remain in the wilderness. Selah.
55:8 I would hasten my escape from the windy storm and tempest.
55:9 Destroy, O Lord, and divide their tongues: for I have seen violence and strife in the city.
55:10 Day and night they go about it upon the walls thereof: mischief also and sorrow are in the midst of it.
55:11 Wickedness is in the midst thereof: deceit and guile depart not from her streets.
55:12 For it was not an enemy that reproached me; then I could have borne it: neither was it he that hated me that did magnify himself against me; then I would have hid myself from him:
55:13 But it was thou, a man mine equal, my guide, and mine acquaintance.
55:14 We took sweet counsel together, and walked unto the house of God in company.
55:15 Let death seize upon them, and let them go down quick into hell: for wickedness is in their dwellings, and among them.
55:16 As for me, I will call upon God; and the Lord shall save me.
55:17 Evening, and morning, and at noon, will I pray, and cry aloud: and he shall hear my voice.
55:18 He hath delivered my soul in peace from the battle that was against me: for there were many with me.
55:19 God shall hear, and afflict them, even he that abideth of old. Selah. Because they have no changes, therefore they fear not God.
55:20 He hath put forth his hands against such as be at peace with him: he hath broken his covenant.
55:21 The words of his mouth were smoother than butter, but war was in his heart: his words were softer than oil, yet were they drawn swords.
55:22 Cast thy burden upon the Lord, and he shall sustain thee: he shall never suffer the righteous to be moved.
55:23 But thou, O God, shalt bring them down into the pit of destruction: bloody and deceitful men shall not live out half their days; but I will trust in thee.
text.JPS1985:
55:1 For the leader; with instrumental music. A maskil of David.
55:2 Give ear, O God, to my prayer; do not ignore my plea;
55:3 pay heed to me and answer me. I am tossed about, complaining and moaning
55:4 at the clamor of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked; for they bring evil upon me and furiously harass me.
55:5 My heart is convulsed within me; terrors of death assail me.
55:6 Fear and trembling invade me; I am clothed with horror.
55:7 I said, “O that I had the wings of a dove! I would fly away and find rest;
55:8 surely, I would flee far off; I would lodge in the wilderness; selah
55:9 I would soon find me a refuge from the sweeping wind, from the tempest.”
55:10 O Lord, confound their speech, confuse it! For I see lawlessness and strife in the city;
55:11 day and night they make their rounds on its walls; evil and mischief are inside it.
55:12 Malice is within it; fraud and deceit never leave its square.
55:13 It is not an enemy who reviles me —I could bear that; it is not my foe who vaunts himself against me —I could hide from him;
55:14 but it is you, my equal, my companion, my friend;
55:15 sweet was our fellowship; we walked together in God’s house.
55:16 Let Him incite death against them; may they go down alive into Sheol! For where they dwell, there evil is.
55:17 As for me, I call to God; the LORD will deliver me.
55:18 Evening, morning, and noon, I complain and moan, and He hears my voice.
55:19 He redeems me unharmed from the battle against me; it is as though many are on my side.
55:20 God who has reigned from the first, who will have no successor, hears and humbles those who have no fear of God. Selah.
55:21 He harmed his ally, he broke his pact;
55:22 his talk was smoother than butter, yet his mind was on war; his words were more soothing than oil, yet they were drawn swords.
55:23 Cast your burden on the LORD and He will sustain you; He will never let the righteous man collapse.
55:24 For You, O God, will bring them down to the nethermost Pit— those murderous, treacherous men; they shall not live out half their days; but I trust in You.

text.Rotherham:
55:1 Give ear, O God, to my prayer, And do not hide thyself from my supplication:
55:2 Attend unto me, and answer me, I may ramble in my complaining, And be driven to and fro—
55:3 Because of the noise of the enemy, By reason of the oppression of the lawless one, For they would let trouble drop upon me, And, in anger, would they entrap me.
55:4 My heart, would writhe within me, And, the terrors of Death, have fallen upon me:
55:5 Fear and trembling, would enter me, And there would have overwhelmed me—a horror!
55:6 Then I said—Oh that there were given me a pinion, like a dove, I would fly away and be at rest:
55:7 Lo! I would take a distant flight, I would tarry in the wilderness. [Selah.]
55:8 I would hasten mine escape, From rushing wind, from storm.
55:9 Confuse, O My Lord, divide their speech, For I have seen violence and contention in the city;
55:10 Day and night, they go round her, upon her walls, And, trouble and misery, are in her midst;
55:11 Engulfing ruin, is in her midst, And there depart not from her broadway, oppression and deceit.
55:12 For it is, not an enemy, that reproacheth me, Or I could bear it,—Not one that hath hated me, who, against me, hath magnified himself, Or I might hide myself from him;
55:13 But it is, thou, a man esteemed as mine equal, mine associate, and mine acquaintance;
55:14 So that together have we been wont to find sweetness in counsel, In the house of God, used we to walk in the throng.
55:15 Desolations on them! Let them go down into hades alive, For, wicked doings, are at home within them.
55:16 I, unto God, will cry,—And, Yahweh, will save me.
55:17 At evening and morning and high noon, have I been wont to lament and complain, And he hath heard my voice!
55:18 He hath completely redeemed my soul, out of the attack upon me, For, in multitudes, were they [in conflict] with me.
55:19 GOD will hear, Yea He will humble them who aforetime sat [enthroned] . [Selah.] With whom are no changings, Neither have they revered God.
55:20 He hath thrust forth his hands, against them he was wont to salute, he hath violated his covenant;
55:21 Smoother than curds, were [the words of] his mouth, But, war, was [in] his heart,—Softer his words than oil, Yet, they, were drawn swords!
55:22 Cast upon Yahweh thy lot, and, he, will sustain thee: He will not suffer, to times age-abiding, the righteous one to be shaken.
55:23 But, thou, O God, wilt bring them down to the pit of destruction, Men of bloodshed and deceit, shall not live out half their days; But, I, will trust in thee.
text.NABRE:
55:1 For the leader. On stringed instruments. A maskil of David.
55:2 I Listen, God, to my prayer; do not hide from my pleading;
55:3 hear me and give answer. I rock with grief; I groan
55:4 at the uproar of the enemy, the clamor of the wicked. They heap trouble upon me, savagely accuse me.
55:5 My heart pounds within me; death’s terrors fall upon me.
55:6 Fear and trembling overwhelm me; shuddering sweeps over me.
55:7 I say, “If only I had wings like a dove that I might fly away and find rest.
55:8 Far away I would flee; I would stay in the desert. Selah
55:9 “I would soon find a shelter from the raging wind and storm.”
55:10 II Lord, check and confuse their tongues. For I see violence and strife in the city
55:11 making rounds on its walls day and night. Within are mischief and trouble;
55:12 treachery is in its midst; oppression and fraud never leave its streets.
55:13 For it is not an enemy that reviled me— that I could bear— Not a foe who viewed me with contempt, from that I could hide.
55:14 But it was you, my other self, my comrade and friend,
55:15 You, whose company I enjoyed, at whose side I walked in the house of God.
55:16 III Let death take them; let them go down alive to Sheol, for evil is in their homes and bellies.
55:17 But I will call upon God, and the Lord will save me.
55:18 At dusk, dawn, and noon I will grieve and complain, and my prayer will be heard.
55:19 He will redeem my soul in peace from those who war against me, though there are many who oppose me.
55:20 God, who sits enthroned forever, will hear me and afflict them. Selah For they will not mend their ways; they have no fear of God.
55:21 He stretched out his hand at his friends and broke his covenant.
55:22 Softer than butter is his speech, but war is in his heart. Smoother than oil are his words, but they are unsheathed swords.
55:23 Cast your care upon the Lord, who will give you support. He will never allow the righteous to stumble.
55:24 But you, God, will bring them down to the pit of destruction. These bloodthirsty liars will not live half their days, but I put my trust in you.
text.DRB:
55:1 Unto the end, for a people, that is made far from the Saints, David in the inscription of the title, when the foreigners held him in Geth.
55:2 Have mercy on me, o God, because man hath trodden upon me: all the day impugning he hath afflicted me.
55:3 Mine enemies have trodden upon me all the day: because they are many that war against me.
55:4 From the height of the day I shall fear: but I will trust in thee.
55:5 In God I will praise my words, in God have I hoped: I will not fear what flesh may do to me.
55:6 All the day did they detest my words; against me all their cogitations are unto evil.
55:7 They will inhabit and keep secret: they will observe my heel. As they have expected my soul,
55:8 for nothing shalt thou save them: in wrath thou wilt break peoples. O God,
55:9 I have shewed my life to thee: thou hast set my tears in thy sight. As also in thy promise:
55:10 then shall mine enemies be turned backward. In what day soever I shall invocate thee: lo I have known that thou art my God.
55:11 In God I will praise the word, in our Lord will I praise thee saying: I have hoped in God, I will not fear what man can do to me.
55:12 In me, o God are thy vows, which I will render, praises to thee.
55:13 Because thou hast delivered my soul from death, and my feet from falling: that I may please before God, in the light of the living.

text.NRSV:
56:1 Be gracious to me, O God, for people trample on me; all day long foes oppress me;
56:2 my enemies trample on me all day long, for many fight against me. O Most High,
56:3 when I am afraid, I put my trust in you.
56:4 In God, whose word I praise, in God I trust; I am not afraid; what can flesh do to me?
56:5 All day long they seek to injure my cause; all their thoughts are against me for evil.
56:6 They stir up strife, they lurk, they watch my steps. As they hoped to have my life,
56:7 so repay them for their crime; in wrath cast down the peoples, O God!
56:8 You have kept count of my tossings; put my tears in your bottle. Are they not in your record?
56:9 Then my enemies will retreat in the day when I call. This I know, that God is for me.
56:10 In God, whose word I praise, in the Lord, whose word I praise,
56:11 in God I trust; I am not afraid. What can a mere mortal do to me?
56:12 My vows to you I must perform, O God; I will render thank offerings to you.
56:13 For you have delivered my soul from death, and my feet from falling, so that I may walk before God in the light of life.
text.Coverdale:
56:1 Have mercy on me, O God, for my enemies are hounding me; * all day long they assault and oppress me.
56:2 They hound me all the day long; * truly there are many who fight against me, O Most High.
56:3 Whenever I am afraid, * I will put my trust in you.
56:4 In God, whose word I praise, in God I trust and will not be afraid, * for what can flesh do to me?
56:5 All day long they damage my cause; * their only thought is to do me evil.
56:6 They band together; they lie in wait; * they spy upon my footsteps; because they seek my life.
56:7 Shall they escape despite their wickedness? * O God, in your anger, cast down the peoples.
56:8 You have noted my lamentation; put my tears into your bottle; * are they not recorded in your book?
56:9 Whenever I call upon you, my enemies will be put to flight; * this I know, for God is on my side.
56:10 In God the LORD, whose word I praise, in God I trust and will not be afraid, * for what can mortals do to me?
56:11 I am bound by the vow I made to you, O God; * I will present to you thank-offerings;
56:12 For you have rescued my soul from death and my feet from stumbling, * that I may walk before God in the light of the living.
text.KJV:
56:1 To the chief Musician upon Jonath–elem–rechokim, Michtam of David, when the Philistines took him in Gath. Be merciful unto me, O God: for man would swallow me up; he fighting daily oppresseth me.
56:2 Mine enemies would daily swallow me up: for they be many that fight against me, O thou most High.
56:3 What time I am afraid, I will trust in thee.
56:4 In God I will praise his word, in God I have put my trust; I will not fear what flesh can do unto me.
56:5 Every day they wrest my words: all their thoughts are against me for evil.
56:6 They gather themselves together, they hide themselves, they mark my steps, when they wait for my soul.
56:7 Shall they escape by iniquity? in thine anger cast down the people, O God.
56:8 Thou tellest my wanderings: put thou my tears into thy bottle: are they not in thy book?
56:9 When I cry unto thee, then shall mine enemies turn back: this I know; for God is for me.
56:10 In God will I praise his word: in the Lord will I praise his word.
56:11 In God have I put my trust: I will not be afraid what man can do unto me.
56:12 Thy vows are upon me, O God: I will render praises unto thee.
56:13 For thou hast delivered my soul from death: wilt not thou deliver my feet from falling, that I may walk before God in the light of the living?
text.JPS1985:
56:1 For the leader; on yonath elem reḥokim. Of David. A michtam; when the Philistines seized him in Gath.
56:2 Have mercy on me, O God, for men persecute me; all day long my adversary oppresses me.
56:3 My watchful foes persecute me all day long; many are my adversaries, O Exalted One.
56:4 When I am afraid, I trust in You,
56:5 in God, whose word I praise, in God I trust; I am not afraid; what can mortals do to me?
56:6 All day long they cause me grief in my affairs, they plan only evil against me.
56:7 They plot, they lie in ambush; they watch my every move, hoping for my death.
56:8 Cast them out for their evil; subdue peoples in Your anger, O God.
56:9 You keep count of my wanderings; put my tears into Your flask, into Your record.
56:10 Then my enemies will retreat when I call on You; this I know, that God is for me.
56:11 In God, whose word I praise, in the LORD, whose word I praise,
56:12 in God I trust; I am not afraid; what can man do to me?
56:13 I must pay my vows to You, O God; I will render thank offerings to You.
56:14 For You have saved me from death, my foot from stumbling, that I may walk before God in the light of life.
text.Grail1963:
.2 Have mercy on me, God, men crush me;
they fight me all day long and oppress me.

for many fight proudly against me.
4 When I fear, I will trust in you,
5 in God, who word I praise.

text.Rotherham:
56:1 Show me favour, O God, For weak man hath panted for me, All the day, a fighter oppresseth me:
56:2 Mine adversaries have panted all the day, For, many, are fighting with me, loftily.
56:3 What day I am afraid, I, unto thee will direct my confidence.
56:4 In God, I will praise his cause,—In God, have I trusted, I will not fear, What can flesh do unto me?
56:5 All the day, they wrest, my words, Against me, all their devices are for mischief;
56:6 They stir up strife—they lie hid, They, mark my steps,—Seeing they have waited for my life.
56:7 Because of iniquity, recompense thou them,—In anger, bring thou down, the peoples, O God.
56:8 My wandering, hast, thou, recorded,—Put thou my tears in thy bottle, Are they not in thy record?
56:9 Then, shall my foes turn back, in the day I cry, This, I know, for God is mine!
56:10 In God, will I praise with good cause: In Yahweh, will I praise with good cause;
56:11 In God, have I trusted, I will not fear, What can a son of earth do unto me!
56:12 Upon me, O God, are thy vows, I will pay back praises unto thee.
56:13 For thou hast rescued my soul from death, Wilt thou not [rescue] my feet from stumbling? That I may walk to and fro, before God, In the light of life.
text.NABRE:
56:1 According to Yonath elem rehoqim. A miktam of David, when the Philistines seized him at Gath.
56:2 I Have mercy on me, God, for I am treated harshly; attackers press me all the day.
56:3 My foes treat me harshly all the day; yes, many are my attackers. O Most High,
56:4 when I am afraid, in you I place my trust.
56:5 I praise the word of God; I trust in God, I do not fear. What can mere flesh do to me?
56:6 II All the day they foil my plans; their every thought is of evil against me.
56:7 They hide together in ambush; they watch my every step; they lie in wait for my life.
56:8 They are evil; watch them, God! Cast the nations down in your anger!
56:9 My wanderings you have noted; are my tears not stored in your flask, recorded in your book?
56:10 My foes turn back when I call on you. This I know: God is on my side.
56:11 I praise the word of God, I praise the word of the Lord.
56:12 In God I trust, I do not fear. What can man do to me?
56:13 III I have made vows to you, God; with offerings I will fulfill them,
56:14 For you have snatched me from death, kept my feet from stumbling, That I may walk before God in the light of the living.
text.DRB:
56:1 Unto the end, destroy not, to David in the inscription of the title, when he fled from the face of Saul into the cave.
56:2 Have mercy on me, o God, have mercy on me: because my soul hath trusted in thee. And I will hope in the shadow of thy wings, until iniquity pass.
56:3 I will cry to God the highest: God that hath done me good.
56:4 He sent from heaven, and delivered me: he hath given into reproach them that trod upon me. God hath sent his mercy and his truth,
56:5 and hath delivered my soul out of the midst of lions' whelps: I slept troubled. The sons of men, their teeth are weapons and arrows: and their tongue a sharp sword.
56:6 Be exalted above the heavens, o God: and thy glory upon all the earth.
56:7 They prepared a snare for my feet: and bowed down my soul. They digged a pit before my face: and they are fallen into it.
56:8 My heart is ready, o God, my heart is ready: I will sing, and say a Psalm.
56:9 Arise my glory, arise psalter and harp: I will arise early.
56:10 I will confess to thee among peoples, o Lord: and I will say a Psalm to thee among the Gentiles.
56:11 Because thy mercy is magnified even to the heavens, and thy truth even to the clouds.
56:12 Be exalted above the heavens o God: and thy glory upon all the earth.

text.NRSV:
57:1 Be merciful to me, O God, be merciful to me, for in you my soul takes refuge; in the shadow of your wings I will take refuge, until the destroying storms pass by.
57:2 I cry to God Most High, to God who fulfills his purpose for me.
57:3 He will send from heaven and save me, he will put to shame those who trample on me. Selah God will send forth his steadfast love and his faithfulness.
57:4 I lie down among lions that greedily devour human prey; their teeth are spears and arrows, their tongues sharp swords.
57:5 Be exalted, O God, above the heavens. Let your glory be over all the earth.
57:6 They set a net for my steps; my soul was bowed down. They dug a pit in my path, but they have fallen into it themselves. Selah
57:7 My heart is steadfast, O God, my heart is steadfast. I will sing and make melody.
57:8 Awake, my soul! Awake, O harp and lyre! I will awake the dawn.
57:9 I will give thanks to you, O Lord, among the peoples; I will sing praises to you among the nations.
57:10 For your steadfast love is as high as the heavens; your faithfulness extends to the clouds.
57:11 Be exalted, O God, above the heavens. Let your glory be over all the earth.
text.Coverdale:
57:1 Be merciful to me, O God, be merciful, for I have taken refuge in you; * in the shadow of your wings will I take refuge until this time of trouble has gone by.
57:2 I will call upon the Most High God, * the God who maintains my cause.
57:3 He will send from heaven and save me; he will confound those who trample upon me; * God will send forth his love and his faithfulness.
57:4 I lie in the midst of lions that devour the people; * their teeth are spears and arrows, their tongue a sharp sword.
57:5 They have laid a net for my feet, and I am bowed low; * they have dug a pit before me, but have fallen into it themselves.
57:6 Exalt yourself above the heavens, O God, * and your glory over all the earth.
57:7 My heart is firmly fixed, O God, my heart is fixed; * I will sing and make melody.
57:8 Wake up, my spirit; awake, lute and harp; * I myself will waken the dawn.
57:9 I will confess you among the peoples, O LORD; * I will sing praise to you among the nations.
57:10 For your loving-kindness is greater than the heavens, * and your faithfulness reaches to the clouds.
57:11 Exalt yourself above the heavens, O God, * and your glory over all the earth.
text.KJV:
57:1 To the chief Musician, Al–taschith, Michtam of David, when he fled from Saul in the cave. Be merciful unto me, O God, be merciful unto me: for my soul trusteth in thee: yea, in the shadow of thy wings will I make my refuge, until these calamities be overpast.
57:2 I will cry unto God most high; unto God that performeth all things for me.
57:3 He shall send from heaven, and save me from the reproach of him that would swallow me up. Selah. God shall send forth his mercy and his truth.
57:4 My soul is among lions: and I lie even among them that are set on fire, even the sons of men, whose teeth are spears and arrows, and their tongue a sharp sword.
57:5 Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens; let thy glory be above all the earth.
57:6 They have prepared a net for my steps; my soul is bowed down: they have digged a pit before me, into the midst whereof they are fallen themselves. Selah.
57:7 My heart is fixed, O God, my heart is fixed: I will sing and give praise.
57:8 Awake up, my glory; awake, psaltery and harp: I myself will awake early.
57:9 I will praise thee, O Lord, among the people: I will sing unto thee among the nations.
57:10 For thy mercy is great unto the heavens, and thy truth unto the clouds.
57:11 Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens: let thy glory be above all the earth.
text.JPS1985:
57:1 For the leader; al tashḥeth. Of David. A michtam; when he fled from Saul into a cave.
57:2 Have mercy on me, O God, have mercy on me, for I seek refuge in You, I seek refuge in the shadow of Your wings, until danger passes.
57:3 I call to God Most High, to God who is good to me.
57:4 He will reach down from heaven and deliver me: God will send down His steadfast love; my persecutor reviles. Selah.
57:5 As for me, I lie down among man-eating lions whose teeth are spears and arrows, whose tongue is a sharp sword.
57:6 Exalt Yourself over the heavens, O God, let Your glory be over all the earth!
57:7 They prepared a net for my feet to ensnare me; they dug a pit for me, but they fell into it. Selah.
57:8 My heart is firm, O God; my heart is firm; I will sing, I will chant a hymn.
57:9 Awake, O my soul! Awake, O harp and lyre! I will wake the dawn.
57:10 I will praise You among the peoples, O Lord; I will sing a hymn to You among the nations;
57:11 for Your faithfulness is as high as heaven; Your steadfastness reaches to the sky.
57:12 Exalt Yourself over the heavens, O God, let Your glory be over all the earth!
text.Grail1963:

text.Rotherham:
57:1 Show me favour, O God, Show me favour, For, in thee, hath my soul sought refuge,—And, in the shadow of thy wings, will I seek refuge, Until the storm of ruin pass by.
57:2 I will cry unto God Most High, Unto GOD, who accomplisheth on my behalf:
57:3 He will send out of heaven, that he may save me, He who is panting for me, hath reproached. [Selah.] God will send out his lovingkindness and his faithfulness.
57:4 My soul, is in the midst of lions, I lie down amidst flames, As for the sons of men, Their teeth, are spear and arrows, And, their tongue, is a sharp sword.
57:5 Be exalted Above the Heavens, O God, Above all the earth, thy glory!
57:6 A net, did they fix for my steps, My soul was bowed down,—They digged before me a pit, They fell into the midst thereof! [Selah.]
57:7 Fixed is my heart, O God, Fixed is my heart, I will sing, and touch the strings.
57:8 Awake, mine honour, Awake, harp and lyre, I will awaken the dawn;
57:9 I will thank thee, among the peoples, O My Lord, I will praise thee in song, among the races of men.
57:10 For, great, unto the heavens, is thy lovingkindness, And, unto the skies, thy faithfulness.
57:11 Be exalted Above the heavens, O God, Above all the earth, thy glory!
text.NABRE:
57:1 A miktam of David, when he fled from Saul into a cave.
57:2 I Have mercy on me, God, have mercy on me. In you I seek refuge. In the shadow of your wings I seek refuge till harm pass by.
57:3 I call to God Most High, to God who provides for me.
57:4 May God send help from heaven to save me, shame those who trample upon me. May God send fidelity and mercy. Selah
57:5 I must lie down in the midst of lions hungry for human prey. Their teeth are spears and arrows; their tongue, a sharpened sword.
57:6 Be exalted over the heavens, God; may your glory appear above all the earth.
57:7 II They have set a trap for my feet; my soul is bowed down; They have dug a pit before me. May they fall into it themselves! Selah
57:8 My heart is steadfast, God, my heart is steadfast. I will sing and chant praise.
57:9 Awake, my soul; awake, lyre and harp! I will wake the dawn.
57:10 I will praise you among the peoples, Lord; I will chant your praise among the nations.
57:11 For your mercy towers to the heavens; your faithfulness reaches to the skies.
57:12 Exalt yourself over the heavens, God; may your glory appear above all the earth.
text.DRB:
57:1 Unto the end, destroy not, to David, in the inscription of the title.
57:2 If in very deed you speak justice: judge right things ye sons of men.
57:3 For in the heart you work iniquities: in the earth your hands forge injustice.
57:4 Sinners are alienated from the matrice, they have erred from the womb: they have spoken false things.
57:5 They have fury according to the similitude of a serpent: as of the asp that is deaf, and stoppeth his ears.
57:6 Which will not hear the voice of the enchanters, and of the sorcerer enchanting wisely.
57:7 God shall break their teeth in their mouth, the cheek-tooth of the lions, our Lord will break in pieces.
57:8 They shall come to nothing as water running down: he hath bent his bow till they be weakened.
57:9 As wax that melteth, shall they be taken away: fire hath fallen on them, and they have not seen the sun.
57:10 Before your thorns did understand the old briar: as living so in wrath he swalloweth them.
57:11 The just shall rejoice when he shall see revenge: he shall wash his hands in the blood of a sinner.
57:12 And man shall say: If certes there be fruit to the just: there is a God certes judging them on the earth.

text.NRSV:
58:1 Do you indeed decree what is right, you gods? Do you judge people fairly?
58:2 No, in your hearts you devise wrongs; your hands deal out violence on earth.
58:3 The wicked go astray from the womb; they err from their birth, speaking lies.
58:4 They have venom like the venom of a serpent, like the deaf adder that stops its ear,
58:5 so that it does not hear the voice of charmers or of the cunning enchanter.
58:6 O God, break the teeth in their mouths; tear out the fangs of the young lions, O Lord!
58:7 Let them vanish like water that runs away; like grass let them be trodden down and wither.
58:8 Let them be like the snail that dissolves into slime; like the untimely birth that never sees the sun.
58:9 Sooner than your pots can feel the heat of thorns, whether green or ablaze, may he sweep them away!
58:10 The righteous will rejoice when they see vengeance done; they will bathe their feet in the blood of the wicked.
58:11 People will say,"Surely there is a reward for the righteous; surely there is a God who judges on earth."
text.Coverdale:
58:1 Do you indeed decree righteousness, you rulers? * do you judge the peoples with equity?
58:2 No; you devise evil in your hearts, * and your hands deal out violence in the land.
58:3 The wicked are perverse from the womb; * liars go astray from their birth.
58:4 They are as venomous as a serpent, * they are like the deaf adder which stops its ears,
58:5 Which does not heed the voice of the charmer, * no matter how skillful his charming.
58:6 O God, break their teeth in their mouths; * pull the fangs of the young lions, O LORD.
58:7 Let them vanish like water that runs off; * let them wither like trodden grass.
58:8 Let them be like the snail that melts away, * like a stillborn child that never sees the sun.
58:9 Before they bear fruit, let them be cut down like a brier; * like thorns and thistles let them be swept away.
58:10 The righteous will be glad when they see the vengeance; * they will bathe their feet in the blood of the wicked.
58:11 And they will say,"Surely, there is a reward for the righteous; * surely, there is a God who rules in the earth."
text.KJV:
58:1 To the chief Musician, Al–taschith, Michtam of David. Do ye indeed speak righteousness, O congregation? do ye judge uprightly, O ye sons of men?
58:2 Yea, in heart ye work wickedness; ye weigh the violence of your hands in the earth.
58:3 The wicked are estranged from the womb: they go astray as soon as they be born, speaking lies.
58:4 Their poison is like the poison of a serpent: they are like the deaf adder that stoppeth her ear;
58:5 Which will not hearken to the voice of charmers, charming never so wisely.
58:6 Break their teeth, O God, in their mouth: break out the great teeth of the young lions, O Lord.
58:7 Let them melt away as waters which run continually: when he bendeth his bow to shoot his arrows, let them be as cut in pieces.
58:8 As a snail which melteth, let every one of them pass away: like the untimely birth of a woman, that they may not see the sun.
58:9 Before your pots can feel the thorns, he shall take them away as with a whirlwind, both living, and in his wrath.
58:10 The righteous shall rejoice when he seeth the vengeance: he shall wash his feet in the blood of the wicked.
58:11 So that a man shall say, Verily there is a reward for the righteous: verily he is a God that judgeth in the earth.
text.JPS1985:
58:1 For the leader; al tashḥeth. Of David. A michtam.
58:2 O mighty ones, do you really decree what is just? Do you judge mankind with equity?
58:3 In your minds you devise wrongdoing in the land; with your hands you deal out lawlessness.
58:4 The wicked are defiant from birth; the liars go astray from the womb.
58:5 Their venom is like that of a snake, a deaf viper that stops its ears
58:6 so as not to hear the voice of charmers or the expert mutterer of spells.
58:7 O God, smash their teeth in their mouth; shatter the fangs of lions, O LORD;
58:8 let them melt, let them vanish like water; let Him aim His arrows that they be cut down;
58:9 like a snail that melts away as it moves; like a woman’s stillbirth, may they never see the sun!
58:10 Before the thorns grow into a bramble, may He whirl them away alive in fury.
58:11 The righteous man will rejoice when he sees revenge; he will bathe his feet in the blood of the wicked.
58:12 Men will say, “There is, then, a reward for the righteous; there is, indeed, divine justice on earth.”
text.Rotherham:
58:1 Are ye, indeed, silent [when] righteousness, ye should speak? When, with equity, ye should judge, O ye sons of men?
58:2 Aye! ye all do work, perversity,—Throughout the land, your hands, weigh out, violence!
58:3 Lawless men have been estranged from birth, They have gone astray from their nativity, speaking falsehood;
58:4 Their poison, is like unto the poison of a serpent, Like the deaf adder, that stoppeth his ear;
58:5 That will not hearken to the voice of whisperers, Though the wise one try to bind him with spells.
58:6 O God! break away their teeth in their mouth, The biters of the young lions, knock thou out, O Yahweh!
58:7 Let such men flow away like waters that disperse themselves: He prepareth his arrow, Like [grass] let them be cut down:
58:8 Like a snail, which melteth away as it goeth, An untimely birth of a woman, which hath not seen the sun:
58:9 Before your kettles can perceive the [kindled] bramble, Be he green or be he withered, he shall be swept away.
58:10 The righteous man will rejoice when he hath seen an avenging, His feet, will he bathe in the blood of the lawless one:—
58:11 So that a son of earth may say—Surely there is fruit for the righteous man! Surely there are gods who judge in the earth!
text.NABRE:
58:1 For the leader. Do not destroy. A miktam of David.
58:2 I Do you indeed pronounce justice, O gods; do you judge fairly you children of Adam?
58:3 No, you freely engage in crime; your hands dispense violence to the earth.
58:4 II The wicked have been corrupt since birth; liars from the womb, they have gone astray.
58:5 Their venom is like the venom of a snake, like that of a serpent stopping its ears,
58:6 So as not to hear the voice of the charmer or the enchanter with cunning spells.
58:7 III O God, smash the teeth in their mouths; break the fangs of these lions, Lord!
58:8 Make them vanish like water flowing away; trodden down, let them wither like grass.
58:9 Let them dissolve like a snail that oozes away, like an untimely birth that never sees the sun.
58:10 Suddenly, like brambles or thistles, have the whirlwind snatch them away.
58:11 Then the just shall rejoice to see the vengeance and bathe their feet in the blood of the wicked.
58:12 Then people will say: “Truly there is a reward for the just; there is a God who is judge on earth!”
text.DRB:
58:1 Unto the end, destroy not, to David in the inscription of the title, when Saul sent, and watched his house to kill him.
58:2 Deliver me from mine enemies, o my God: and from them that rise up against me defend me.
58:3 Deliver me from them that work iniquity: and from bloody men save me.
58:4 Because lo they have taken my soul: the strong have fallen violently upon me.
58:5 Neither is it mine iniquity, nor my sin, o Lord: without iniquity have I run, and gone directly.
58:6 Rise up to meet me, and see: and thou, o Lord, the God of powers, God of Israel, attend to visit all nations: have no mercy on all that work iniquity.
58:7 They will return at evening: and they shall suffer famine as dogs, and shall compass the city.
58:8 Behold they will speak in their mouth, and a sword in their lips: because who hath heard?
58:9 And thou, o Lord wilt scorn them: thou wilt bring to nought all the nations.
58:10 I will keep my strength to thee: because thou art my receiver:
58:11 my God, thy mercy shall prevent me.
58:12 God will shew unto me concerning mine enemies, kill them not lest sometime my peoples forget. Disperse them in thy strength: and depose them my protector, o Lord.
58:13 The sin of their mouth, the word of their lips: and let them be taken in their pride. And for cursing and lying they shall be talked of
58:14 in consummation: in wrath of consummation and they shall not be. And they shall know that God will rule over Jacob: and over the ends of the earth.
58:15 They shall be turned at evening, and shall suffer famine as dogs: & shall compass the city.
58:16 They shall be dispersed to eat: and if they be not filled, they will murmur also.
58:17 But I will sing thy strength: and will exalt thy mercy in the morning. Because thou art become my receiver, and my refuge in the day of my tribulation.
58:18 My helper, I will sing to thee, because thou art God my receiver: my God, my mercy.

text.NRSV:
59:1 Deliver me from my enemies, O my God; protect me from those who rise up against me.
59:2 Deliver me from those who work evil; from the bloodthirsty save me.
59:3 Even now they lie in wait for my life; the mighty stir up strife against me. For no transgression or sin of mine, O Lord,
59:4 for no fault of mine, they run and make ready. Rouse yourself, come to my help and see!
59:5 You, Lord God of hosts, are God of Israel. Awake to punish all the nations; spare none of those who treacherously plot evil. Selah
59:6 Each evening they come back, howling like dogs and prowling about the city.
59:7 There they are, bellowing with their mouths, with sharp words on their lips— for"Who,"they think,"will hear us?"
59:8 But you laugh at them, O Lord; you hold all the nations in derision.
59:9 O my strength, I will watch for you; for you, O God, are my fortress.
59:10 My God in his steadfast love will meet me; my God will let me look in triumph on my enemies.
59:11 Do not kill them, or my people may forget; make them totter by your power, and bring them down, O Lord, our shield.
59:12 For the sin of their mouths, the words of their lips, let them be trapped in their pride. For the cursing and lies that they utter,
59:13 consume them in wrath; consume them until they are no more. Then it will be known to the ends of the earth that God rules over Jacob. Selah
59:14 Each evening they come back, howling like dogs and prowling about the city.
59:15 They roam about for food, and growl if they do not get their fill.
59:16 But I will sing of your might; I will sing aloud of your steadfast love in the morning. For you have been a fortress for me and a refuge in the day of my distress.
59:17 O my strength, I will sing praises to you, for you, O God, are my fortress, the God who shows me steadfast love.
text.Coverdale:
59:1 Rescue me from my enemies, O God; * protect me from those who rise up against me.
59:2 Rescue me from evildoers * and save me from those who thirst for my blood.
59:3 See how they lie in wait for my life, how the mighty gather together against me; * not for any offense or fault of mine, O LORD.
59:4 Not because of any guilt of mine * they run and prepare themselves for battle.
59:5 Rouse yourself, come to my side, and see; * for you, LORD God of hosts, are Israel's God.
59:6 Awake, and punish all the ungodly; * show no mercy to those who are faithless and evil.
59:7 They go to and fro in the evening; * they snarl like dogs and run about the city.
59:8 Behold, they boast with their mouths, and taunts are on their lips; *"For who,"they say,"will hear us?"
59:9 But you, O LORD, you laugh at them; * you laugh all the ungodly to scorn.
59:10 My eyes are fixed on you, O my Strength; * for you, O God, are my stronghold.
59:11 My merciful God comes to meet me; * God will let me look in triumph on my enemies.
59:12 Slay them, O God, lest my people forget; * send them reeling by your might and put them down, O Lord our shield.
59:13 For the sins of their mouths, for the words of their lips, for the cursing and lies that they utter, * let them be caught in their pride.
59:14 Make an end of them in your wrath; * make an end of them, and they shall be no more.
59:15 Let everyone know that God rules in Jacob, * and to the ends of the earth.
59:16 They go to and fro in the evening; * they snarl like dogs and run about the city.
59:17 They forage for food, * and if they are not filled, they howl.
59:18 For my part, I will sing of your strength; * I will celebrate your love in the morning;
59:19 For you have become my stronghold, * a refuge in the day of my trouble.
59:20 To you, O my Strength, will I sing; * for you, O God, are my stronghold and my merciful God.
text.KJV:
59:1 To the chief Musician, Al–taschith, Michtam of David; when Saul sent, and they watched the house to kill him. Deliver me from mine enemies, O my God: defend me from them that rise up against me.
59:2 Deliver me from the workers of iniquity, and save me from bloody men.
59:3 For, lo, they lie in wait for my soul: the mighty are gathered against me; not for my transgression, nor for my sin, O Lord.
59:4 They run and prepare themselves without my fault: awake to help me, and behold.
59:5 Thou therefore, O Lord God of hosts, the God of Israel, awake to visit all the heathen: be not merciful to any wicked transgressors. Selah.
59:6 They return at evening: they make a noise like a dog, and go round about the city.
59:7 Behold, they belch out with their mouth: swords are in their lips: for who, say they, doth hear?
59:8 But thou, O Lord, shalt laugh at them; thou shalt have all the heathen in derision.
59:9 Because of his strength will I wait upon thee: for God is my defence.
59:10 The God of my mercy shall prevent me: God shall let me see my desire upon mine enemies.
59:11 Slay them not, lest my people forget: scatter them by thy power; and bring them down, O Lord our shield.
59:12 For the sin of their mouth and the words of their lips let them even be taken in their pride: and for cursing and lying which they speak.
59:13 Consume them in wrath, consume them, that they may not be: and let them know that God ruleth in Jacob unto the ends of the earth. Selah.
59:14 And at evening let them return; and let them make a noise like a dog, and go round about the city.
59:15 Let them wander up and down for meat, and grudge if they be not satisfied.
59:16 But I will sing of thy power; yea, I will sing aloud of thy mercy in the morning: for thou hast been my defence and refuge in the day of my trouble.
59:17 Unto thee, O my strength, will I sing: for God is my defence, and the God of my mercy.
text.JPS1985:
59:1 For the leader; al tashḥeth. Of David. A michtam; when Saul sent men to watch his house in order to put him to death.
59:2 Save me from my enemies, O my God; secure me against my assailants.
59:3 Save me from evildoers; deliver me from murderers.
59:4 For see, they lie in wait for me; fierce men plot against me for no offense of mine, for no transgression, O LORD;
59:5 for no guilt of mine do they rush to array themselves against me. Look, rouse Yourself on my behalf!
59:6 You, O LORD God of hosts, God of Israel, bestir Yourself to bring all nations to account; have no mercy on any treacherous villain. Selah.
59:7 They come each evening growling like dogs, roaming the city.
59:8 They rave with their mouths, sharp words are on their lips; [they think, ] “Who hears? ”
59:9 But You, O LORD, laugh at them; You mock all the nations.
59:10 O my strength, I wait for You; for God is my haven.
59:11 My faithful God will come to aid me; God will let me gloat over my watchful foes.
59:12 Do not kill them lest my people be unmindful; with Your power make wanderers of them; bring them low, O our shield, the Lord,
59:13 because of their sinful mouths, the words on their lips. Let them be trapped by their pride, and by the imprecations and lies they utter.
59:14 In Your fury put an end to them; put an end to them that they be no more; that it may be known to the ends of the earth that God does rule over Jacob. Selah.
59:15 They come each evening growling like dogs, roaming the city.
59:16 They wander in search of food; and whine if they are not satisfied.
59:17 But I will sing of Your strength, extol each morning Your faithfulness; for You have been my haven, a refuge in time of trouble.
59:18 O my strength, to You I sing hymns; for God is my haven, my faithful God.

text.Rotherham:
59:1 Rescue me from my foes, O my God, From them who lift themselves up against me, wilt thou set me on high:
59:2 Rescue me, from the workers of iniquity, And, from the men of bloodshed, save me.
59:3 For lo! they have lain in wait for my life, Mighty ones stir up strife against me, Without transgression of mine, and without sin of mine, O Yahweh;
59:4 For no iniquity, do they run that they may take their stand, Rouse thyself to meet me, and see.
59:5 Thou, therefore, O Yahweh, God of hosts, God of Israel, Awake, to visit all the nations, Do not show favour to any iniquitous traitors. [Selah.]
59:6 They return at evening, They grow like a dog, And go round the city.
59:7 Lo! they belch forth with their mouth, Swords, are in their lips, For [say they], Who doth hear?
59:8 Thou, therefore, O Yahweh, wilt laugh at them, Thou wilt mock at all nations.
59:9 O my Strength, unto thee, will I make melody, For, God, is my high tower.
59:10 My God of lovingkindness, will come to meet me, God, will let me look on mine adversaries.
59:11 Do not slay them, lest my people forget, Cause them to wander by thy strength, and prostrate them, [Thou] our shield, O Lord!
59:12 The sin of their mouth, The word of their lips, Let them then be captured in their pride, Both for the oath and for the deception they record.
59:13 Bring to a full end in wrath, Bring to a full end, that they be no more,—That men may know that, God, is ruling in Jacob, Unto the ends of the earth. [Selah.]
59:14 Let them return, then, at evening, Let them growl like a dog, And go round the city.
59:15 They, may prowl about for food,—And, if they are not satisfied, then let them whine!
59:16 But, I, will sing thy power, And will shout aloud, in the morning, thy lovingkindness,—For thou has become a refuge for me, And a place to flee to in the day of my distress.
59:17 O my Strength, unto thee, will I make melody, For, God, is my high tower, my God of lovingkindness.
text.NABRE:
59:1 A miktam of David, when Saul sent people to watch his house and kill him.
59:2 I Rescue me from my enemies, my God; lift me out of reach of my foes.
59:3 Deliver me from evildoers; from the bloodthirsty save me.
59:4 They have set an ambush for my life; the powerful conspire against me. For no offense or misdeed of mine, Lord,
59:5 for no fault they hurry to take up arms. Come near and see my plight!
59:6 You, Lord God of hosts, are the God of Israel! Awake! Punish all the nations. Have no mercy on these worthless traitors. Selah
59:7 Each evening they return, growling like dogs, prowling the city.
59:8 Their mouths pour out insult; sharp words are on their lips. They say: “Who is there to hear?”
59:9 But you, Lord, laugh at them; you deride all the nations.
59:10 My strength, for you I watch; you, God, are my fortress,
59:11 my loving God. II May God go before me, and show me my fallen foes.
59:12 Slay them, God, lest they deceive my people. Shake them by your power; Lord, our shield, bring them down.
59:13 For the sinful words of their mouths and lips let them be caught in their pride. For the lies they have told under oath
59:14 destroy them in anger, destroy till they are no more. Then people will know God rules over Jacob, yes, even to the ends of the earth. Selah
59:15 Each evening they return, growling like dogs, prowling the city.
59:16 They roam about as scavengers; if they are not filled, they howl.
59:17 III But I shall sing of your strength, extol your mercy at dawn, For you are my fortress, my refuge in time of trouble.
59:18 My strength, your praise I will sing; you, God, are my fortress, my loving God.
text.DRB:
59:1 Unto the end, for them that shall be changed, in the inscription of the title to David himself, for doctrine,
59:2 when he set fire on Mesopotamia of Syria, and in Sobal, and Joab returned, and struck Idumæa in the valley of salt-pits twelve thousand.
59:3 O God thou hast repelled us, & hast destroyed us: thou wast angry and hast had mercy on us.
59:4 Thou hast moved the earth, and hast troubled it: heal the breaches thereof, because it is moved.
59:5 Thou hast shewed unto thy people hard things: thou hast made us drink the wine of compunction.
59:6 Thou hast given a signification to them that fear thee: that they fly from the face of the bow.
59:7 That thy beloved may be delivered, save me with thy right hand, and hear me.
59:8 God hath spoken in his holy: I shall rejoice, and shall divide Sichem: and shall measure the valley of tabernacles.
59:9 Galaad is mine, and Manasses is mine: and Ephraim the strength of my head.
59:10 Juda my king: Moab the pot of my hope. Into Edom I will stretch out my shoe: the foreigners are subject to me.
59:11 Who shall conduct me into a fenced city? Who shall conduct me even into Idumea?
59:12 Shall not thou, o God, that hast expelled us: and wilt not thou o God, go forth in our hosts?
59:13 Give us aid from tribulation: because man's salvation is vain.
59:14 In God we shall do strength: and he shall bring to nothing them that afflict us.

text.NRSV:
60:1 O God, you have rejected us, broken our defenses; you have been angry; now restore us!
60:2 You have caused the land to quake; you have torn it open; repair the cracks in it, for it is tottering.
60:3 You have made your people suffer hard things; you have given us wine to drink that made us reel.
60:4 You have set up a banner for those who fear you, to rally to it out of bowshot. Selah
60:5 Give victory with your right hand, and answer us, so that those whom you love may be rescued.
60:6 God has promised in his sanctuary:"With exultation I will divide up Shechem, and portion out the Vale of Succoth.
60:7 Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine; Ephraim is my helmet; Judah is my scepter.
60:8 Moab is my washbasin; on Edom I hurl my shoe; over Philistia I shout in triumph."
60:9 Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?
60:10 Have you not rejected us, O God? You do not go out, O God, with our armies.
60:11 O grant us help against the foe, for human help is worthless.
60:12 With God we shall do valiantly; it is he who will tread down our foes.
text.Coverdale:
60:1 O God, you have cast us off and broken us; * you have been angry; oh, take us back to you again.
60:2 You have shaken the earth and split it open; * repair the cracks in it, for it totters.
60:3 You have made your people know hardship; * you have given us wine that makes us stagger.
60:4 You have set up a banner for those who fear you, * to be a refuge from the power of the bow.
60:5 Save us by your right hand and answer us, * that those who are dear to you may be delivered.
60:6 God spoke from his holy place and said: *"I will exult and parcel out Shechem; I will divide the valley of Succoth.
60:7 Gilead is mine and Manasseh is mine; * Ephraim is my helmet and Judah my scepter.
60:8 Moab is my wash-basin, on Edom I throw down my sandal to claim it, * and over Philistia will I shout in triumph."
60:9 Who will lead me into the strong city? * who will bring me to Edom?
60:10 Have you not cast us off, O God? * you no longer go out, O God, with our armies.
60:11 Grant us your help against the enemy, * for vain is the help of man.
60:12 With God we will do valiant deeds, * and he shall tread our enemies under foot.
text.KJV:
60:1 To the chief Musician upon Shushan–eduth, Michtam of David, to teach; when he strove with Aram–naharaim and with Aram–zobah, when Joab returned, and smote of Edom in the valley of salt twelve thousand. O God, thou hast cast us off, thou hast scattered us, thou hast been displeased; O turn thyself to us again.
60:2 Thou hast made the earth to tremble; thou hast broken it: heal the breaches thereof; for it shaketh.
60:3 Thou hast shewed thy people hard things: thou hast made us to drink the wine of astonishment.
60:4 Thou hast given a banner to them that fear thee, that it may be displayed because of the truth. Selah.
60:5 That thy beloved may be delivered; save with thy right hand, and hear me.
60:6 God hath spoken in his holiness; I will rejoice, I will divide Shechem, and mete out the valley of Succoth.
60:7 Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine; Ephraim also is the strength of mine head; Judah is my lawgiver;
60:8 Moab is my washpot; over Edom will I cast out my shoe: Philistia, triumph thou because of me.
60:9 Who will bring me into the strong city? who will lead me into Edom?
60:10 Wilt not thou, O God, which hadst cast us off? and thou, O God, which didst not go out with our armies?
60:11 Give us help from trouble: for vain is the help of man.
60:12 Through God we shall do valiantly: for he it is that shall tread down our enemies.
text.JPS1985:
60:1 For the leader; on shushan eduth. A michtam of David (to be taught),
60:2 when he fought with Aram-Naharaim and Aram-Zobah, and Joab returned and defeated Edom—[an army] of twelve thousand men—in the Valley of Salt.
60:3 O God, You have rejected us, You have made a breach in us; You have been angry; restore us!
60:4 You have made the land quake; You have torn it open. Mend its fissures, for it is collapsing.
60:5 You have made Your people suffer hardship; You have given us wine that makes us reel.
60:6 Give those who fear You because of Your truth a banner for rallying. Selah.
60:7 That those whom You love might be rescued, deliver with Your right hand and answer me.
60:8 God promised in His sanctuary that I would exultingly divide up Shechem, and measure the Valley of Sukkoth;
60:9 Gilead and Manasseh would be mine, Ephraim my chief stronghold, Judah my scepter;
60:10 Moab would be my washbasin; on Edom I would cast my shoe; acclaim me, O Philistia!
60:11 Would that I were brought to the bastion! Would that I were led to Edom!
60:12 But You have rejected us, O God; God, You do not march with our armies.
60:13 Grant us Your aid against the foe, for the help of man is worthless.
60:14 With God we shall triumph; He will trample our foes.
text.Grail1963:
.3 O God, you have rejected us and broken us.
You have been angry; come back to us.
4 You have made the earth quake, torn it open.
Repair what is shattered for it sways.

and made us drink a wine that dazed us.

help with your right hand and reply.
* * *
8 From his holy place God has made this promise;

9 Gilead is mine and Manasseh,
Ephraim I take for my helmet,
Judah for my commander's staff.

Over the Philistines I will shout in triumph."

for the help of man is vain.

text.Rotherham:
60:1 O God, thou hast rejected us—hast scattered us, Thou hast been angry, Wilt thou restore us?
60:2 Thou hast shaken the land—hast rent it, Heal thou the fractures thereof—for it hath tottered:
60:3 Thou hast suffered thy people to see hardship, Thou hast let them drink the wine of confusion.
60:4 Thou hadst given—to them that revere thee—a banner, to float aloft, Because of [thy] faithfulness. [Selah.]
60:5 That thy beloved ones may be delivered, Save thou with thine own right hand—and answer us.
60:6 God, hath spoken in his holiness—I will exult! I will apportion Shechem, And, the Vale of Succoth, will I measure out;
60:7 Mine, is Gilead—and mine, Manasseh, But, Ephraim, is the defence, of my head, Judah, is my commander’s staff;
60:8 Moab, is my wash-bowl, Upon Edom, will I throw my shoe, Over Philistia! raise shout of triumph.
60:9 Who will conduct me to a fortified city? Who will lead me as far as Edom!
60:10 Is it not, thou, O God?—thou hast rejected us! And wilt thou not go forth, O God, with our hosts?
60:11 Grant us help out of distress, For, vain, is the deliverance of man:
60:12 In God, we shall do valiantly, He himself, therefore, will tread down our adversaries.
text.NABRE:
60:1 For the leader; according to “The Lily of.…” A miktam of David (for teaching),
60:2 when he fought against Aram-Naharaim and Aram-Zobah; and Joab, coming back, killed twelve thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt.
60:3 I O God, you rejected us, broke our defenses; you were angry but now revive us.
60:4 You rocked the earth, split it open; repair the cracks for it totters.
60:5 You made your people go through hardship, made us stagger from the wine you gave us.
60:6 Raise up a banner for those who revere you, a refuge for them out of bow shot. Selah
60:7 Help with your right hand and answer us that your loved ones may escape.
60:8 II In the sanctuary God promised: “I will exult, will apportion Shechem; the valley of Succoth I will measure out.
60:9 Gilead is mine, mine is Manasseh; Ephraim is the helmet for my head, Judah, my own scepter.
60:10 Moab is my washbowl; upon Edom I cast my sandal. I will triumph over Philistia.”
60:11 III Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me into Edom?
60:12 Was it not you who rejected us, God? Do you no longer march with our armies?
60:13 Give us aid against the foe; worthless is human help.
60:14 We will triumph with the help of God, who will trample down our foes.
text.DRB:
60:1 Unto the end, in hymns to David.
60:2 Hear, o God, my petition: attend to my prayer.
60:3 From the ends of the earth I have cried to thee: whilst my heart was in anguish, thou didst exalt me on a rock.
60:4 Thou hast conducted me, because thou art made my hope: a tower of strength from the face of the enemy.
60:5 Ishall inhabit in thy tabernacle for ever: I shall be protected in the covert of thy wings.
60:6 Because thou my God hast heard my prayer: thou hast given inheritance to those that fear thy name.
60:7 Thou wilt add days upon the days of the king: his years even unto the day of generation and generation.
60:8 He is permanent for ever in the sight of God: his mercy and truth who shall require?
60:9 So will I say a Psalm to thy name for ever and ever: that I may render my vows from day to day.

text.NRSV:
61:1 Hear my cry, O God; listen to my prayer.
61:2 From the end of the earth I call to you, when my heart is faint. Lead me to the rock that is higher than I;
61:3 for you are my refuge, a strong tower against the enemy.
61:4 Let me abide in your tent forever, find refuge under the shelter of your wings. Selah
61:5 For you, O God, have heard my vows; you have given me the heritage of those who fear your name.
61:6 Prolong the life of the king; may his years endure to all generations!
61:7 May he be enthroned forever before God; appoint steadfast love and faithfulness to watch over him!
61:8 So I will always sing praises to your name, as I pay my vows day after day.
text.Coverdale:
61:1 Hear my cry, O God, * and listen to my prayer.
61:2 I call upon you from the ends of the earth with heaviness in my heart; * set me upon the rock that is higher than I.
61:3 For you have been my refuge, * a strong tower against the enemy.
61:4 I will dwell in your house for ever; * I will take refuge under the cover of your wings.
61:5 For you, O God, have heard my vows; * you have granted me the heritage of those who fear your Name.
61:6 Add length of days to the king's life; * let his years extend over many generations.
61:7 Let him sit enthroned before God for ever; * bid love and faithfulness watch over him.
61:8 So will I always sing the praise of your Name, * and day by day I will fulfill my vows.
text.KJV:
61:1 To the chief Musician upon Neginah, A Psalm of David. Hear my cry, O God; attend unto my prayer.
61:2 From the end of the earth will I cry unto thee, when my heart is overwhelmed: lead me to the rock that is higher than I.
61:3 For thou hast been a shelter for me, and a strong tower from the enemy.
61:4 I will abide in thy tabernacle for ever: I will trust in the covert of thy wings. Selah.
61:5 For thou, O God, hast heard my vows: thou hast given me the heritage of those that fear thy name.
61:6 Thou wilt prolong the king’s life: and his years as many generations.
61:7 He shall abide before God for ever: O prepare mercy and truth, which may preserve him.
61:8 So will I sing praise unto thy name for ever, that I may daily perform my vows.
text.JPS1985:
61:1 For the leader; with instrumental music. Of David.
61:2 Hear my cry, O God, heed my prayer.
61:3 From the end of the earth I call to You; when my heart is faint, You lead me to a rock that is high above me.
61:4 For You have been my refuge, a tower of strength against the enemy.
61:5 O that I might dwell in Your tent forever, take refuge under Your protecting wings. Selah.
61:6 O God, You have heard my vows; grant the request of those who fear Your name.
61:7 Add days to the days of the king; may his years extend through generations;
61:8 may he dwell in God’s presence forever; appoint steadfast love to guard him.
61:9 So I will sing hymns to Your name forever, as I fulfill my vows day after day.
text.Grail1963:

text.Rotherham:
61:1 Hear, O God, my loud cry, Attend unto my prayer:
61:2 From the end of the earth, unto thee do I cry, When my heart fainteth away, Unto a rock that is higher than I, wilt thou lead me.
61:3 For thou hast been, A Refuge to me, A Tower of Strength, from the face of the foe.
61:4 I would be a guest in thy tent to the ages, I would seek refuge in the concealment of thy wings. [Selah.]
61:5 For, thou, O God, hast hearkened to my vows, Thou hast granted a possession, unto them who revere thy Name.
61:6 Days—unto the days of the king, wilt thou add. His years, as of generation after generation:
61:7 Let him retain his seat age-abidingly before God, Appoint that, lovingkindness and faithfulness, my watch over him!
61:8 So, will I sing thy Name unto futurity, Paying my vows, day by day.
text.NABRE:
61:1 For the leader; with stringed instruments. Of David.
61:2 I Hear my cry, O God, listen to my prayer!
61:3 From the ends of the earth I call; my heart grows faint. Raise me up, set me on a rock,
61:4 for you are my refuge, a tower of strength against the foe.
61:5 Let me dwell in your tent forever, take refuge in the shelter of your wings. Selah
61:6 II For you, O God, have heard my vows, you have granted me the heritage of those who revere your name.
61:7 Add days to the life of the king; may his years be as from generation to generation;
61:8 May he reign before God forever; send your love and fidelity to preserve him—
61:9 I will duly sing to your name forever, fulfill my vows day after day.
text.DRB:
61:1 Unto the end, for Idithun a Psalm of David.
61:2 Shall not my soul be subject to God? For of him is my salvation.
61:3 For he is my God, and my saviour: my receiver, I shall be moved no more.
61:4 How long set you violently upon a man: you all do kill, as it were upon a wall that is leaning, and a wall shaken.
61:5 But yet they thought to repel my price. I ran in thirst: they blessed with their mouth, and cursed with their heart.
61:6 But yet my soul be thou subject to God: because my patience is from him.
61:7 Because he is my God, and my saviour: my helper I shall not remove.
61:8 In God is my salvation, and my glory: the God of my help, and my hope is in God.
61:9 Hope in him all ye the congregation of people: pour out your hearts before him: God is our helper for ever.
61:10 But yet the children of men are vain, the children of men are liars in balances: that they may deceive by vanity together.
61:11 Hope not in iniquity, and covet not robberies: if riches abound set not your heart upon them.
61:12 Once hath God spoken, these two things have I heard:
61:13 That power is God's, and mercy, o Lord, is to thee: because thou wilt render to every one according to his works.

text.NRSV:
62:1 For God alone my soul waits in silence; from him comes my salvation.
62:2 He alone is my rock and my salvation, my fortress; I shall never be shaken.
62:3 How long will you assail a person, will you batter your victim, all of you, as you would a leaning wall, a tottering fence?
62:4 Their only plan is to bring down a person of prominence. They take pleasure in falsehood; they bless with their mouths, but inwardly they curse. Selah
62:5 For God alone my soul waits in silence, for my hope is from him.
62:6 He alone is my rock and my salvation, my fortress; I shall not be shaken.
62:7 On God rests my deliverance and my honor; my mighty rock, my refuge is in God.
62:8 Trust in him at all times, O people; pour out your heart before him; God is a refuge for us. Selah
62:9 Those of low estate are but a breath, those of high estate are a delusion; in the balances they go up; they are together lighter than a breath.
62:10 Put no confidence in extortion, and set no vain hopes on robbery; if riches increase, do not set your heart on them.
62:11 Once God has spoken; twice have I heard this: that power belongs to God,
62:12 and steadfast love belongs to you, O Lord. For you repay to all according to their work.
text.Coverdale:
62:1 For God alone my soul in silence waits; * from him comes my salvation.
62:2 He alone is my rock and my salvation, * my stronghold, so that I shall not be greatly shaken.
62:3 How long will you assail me to crush me, all of you together, * as if you were a leaning fence, a toppling wall?
62:4 They seek only to bring me down from my place of honor; * lies are their chief delight.
62:5 They bless with their lips, * but in their hearts they curse.
62:6 For God alone my soul in silence waits; * truly, my hope is in him.
62:7 He alone is my rock and my salvation, * my stronghold, so that I shall not be shaken.
62:8 In God is my safety and my honor; * God is my strong rock and my refuge.
62:9 Put your trust in him always, O people, * pour out your hearts before him, for God is our refuge.
62:10 Those of high degree are but a fleeting breath, * even those of low estate cannot be trusted.
62:11 On the scales they are lighter than a breath, * all of them together.
62:12 Put no trust in extortion; in robbery take no empty pride; * though wealth increase, set not your heart upon it.
62:13 God has spoken once, twice have I heard it, * that power belongs to God.
62:14 Steadfast love is yours, O Lord, * for you repay everyone according to his deeds.
text.KJV:
62:1 To the chief Musician, to Jeduthun, A Psalm of David. Truly my soul waiteth upon God: from him cometh my salvation.
62:2 He only is my rock and my salvation; he is my defence; I shall not be greatly moved.
62:3 How long will ye imagine mischief against a man? ye shall be slain all of you: as a bowing wall shall ye be, and as a tottering fence.
62:4 They only consult to cast him down from his excellency: they delight in lies: they bless with their mouth, but they curse inwardly. Selah.
62:5 My soul, wait thou only upon God; for my expectation is from him.
62:6 He only is my rock and my salvation: he is my defence; I shall not be moved.
62:7 In God is my salvation and my glory: the rock of my strength, and my refuge, is in God.
62:8 Trust in him at all times; ye people, pour out your heart before him: God is a refuge for us. Selah.
62:9 Surely men of low degree are vanity, and men of high degree are a lie: to be laid in the balance, they are altogether lighter than vanity.
62:10 Trust not in oppression, and become not vain in robbery: if riches increase, set not your heart upon them.
62:11 God hath spoken once; twice have I heard this; that power belongeth unto God.
62:12 Also unto thee, O Lord, belongeth mercy: for thou renderest to every man according to his work.
text.JPS1985:
62:1 For the leader; on Jeduthun. A psalm of David.
62:2 Truly my soul waits quietly for God; my deliverance comes from Him.
62:3 Truly He is my rock and deliverance, my haven; I shall never be shaken.
62:4 How long will all of you attack a man, to crush him, as though he were a leaning wall, a tottering fence?
62:5 They lay plans to topple him from his rank; they delight in falsehood; they bless with their mouths, while inwardly they curse. Selah.
62:6 Truly, wait quietly for God, O my soul, for my hope comes from Him.
62:7 He is my rock and deliverance, my haven; I shall not be shaken.
62:8 I rely on God, my deliverance and glory, my rock of strength; in God is my refuge.
62:9 Trust in Him at all times, O people; pour out your hearts before Him; God is our refuge. Selah.
62:10 Men are mere breath; mortals, illusion; placed on a scale all together, they weigh even less than a breath.
62:11 Do not trust in violence, or put false hopes in robbery; if force bears fruit pay it no mind.
62:12 One thing God has spoken; two things have I heard: that might belongs to God,
62:13 and faithfulness is Yours, O Lord, to reward each man according to his deeds.
