text.Rotherham:
62:1 Surely, towards God, silence [becometh] my soul, From him, is my salvation:
62:2 Surely, he, is my rock and my salvation, My high tower—I shall not be greatly shaken!
62:3 How long will ye shout at a man? Ye shall be crushed all of you,—Like a wall that bulgeth,—a fence pushed in!
62:4 Surely, from his elevation, they have taken counsel to thrust him down, They accept falsehood,—With his mouth, they each of them bless, But, inwardly, they revile. [Selah.]
62:5 Surely, towards God, be thou silent, my soul, For, from him, is mine expectation:
62:6 Surely, he, is my rock, and my salvation, My high tower—I shall not be shaken!
62:7 Upon God, [depend] my salvation and mine honour, My rock of strength, my refuge, are in God.
62:8 Trust ye in him all ye assembly of the people, Pour out, before him, your heart, God, is a refuge for us. [Selah.]
62:9 Surely, vanity, are men of low degree, Deception, men of high degree,—In the balances, they go up, They, are [made] of vanity, altogether.
62:10 Do not trust in extortion, Nor, with robbery, become vain, As for wealth, when it beareth fruit, Do not set [thereon your] heart.
62:11 One thing, hath God spoken, Two things, [there are] which I have heard, That, power, belongeth unto God;
62:12 And, thine, O My Lord, is lovingkindness,—For, thou, wilt pay back unto every man—according to his deed.
text.NABRE:
62:1 For the leader; ‘al Jeduthun. A psalm of David.
62:2 I My soul rests in God alone, from whom comes my salvation.
62:3 God alone is my rock and salvation, my fortress; I shall never fall.
62:4 How long will you set yourself against a man? You shall all be destroyed, Like a sagging wall or a tumbled down fence!
62:5 Even highly placed people plot to overthrow him. They delight in lies; they bless with their mouths, but inwardly they curse. Selah
62:6 II My soul, be at rest in God alone, from whom comes my hope.
62:7 God alone is my rock and my salvation, my fortress; I shall not fall.
62:8 My deliverance and honor are with God, my strong rock; my refuge is with God.
62:9 Trust God at all times, my people! Pour out your hearts to God our refuge! Selah
62:10 III Mortals are a mere breath, the sons of man but an illusion; On a balance they rise; together they weigh nothing.
62:11 Do not trust in extortion; in plunder put no empty hope. On wealth that increases, do not set your heart.
62:12 One thing God has said; two things I have heard: Strength belongs to God;
62:13 so too, my Lord, does mercy, For you repay each man according to his deeds.
text.DRB:
62:1 A Psalm of David when he was in the desert of Juda.
62:2 O God my God to thee I watch, from the morning light. My soul hath thirsted to thee, my flesh to thee very many ways.
62:3 In a desert land, and inaccessible, and without water, so in the holy have I appeared to thee, that I might see thy strength, and thy glory.
62:4 Because thy mercy is better than lives, my lips shall praise thee.
62:5 So will I bless thee in my life: and in thy name I will lift up my hands.
62:6 As with marrow and fatness let my soul be filled: and my mouth shall praise with lips of exultation.
62:7 If I have been mindful of thee upon my bed, in the morning I will meditate on thee:
62:8 because thou hast been my helper.
62:9 And in the covert of thy wings I will rejoice, my soul hath cleaved after thee: thy right hand hath received me.
62:10 But they in vain have sought my soul, they shall enter into the inferior parts of the earth.
62:11 They shall be delivered into the hands of the sword, they shall be the portions of foxes.
62:12 But the king shall rejoice in God, all shall be praised that swear by him: because the mouth is stopped of those that speak wicked things.

text.NRSV:
63:1 O God, you are my God, I seek you, my soul thirsts for you; my flesh faints for you, as in a dry and weary land where there is no water.
63:2 So I have looked upon you in the sanctuary, beholding your power and glory.
63:3 Because your steadfast love is better than life, my lips will praise you.
63:4 So I will bless you as long as I live; I will lift up my hands and call on your name.
63:5 My soul is satisfied as with a rich feast, and my mouth praises you with joyful lips
63:6 when I think of you on my bed, and meditate on you in the watches of the night;
63:7 for you have been my help, and in the shadow of your wings I sing for joy.
63:8 My soul clings to you; your right hand upholds me.
63:9 But those who seek to destroy my life shall go down into the depths of the earth;
63:10 they shall be given over to the power of the sword, they shall be prey for jackals.
63:11 But the king shall rejoice in God; all who swear by him shall exult, for the mouths of liars will be stopped.
text.Coverdale:
63:1 O God, you are my God; eagerly I seek you; * my soul thirsts for you, my flesh faints for you, as in a barren and dry land where there is no water.
63:2 Therefore I have gazed upon you in your holy place, * that I might behold your power and your glory.
63:3 For your loving-kindness is better than life itself; * my lips shall give you praise.
63:4 So will I bless you as long as I live * and lift up my hands in your Name.
63:5 My soul is content, as with marrow and fatness, * and my mouth praises you with joyful lips,
63:6 When I remember you upon my bed, * and meditate on you in the night watches.
63:7 For you have been my helper, * and under the shadow of your wings I will rejoice.
63:8 My soul clings to you; * your right hand holds me fast.
63:9 May those who seek my life to destroy it * go down into the depths of the earth;
63:10 Let them fall upon the edge of the sword, * and let them be food for jackals.
63:11 But the king will rejoice in God; all those who swear by him will be glad; * for the mouth of those who speak lies shall be stopped.
text.KJV:
63:1 A Psalm of David, when he was in the wilderness of Judah. O God, thou art my God; early will I seek thee: my soul thirsteth for thee, my flesh longeth for thee in a dry and thirsty land, where no water is;
63:2 To see thy power and thy glory, so as I have seen thee in the sanctuary.
63:3 Because thy lovingkindness is better than life, my lips shall praise thee.
63:4 Thus will I bless thee while I live: I will lift up my hands in thy name.
63:5 My soul shall be satisfied as with marrow and fatness; and my mouth shall praise thee with joyful lips:
63:6 When I remember thee upon my bed, and meditate on thee in the night watches.
63:7 Because thou hast been my help, therefore in the shadow of thy wings will I rejoice.
63:8 My soul followeth hard after thee: thy right hand upholdeth me.
63:9 But those that seek my soul, to destroy it, shall go into the lower parts of the earth.
63:10 They shall fall by the sword: they shall be a portion for foxes.
63:11 But the king shall rejoice in God; every one that sweareth by him shall glory: but the mouth of them that speak lies shall be stopped.
text.JPS1985:
63:1 A psalm of David, when he was in the Wilderness of Judah.
63:2 God, You are my God; I search for You, my soul thirsts for You, my body yearns for You, as a parched and thirsty land that has no water.
63:3 I shall behold You in the sanctuary, and see Your might and glory,
63:4 Truly Your faithfulness is better than life; my lips declare Your praise.
63:5 I bless You all my life; I lift up my hands, invoking Your name.
63:6 I am sated as with a rich feast, I sing praises with joyful lips
63:7 when I call You to mind upon my bed, when I think of You in the watches of the night;
63:8 for You are my help, and in the shadow of Your wings I shout for joy.
63:9 My soul is attached to You; Your right hand supports me.
63:10 May those who seek to destroy my life enter the depths of the earth.
63:11 May they be gutted by the sword; may they be prey to jackals.
63:12 But the king shall rejoice in God; all who swear by Him shall exult, when the mouth of liars is stopped.

text.Rotherham:
63:1 O God, my GOD, thou art, Earnestly do I desire thee,—My soul thirsteth for thee, My flesh fainteth for thee, In a land—dry, and weary for want of water,—
63:2 In like manner as, in the sanctuary, I have had vision of thee, To behold thy power and thy glory.
63:3 Because better is thy lovingkindness than life, My lips, aloud shall praise thee!
63:4 Thus, will I bless thee while I live, In thy Name, will I lift up mine outspread hands:
63:5 As with fatness and richness, shall my soul be satisfied, And, with joyfully shouting lips, shall my mouth utter praise.
63:6 Should I call thee to mind upon my couch, In the watches of the night, would I breathe soft speech of thee.
63:7 For thou hast become a help unto me,—And, in the shadow of thy wings, will I shout for joy.
63:8 My soul hath run clinging to thee, On me, hath thy right hand laid hold.
63:9 When, they, to crush it, would seek my life, They shall go into the lower parts of the earth:
63:10 Every one shall be given up into the power of the sword, The portion of jackals, shall they become.
63:11 But, the king, shall rejoice in God,—Every one shall glory who sweareth by him, For the mouth of them who speak falsehood, shall be stopped.
text.NABRE:
63:1 A psalm of David, when he was in the wilderness of Judah.
63:2 I O God, you are my God— it is you I seek! For you my body yearns; for you my soul thirsts, In a land parched, lifeless, and without water.
63:3 I look to you in the sanctuary to see your power and glory.
63:4 For your love is better than life; my lips shall ever praise you!
63:5 II I will bless you as long as I live; I will lift up my hands, calling on your name.
63:6 My soul shall be sated as with choice food, with joyous lips my mouth shall praise you!
63:7 I think of you upon my bed, I remember you through the watches of the night
63:8 You indeed are my savior, and in the shadow of your wings I shout for joy.
63:9 My soul clings fast to you; your right hand upholds me.
63:10 III But those who seek my life will come to ruin; they shall go down to the depths of the netherworld!
63:11 Those who would hand over my life to the sword shall become the prey of jackals!
63:12 But the king shall rejoice in God; all who swear by the Lord shall exult, but the mouths of liars will be shut!
text.DRB:
63:1 Unto the end, a Psalm of David.
63:2 Hear o God my prayer when I make petition: from the fear of my enemy deliver my soul.
63:3 Thou hast protected me from the assembly of the malignant: from the multitude of them that work iniquity.
63:4 Because they have sharpened their tongues as a sword: they have bent the bow a bitter thing,
63:5 that they may shoot in secrets at the immaculate.
63:6 Suddenly they will shoot at him, and will not fear: they have confirmed to themselves a wicked word. They have talked to hide snares: they have said, Who shall see them?
63:7 They have searched iniquities: they have failed searching with scrutiny.
63:8 Man shall come to a deep heart: and God shall be exalted. Children's arrows are made their wounds:
63:9 and their tongues are weakened against them. All that saw them were troubled:
63:10 and every man feared. And they shewed forth the works of God: and they understood his doings.
63:11 The just shall rejoice in our Lord, and shall hope in him, & all the right of heart shall be praised.

text.NRSV:
64:1 Hear my voice, O God, in my complaint; preserve my life from the dread enemy.
64:2 Hide me from the secret plots of the wicked, from the scheming of evildoers,
64:3 who whet their tongues like swords, who aim bitter words like arrows,
64:4 shooting from ambush at the blameless; they shoot suddenly and without fear.
64:5 They hold fast to their evil purpose; they talk of laying snares secretly, thinking,"Who can see us?
64:6 Who can search out our crimes? We have thought out a cunningly conceived plot."For the human heart and mind are deep.
64:7 But God will shoot his arrow at them; they will be wounded suddenly.
64:8 Because of their tongue he will bring them to ruin; all who see them will shake with horror.
64:9 Then everyone will fear; they will tell what God has brought about, and ponder what he has done.
64:10 Let the righteous rejoice in the Lord and take refuge in him. Let all the upright in heart glory.
text.Coverdale:
64:1 Hear my voice, O God, when I complain; * protect my life from fear of the enemy.
64:2 Hide me from the conspiracy of the wicked, * from the mob of evildoers.
64:3 They sharpen their tongue like a sword, * and aim their bitter words like arrows,
64:4 That they may shoot down the blameless from ambush; * they shoot without warning and are not afraid.
64:5 They hold fast to their evil course; * they plan how they may hide their snares.
64:6 They say,"Who will see us? who will find out our crimes? * we have thought out a perfect plot."
64:7 The human mind and heart are a mystery; * but God will loose an arrow at them, and suddenly they will be wounded.
64:8 He will make them trip over their tongues, * and all who see them will shake their heads.
64:9 Everyone will stand in awe and declare God's deeds; * they will recognize his works.
64:10 The righteous will rejoice in the LORD and put their trust in him, * and all who are true of heart will glory.
text.KJV:
64:1 To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. Hear my voice, O God, in my prayer: preserve my life from fear of the enemy.
64:2 Hide me from the secret counsel of the wicked; from the insurrection of the workers of iniquity:
64:3 Who whet their tongue like a sword, and bend their bows to shoot their arrows, even bitter words:
64:4 That they may shoot in secret at the perfect: suddenly do they shoot at him, and fear not.
64:5 They encourage themselves in an evil matter: they commune of laying snares privily; they say, Who shall see them?
64:6 They search out iniquities; they accomplish a diligent search: both the inward thought of every one of them, and the heart, is deep.
64:7 But God shall shoot at them with an arrow; suddenly shall they be wounded.
64:8 So they shall make their own tongue to fall upon themselves: all that see them shall flee away.
64:9 And all men shall fear, and shall declare the work of God; for they shall wisely consider of his doing.
64:10 The righteous shall be glad in the Lord, and shall trust in him; and all the upright in heart shall glory.
text.JPS1985:
64:1 For the leader. A psalm of David.
64:2 Hear my voice, O God, when I plead; guard my life from the enemy’s terror.
64:3 Hide me from a band of evil men, from a crowd of evildoers,
64:4 who whet their tongues like swords; they aim their arrows—cruel words—
64:5 to shoot from hiding at the blameless man; they shoot him suddenly and without fear.
64:6 They arm themselves with an evil word; when they speak, it is to conceal traps; they think, “Who will see them? ”
64:7 Let the wrongdoings they have concealed, each one inside him, his secret thoughts, be wholly exposed.
64:8 God shall shoot them with arrows; they shall be struck down suddenly.
64:9 Their tongue shall be their downfall; all who see them shall recoil in horror;
64:10 all men shall stand in awe; they shall proclaim the work of God and His deed which they perceived.
64:11 The righteous shall rejoice in the LORD, and take refuge in Him; all the upright shall exult.
text.Grail1963:
.2 Hear my voice, O God, as I complain,
guard my life from dread of the foe.

from the throng of those who do evil.

they aim bitter words like arrows

5 to shoot at the innocent from ambush,
shooting suddenly and recklessly.

* * *
He will search who searches the mind
and knows the depths of the heart.
8 God has shot them with his arrow
and dealt them sudden wounds.
9 Their own tongue has brought them to ruin
and all who see them mock.
10 Then will all men fear;
they will tell what God has done.
They will understand God's deeds.
11 The just will rejoice in the Lord
and fly to him for refuge.
All the upright hearts will glory.
text.Rotherham:
64:1 Hear, O God, my voice when I complain, From dread peril by the foe, wilt thou guard my life.
64:2 Wilt thou hide me, From the conclave of evil-doers, From the crowd of workers of iniquity.
64:3 Who have sharpened, like a sword, their tongue, Have made ready their arrow—a bitter word;
64:4 To shoot, in secret places, at the blameless one, Suddenly they shoot at him, and fear not.
64:5 They strengthen for them a wicked word, They talk of hiding snares, They have said, Who can see them?
64:6 They devise perverse things, They have completed the device well devised, Both the intent of each one, and the mind, are unsearchable.
64:7 Once let God have shot at them an arrow, Suddenly have appeared their own wounds!
64:8 When they were to have ruined another, their tongue smote themselves, All who observe them take flight.
64:9 Therefore have all men feared,—And have told the doing of God, And, his work, have considered.
64:10 The righteous man shall rejoice in Yahweh, and seek refuge in him, Then shall glory—all who are upright in heart.
text.NABRE:
64:1 For the leader. A psalm of David.
64:2 I O God, hear my anguished voice; from a dreadful foe protect my life.
64:3 Hide me from the malicious crowd, the mob of evildoers.
64:4 They sharpen their tongues like swords, bend their bows of poison words.
64:5 They shoot at the innocent from ambush, they shoot him in a moment and do not fear.
64:6 They resolve on their wicked plan; they conspire to set snares; they say: “Who will see us?”
64:7 They devise wicked schemes, conceal the schemes they devise; the designs of their hearts are hidden.
64:8 II God shoots an arrow at them; in a moment they are struck down.
64:9 They are brought down by their own tongues; all who see them flee.
64:10 Every person fears and proclaims God’s actions, they ponder his deeds.
64:11 The righteous rejoices and takes refuge in the Lord; all the upright give praise.
text.DRB:
64:1 To the end, a Psalm of David, the Canticle of Jeremy, and Ezechiel, to the people of the transmigration, when they began to go forth.
64:2 An hymn, o God, becometh thee in Sion: & a vow shall be rendered to thee in Jerusalem.
64:3 Hear my prayer: all flesh shall come to thee.
64:4 The words of the wicked have prevailed upon us: and thou wilt be propitious to our impieties.
64:5 Blessed is he whom thou hast chosen and taken: he shall dwell in thy courts.
64:6 We shall be replenished in the goods of thy house: holy is thy temple, marvelous in equity. Hear us, o God our saviour, the hope of all the ends of the earth, and in the sea far.
64:7 Preparing mountains in thy strength, girded with might:
64:8 which troublest the depths of the sea, the sound of the waves thereof.
64:9 The Gentiles shall be troubled, and they that inhabit the borders shall be afraid of thy signs: the out-goings of the morning and evening thou shalt delight.
64:10 Thou hast visited the earth, and hast inebriated it: thou hast multiplied to enrich it. The river of God is replenished with waters, thou hast prepared their meat: because so is the preparation thereof.
64:11 Inebriate her rivers, multiply her fruits: in her drops she shall rejoice springing.
64:12 Thou wilt bless the crown of the year of thy goodness: and thy fields shall be replenished with plenty.
64:13 The beautiful places of the desert shall be fat: and the little hills shall be girded about with exultation.
64:14 The rams of the sheep are clothed, and the valleys shall abound with corn: they will cry, yea they will say an hymn.

text.NRSV:
65:1 Praise is due to you, O God, in Zion; and to you shall vows be performed,
65:2 O you who answer prayer! To you all flesh shall come.
65:3 When deeds of iniquity overwhelm us, you forgive our transgressions.
65:4 Happy are those whom you choose and bring near to live in your courts. We shall be satisfied with the goodness of your house, your holy temple.
65:5 By awesome deeds you answer us with deliverance, O God of our salvation; you are the hope of all the ends of the earth and of the farthest seas.
65:6 By your strength you established the mountains; you are girded with might.
65:7 You silence the roaring of the seas, the roaring of their waves, the tumult of the peoples.
65:8 Those who live at earth's farthest bounds are awed by your signs; you make the gateways of the morning and the evening shout for joy.
65:9 You visit the earth and water it, you greatly enrich it; the river of God is full of water; you provide the people with grain, for so you have prepared it.
65:10 You water its furrows abundantly, settling its ridges, softening it with showers, and blessing its growth.
65:11 You crown the year with your bounty; your wagon tracks overflow with richness.
65:12 The pastures of the wilderness overflow, the hills gird themselves with joy,
65:13 the meadows clothe themselves with flocks, the valleys deck themselves with grain, they shout and sing together for joy.
text.Coverdale:
65:1 You are to be praised, O God, in Zion; * to you shall vows be performed in Jerusalem.
65:2 To you that hear prayer shall all flesh come, * because of their transgressions.
65:3 Our sins are stronger than we are, * but you will blot them out.
65:4 Happy are they whom you choose and draw to your courts to dwell there! * they will be satisfied by the beauty of your house, by the holiness of your temple.
65:5 Awesome things will you show us in your righteousness, O God of our salvation, * O Hope of all the ends of the earth and of the seas that are far away.
65:6 You make fast the mountains by your power; * they are girded about with might.
65:7 You still the roaring of the seas, * the roaring of their waves, and the clamor of the peoples.
65:8 Those who dwell at the ends of the earth will tremble at your marvelous signs; * you make the dawn and the dusk to sing for joy.
65:9 You visit the earth and water it abundantly; you make it very plenteous; * the river of God is full of water.
65:10 You prepare the grain, * for so you provide for the earth.
65:11 You drench the furrows and smooth out the ridges; * with heavy rain you soften the ground and bless its increase.
65:12 You crown the year with your goodness, * and your paths overflow with plenty.
65:13 May the fields of the wilderness be rich for grazing, * and the hills be clothed with joy.
65:14 May the meadows cover themselves with flocks, and the valleys cloak themselves with grain; * let them shout for joy and sing.
text.KJV:
65:1 To the chief Musician, A Psalm and Song of David. Praise waiteth for thee, O God, in Sion: and unto thee shall the vow be performed.
65:2 O thou that hearest prayer, unto thee shall all flesh come.
65:3 Iniquities prevail against me: as for our transgressions, thou shalt purge them away.
65:4 Blessed is the man whom thou choosest, and causest to approach unto thee, that he may dwell in thy courts: we shall be satisfied with the goodness of thy house, even of thy holy temple.
65:5 By terrible things in righteousness wilt thou answer us, O God of our salvation; who art the confidence of all the ends of the earth, and of them that are afar off upon the sea:
65:6 Which by his strength setteth fast the mountains; being girded with power:
65:7 Which stilleth the noise of the seas, the noise of their waves, and the tumult of the people.
65:8 They also that dwell in the uttermost parts are afraid at thy tokens: thou makest the outgoings of the morning and evening to rejoice.
65:9 Thou visitest the earth, and waterest it: thou greatly enrichest it with the river of God, which is full of water: thou preparest them corn, when thou hast so provided for it.
65:10 Thou waterest the ridges thereof abundantly: thou settlest the furrows thereof: thou makest it soft with showers: thou blessest the springing thereof.
65:11 Thou crownest the year with thy goodness; and thy paths drop fatness.
65:12 They drop upon the pastures of the wilderness: and the little hills rejoice on every side.
65:13 The pastures are clothed with flocks; the valleys also are covered over with corn; they shout for joy, they also sing.
text.JPS1985:
65:1 For the leader. A psalm of David. A song.
65:2 Praise befits You in Zion, O God; vows are paid to You;
65:3 all mankind comes to You, You who hear prayer.
65:4 When all manner of sins overwhelm me, it is You who forgive our iniquities.
65:5 Happy is the man You choose and bring near to dwell in Your courts; may we be sated with the blessings of Your house, Your holy temple.
65:6 Answer us with victory through awesome deeds, O God, our deliverer, in whom all the ends of the earth and the distant seas put their trust;
65:7 who by His power fixed the mountains firmly, who is girded with might,
65:8 who stills the raging seas, the raging waves, and tumultuous peoples.
65:9 Those who live at the ends of the earth are awed by Your signs; You make the lands of sunrise and sunset shout for joy.
65:10 You take care of the earth and irrigate it; You enrich it greatly, with the channel of God full of water; You provide grain for men; for so do You prepare it.
65:11 Saturating its furrows, leveling its ridges, You soften it with showers, You bless its growth.
65:12 You crown the year with Your bounty; fatness is distilled in Your paths;
65:13 the pasturelands distill it; the hills are girded with joy.
65:14 The meadows are clothed with flocks, the valleys mantled with grain; they raise a shout, they break into song.
text.Grail1963:
.2 To you our praise is due
in Zion, O God.
To you we pay our vows,
3 you who hear our prayer.
To you all flesh will come
4 with its burden of sin.
Too heavy for us, our offenses,
but you wipe them away.
5 Blessed is he whom you choose and call
to dwell in your courts.
We are filled with the blessings of your house,
of your holy temple.
6 You keep your pledge with wonders,
O God our savior,
the hope of all the earth
and of far distant isles.
7 You uphold the mountains with your strength,
you are girded with power.
8 You still the roaring of the seas,
(the roaring of their waves,)
and the tumult of the peoples.
9 The ends of the earth stand in awe
at the sight of your wonders.
The lands of sunrise and sunset

you fill with your joy.
10 You care for the earth, give it water;
you fill it with riches.
Your river in heaven brims over
to provide its grain.
And thus you provide for the earth;
11 you drench its furrows;
you level it, soften it with showers;
you bless its growth.
12 You crown the year with your goodness.
Abundance flows in your steps;
13 in the pastures of the wilderness it flows.
The hills are girded with joy,

the valleys are decked with wheat.
They shout for joy, yes they sing.
text.Rotherham:
65:1 Thine, are silence [and] praise, O God, in Zion,—And, to thee, shall be paid the vow.
65:2 Thou hearer of prayer! Unto thee, shall all flesh come.
65:3 Iniquitous things, have been too strong for me, As for our transgressions, wilt, thou, by propitiation remove them.
65:4 How happy the man thou shalt choose and bring near! He shall abide in thy courts,—We shall be satisfied with, The blessing of thy house, The holiness of thy temple.
65:5 By things reverend in righteousness, wilt thou answer us, O God of our salvation, The confidence of all the ends of the earth, And of the sea far away;
65:6 Who setteth fast the mountains by his strength, Being girded with might;
65:7 Who stilleth, The noise of the seas, The noise of their rolling waves, and The tumult of races of men?
65:8 Yea the dwellers in the uttermost parts have feared at thy tokens, The goings forth of morning and evening, thou causest to shout for joy.
65:9 Thou hast visited the earth, and made it abound, Abundantly, dost thou enrich it—The channel of God, is full of waters, Thou preparest their corn, Yea, thus, dost thou prepare it:
65:10 The ridges thereof, drenching, Settling the furrows thereof, With myriad drops, dost thou soften it, The sprouting thereof, doth thou bless.
65:11 Thou hast set a crown upon thy year of bounty, And, thy tracks, drop fatness;
65:12 Fruitful are the pastures of the wilderness, And, with exultation, the hills do gird themselves.
65:13 Clothed are the pastures with flocks, The valleys also, cover themselves with corn, They shout for joy, yea they sing.
text.NABRE:
65:1 For the leader. A psalm of David. A song.
65:2 I To you we owe our hymn of praise, O God on Zion; To you our vows must be fulfilled,
65:3 you who hear our prayers. To you all flesh must come
65:4 with its burden of wicked deeds. We are overcome by our sins; only you can pardon them.
65:5 Blessed the one whom you will choose and bring to dwell in your courts. May we be filled with the good things of your house, your holy temple!
65:6 II You answer us with awesome deeds of justice, O God our savior, The hope of all the ends of the earth and of those far off across the sea.
65:7 You are robed in power, you set up the mountains by your might.
65:8 You still the roaring of the seas, the roaring of their waves, the tumult of the peoples.
65:9 Distant peoples stand in awe of your marvels; the places of morning and evening you make resound with joy.
65:10 You visit the earth and water it, make it abundantly fertile. God’s stream is filled with water; you supply their grain. Thus do you prepare it:
65:11 you drench its plowed furrows, and level its ridges. With showers you keep it soft, blessing its young sprouts.
65:12 You adorn the year with your bounty; your paths drip with fruitful rain.
65:13 The meadows of the wilderness also drip; the hills are robed with joy.
65:14 The pastures are clothed with flocks, the valleys blanketed with grain; they cheer and sing for joy.
text.DRB:
65:1 Unto the end, a Canticle of resurrection. Make ye jubilation to God all the earth,
65:2 say a Psalm to his name: give glory to his praise.
65:3 Say ye to God: How terrible are thy works, o Lord! In the multitude of thy strength thine enemies shall lie to thee.
65:4 Let all the earth adore thee, and sing to thee: let it sing a Psalm to thy name.
65:5 Come ye, and see the works of God: terrible in counsels over the children of men.
65:6 Who turneth the sea into dry land, in the river they shall pass on foot, there we shall rejoice in him.
65:7 Who ruleth in his strength for ever his eyes look upon the gentiles: they that exasperate him let them not be exalted in themselves.
65:8 Ye Gentiles bless our God: and make the voice of his praise heard.
65:9 Who hath put my soul in life: and hath not given my feet to be moved.
65:10 Because thou hast proved us, o God: by fire thou hast tried us, as silver is tried.
65:11 Thou hast brought us into a snare, thou hast laid tribulations on our back: thou hast set men upon our heads.
65:12 We have passed through fire and water: and thou hast brought us out into refreshing.
65:13 I will go into thy house with holocausts: I will render thee my vows:
65:14 which my lips have distinguished. And my mouth hath spoken in my tribulation.
65:15 Holocausts with marrow will I offer to thee, with incense of rams: I will offer to thee oxen with buck goats.
65:16 Come ye, hear, and I will tell all ye that fear God, what great things he hath done for my soul.
65:17 To him have I cried with my mouth, and have exulted under my tongue.
65:18 If I have beheld iniquity in my heart, our Lord will not hear.
65:19 Therefore hath God heard, and hath attended to the voice of my petition.
65:20 Blessed be God who hath not removed my prayer, and his mercy from me.

text.NRSV:
66:1 Make a joyful noise to God, all the earth;
66:2 sing the glory of his name; give to him glorious praise.
66:3 Say to God,"How awesome are your deeds! Because of your great power, your enemies cringe before you.
66:4 All the earth worships you; they sing praises to you, sing praises to your name."Selah
66:5 Come and see what God has done: he is awesome in his deeds among mortals.
66:6 He turned the sea into dry land; they passed through the river on foot. There we rejoiced in him,
66:7 who rules by his might forever, whose eyes keep watch on the nations— let the rebellious not exalt themselves. Selah
66:8 Bless our God, O peoples, let the sound of his praise be heard,
66:9 who has kept us among the living, and has not let our feet slip.
66:10 For you, O God, have tested us; you have tried us as silver is tried.
66:11 You brought us into the net; you laid burdens on our backs;
66:12 you let people ride over our heads; we went through fire and through water; yet you have brought us out to a spacious place.
66:13 I will come into your house with burnt offerings; I will pay you my vows,
66:14 those that my lips uttered and my mouth promised when I was in trouble.
66:15 I will offer to you burnt offerings of fatlings, with the smoke of the sacrifice of rams; I will make an offering of bulls and goats. Selah
66:16 Come and hear, all you who fear God, and I will tell what he has done for me.
66:17 I cried aloud to him, and he was extolled with my tongue.
66:18 If I had cherished iniquity in my heart, the Lord would not have listened.
66:19 But truly God has listened; he has given heed to the words of my prayer.
66:20 Blessed be God, because he has not rejected my prayer or removed his steadfast love from me.
text.Coverdale:
66:1 Be joyful in God, all you lands; * sing the glory of his Name; sing the glory of his praise.
66:2 Say to God,"How awesome are your deeds! * because of your great strength your enemies cringe before you.
66:3 All the earth bows down before you, * sings to you, sings out your Name."
66:4 Come now and see the works of God, * how wonderful he is in his doing toward all people.
66:5 He turned the sea into dry land, so that they went through the water on foot, * and there we rejoiced in him.
66:6 In his might he rules for ever; his eyes keep watch over the nations; * let no rebel rise up against him.
66:7 Bless our God, you peoples; * make the voice of his praise to be heard;
66:8 Who holds our souls in life, * and will not allow our feet to slip.
66:9 For you, O God, have proved us; * you have tried us just as silver is tried.
66:10 You brought us into the snare; * you laid heavy burdens upon our backs.
66:11 You let enemies ride over our heads; we went through fire and water; * but you brought us out into a place of refreshment.
66:12 I will enter your house with burnt-offerings and will pay you my vows, * which I promised with my lips and spoke with my mouth when I was in trouble.
66:13 I will offer you sacrifices of fat beasts with the smoke of rams; * I will give you oxen and goats.
66:14 Come and listen, all you who fear God, * and I will tell you what he has done for me.
66:15 I called out to him with my mouth, * and his praise was on my tongue.
66:16 If I had found evil in my heart, * the Lord would not have heard me;
66:17 But in truth God has heard me; * he has attended to the voice of my prayer.
66:18 Blessed be God, who has not rejected my prayer, * nor withheld his love from me.
text.KJV:
66:1 To the chief Musician, A Song or Psalm. Make a joyful noise unto God, all ye lands:
66:2 Sing forth the honour of his name: make his praise glorious.
66:3 Say unto God, How terrible art thou in thy works! through the greatness of thy power shall thine enemies submit themselves unto thee.
66:4 All the earth shall worship thee, and shall sing unto thee; they shall sing to thy name. Selah.
66:5 Come and see the works of God: he is terrible in his doing toward the children of men.
66:6 He turned the sea into dry land: they went through the flood on foot: there did we rejoice in him.
66:7 He ruleth by his power for ever; his eyes behold the nations: let not the rebellious exalt themselves. Selah.
66:8 O bless our God, ye people, and make the voice of his praise to be heard:
66:9 Which holdeth our soul in life, and suffereth not our feet to be moved.
66:10 For thou, O God, hast proved us: thou hast tried us, as silver is tried.
66:11 Thou broughtest us into the net; thou laidst affliction upon our loins.
66:12 Thou hast caused men to ride over our heads; we went through fire and through water: but thou broughtest us out into a wealthy place.
66:13 I will go into thy house with burnt offerings: I will pay thee my vows,
66:14 Which my lips have uttered, and my mouth hath spoken, when I was in trouble.
66:15 I will offer unto thee burnt sacrifices of fatlings, with the incense of rams; I will offer bullocks with goats. Selah.
66:16 Come and hear, all ye that fear God, and I will declare what he hath done for my soul.
66:17 I cried unto him with my mouth, and he was extolled with my tongue.
66:18 If I regard iniquity in my heart, the Lord will not hear me:
66:19 But verily God hath heard me; he hath attended to the voice of my prayer.
66:20 Blessed be God, which hath not turned away my prayer, nor his mercy from me.
text.JPS1985:
66:1 For the leader. A song. A psalm. Raise a shout for God, all the earth;
66:2 sing the glory of His name, make glorious His praise.
66:3 Say to God, “How awesome are Your deeds, Your enemies cower before Your great strength;
66:4 all the earth bows to You, and sings hymns to You; all sing hymns to Your name.” Selah.
66:5 Come and see the works of God, who is held in awe by men for His acts.
66:6 He turned the sea into dry land; they crossed the river on foot; we therefore rejoice in Him.
66:7 He rules forever in His might; His eyes scan the nations; let the rebellious not assert themselves. Selah.
66:8 O peoples, bless our God, celebrate His praises;
66:9 who has granted us life, and has not let our feet slip.
66:10 You have tried us, O God, refining us, as one refines silver.
66:11 You have caught us in a net, caught us in trammels.
66:12 You have let men ride over us; we have endured fire and water, and You have brought us through to prosperity.
66:13 I enter Your house with burnt offerings, I pay my vows to You,
66:14 [vows] that my lips pronounced, that my mouth uttered in my distress.
66:15 I offer up fatlings to You, with the odor of burning rams; I sacrifice bulls and he-goats. Selah.
66:16 Come and hear, all God-fearing men, as I tell what He did for me.
66:17 I called aloud to Him, glorification on my tongue.
66:18 Had I an evil thought in my mind, the Lord would not have listened.
66:19 But God did listen; He paid heed to my prayer.
66:20 Blessed is God who has not turned away my prayer, or His faithful care from me.

text.Rotherham:
66:1 Make a joyful noise unto God, all the earth;
66:2 Praise ye in song the glory of his Name, Celebrate the glory of his praise:
66:3 Say unto God—How fearful in thy doings, Through the abounding of thy power, shall thy foes come cringing unto thee;
66:4 All the earth, shall bow themselves down to thee, And sing praises unto thee,. Shall praise in song thy Name. [Selah.]
66:5 Come and see the doings of God,—Fearful in deed toward the sons of men:
66:6 He turned the sea into dry land, Through the stream, crossed they over on foot, There did we rejoice in him:
66:7 Who ruleth, in his might, unto times age abiding, His eyes, over the nations, keep watch, The rebellious, let them not exalt themselves. [Selah.]
66:8 Bless our God, O ye peoples, And cause to be heard, the sound of his praise;—
66:9 Who hath set our soul among the living, And hath not suffered, our foot, to slip.
66:10 For thou didst prove us, O God, Thou didst refine us, according to the refining of silver:
66:11 Thou didst bring us into the hunter’s net, Thou didst lay a load upon our loins;
66:12 Thou didst let men ride at our head; We went into fire and into water, But thou didst bring us forth into freedom.
66:13 I will enter thy house with ascending-sacrifices, I will pay unto thee my vows,
66:14 Which my lips uttered, And my mouth spake, in my distress.
66:15 Ascending-sacrifices of fatlings, will I cause to ascend unto thee, With the perfume of rams, I will offer bulls, with he-goats. [Selah.]
66:16 Come! hearken—that I may recount, all ye reverers of God, What he hath done for my soul:—
66:17 Unto him—with my mouth, did I cry, And high praise was under my tongue.
66:18 If, iniquity, I had cared for in my heart, My Lord, had not heard me!
66:19 But in truth, God hath heard, He hath attended to the voice of my prayer.
66:20 Blessed be God, Who hath not turned away my prayer, Nor his own lovingkindness from me.
text.NABRE:
66:1 For the leader. A song; a psalm.
66:2 I Shout joyfully to God, all the earth; sing of his glorious name; give him glorious praise.
66:3 Say to God: “How awesome your deeds! Before your great strength your enemies cringe.
66:4 All the earth falls in worship before you; they sing of you, sing of your name!” Selah
66:5 II Come and see the works of God, awesome in deeds before the children of Adam.
66:6 He changed the sea to dry land; through the river they passed on foot. There we rejoiced in him,
66:7 who rules by his might forever, His eyes are fixed upon the nations. Let no rebel rise to challenge! Selah
66:8 Bless our God, you peoples; loudly sound his praise,
66:9 Who has kept us alive and not allowed our feet to slip.
66:10 You tested us, O God, tried us as silver tried by fire.
66:11 You led us into a snare; you bound us at the waist as captives.
66:12 You let captors set foot on our neck; we went through fire and water; then you led us out to freedom.
66:13 III I will bring burnt offerings to your house; to you I will fulfill my vows,
66:14 Which my lips pronounced and my mouth spoke in my distress.
66:15 Burnt offerings of fatlings I will offer you and sacrificial smoke of rams; I will sacrifice oxen and goats. Selah
66:16 Come and hear, all you who fear God, while I recount what has been done for me.
66:17 I called to him with my mouth; praise was upon my tongue.
66:18 Had I cherished evil in my heart, the Lord would not have heard.
66:19 But God did hear and listened to my voice in prayer.
66:20 Blessed be God, who did not reject my prayer and refuse his mercy.
text.DRB:
66:1 Unto the end, in hymns, a Psalm of Canticle to David.
66:2 God have mercy upon us, and bless us: illuminate his countenance upon us, and have mercy on us.
66:3 That we may know thy way upon earth: in all nations thy salvation.
66:4 Let peoples, o God, confess to thee: let all peoples confess to thee.
66:5 Let nations be glad & rejoice: because thou judgest peoples in equity, and the nations in earth thou doest direct.
66:6 Let peoples, o God, confess to thee, let all peoples confess to thee:
66:7 the earth hath yielded her fruit. God, our God bless us,
66:8 God bless us: and let all the ends of the earth fear him.
