text.Rotherham:
80:1 O Shepherd of Israel, give ear, Thou who leddest forth Joseph like a rock, Thou who art throned on the cherubim, appear!
80:2 Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh, stir up thy strength, And come! to our salvation.
80:3 O God, bring us back, And light up thy face, That we may be saved.
80:4 O Yahweh, God of hosts! How long hast thou been wroth with the prayer of thy people?
80:5 Thou hast fed them with the food of tears, And hast caused them to drink the water of weeping in threefold abundance.
80:6 Thou dost make us an object of contention to our neighbours, And, our foes, find mockery for themselves.
80:7 O God of hosts, bring us back, And light up thy face, That we may be saved.
80:8 A vine out of Egypt, thou didst remove, Thou didst cast out nations, and plant it;
80:9 Thou didst make a clear space before it, So it rooted well its roots, and filled up the land;
80:10 Covered were the mountains with its shade, And, with its boughs, the mighty cedars.
80:11 It thrust forth its branches as far as the sea,—And, unto the River, its shoots.
80:12 Wherefore hast thou broken down its fences, So that all who pass along the way, pluck its fruit?
80:13 The boar out of the forest, browseth upon it, And, the wild beast of the field, pastureth thereon.
80:14 O God of hosts, return, we pray thee,—Look down out of the heavens, and see, And inspect this vine:
80:15 Yea the stock which thy right hand planted, Even upon the son thou didst secure for thyself.
80:16 To be burned with fire, it is cut down,—At the rebuke of thy countenance, they will perish.
80:17 Let thy hand be, Upon the Man of thy right hand, Upon the Son of Man thou didst secure for thyself;
80:18 So will we not draw back from thee, Thou wilt bring us to life, And, on thy Name, will we call.
80:19 O Yahweh, God of hosts! bring us back, Light up thy face, That we may be saved.
text.NABRE:
80:1 For the leader; according to “Lilies.” Eduth. A psalm of Asaph.
80:2 I O Shepherd of Israel, lend an ear, you who guide Joseph like a flock! Seated upon the cherubim, shine forth
80:3 upon Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasseh. Stir up your power, and come to save us.
80:4 O God, restore us; light up your face and we shall be saved.
80:5 II Lord of hosts, how long will you smolder in anger while your people pray?
80:6 You have fed them the bread of tears, made them drink tears in great measure.
80:7 You have left us to be fought over by our neighbors; our enemies deride us.
80:8 O God of hosts, restore us; light up your face and we shall be saved.
80:9 III You brought a vine out of Egypt; you drove out nations and planted it.
80:10 You cleared out what was before it; it took deep root and filled the land.
80:11 The mountains were covered by its shadow, the cedars of God by its branches.
80:12 It sent out its boughs as far as the sea, its shoots as far as the river.
80:13 Why have you broken down its walls, so that all who pass along the way pluck its fruit?
80:14 The boar from the forest strips the vine; the beast of the field feeds upon it.
80:15 Turn back again, God of hosts; look down from heaven and see; Visit this vine,
80:16 the stock your right hand has planted, and the son whom you made strong for yourself.
80:17 Those who would burn or cut it down— may they perish at your rebuke.
80:18 May your hand be with the man on your right, with the son of man whom you made strong for yourself.
80:19 Then we will not withdraw from you; revive us, and we will call on your name.
80:20 Lord God of hosts, restore us; light up your face and we shall be saved.
text.DRB:
80:1 Unto the end, for wine presses, a Psalm to Asaph himself.
80:2 Rejoice to God our helper: make Jubilation to the God of Jacob.
80:3 Take ye Psalm, and give timbrel: pleasant Psalter with the harp.
80:4 Sound ye with trumpet in the new moon, in the notable day of your solemnity.
80:5 Because it is a precept in Israel, and judgment to the God of Jacob.
80:6 He put it for a testimony in Joseph, when he came out of the Land of Ægypt: he heard a tongue which he knew not.
80:7 He turned away his back from burdens: his hands served in baskets.
80:8 In tribulation thou didst invocate me, and I heard thee: I heard thee in the secret of the tempest: I proved thee at the water of contradiction.
80:9 Hear, o my people, and I will contest thee: Israel if thou wilt hear me.
80:10 There shall be no new god in thee, neither shalt thou adore a strange god.
80:11 For I am the Lord thy God, which brought thee out of the land of Ægypt: dilate thy mouth and I will fill it.
80:12 And my People heard not my voice: and Israel attended not to me.
80:13 And I let them alone, according to the desires of their heart, they shall go in their own inventions.
80:14 If my people had heard me, if Israel had walked in my ways:
80:15 I had for nothing perhaps humbled their enemies: and had laid my hand upon those that afflict them.
80:16 The enemies of our Lord have lied to him: and their time shall be for ever.
80:17 And he fed them of the fat of corn: and out of the rock with honey he filled them.

text.NRSV:
81:1 Sing aloud to God our strength; shout for joy to the God of Jacob.
81:2 Raise a song, sound the tambourine, the sweet lyre with the harp.
81:3 Blow the trumpet at the new moon, at the full moon, on our festal day.
81:4 For it is a statute for Israel, an ordinance of the God of Jacob.
81:5 He made it a decree in Joseph, when he went out over the land of Egypt. I hear a voice I had not known:
81:6 "I relieved your shoulder of the burden; your hands were freed from the basket.
81:7 In distress you called, and I rescued you; I answered you in the secret place of thunder; I tested you at the waters of Meribah. Selah
81:8 Hear, O my people, while I admonish you; O Israel, if you would but listen to me!
81:9 There shall be no strange god among you; you shall not bow down to a foreign god.
81:10 I am the Lord your God, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt. Open your mouth wide and I will fill it.
81:11 "But my people did not listen to my voice; Israel would not submit to me.
81:12 So I gave them over to their stubborn hearts, to follow their own counsels.
81:13 O that my people would listen to me, that Israel would walk in my ways!
81:14 Then I would quickly subdue their enemies, and turn my hand against their foes.
81:15 Those who hate the Lord would cringe before him, and their doom would last forever.
81:16 I would feed you with the finest of the wheat, and with honey from the rock I would satisfy you."
text.Coverdale:
81:1 Sing with joy to God our strength * and raise a loud shout to the God of Jacob.
81:2 Raise a song and sound the timbrel, * the merry harp, and the lyre.
81:3 Blow the ram's-horn at the new moon, * and at the full moon, the day of our feast.
81:4 For this is a statute for Israel, * a law of the God of Jacob.
81:5 He laid it as a solemn charge upon Joseph, * when he came out of the land of Egypt.
81:6 I heard an unfamiliar voice saying, *"I eased his shoulder from the burden; his hands were set free from bearing the load."
81:7 You called on me in trouble, and I saved you; * I answered you from the secret place of thunder and tested you at the waters of Meribah.
81:8 Hear, O my people, and I will admonish you: * O Israel, if you would but listen to me!
81:9 There shall be no strange god among you; * you shall not worship a foreign god.
81:10 I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt and said, *"Open your mouth wide, and I will fill it."
81:11 And yet my people did not hear my voice, * and Israel would not obey me.
81:12 So I gave them over to the stubbornness of their hearts, * to follow their own devices.
81:13 Oh, that my people would listen to me! * that Israel would walk in my ways!
81:14 I should soon subdue their enemies * and turn my hand against their foes.
81:15 Those who hate the LORD would cringe before him, * and their punishment would last for ever.
81:16 But Israel would I feed with the finest wheat * and satisfy him with honey from the rock.
text.KJV:
81:1 To the chief Musician upon Gittith, A Psalm of Asaph. Sing aloud unto God our strength: make a joyful noise unto the God of Jacob.
81:2 Take a psalm, and bring hither the timbrel, the pleasant harp with the psaltery.
81:3 Blow up the trumpet in the new moon, in the time appointed, on our solemn feast day.
81:4 For this was a statute for Israel, and a law of the God of Jacob.
81:5 This he ordained in Joseph for a testimony, when he went out through the land of Egypt: where I heard a language that I understood not.
81:6 I removed his shoulder from the burden: his hands were delivered from the pots.
81:7 Thou calledst in trouble, and I delivered thee; I answered thee in the secret place of thunder: I proved thee at the waters of Meribah. Selah.
81:8 Hear, O my people, and I will testify unto thee: O Israel, if thou wilt hearken unto me;
81:9 There shall no strange god be in thee; neither shalt thou worship any strange god.
81:10 I am the Lord thy God, which brought thee out of the land of Egypt: open thy mouth wide, and I will fill it.
81:11 But my people would not hearken to my voice; and Israel would none of me.
81:12 So I gave them up unto their own hearts’ lust: and they walked in their own counsels.
81:13 Oh that my people had hearkened unto me, and Israel had walked in my ways!
81:14 I should soon have subdued their enemies, and turned my hand against their adversaries.
81:15 The haters of the Lord should have submitted themselves unto him: but their time should have endured for ever.
81:16 He should have fed them also with the finest of the wheat: and with honey out of the rock should I have satisfied thee.
text.JPS1985:
81:1 For the leader; on the gittith. Of Asaph.
81:2 Sing joyously to God, our strength; raise a shout for the God of Jacob.
81:3 Take up the song, sound the timbrel, the melodious lyre and harp.
81:4 Blow the horn on the new moon, on the full moon for our feast day.
81:5 For it is a law for Israel, a ruling of the God of Jacob;
81:6 He imposed it as a decree upon Joseph when he went forth from the land of Egypt; I heard a language that I knew not.
81:7 I relieved his shoulder of the burden, his hands were freed from the basket.
81:8 In distress you called and I rescued you; I answered you from the secret place of thunder I tested you at the waters of Meribah. Selah.
81:9 Hear, My people, and I will admonish you; Israel, if you would but listen to Me!
81:10 You shall have no foreign god, you shall not bow to an alien god.
81:11 I the LORD am your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt; open your mouth wide and I will fill it.
81:12 But My people would not listen to Me, Israel would not obey Me.
81:13 So I let them go after their willful heart that they might follow their own devices.
81:14 If only My people would listen to Me, if Israel would follow My paths,
81:15 then would I subdue their enemies at once, strike their foes again and again.
81:16 Those who hate the LORD shall cower before Him; their doom shall be eternal.
81:17 He fed them the finest wheat; I sated you with honey from the rock.
text.Grail1963:
.2 Ring out your joy to God our strength,
shout in triumph to the God of Jacob.

the sweetsounding harp and the lute;
4 blow the trumpet at the new moon,
when the moon is full, on our feast.

a command of the God of Jacob.

when he went out against the land of Egypt.

A voice I did not know said to me:
7 "I freed your shoulder from the burden;
your hands were freed from the load.

I answered, concealed in the storm cloud;
at the waters of Meribah I tested you.
9 Listen, my people, to my warning.
O Israel, if only you would heed!
10 Let there be no foreign god among you.
no worship of an alien god.
11 I am the Lord your God,
who brought you from the land of Egypt.
Open wide your mouth and I will fill it.
12 But my people did not heed my voice
and Israel would not obey,
13 so I left them in their stubbornness of heart
to follow their own designs.

text.Rotherham:
81:1 Shout ye for joy, unto God our strength, Sound the note of triumph, to the God of Jacob;
81:2 Raise a melody, and strike the timbrel, The lyre so sweet, with the harp:
81:3 Blow, at the new moon, the horn, At the full moon, for the day of our sacred festival:
81:4 For, a statute to Israel, it is, A regulation, by the God of Jacob;
81:5 A testimony in Joseph, he appointed it, When he went forth over the land of Egypt: A language I liked not, used I to hear;
81:6 I took away, from the burden, his shoulder, his hands, from the clay, were set free.
81:7 In distress, thou didst cry, and I delivered thee,—I answered thee, within a hiding-place of thunder, I proved thee by the waters of Meribah. [Selah.]
81:8 Hear, O my people, and I will adjure thee, O Israel, if thou wilt hearken unto me!
81:9 There shall not be, within thee, a foreign GOD,—Neither shalt thou bow down to a strange GOD:
81:10 I, Yahweh, am thy God, Who brought thee up out of the land of Egypt,—Open wide thy mouth, that I may fill it.
81:11 But my people, hearkened not, unto my voice, Even, Israel, inclined not unto me.
81:12 So then I let them go on in the stubbornness of their own heart,[—] They might walk in their own counsels!
81:13 If, my people, were hearkening unto me, [If,] Israel, in my ways, would walk,
81:14 Right soon, their foes, would I subdue, And, against their adversaries, would I turn my hand:
81:15 The haters of Yahweh, should come cringing unto him, Then let their own good time be age-abiding!
81:16 Then would he feed them from the marrow of the wheat, Yea, out of the rock—with honey, would I satisfy thee.
text.NABRE:
81:1 For the leader; “upon the gittith.” Of Asaph.
81:2 I Sing joyfully to God our strength; raise loud shouts to the God of Jacob!
81:3 Take up a melody, sound the timbrel, the pleasant lyre with a harp.
81:4 Blow the shofar at the new moon, at the full moon, on our solemn feast.
81:5 For this is a law for Israel, an edict of the God of Jacob,
81:6 He made it a decree for Joseph when he came out of the land of Egypt.
81:7 II I heard a tongue I did not know: “I removed his shoulder from the burden; his hands moved away from the basket.
81:8 In distress you called and I rescued you; I answered you in secret with thunder; At the waters of Meribah I tested you:
81:9 ‘Listen, my people, I will testify against you Selah If only you will listen to me, Israel!
81:10 There shall be no foreign god among you; you shall not bow down to an alien god.
81:11 ‘I am the Lord your God, who brought you up from the land of Egypt. Open wide your mouth that I may fill it.’
81:12 But my people did not listen to my words; Israel would not submit to me.
81:13 So I thrust them away to the hardness of their heart; ‘Let them walk in their own machinations.’
81:14 O that my people would listen to me, that Israel would walk in my ways,
81:15 In a moment I would humble their foes, and turn back my hand against their oppressors.
81:16 Those who hate the Lord will try flattering him, but their fate is fixed forever.
81:17 But Israel I will feed with the finest wheat, I will satisfy them with honey from the rock.”
text.DRB:
81:1 A Psalm to Asaph. God stood in the assembly of gods, and in the midst he judgeth gods.
81:2 How long judge ye iniquity: and accept ye the persons of sinners.
81:3 Judge ye for the needy and the pupil: justify ye the humble, and the poor.
81:4 Take away the poor: and deliver the needy from the hand of the sinner.
81:5 They knew not, neither did they understand, they walk in darkness: all the foundations of the earth shall be moved.
81:6 Isaid: You are gods, and the sons of the highest all.
81:7 But you shall die as men: and fall as one of the Princes.
81:8 Arise, o God, judge the earth: because thou shalt inherit in all the Gentiles.

text.NRSV:
82:1 God has taken his place in the divine council; in the midst of the gods he holds judgment:
82:2 "How long will you judge unjustly and show partiality to the wicked? Selah
82:3 Give justice to the weak and the orphan; maintain the right of the lowly and the destitute.
82:4 Rescue the weak and the needy; deliver them from the hand of the wicked."
82:5 They have neither knowledge nor understanding, they walk around in darkness; all the foundations of the earth are shaken.
82:6 I say,"You are gods, children of the Most High, all of you;
82:7 nevertheless, you shall die like mortals, and fall like any prince."
82:8 Rise up, O God, judge the earth; for all the nations belong to you!
text.Coverdale:
82:1 God takes his stand in the council of heaven; * he gives judgment in the midst of the gods:
82:2 "How long will you judge unjustly, * and show favor to the wicked?
82:3 Save the weak and the orphan; * defend the humble and needy;
82:4 Rescue the weak and the poor; * deliver them from the power of the wicked.
82:5 They do not know, neither do they understand; they go about in darkness; * all the foundations of the earth are shaken.
82:6 Now I say to you,'You are gods, * and all of you children of the Most High;
82:7 Nevertheless, you shall die like mortals, * and fall like any prince.'"
82:8 Arise, O God, and rule the earth, * for you shall take all nations for your own.
text.KJV:
82:1 A Psalm of Asaph. God standeth in the congregation of the mighty; he judgeth among the gods.
82:2 How long will ye judge unjustly, and accept the persons of the wicked? Selah.
82:3 Defend the poor and fatherless: do justice to the afflicted and needy.
82:4 Deliver the poor and needy: rid them out of the hand of the wicked.
82:5 They know not, neither will they understand; they walk on in darkness: all the foundations of the earth are out of course.
82:6 I have said, Ye are gods; and all of you are children of the most High.
82:7 But ye shall die like men, and fall like one of the princes.
82:8 Arise, O God, judge the earth: for thou shalt inherit all nations.
text.JPS1985:
82:1 A psalm of Asaph. God stands in the divine assembly; among the divine beings He pronounces judgment.
82:2 How long will you judge perversely, showing favor to the wicked? Selah.
82:3 Judge the wretched and the orphan, vindicate the lowly and the poor,
82:4 rescue the wretched and the needy; save them from the hand of the wicked.
82:5 They neither know nor understand, they go about in darkness; all the foundations of the earth totter.
82:6 I had taken you for divine beings, sons of the Most High, all of you;
82:7 but you shall die as men do, fall like any prince.
82:8 Arise, O God, judge the earth, for all the nations are Your possession.

text.Rotherham:
82:1 God, hath taken his place in the august assembly, In the midst of the gods, will he judge.
82:2 How long will ye judge perversely, And, the countenances of the lawless, uplift? [Selah.]
82:3 Vindicate the weak and the fatherless, The oppressed and the poor, see righted;
82:4 Deliver the weak and the needy, Out of the hand of the lawless, make rescue.
82:5 They know not, neither can they perceive, In darkness, they wander, All the foundations of the earth do shake.
82:6 I, said, Gods, ye are, Yea, sons of the Highest, are ye all;
82:7 But indeed, like the earth-born, shall ye die! And, like one of the princes, shall ye fall!
82:8 Arise! O God, judge thou the earth, For, thou, wilt inherit all the nations.
text.NABRE:
82:1 A psalm of Asaph. God takes a stand in the divine council, gives judgment in the midst of the gods.
82:2 “How long will you judge unjustly and favor the cause of the wicked? Selah
82:3 “Defend the lowly and fatherless; render justice to the afflicted and needy.
82:4 Rescue the lowly and poor; deliver them from the hand of the wicked.”
82:5 II The gods neither know nor understand, wandering about in darkness, and all the world’s foundations shake.
82:6 I declare: “Gods though you be, offspring of the Most High all of you,
82:7 Yet like any mortal you shall die; like any prince you shall fall.”
82:8 Arise, O God, judge the earth, for yours are all the nations.
text.DRB:
82:1 A Canticle of Psalm to Asaph.
82:2 O God who shall be like to thee? Hold not thy peace, neither be thou appeased, o God:
82:3 for behold thine enemies have made a sound: they that hate thee have lifted up the head.
82:4 They have taken malignant counsel upon thy people: and they have devised against thy Saints.
82:5 They have said: Come, and let us destroy them out of the nation: and let the name of Israel be remembered no more.
82:6 Because they have devised with one consent: they have together made a covenant against thee.
82:7 The tabernacles of the Idumeians, and the Ismahelites, Moab, and the Agarens.
82:8 Gebal, and Ammon, Amalec: the foreigners, with the inhabitants of Tyre.
82:9 Yea and Assur also is come with them: they are made an aid to the children of Lot.
82:10 Do to them as to Madian, and Sisara: as to Jabin in the torrent Cisson.
82:11 They perished in Endor: they were made as the dung of the earth.
82:12 Put their Princes as Oreb, and Zeb, and Zebee, and Salmana. All their Princes:
82:13 which have said: Let us possess the Sanctuary of God for an inheritance.
82:14 My God put them as a wheel: and as stubble before the face of the wind.
82:15 Even as fire that burneth a wood: & as a flame that burneth the mountains.
82:16 So shalt thou pursue them in thy tempest: and in thy wrath thou shalt trouble them.
82:17 Fill their faces with ignominy: and they will seek thy name, o Lord.
82:18 Let them be ashamed, and troubled for ever and ever: and let them be confounded, and perish.
82:19 And let them know that Lord is thy name, thou only the Highest in all the earth.

text.NRSV:
83:1 O God, do not keep silence; do not hold your peace or be still, O God!
83:2 Even now your enemies are in tumult; those who hate you have raised their heads.
83:3 They lay crafty plans against your people; they consult together against those you protect.
83:4 They say,"Come, let us wipe them out as a nation; let the name of Israel be remembered no more."
83:5 They conspire with one accord; against you they make a covenant—
83:6 the tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites, Moab and the Hagrites,
83:7 Gebal and Ammon and Amalek, Philistia with the inhabitants of Tyre;
83:8 Assyria also has joined them; they are the strong arm of the children of Lot. Selah
83:9 Do to them as you did to Midian, as to Sisera and Jabin at the Wadi Kishon,
83:10 who were destroyed at En-dor, who became dung for the ground.
83:11 Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb, all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna,
83:12 who said,"Let us take the pastures of God for our own possession."
83:13 O my God, make them like whirling dust, like chaff before the wind.
83:14 As fire consumes the forest, as the flame sets the mountains ablaze,
83:15 so pursue them with your tempest and terrify them with your hurricane.
83:16 Fill their faces with shame, so that they may seek your name, O Lord.
83:17 Let them be put to shame and dismayed forever; let them perish in disgrace.
83:18 Let them know that you alone, whose name is the Lord, are the Most High over all the earth.
text.Coverdale:
83:1 O God, do not be silent; * do not keep still nor hold your peace, O God;
83:2 For your enemies are in tumult, * and those who hate you have lifted up their heads.
83:3 They take secret counsel against your people * and plot against those whom you protect.
83:4 They have said,"Come, let us wipe them out from among the nations; * let the name of Israel be remembered no more."
83:5 They have conspired together; * they have made an alliance against you:
83:6 The tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites; * the Moabites and the Hagarenes;
83:7 Gebal, and Ammon, and Amalek; * the Philistines and those who dwell in Tyre.
83:8 The Assyrians also have joined them, * and have come to help the people of Lot.
83:9 Do to them as you did to Midian, * to Sisera, and to Jabin at the river of Kishon:
83:10 They were destroyed at Endor; * they became like dung upon the ground.
83:11 Make their leaders like Oreb and Zeëb, * and all their commanders like Zebah and Zalmunna,
83:12 Who said,"Let us take for ourselves * the fields of God as our possession."
83:13 O my God, make them like whirling dust * and like chaff before the wind;
83:14 Like fire that burns down a forest, * like the flame that sets mountains ablaze.
83:15 Drive them with your tempest * and terrify them with your storm;
83:16 Cover their faces with shame, O LORD, * that they may seek your Name.
83:17 Let them be disgraced and terrified for ever; * let them be put to confusion and perish.
83:18 Let them know that you, whose Name is YAHWEH, * you alone are the Most High over all the earth.
text.KJV:
83:1 A Song or Psalm of Asaph. Keep not thou silence, O God: hold not thy peace, and be not still, O God.
83:2 For, lo, thine enemies make a tumult: and they that hate thee have lifted up the head.
83:3 They have taken crafty counsel against thy people, and consulted against thy hidden ones.
83:4 They have said, Come, and let us cut them off from being a nation; that the name of Israel may be no more in remembrance.
83:5 For they have consulted together with one consent: they are confederate against thee:
83:6 The tabernacles of Edom, and the Ishmaelites; of Moab, and the Hagarenes;
83:7 Gebal, and Ammon, and Amalek; the Philistines with the inhabitants of Tyre;
83:8 Assur also is joined with them: they have holpen the children of Lot. Selah.
83:9 Do unto them as unto the Midianites; as to Sisera, as to Jabin, at the brook of Kison:
83:10 Which perished at En–dor: they became as dung for the earth.
83:11 Make their nobles like Oreb, and like Zeeb: yea, all their princes as Zebah, and as Zalmunna:
83:12 Who said, Let us take to ourselves the houses of God in possession.
83:13 O my God, make them like a wheel; as the stubble before the wind.
83:14 As the fire burneth a wood, and as the flame setteth the mountains on fire;
83:15 So persecute them with thy tempest, and make them afraid with thy storm.
83:16 Fill their faces with shame; that they may seek thy name, O Lord.
83:17 Let them be confounded and troubled for ever; yea, let them be put to shame, and perish:
83:18 That men may know that thou, whose name alone is Jehovah, art the most high over all the earth.
text.JPS1985:
83:1 A song, a psalm of Asaph.
83:2 O God, do not be silent; do not hold aloof; do not be quiet, O God!
83:3 For Your enemies rage, Your foes assert themselves.
83:4 They plot craftily against Your people, take counsel against Your treasured ones.
83:5 They say, “Let us wipe them out as a nation; Israel’s name will be mentioned no more.”
83:6 Unanimous in their counsel they have made an alliance against You—
83:7 the clans of Edom and the Ishmaelites, Moab and the Hagrites,
83:8 Gebal, Ammon, and Amalek, Philistia with the inhabitants of Tyre;
83:9 Assyria too joins forces with them; they give support to the sons of Lot. Selah.
83:10 Deal with them as You did with Midian, with Sisera, with Jabin, at the brook Kishon—
83:11 who were destroyed at En-dor, who became dung for the field.
83:12 Treat their great men like Oreb and Zeeb, all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna,
83:13 who said, “Let us take the meadows of God as our possession.”
83:14 O my God, make them like thistledown, like stubble driven by the wind.
83:15 As a fire burns a forest, as flames scorch the hills,
83:16 pursue them with Your tempest, terrify them with Your storm.
83:17 Cover their faces with shame so that they seek Your name, O LORD.
83:18 May they be frustrated and terrified, disgraced and doomed forever.
83:19 May they know that Your name, Yours alone, is the LORD, supreme over all the earth.

83:6 They scheme with one mind, they have entered into a covenant against you:

83:8 Gebal, Ammon, and Amalek, Philistia and the inhabitants of Tyre.

83:11 Those destroyed at Endor, who became dung for the ground.
83:12 Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb, all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna,
83:13 Who made a plan together, “Let us take for ourselves the pastures of God.”
83:14 My God, make them like tumbleweed, into chaff flying before the wind.
83:15 As a fire raging through a forest, a flame setting mountains ablaze,
83:16 Pursue them with your tempest; terrify them with your storm-wind.
83:17 Cover their faces with shame, till they seek your name, Lord.
83:18 Let them be ashamed and terrified forever; let them perish in disgrace.
83:19 Let them know that your name is Lord, you alone are the Most High over all the earth.
text.DRB:
83:1 Unto the end, for wine presses, to the children of Core, a Psalm.
83:2 How beloved are thy tabernacles, o Lord of hosts!
83:3 my soul coveteth and fainteth unto the courts of our Lord. My heart and my flesh have rejoiced toward the living God.
83:4 For the sparrow also hath found her an house: and the turtle-dove a nest for herself, where she may lay her young ones. Thine altars, o Lord of hosts: my King, and my God.
83:5 Blessed are they that dwell in thy house, o Lord: for ever and ever they shall praise thee.
83:6 Blessed is the man, whose help is from thee: he hath disposed ascensions in his heart,
83:7 in the vale of tears, in the place which he hath appointed.
83:8 For the law-giver shall give blessing, they shall go from virtue into virtue: the God of gods shall be seen in Sion.
83:9 Lord God of hosts hear my prayer: receive with thine ear, o God of Jacob.
83:10 Behold, o God our protector: and look upon the face of thy Christ.
83:11 Because better is one day in thy courts, above thousands. I have chosen to be an abject in the house of my God: rather than to dwell in the tabernacles of sinners.
83:12 Because God loveth mercy and truth: our Lord will give grace and glory.
83:13 He will not deprive them of good things, that walk in innocency: o Lord of hosts, blessed is the man that hopeth in thee.

text.NRSV:
84:1 How lovely is your dwelling place, O Lord of hosts!
84:2 My soul longs, indeed it faints for the courts of the Lord; my heart and my flesh sing for joy to the living God.
84:3 Even the sparrow finds a home, and the swallow a nest for herself, where she may lay her young, at your altars, O Lord of hosts, my King and my God.
84:4 Happy are those who live in your house, ever singing your praise. Selah
84:5 Happy are those whose strength is in you, in whose heart are the highways to Zion.
84:6 As they go through the valley of Baca they make it a place of springs; the early rain also covers it with pools.
84:7 They go from strength to strength; the God of gods will be seen in Zion.
84:8 O Lord God of hosts, hear my prayer; give ear, O God of Jacob! Selah
84:9 Behold our shield, O God; look on the face of your anointed.
84:10 For a day in your courts is better than a thousand elsewhere. I would rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God than live in the tents of wickedness.
84:11 For the Lord God is a sun and shield; he bestows favor and honor. No good thing does the Lord withhold from those who walk uprightly.
84:12 O Lord of hosts, happy is everyone who trusts in you.
text.Coverdale:
84:1 How dear to me is your dwelling, O LORD of hosts! * My soul has a desire and longing for the courts of the LORD; my heart and my flesh rejoice in the living God.
84:2 The sparrow has found her a house and the swallow a nest where she may lay her young; * by the side of your altars, O LORD of hosts, my King and my God.
84:3 Happy are they who dwell in your house! * they will always be praising you.
84:4 Happy are the people whose strength is in you! * whose hearts are set on the pilgrims'way.
84:5 Those who go through the desolate valley will find it a place of springs, * for the early rains have covered it with pools of water.
84:6 They will climb from height to height, * and the God of gods will reveal himself in Zion.
84:7 LORD God of hosts, hear my prayer; * hearken, O God of Jacob.
84:8 Behold our defender, O God; * and look upon the face of your Anointed.
84:9 For one day in your courts is better than a thousand in my own room, * and to stand at the threshold of the house of my God than to dwell in the tents of the wicked.
84:10 For the LORD God is both sun and shield; * he will give grace and glory;
84:11 No good thing will the LORD withhold * from those who walk with integrity.
84:12 O LORD of hosts, * happy are they who put their trust in you!
text.KJV:
84:1 To the chief Musician upon Gittith, A Psalm for the sons of Korah. How amiable are thy tabernacles, O Lord of hosts!
84:2 My soul longeth, yea, even fainteth for the courts of the Lord: my heart and my flesh crieth out for the living God.
84:3 Yea, the sparrow hath found an house, and the swallow a nest for herself, where she may lay her young, even thine altars, O Lord of hosts, my King, and my God.
84:4 Blessed are they that dwell in thy house: they will be still praising thee. Selah.
84:5 Blessed is the man whose strength is in thee; in whose heart are the ways of them.
84:6 Who passing through the valley of Baca make it a well; the rain also filleth the pools.
84:7 They go from strength to strength, every one of them in Zion appeareth before God.
84:8 O Lord God of hosts, hear my prayer: give ear, O God of Jacob. Selah.
84:9 Behold, O God our shield, and look upon the face of thine anointed.
84:10 For a day in thy courts is better than a thousand. I had rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God, than to dwell in the tents of wickedness.
84:11 For the Lord God is a sun and shield: the Lord will give grace and glory: no good thing will he withhold from them that walk uprightly.
84:12 O Lord of hosts, blessed is the man that trusteth in thee.
text.JPS1985:
84:1 For the leader; on the gittith. Of the Korahites. A psalm.
84:2 How lovely is Your dwelling-place, O LORD of hosts.
84:3 I long, I yearn for the courts of the LORD; my body and soul shout for joy to the living God.
84:4 Even the sparrow has found a home, and the swallow a nest for herself in which to set her young, near Your altar, O LORD of hosts, my king and my God.
84:5 Happy are those who dwell in Your house; they forever praise You. Selah.
84:6 Happy is the man who finds refuge in You, whose mind is on the [pilgrim] highways.
84:7 They pass through the Valley of Baca, regarding it as a place of springs, as if the early rain had covered it with blessing.
84:8 They go from rampart to rampart, appearing before God in Zion.
84:9 O LORD, God of hosts, hear my prayer; give ear, O God of Jacob. Selah.
84:10 O God, behold our shield, look upon the face of Your anointed.
84:11 Better one day in Your courts than a thousand [anywhere else]; I would rather stand at the threshold of God’s house than dwell in the tents of the wicked.
84:12 For the LORD God is sun and shield; the LORD bestows grace and glory; He does not withhold His bounty from those who live without blame.
84:13 O LORD of hosts, happy is the man who trusts in You.
text.Grail1963:
.2 How lovely is your dwelling place,
Lord, God of hosts.

is yearning for the courts of the Lord.
My heart and my soul ring out their joy
to God, the living God.

4 The sparrow herself finds a home

Lord of hosts, my king and my God.

they will see the God of gods in Zion.

The threshold of the house of God

text.Rotherham:
84:1 How lovely are thy habitations, O Yahweh of hosts!
84:2 My soul, longeth—yea even languisheth—for the courts of Yahweh,—My heart and my flesh, shout aloud for a Living GOD.
84:3 Even the sparrow, hath found a home, And, the swallow, a nest for herself, where she hath laid her young, Thine altars, O Yahweh of hosts, My king and my God!
84:4 How happy are they who abide in thy house,—Still are they praising thee. [Selah.]
84:5 How happy the men whose strength is in thee, Festive processions are in their heart.
84:6 Passing through the balsam-vale, A place of fountains, they make it, Yea, with blessings, is it covered by the early rain.
84:7 They go from strength to strength, Each one appeareth before God in Zion.
84:8 O Yahweh, God of hosts, hear thou my prayer,—Give hear, thou God of Jacob. [Selah.]
84:9 Our Shield, behold thou, O God, And look upon the face of thine Anointed One.
84:10 For better is a day in thy courts, than a thousand, I choose rather to stand at the threshold in the house of my God, Than to dwell in the tents of lawlessness.
84:11 For, a sun and shield, is Yahweh God,—Grace and glory, will Yahweh give, He will not withhold what is good, from them who walk without blame.
84:12 O Yahweh of hosts! How happy the man who trusteth in thee!
text.NABRE:
84:1 For the leader; “upon the gittith.” A psalm of the Korahites.
84:2 I How lovely your dwelling, O Lord of hosts!
84:3 My soul yearns and pines for the courts of the Lord. My heart and flesh cry out for the living God.
84:4 As the sparrow finds a home and the swallow a nest to settle her young, My home is by your altars, Lord of hosts, my king and my God!
84:5 Blessed are those who dwell in your house! They never cease to praise you. Selah
84:6 II Blessed the man who finds refuge in you, in their hearts are pilgrim roads.
84:7 As they pass through the Baca valley, they find spring water to drink. The early rain covers it with blessings.
84:8 They will go from strength to strength and see the God of gods on Zion.
84:9 III Lord God of hosts, hear my prayer; listen, God of Jacob. Selah
84:10 O God, watch over our shield; look upon the face of your anointed.
84:11 IV Better one day in your courts than a thousand elsewhere. Better the threshold of the house of my God than a home in the tents of the wicked.
84:12 For a sun and shield is the Lord God, bestowing all grace and glory. The Lord withholds no good thing from those who walk without reproach.
84:13 O Lord of hosts, blessed the man who trusts in you!
text.DRB:
84:1 Unto the end, to the children of Core, a Psalm.
84:2 O Lord thou hast blessed thy land: thou hast turned away the captivity of Jacob.
84:3 Thou hast forgiven the iniquity of thy people: thou hast covered all their sins.
84:4 Thou hast mitigated all thy wrath: thou hast turned away from the wrath of thine indignation.
84:5 Convert us, o God our Saviour: and avert thy wrath from us.
84:6 Wilt thou be wrath with us for ever? Or wilt thou extend thy wrath from generation unto generation?
84:7 O God thou being turned shalt quicken us: and thy people shall rejoice in thee.
84:8 Shew us, o Lord, thy mercy and give us thy salvation.
84:9 I will hear what our Lord God will speak in me: because he will speak peace upon his people. And upon his saints: and upon them, that are converted to the heart.
84:10 But yet his salvation is nigh to them that fear him: that glory may inhabit in our land.
84:11 Mercy and truth have met each other: justice and peace have kissed.
84:12 Truth is risen out of the earth: and justice hath looked down from heaven.
84:13 For our Lord certes will give benignity: and our land shall give her fruit.
84:14 Justice shall walk before him: and shall set his steps in the way.

text.NRSV:
85:1 Lord, you were favorable to your land; you restored the fortunes of Jacob.
85:2 You forgave the iniquity of your people; you pardoned all their sin. Selah
85:3 You withdrew all your wrath; you turned from your hot anger.
85:4 Restore us again, O God of our salvation, and put away your indignation toward us.
85:5 Will you be angry with us forever? Will you prolong your anger to all generations?
85:6 Will you not revive us again, so that your people may rejoice in you?
85:7 Show us your steadfast love, O Lord, and grant us your salvation.
85:8 Let me hear what God the Lord will speak, for he will speak peace to his people, to his faithful, to those who turn to him in their hearts.
85:9 Surely his salvation is at hand for those who fear him, that his glory may dwell in our land.
85:10 Steadfast love and faithfulness will meet; righteousness and peace will kiss each other.
85:11 Faithfulness will spring up from the ground, and righteousness will look down from the sky.
85:12 The Lord will give what is good, and our land will yield its increase.
85:13 Righteousness will go before him, and will make a path for his steps.
text.Coverdale:
85:1 You have been gracious to your land, O LORD, * you have restored the good fortune of Jacob.
85:2 You have forgiven the iniquity of your people * and blotted out all their sins.
85:3 You have withdrawn all your fury * and turned yourself from your wrathful indignation.
85:4 Restore us then, O God our Savior; * let your anger depart from us.
85:5 Will you be displeased with us for ever? * will you prolong your anger from age to age?
85:6 Will you not give us life again, * that your people may rejoice in you?
85:7 Show us your mercy, O LORD, * and grant us your salvation.
85:8 I will listen to what the LORD God is saying, * for he is speaking peace to his faithful people and to those who turn their hearts to him.
85:9 Truly, his salvation is very near to those who fear him, * that his glory may dwell in our land.
85:10 Mercy and truth have met together; * righteousness and peace have kissed each other.
85:11 Truth shall spring up from the earth, * and righteousness shall look down from heaven.
85:12 The LORD will indeed grant prosperity, * and our land will yield its increase.
85:13 Righteousness shall go before him, * and peace shall be a pathway for his feet.
text.KJV:
85:1 To the chief Musician, A Psalm for the sons of Korah. Lord, thou hast been favourable unto thy land: thou hast brought back the captivity of Jacob.
85:2 Thou hast forgiven the iniquity of thy people, thou hast covered all their sin. Selah.
85:3 Thou hast taken away all thy wrath: thou hast turned thyself from the fierceness of thine anger.
85:4 Turn us, O God of our salvation, and cause thine anger toward us to cease.
85:5 Wilt thou be angry with us for ever? wilt thou draw out thine anger to all generations?
85:6 Wilt thou not revive us again: that thy people may rejoice in thee?
85:7 Shew us thy mercy, O Lord, and grant us thy salvation.
85:8 I will hear what God the Lord will speak: for he will speak peace unto his people, and to his saints: but let them not turn again to folly.
85:9 Surely his salvation is nigh them that fear him; that glory may dwell in our land.
85:10 Mercy and truth are met together; righteousness and peace have kissed each other.
85:11 Truth shall spring out of the earth; and righteousness shall look down from heaven.
85:12 Yea, the Lord shall give that which is good; and our land shall yield her increase.
85:13 Righteousness shall go before him; and shall set us in the way of his steps.
text.JPS1985:
85:1 For the leader. Of the Korahites. A psalm.
85:2 O LORD, You will favor Your land, restore Jacob’s fortune;
85:3 You will forgive Your people’s iniquity, pardon all their sins; selah
85:4 You will withdraw all Your anger, turn away from Your rage.
85:5 Turn again, O God, our helper, revoke Your displeasure with us.
85:6 Will You be angry with us forever, prolong Your wrath for all generations?
85:7 Surely You will revive us again, so that Your people may rejoice in You.
85:8 Show us, O LORD, Your faithfulness; grant us Your deliverance.
85:9 Let me hear what God, the LORD, will speak; He will promise well-being to His people, His faithful ones; may they not turn to folly.
85:10 His help is very near those who fear Him, to make His glory dwell in our land.
85:11 Faithfulness and truth meet; justice and well-being kiss.
85:12 Truth springs up from the earth; justice looks down from heaven.
85:13 The LORD also bestows His bounty; our land yields its produce.
85:14 Justice goes before Him as He sets out on His way.

text.Rotherham:
85:1 Thou hast accepted, O Yahweh, thy land, Thou hast brought back the captives of Jacob;
85:2 Thou hast taken away, the iniquity of thy people, Thou hast covered, all their sin. [Selah.]
85:3 Thou hast withdrawn all thine indignation, Thou hast ceased from the glow of thine anger.
85:4 Restore us, O God of our salvation, And take away thy vexation towards us.
85:5 To times age-abiding, wilt thou be angry with us? Wilt thou prolong thine anger, from generation to generation?
85:6 Wilt not, thou thyself, again give us life, That, thy people, may rejoice in thee.
85:7 Show us, O Yahweh, thy lovingkindness, And, thy salvation, wilt thou grant us.
85:8 I will hear, what GOD—Yahweh—will speak,—For he will bespeak prosperity to his people, And to his men of lovingkindness, And to them who return with their heart unto him.
85:9 Surely, near unto them who revere him, is his salvation, That the Glory, may settle down, in our land.
85:10 Lovingkindness and faithfulness, have met together, Righteousness and prosperity, have kissed each other;
85:11 Faithfulness, out of the earth, doth spring forth, And, righteousness, out of the heavens, hath looked down.
85:12 Yahweh himself too, will give us the blessing, And, our land, shall yield her increase.
85:13 Righteousness, before him, shall march long,—That he may make, into a way, the steps of its feet.
text.NABRE:
85:1 For the leader. A psalm of the Korahites.
85:2 I You once favored, Lord, your land, restored the captives of Jacob.
85:3 You forgave the guilt of your people, pardoned all their sins. Selah
85:4 You withdrew all your wrath, turned back from your burning anger.
85:5 II Restore us, God of our salvation; let go of your displeasure with us.
85:6 Will you be angry with us forever, prolong your anger for all generations?
85:7 Certainly you will again restore our life, that your people may rejoice in you.
85:8 Show us, Lord, your mercy; grant us your salvation.
85:9 III I will listen for what God, the Lord, has to say; surely he will speak of peace To his people and to his faithful. May they not turn to foolishness!
85:10 Near indeed is his salvation for those who fear him; glory will dwell in our land.
85:11 Love and truth will meet; justice and peace will kiss.
85:12 Truth will spring from the earth; justice will look down from heaven.
85:13 Yes, the Lord will grant his bounty; our land will yield its produce.
85:14 Justice will march before him, and make a way for his footsteps.
text.DRB:
85:1 Aprayer to David himself. Incline thine ear, o Lord, & hear me: because I am needy and poor.
85:2 Keep my soul, because I am holy: save thy servant my God, that hopeth in thee.
85:3 Have mercy on me, o Lord, because I have cried to thee all the day:
85:4 make joyful the soul of thy servant, because to thee, o Lord, have I lifted up my soul.
85:5 Because thou, o Lord, art sweet, and mild: and of much mercy to all that invocate thee.
85:6 Receive, o Lord, my prayer with thine ears: and attend to the voice of my petition.
85:7 In the day of my tribulation I have called to thee: because thou hast heard me.
85:8 There is not the like to thee amongst gods, o Lord: and there is not according to thy works.
85:9 All nations whatsoever thou hast made, shall come, and shall adore before thee, o Lord: and they shall glorify thy name.
85:10 Because thou art great and doing marvelous things: thou only art God.
85:11 Conduct me, o Lord, in thy way, and I will walk in thy truth: let my heart rejoice that it may fear thy name.
85:12 I will confess to thee, o Lord my God, with all my heart, and will glorify thy name for ever.
85:13 Because thy mercy is great upon me: & thou hast delivered my soul out of the lower hell.
85:14 O God the wicked are risen up upon me, and the synagogue of the mighty have sought my soul: and they have not set thee in their sight.
85:15 And thou, o Lord, the God of compassion and merciful, patient, and of much mercy, and true.
85:16 Have respect to me and have mercy on me, give thine empire to thy servant: and save the son of thy handmaid.
85:17 Make with me a sign unto God, that they may see which hate me, and may be confounded: because thou o Lord hast holpen me.

text.NRSV:
86:1 Incline your ear, O Lord, and answer me, for I am poor and needy.
86:2 Preserve my life, for I am devoted to you; save your servant who trusts in you. You are my God;
86:3 be gracious to me, O Lord, for to you do I cry all day long.
86:4 Gladden the soul of your servant, for to you, O Lord, I lift up my soul.
86:5 For you, O Lord, are good and forgiving, abounding in steadfast love to all who call on you.
86:6 Give ear, O Lord, to my prayer; listen to my cry of supplication.
86:7 In the day of my trouble I call on you, for you will answer me.
86:8 There is none like you among the gods, O Lord, nor are there any works like yours.
86:9 All the nations you have made shall come and bow down before you, O Lord, and shall glorify your name.
86:10 For you are great and do wondrous things; you alone are God.
86:11 Teach me your way, O Lord, that I may walk in your truth; give me an undivided heart to revere your name.
86:12 I give thanks to you, O Lord my God, with my whole heart, and I will glorify your name forever.
86:13 For great is your steadfast love toward me; you have delivered my soul from the depths of Sheol.
86:14 O God, the insolent rise up against me; a band of ruffians seeks my life, and they do not set you before them.
86:15 But you, O Lord, are a God merciful and gracious, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness.
86:16 Turn to me and be gracious to me; give your strength to your servant; save the child of your serving girl.
86:17 Show me a sign of your favor, so that those who hate me may see it and be put to shame, because you, Lord, have helped me and comforted me.
text.Coverdale:
86:1 Bow down your ear, O LORD, and answer me, * for I am poor and in misery.
86:2 Keep watch over my life, for I am faithful; * save your servant who puts his trust in you.
86:3 Be merciful to me, O LORD, for you are my God; * I call upon you all the day long.
86:4 Gladden the soul of your servant, * for to you, O LORD, I lift up my soul.
86:5 For you, O LORD, are good and forgiving, * and great is your love toward all who call upon you.
86:6 Give ear, O LORD, to my prayer, * and attend to the voice of my supplications.
86:7 In the time of my trouble I will call upon you, * for you will answer me.
86:8 Among the gods there is none like you, O LORD, * nor anything like your works.
86:9 All the nations you have made will come and worship you, O LORD, * and glorify your Name.
86:10 For you are great; you do wondrous things; * and you alone are God.
86:11 Teach me your way, O LORD, and I will walk in your truth; * knit my heart to you that I may fear your Name.
86:12 I will thank you, O LORD my God, with all my heart, * and glorify your Name for evermore.
86:13 For great is your love toward me; * you have delivered me from the nethermost Pit.
86:14 The arrogant rise up against me, O God, and a band of violent men seeks my life; * they have not set you before their eyes.
86:15 But you, O LORD, are gracious and full of compassion, * slow to anger, and full of kindness and truth.
86:16 Turn to me and have mercy upon me; * give your strength to your servant; and save the child of your handmaid.
86:17 Show me a sign of your favor, so that those who hate me may see it and be ashamed; * because you, O LORD, have helped me and comforted me.
text.KJV:
86:1 A Prayer of David. Bow down thine ear, O Lord, hear me: for I am poor and needy.
86:2 Preserve my soul; for I am holy: O thou my God, save thy servant that trusteth in thee.
86:3 Be merciful unto me, O Lord: for I cry unto thee daily.
86:4 Rejoice the soul of thy servant: for unto thee, O Lord, do I lift up my soul.
86:5 For thou, Lord, art good, and ready to forgive; and plenteous in mercy unto all them that call upon thee.
86:6 Give ear, O Lord, unto my prayer; and attend to the voice of my supplications.
86:7 In the day of my trouble I will call upon thee: for thou wilt answer me.
86:8 Among the gods there is none like unto thee, O Lord; neither are there any works like unto thy works.
86:9 All nations whom thou hast made shall come and worship before thee, O Lord; and shall glorify thy name.
86:10 For thou art great, and doest wondrous things: thou art God alone.
86:11 Teach me thy way, O Lord; I will walk in thy truth: unite my heart to fear thy name.
86:12 I will praise thee, O Lord my God, with all my heart: and I will glorify thy name for evermore.
86:13 For great is thy mercy toward me: and thou hast delivered my soul from the lowest hell.
86:14 O God, the proud are risen against me, and the assemblies of violent men have sought after my soul; and have not set thee before them.
86:15 But thou, O Lord, art a God full of compassion, and gracious, longsuffering, and plenteous in mercy and truth.
86:16 O turn unto me, and have mercy upon me; give thy strength unto thy servant, and save the son of thine handmaid.
86:17 Shew me a token for good; that they which hate me may see it, and be ashamed: because thou, Lord, hast holpen me, and comforted me.
text.JPS1985:
86:1 A prayer of David. Incline Your ear, O LORD, answer me, for I am poor and needy.
86:2 Preserve my life, for I am steadfast; O You, my God, deliver Your servant who trusts in You.
86:3 Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I call to You all day long;
86:4 bring joy to Your servant’s life, for on You, Lord, I set my hope.
86:5 For You, Lord, are good and forgiving, abounding in steadfast love to all who call on You.
86:6 Give ear, O LORD, to my prayer; heed my plea for mercy.
86:7 In my time of trouble I call You, for You will answer me.
86:8 There is none like You among the gods, O Lord, and there are no deeds like Yours.
86:9 All the nations You have made will come to bow down before You, O Lord, and they will pay honor to Your name.
86:10 For You are great and perform wonders; You alone are God.
86:11 Teach me Your way, O LORD; I will walk in Your truth; let my heart be undivided in reverence for Your name.
86:12 I will praise You, O Lord, my God, with all my heart and pay honor to Your name forever.
86:13 For Your steadfast love toward me is great; You have saved me from the depths of Sheol.
86:14 O God, arrogant men have risen against me; a band of ruthless men seek my life; they are not mindful of You.
86:15 But You, O Lord, are a God compassionate and merciful, slow to anger, abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness.
86:16 Turn to me and have mercy on me; grant Your strength to Your servant and deliver the son of Your maidservant.
86:17 Show me a sign of Your favor, that my enemies may see and be frustrated because You, O LORD, have given me aid and comfort.
text.Grail1963:

text.Rotherham:
86:1 Bow down, O Yahweh, thine ear—answer me, For, oppressed and needy, am I;
86:2 O guard my life, For, a man of lovingkindness, am I,—Save thy servant, O thou my God, [Thy servant] who trusteth in thee;
86:3 Show me favour, O My Lord, For, unto thee, do I cry, all the day;
86:4 Rejoice the soul of thy servant, For, unto thee, O My Lord, my soul, do I lift.
86:5 For, thou, O My Lord, art good and forgiving, And abundant in lovingkindness, to all who call upon thee.
86:6 Give ear, O Yahweh, unto my prayer, And attend unto the voice of my supplications.
86:7 In the day of my distress, will I call upon thee, For thou wilt answer me.
86:8 There is none like unto thee, among the gods, O My Lord, And nothing like thy works.
86:9 All nations whom thou hast made, Shall come in and bow down before thee, O My Lord, That they may glorify thy Name.
86:10 For great thou art, and doest wondrous things, Thou, O God, of thyself alone.
86:11 Point out to me, O Yahweh, thy way, I will walk steadfastly in thy truth, My heart will rejoice to revere thy Name.
86:12 I will give thee thanks, Adonay, my God, with all my heart, And will glorify thy Name unto times age-abiding.
86:13 For, thy lovingkindness, is great towards me, And thou hast rescued my soul from Hades beneath.
86:14 O God, the insolent, have arisen against me, And, the assembly of tyrants, have sought my life, And have not set thee before them.
86:15 But, thou, O My Lord, art, A God of compassion and favour, Slow to anger, and abundant in lovingkindness a faithfulness.
86:16 Turn thou unto me, and show me favour,—Give thy strength to thy servant, And save the son of thy handmaid.
86:17 Perform with me a token for good,—That they who hate me may see and be ashamed, In that, thou, Yahweh, hast helped me and comforted me.
text.NABRE:
86:1 A prayer of David. Incline your ear, Lord, and answer me, for I am poor and oppressed.
86:2 Preserve my life, for I am devoted; save your servant who trusts in you. You are my God;
86:3 be gracious to me, Lord; to you I call all the day.
86:4 Gladden the soul of your servant; to you, Lord, I lift up my soul.
86:5 Lord, you are good and forgiving, most merciful to all who call on you.
86:6 Lord, hear my prayer; listen to my cry for help.
86:7 On the day of my distress I call to you, for you will answer me.
86:8 II None among the gods can equal you, O Lord; nor can their deeds compare to yours.
86:9 All the nations you have made shall come to bow before you, Lord, and give honor to your name.
86:10 For you are great and do wondrous deeds; and you alone are God.
86:11 III Teach me, Lord, your way that I may walk in your truth, single-hearted and revering your name.
86:12 I will praise you with all my heart, glorify your name forever, Lord my God.
86:13 Your mercy to me is great; you have rescued me from the depths of Sheol.
86:14 O God, the arrogant have risen against me; a ruthless band has sought my life; to you they pay no heed.
86:15 But you, Lord, are a compassionate and gracious God, slow to anger, abounding in mercy and truth.
86:16 Turn to me, be gracious to me; give your strength to your servant; save the son of your handmaid.
86:17 Give me a sign of your favor: make my enemies see, to their confusion, that you, Lord, help and comfort me.
text.DRB:
86:1 To the children of Core, a Psalm of Canticle. The foundations thereof in the holy mountains:
86:2 our Lord loveth the gates of Sion above all the Tabernacles of Jacob.
86:3 Glorious things are said of thee, o city of God.
86:4 I will be mindful of Rahab, and Babylon knowing me. Behold the foreigners, and Tyre, & the people of the Æthiopians, these were there.
86:5 Shall it not be said of Sion: Man and man, is born in her; and the Highest himself founded her?
86:6 Our Lord will declare in scriptures of peoples, and of Princes: of those that have been in her.
86:7 The habitation in thee, is as it were of all rejoicing.

text.NRSV:
87:1 On the holy mount stands the city he founded;
87:2 the Lord loves the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.
87:3 Glorious things are spoken of you, O city of God. Selah
87:4 Among those who know me I mention Rahab and Babylon; Philistia too, and Tyre, with Ethiopia—"This one was born there,"they say.
87:5 And of Zion it shall be said,"This one and that one were born in it"; for the Most High himself will establish it.
87:6 The Lord records, as he registers the peoples,"This one was born there."Selah
87:7 Singers and dancers alike say,"All my springs are in you."
text.Coverdale:
87:1 On the holy mountain stands the city he has founded; * the LORD loves the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.
87:2 Glorious things are spoken of you, * O city of our God.
87:3 I count Egypt and Babylon among those who know me; * behold Philistia, Tyre, and Ethiopia: in Zion were they born.
87:4 Of Zion it shall be said,"Everyone was born in her, * and the Most High himself shall sustain her."
87:5 The LORD will record as he enrolls the peoples, *"These also were born there."
87:6 The singers and the dancers will say, *"All my fresh springs are in you."
text.KJV:
87:1 A Psalm or Song for the sons of Korah. His foundation is in the holy mountains.
87:2 The Lord loveth the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.
87:3 Glorious things are spoken of thee, O city of God. Selah.
87:4 I will make mention of Rahab and Babylon to them that know me: behold Philistia, and Tyre, with Ethiopia; this man was born there.
87:5 And of Zion it shall be said, This and that man was born in her: and the highest himself shall establish her.
87:6 The Lord shall count, when he writeth up the people, that this man was born there. Selah.
87:7 As well the singers as the players on instruments shall be there: all my springs are in thee.
text.JPS1985:
87:1 Of the Korahites. A psalm. A song.
87:2 The LORD loves the gates of Zion, His foundation on the holy mountains, more than all the dwellings of Jacob.
87:3 Glorious things are spoken of you, O city of God. Selah.
87:4 I mention Rahab and Babylon among those who acknowledge Me; Philistia, and Tyre, and Cush—each was born there.
87:5 Indeed, it shall be said of Zion, “Every man was born there.” He, the Most High, will preserve it.
87:6 The LORD will inscribe in the register of peoples that each was born there. Selah.
87:7 Singers and dancers alike [will say]: “All my roots are in You.”
text.Grail1963:

text.Rotherham:
87:1 His foundation, is in the holy mountains:
87:2 Yahweh loveth the gates of Zion, More than all the dwellings of Jacob.
87:3 Glorious things, are to be spoken of thee, O city of God. [Selah.]
87:4 I will mention Rahab and Babylon, to them who know me—Lo! Philistia and Tyre with Ethiopia, This one was born there.
87:5 But, of Zion, it shall be said—This man and that were born in her, And the Highest himself shall establish her.
87:6 Yahweh, will record, when he enrolleth the peoples, This one was born there. [Selah.]
87:7 As well the singers as the flute-players [are saying],—All my springs, are in thee!
text.NABRE:
87:1 A psalm of the Korahites. A song. His foundation is on holy mountains,
87:2 The Lord loves the gates of Zion more than any dwelling in Jacob.
87:3 Glorious things are said of you, O city of God! Selah
87:4 II Rahab and Babylon I count among those who know me. See, Philistia and Tyre, with Ethiopia, “This one was born there.”
87:5 And of Zion it will be said: “Each one was born in it.” The Most High will establish it;
87:6 the Lord notes in the register of the peoples: “This one was born there.” Selah
87:7 So singers and dancers: “All my springs are in you.”
text.DRB:
87:1 A Canticle of a Psalm, to the children of Core, unto the end, for Maheleth to answer, of understanding to Eman the Ezrahite.
87:2 O Lord the God of my salvation: in the day have I cried, and in the night before thee.
87:3 Let my prayer enter in thy sight: incline thine ear to my petition.
87:4 Because my soul is replenished with evils: & my life hath approached to hell.
87:5 I am accounted with them that descend into the lake. I am become as a man without help,
87:6 free among the dead, as the wounded sleeping in the sepulchres, of whom thou art mindful no more: and they are cast off from thy hand.
87:7 They have put me in the lower lake: in the dark places, and in the shadow of death.
87:8 Thy fury is confirmed upon me: and all thy waves thou hast brought in upon me.
87:9 Thou hast made my familiars far from me: they have put me abomination to themselves. I was delivered and came not forth:
87:10 mine eyes languished for poverty. I cried to thee o Lord all the day: I stretched out my hands to thee.
87:11 Wilt thou do marvels to the dead: or shall physicians raise to life, and they confess to thee?
87:12 Shall any in the sepulchre declare thy mercy, and thy truth in perdition?
87:13 Shall thy marvelous works be known in darkness: and thy justice in the land of oblivion.
87:14 And I o Lord have cried to thee: and in the morning shall my prayer prevent thee.
87:15 Why doest thou, o Lord, reject my prayer: turnest away thy face from me?
87:16 I am poor, and in labours from my youth: and being exalted, humbled, and troubled.
87:17 Thy wraths have passed upon me: and thy terrors have troubled me.
87:18 They have compassed me as water all the day: they compassed me together.
87:19 Thou hast made friend, and neighbour far from me and my familiars because of misery.

text.NRSV:
88:1 O Lord, God of my salvation, when, at night, I cry out in your presence,
88:2 let my prayer come before you; incline your ear to my cry.
88:3 For my soul is full of troubles, and my life draws near to Sheol.
88:4 I am counted among those who go down to the Pit; I am like those who have no help,
88:5 like those forsaken among the dead, like the slain that lie in the grave, like those whom you remember no more, for they are cut off from your hand.
88:6 You have put me in the depths of the Pit, in the regions dark and deep.
88:7 Your wrath lies heavy upon me, and you overwhelm me with all your waves. Selah
88:8 You have caused my companions to shun me; you have made me a thing of horror to them. I am shut in so that I cannot escape;
88:9 my eye grows dim through sorrow. Every day I call on you, O Lord; I spread out my hands to you.
88:10 Do you work wonders for the dead? Do the shades rise up to praise you? Selah
88:11 Is your steadfast love declared in the grave, or your faithfulness in Abaddon?
88:12 Are your wonders known in the darkness, or your saving help in the land of forgetfulness?
88:13 But I, O Lord, cry out to you; in the morning my prayer comes before you.
88:14 O Lord, why do you cast me off? Why do you hide your face from me?
88:15 Wretched and close to death from my youth up, I suffer your terrors; I am desperate.
88:16 Your wrath has swept over me; your dread assaults destroy me.
88:17 They surround me like a flood all day long; from all sides they close in on me.
88:18 You have caused friend and neighbor to shun me; my companions are in darkness.
text.Coverdale:
88:1 O LORD, my God, my Savior, * by day and night I cry to you.
88:2 Let my prayer enter into your presence; * incline your ear to my lamentation.
88:3 For I am full of trouble; * my life is at the brink of the grave.
88:4 I am counted among those who go down to the Pit; * I have become like one who has no strength;
88:5 Lost among the dead, * like the slain who lie in the grave,
88:6 Whom you remember no more, * for they are cut off from your hand.
88:7 You have laid me in the depths of the Pit, * in dark places, and in the abyss.
88:8 Your anger weighs upon me heavily, * and all your great waves overwhelm me.
88:9 You have put my friends far from me; you have made me to be abhorred by them; * I am in prison and cannot get free.
88:10 My sight has failed me because of trouble; * LORD, I have called upon you daily; I have stretched out my hands to you.
88:11 Do you work wonders for the dead? * will those who have died stand up and give you thanks?
88:12 Will your loving-kindness be declared in the grave? * your faithfulness in the land of destruction?
88:13 Will your wonders be known in the dark? * or your righteousness in the country where all is forgotten?
88:14 But as for me, O LORD, I cry to you for help; * in the morning my prayer comes before you.
88:15 LORD, why have you rejected me? * why have you hidden your face from me?
88:16 Ever since my youth, I have been wretched and at the point of death; * I have borne your terrors with a troubled mind.
88:17 Your blazing anger has swept over me; * your terrors have destroyed me;
88:18 They surround me all day long like a flood; * they encompass me on every side.
88:19 My friend and my neighbor you have put away from me, * and darkness is my only companion.
text.KJV:
88:1 A Song or Psalm for the sons of Korah, to the chief Musician upon Mahalath Leannoth, Maschil of Heman the Ezrahite. O Lord God of my salvation, I have cried day and night before thee:
88:2 Let my prayer come before thee: incline thine ear unto my cry;
88:3 For my soul is full of troubles: and my life draweth nigh unto the grave.
88:4 I am counted with them that go down into the pit: I am as a man that hath no strength:
88:5 Free among the dead, like the slain that lie in the grave, whom thou rememberest no more: and they are cut off from thy hand.
88:6 Thou hast laid me in the lowest pit, in darkness, in the deeps.
88:7 Thy wrath lieth hard upon me, and thou hast afflicted me with all thy waves. Selah.
88:8 Thou hast put away mine acquaintance far from me; thou hast made me an abomination unto them: I am shut up, and I cannot come forth.
88:9 Mine eye mourneth by reason of affliction: Lord, I have called daily upon thee, I have stretched out my hands unto thee.
88:10 Wilt thou shew wonders to the dead? shall the dead arise and praise thee? Selah.
88:11 Shall thy lovingkindness be declared in the grave? or thy faithfulness in destruction?
88:12 Shall thy wonders be known in the dark? and thy righteousness in the land of forgetfulness?
88:13 But unto thee have I cried, O Lord; and in the morning shall my prayer prevent thee.
88:14 Lord, why castest thou off my soul? why hidest thou thy face from me?
88:15 I am afflicted and ready to die from my youth up: while I suffer thy terrors I am distracted.
88:16 Thy fierce wrath goeth over me; thy terrors have cut me off.
88:17 They came round about me daily like water; they compassed me about together.
88:18 Lover and friend hast thou put far from me, and mine acquaintance into darkness.
text.JPS1985:
88:1 A song. A psalm of the Korahites. For the leader; on maḥalath leannoth. A maskil of Heman the Ezrahite.
88:2 O LORD, God of my deliverance, when I cry out in the night before You,
88:3 let my prayer reach You; incline Your ear to my cry.
88:4 For I am sated with misfortune; I am at the brink of Sheol.
88:5 I am numbered with those who go down to the Pit; I am a helpless man
88:6 abandoned among the dead, like bodies lying in the grave of whom You are mindful no more, and who are cut off from Your care.
88:7 You have put me at the bottom of the Pit, in the darkest places, in the depths.
88:8 Your fury lies heavy upon me; You afflict me with all Your breakers. Selah.
88:9 You make my companions shun me; You make me abhorrent to them; I am shut in and do not go out.
88:10 My eyes pine away from affliction; I call to You, O LORD, each day; I stretch out my hands to You.
88:11 Do You work wonders for the dead? Do the shades rise to praise You? Selah.
88:12 Is Your faithful care recounted in the grave, Your constancy in the place of perdition?
88:13 Are Your wonders made known in the netherworld, Your beneficent deeds in the land of oblivion?
88:14 As for me, I cry out to You, O LORD; each morning my prayer greets You.
88:15 Why, O LORD, do You reject me, do You hide Your face from me?
88:16 From my youth I have been afflicted and near death; I suffer Your terrors wherever I turn.
88:17 Your fury overwhelms me; Your terrors destroy me.
88:18 They swirl about me like water all day long; they encircle me on every side.
88:19 You have put friend and neighbor far from me and my companions out of my sight.
text.Grail1963:
.2 Lord my God, I call for help by day;
I cry at night before you.
3 Let my prayer come into your presence.
O turn your ear to my cry.
4 For my soul is filled with evils;
my life is on the brink of the grave.
5 I am reckoned as one in the tomb;
I have reached the end of my strength,
6 Like one alone among the dead,
like the slain lying in their graves,
like those you remember no more,
cut off, as they are, from your hand.

7 You have laid me in the depths of the tomb,
in places that are dark, in the depths.
8 Your anger weighs down upon me;
I am drowned beneath your waves.
9 You have taken away my friends
and made me hateful in their sight.
Imprisoned, I cannot escape;
10 my eyes are sunken with grief.
I call to you, Lord, all the day long;
to you I stretch out my hands.
11 Will you work your wonders for the dead?
Will the shades stand and praise you?
12 Will your love be told in the grave
or your faithfulness among the dead?
13 Will your wonders be known in the dark
or your justice in the land of oblivion?
14 As for me, Lord, I call to you for help;
in the morning my prayer comes before you.
15 Lord, why do you reject me?
Why do you hide your face?
16 Wretched, close to death from my youth,
I have borne your trials; I am numb.
17 Your fury has swept down upon me;
