text.NABRE:
92:1 A psalm. A sabbath song.
92:2 I It is good to give thanks to the Lord, to sing praise to your name, Most High,
92:3 To proclaim your love at daybreak, your faithfulness in the night,
92:4 With the ten-stringed harp, with melody upon the lyre.
92:5 For you make me jubilant, Lord, by your deeds; at the works of your hands I shout for joy.
92:6 II How great are your works, Lord! How profound your designs!
92:7 A senseless person cannot know this; a fool cannot comprehend.
92:8 Though the wicked flourish like grass and all sinners thrive, They are destined for eternal destruction;
92:9 but you, Lord, are forever on high.
92:10 Indeed your enemies, Lord, indeed your enemies shall perish; all sinners shall be scattered.
92:11 III You have given me the strength of a wild ox; you have poured rich oil upon me.
92:12 My eyes look with glee on my wicked enemies; my ears shall hear what happens to my wicked foes.
92:13 The just shall flourish like the palm tree, shall grow like a cedar of Lebanon.
92:14 Planted in the house of the Lord, they shall flourish in the courts of our God.
92:15 They shall bear fruit even in old age, they will stay fresh and green,
92:16 To proclaim: “The Lord is just; my rock, in whom there is no wrong.”
text.DRB:
92:1 Praise of Canticle to David himself in the day before the sabbath, when the earth was founded. Our Lord hath reigned, he hath put on beauty: our Lord hath put on strength, and hath girded himself. For he hath established the round world, which shall not be moved.
92:2 Thy seat is prepared from that time: thou art from everlasting.
92:3 The rivers o Lord have lifted up: the rivers have lifted up their voice. The rivers have lifted up their waves,
92:4 above the voices of many waters. The surges of the sea are marvelous, marvelous is our Lord on high.
92:5 Thy testimonies are made credible exceedingly: holiness becometh thy house, o Lord for length of days.

text.NRSV:
93:1 The Lord is king, he is robed in majesty; the Lord is robed, he is girded with strength. He has established the world; it shall never be moved;
93:2 your throne is established from of old; you are from everlasting.
93:3 The floods have lifted up, O Lord, the floods have lifted up their voice; the floods lift up their roaring.
93:4 More majestic than the thunders of mighty waters, more majestic than the waves of the sea, majestic on high is the Lord!
93:5 Your decrees are very sure; holiness befits your house, O Lord, forevermore.
text.Coverdale:
93:1 The LORD is King; he has put on splendid apparel; * the LORD has put on his apparel and girded himself with strength.
93:2 He has made the whole world so sure * that it cannot be moved;
93:3 Ever since the world began, your throne has been established; * you are from everlasting.
93:4 The waters have lifted up, O LORD, the waters have lifted up their voice; * the waters have lifted up their pounding waves.
93:5 Mightier than the sound of many waters, mightier than the breakers of the sea, * mightier is the LORD who dwells on high.
93:6 Your testimonies are very sure, * and holiness adorns your house, O LORD, for ever and for evermore.
text.KJV:
93:1 The Lord reigneth, he is clothed with majesty; the Lord is clothed with strength, wherewith he hath girded himself: the world also is stablished, that it cannot be moved.
93:2 Thy throne is established of old: thou art from everlasting.
93:3 The floods have lifted up, O Lord, the floods have lifted up their voice; the floods lift up their waves.
93:4 The Lord on high is mightier than the noise of many waters, yea, than the mighty waves of the sea.
93:5 Thy testimonies are very sure: holiness becometh thine house, O Lord, for ever.
text.JPS1985:
93:1 The LORD is king, He is robed in grandeur; the LORD is robed, He is girded with strength. The world stands firm; it cannot be shaken.
93:2 Your throne stands firm from of old; from eternity You have existed.
93:3 The ocean sounds, O LORD, the ocean sounds its thunder, the ocean sounds its pounding.
93:4 Above the thunder of the mighty waters, more majestic than the breakers of the sea is the LORD, majestic on high.
93:5 Your decrees are indeed enduring; holiness befits Your house, O LORD, for all times.
text.Grail1963:
1 The Lord is king, with majesty enrobed;
the Lord has robed himself with might,
he has girded himself with power.
The world you made firm, not to be moved;
2 your throne has stood firm from of old.
From all eternity, O Lord, you are.
3 The waters have lifted up, O Lord,
the waters have lifted up their voice,
the waters have lifted up their thunder.
4 Greater than the roar of mighty waters
more glorious than the surgings of the sea,
the Lord is glorious on high.
5 Truly your decrees are to be trusted.
Holiness is fitting to your house,
O Lord, until the end of time.
text.Rotherham:
93:1 Yahweh, hath become king, With majesty, is he clothed,—Clothed is Yahweh, With strength, hath he girded himself, Surely he hath fixed the world, It shall not be shaken.
93:2 Established hath been thy throne from of old, From age-past times, thou art.
93:3 The floods have lifted up, O Yahweh, The floods have lifted up—their voice, The floods have lifted up—their dashing waves.
93:4 With the sounds of many waters, Majestic are the breakers of the sea, Majestic on high, is Yahweh.
93:5 Thy testimonies, are strongly confirmed, To thy house, befitting is holiness, O Yahweh—to length of days.
text.NABRE:
93:1 God Is a Mighty King The Lord is king, robed with majesty; the Lord is robed, girded with might. The world will surely stand in place, never to be moved.
93:2 Your throne stands firm from of old; you are from everlasting.
93:3 The flood has raised up, Lord; the flood has raised up its roar; the flood has raised its pounding waves.
93:4 More powerful than the roar of many waters, more powerful than the breakers of the sea, powerful in the heavens is the Lord.
93:5 Your decrees are firmly established; holiness befits your house, Lord, for all the length of days.
text.DRB:
93:1 To David himself, in the fourth of the sabbath. Our Lord God of revenges: the God of revenges hath done freely.
93:2 Be exalted thou that judgest the earth: render retribution to the proud.
93:3 How long shall sinners, o Lord: how long shall sinners glory?
93:4 Shall they utter, and speak iniquity? Shall all they speak that work injustice?
93:5 Thy people, o Lord, they have humbled: and thine inheritance they have vexed.
93:6 The widow, and the stranger they have slain: and the pupils they have killed.
93:7 And they have said: The Lord shall not see, neither shall the God of Jacob understand.
93:8 Understand ye foolish in the people: and ye fools be wise at sometime.
93:9 He that planted the ear, shall he not hear? Or he that made the eye doth he not consider?
93:10 He that chastiseth nations, shall he not rebuke: he that teacheth man knowledge?
93:11 Our Lord knoweth the cogitations of men: that they be vain.
93:12 Blessed is the man whom thou shalt instruct, o Lord, and shalt teach out of thy law.
93:13 That thou mayst give him quietness from the evil days: till a pit be digged for the sinner.
93:14 Because our Lord will not reject his people: and his inheritance he will not forsake.
93:15 Until justice be turned into judgment: and they who are near it, are all that are right of heart.
93:16 Who shall rise for me against the malignant? Or who shall stand with me against them that work iniquity?
93:17 But that our Lord hath holpen me: within very little my soul had dwelt in hell.
93:18 If I said: My foot is moved: thy mercy, o Lord, did help me.
93:19 According to the multitude of my sorrows in my heart: thy consolations have made my soul joyful.
93:20 Doth the seat of iniquity cleave to thee: which makest labour in precept?
93:21 They will hunt after the soul of the just: and will condemn innocent blood.
93:22 And our Lord became my refuge: and my God the help of my hope.
93:23 And he will repay them their iniquity: and in their malice he will destroy them: the Lord our God will destroy them.

text.NRSV:
94:1 O Lord, you God of vengeance, you God of vengeance, shine forth!
94:2 Rise up, O judge of the earth; give to the proud what they deserve!
94:3 O Lord, how long shall the wicked, how long shall the wicked exult?
94:4 They pour out their arrogant words; all the evildoers boast.
94:5 They crush your people, O Lord, and afflict your heritage.
94:6 They kill the widow and the stranger, they murder the orphan,
94:7 and they say,"The Lord does not see; the God of Jacob does not perceive."
94:8 Understand, O dullest of the people; fools, when will you be wise?
94:9 He who planted the ear, does he not hear? He who formed the eye, does he not see?
94:10 He who disciplines the nations, he who teaches knowledge to humankind, does he not chastise?
94:11 The Lord knows our thoughts, that they are but an empty breath.
94:12 Happy are those whom you discipline, O Lord, and whom you teach out of your law,
94:13 giving them respite from days of trouble, until a pit is dug for the wicked.
94:14 For the Lord will not forsake his people; he will not abandon his heritage;
94:15 for justice will return to the righteous, and all the upright in heart will follow it.
94:16 Who rises up for me against the wicked? Who stands up for me against evildoers?
94:17 If the Lord had not been my help, my soul would soon have lived in the land of silence.
94:18 When I thought,"My foot is slipping,"your steadfast love, O Lord, held me up.
94:19 When the cares of my heart are many, your consolations cheer my soul.
94:20 Can wicked rulers be allied with you, those who contrive mischief by statute?
94:21 They band together against the life of the righteous, and condemn the innocent to death.
94:22 But the Lord has become my stronghold, and my God the rock of my refuge.
94:23 He will repay them for their iniquity and wipe them out for their wickedness; the Lord our God will wipe them out.
text.Coverdale:
94:1 O LORD God of vengeance, * O God of vengeance, show yourself.
94:2 Rise up, O Judge of the world; * give the arrogant their just deserts.
94:3 How long shall the wicked, O LORD, * how long shall the wicked triumph?
94:4 They bluster in their insolence; * all evildoers are full of boasting.
94:5 They crush your people, O LORD, * and afflict your chosen nation.
94:6 They murder the widow and the stranger * and put the orphans to death.
94:7 Yet they say,"The LORD does not see, * the God of Jacob takes no notice."
94:8 Consider well, you dullards among the people; * when will you fools understand?
94:9 He that planted the ear, does he not hear? * he that formed the eye, does he not see?
94:10 He who admonishes the nations, will he not punish? * he who teaches all the world, has he no knowledge?
94:11 The LORD knows our human thoughts; * how like a puff of wind they are.
94:12 Happy are they whom you instruct, O Lord! * whom you teach out of your law;
94:13 To give them rest in evil days, * until a pit is dug for the wicked.
94:14 For the LORD will not abandon his people, * nor will he forsake his own.
94:15 For judgment will again be just, * and all the true of heart will follow it.
94:16 Who rose up for me against the wicked? * who took my part against the evildoers?
94:17 If the LORD had not come to my help, * I should soon have dwelt in the land of silence.
94:18 As often as I said,"My foot has slipped,"* your love, O LORD, upheld me.
94:19 When many cares fill my mind, * your consolations cheer my soul.
94:20 Can a corrupt tribunal have any part with you, * one which frames evil into law?
94:21 They conspire against the life of the just * and condemn the innocent to death.
94:22 But the LORD has become my stronghold, * and my God the rock of my trust.
94:23 He will turn their wickedness back upon them and destroy them in their own malice; * the LORD our God will destroy them.
text.KJV:
94:1 O Lord God, to whom vengeance belongeth; O God, to whom vengeance belongeth, shew thyself.
94:2 Lift up thyself, thou judge of the earth: render a reward to the proud.
94:3 Lord, how long shall the wicked, how long shall the wicked triumph?
94:4 How long shall they utter and speak hard things? and all the workers of iniquity boast themselves?
94:5 They break in pieces thy people, O Lord, and afflict thine heritage.
94:6 They slay the widow and the stranger, and murder the fatherless.
94:7 Yet they say, The Lord shall not see, neither shall the God of Jacob regard it.
94:8 Understand, ye brutish among the people: and ye fools, when will ye be wise?
94:9 He that planted the ear, shall he not hear? he that formed the eye, shall he not see?
94:10 He that chastiseth the heathen, shall not he correct? he that teacheth man knowledge, shall not he know?
94:11 The Lord knoweth the thoughts of man, that they are vanity.
94:12 Blessed is the man whom thou chastenest, O Lord, and teachest him out of thy law;
94:13 That thou mayest give him rest from the days of adversity, until the pit be digged for the wicked.
94:14 For the Lord will not cast off his people, neither will he forsake his inheritance.
94:15 But judgment shall return unto righteousness: and all the upright in heart shall follow it.
94:16 Who will rise up for me against the evildoers? or who will stand up for me against the workers of iniquity?
94:17 Unless the Lord had been my help, my soul had almost dwelt in silence.
94:18 When I said, My foot slippeth; thy mercy, O Lord, held me up.
94:19 In the multitude of my thoughts within me thy comforts delight my soul.
94:20 Shall the throne of iniquity have fellowship with thee, which frameth mischief by a law?
94:21 They gather themselves together against the soul of the righteous, and condemn the innocent blood.
94:22 But the Lord is my defence; and my God is the rock of my refuge.
94:23 And he shall bring upon them their own iniquity, and shall cut them off in their own wickedness; yea, the Lord our God shall cut them off.
text.JPS1985:
94:1 God of retribution, LORD, God of retribution, appear!
94:2 Rise up, judge of the earth, give the arrogant their deserts!
94:3 How long shall the wicked, O LORD, how long shall the wicked exult,
94:4 shall they utter insolent speech, shall all evildoers vaunt themselves?
94:5 They crush Your people, O LORD, they afflict Your very own;
94:6 they kill the widow and the stranger; they murder the fatherless,
94:7 thinking, “The LORD does not see it, the God of Jacob does not pay heed.”
94:8 Take heed, you most brutish people; fools, when will you get wisdom?
94:9 Shall He who implants the ear not hear, He who forms the eye not see?
94:10 Shall He who disciplines nations not punish, He who instructs men in knowledge?
94:11 The LORD knows the designs of men to be futile.
94:12 Happy is the man whom You discipline, O LORD, the man You instruct in Your teaching,
94:13 to give him tranquillity in times of misfortune, until a pit be dug for the wicked.
94:14 For the LORD will not forsake His people; He will not abandon His very own.
94:15 Judgment shall again accord with justice and all the upright shall rally to it.
94:16 Who will take my part against evil men? Who will stand up for me against wrongdoers?
94:17 Were not the LORD my help, I should soon dwell in silence.
94:18 When I think my foot has given way, Your faithfulness, O LORD, supports me.
94:19 When I am filled with cares, Your assurance soothes my soul.
94:20 Shall the seat of injustice be Your partner, that frames mischief by statute?
94:21 They band together to do away with the righteous; they condemn the innocent to death.
94:22 But the LORD is my haven; my God is my sheltering rock.
94:23 He will make their evil recoil upon them, annihilate them through their own wickedness; the LORD our God will annihilate them.
text.Grail1963:
1 O Lord, avenging God,
avenging God, appear!
2 Judge of the earth, arise,
give the proud what they deserve!
3 How long, O Lord, shall the wicked,
how long shall the wicked triumph?
4 They bluster with arrogant speech;
the evildoers boast to each other.
5 They crush your people, Lord,
they afflict the ones you have chosen
6 They kill the widow and the stranger
and murder the fatherless child.
7 And they say: "The Lord does not see;
the God of Jacob pays no heed."
8 Mark this, most senseless of people;
fools, when will you understand?
9 Can he who made the ear, not hear?
Can he who formed the eye, not see?
10 Will he who trains nations not punish?
Will he who instructs us not have knowledge?
11 (The Lord knows the thoughts of men.
He knows they are no more than a breath.)
12 Happy the man whom you teach, O Lord,
whom you train by means of your law;
13 to him you give peace in evil days
while the pit is being dug for the wicked.
14 The Lord will not abandon his people
nor forsake those who are his own;
15 for judgment shall again be just
and all true hearts shall uphold it.
16 Who will stand up for me against the wicked?
Who will defend me from those who do evil?
17 If the Lord were not to help me,
I would soon go down into the silence.
18 When I think: "I have lost my foothold";
your mercy, Lord, holds me up.
19 When cares increase in my heart
your consolation calms my soul.
20 Can judges who do evil be your friends?
They do injustice under cover of law;
21 they attack the life of the just
and condemn innocent blood.

text.Rotherham:
94:1 O GOD of avenging—Yahweh, GOD of avenging, shine forth:
94:2 Lift up thyself, O judge of the earth, Render a recompense unto the proud.
94:3 How long shall the lawless, O Yahweh, How long shall the lawless exult?
94:4 They pour forth [words], they speak arrogantly, All the workers of iniquity, do boast:
94:5 Thy people, O Yahweh, they will crush, And, thine inheritance, tread down;
94:6 The widow and sojourner, they will slay, And, the fatherless, murder.
94:7 Yet have they said—Yah, doth not see, The God of Jacob, doth not understand.
94:8 Understand, ye brutish among the people, And, ye dullards, when will ye show discretion?
94:9 He that planteth the ear, shall he not hear? Or, that fashioneth the eye, shall he not have power to see?
94:10 He that correcteth nations, shall he not reprove? He that teacheth man knowledge!
94:11 Yahweh, knoweth the plans of men, That, they, are a breath!
94:12 How happy the man whom thou correctest, O Yah! And whom, out of thy law, thou instructest!
94:13 That thou mayest give him rest from the days of misfortune, Until there be digged—for the lawless one—a pit.
94:14 For Yahweh, will not abandon, his people, And, his inheritance, will he not forsake;
94:15 For, unto righteousness, shall the judicial sentence return, Then shall follow it—all the upright in heart.
94:16 Who will rise up for me, against the evil-doers? Who will make a stand for me, against the workers of iniquity?
94:17 If, Yahweh, had not been a help to me, Soon had sunk into silence—my soul!
94:18 If I say, My foot, hath slipped, Thy lovingkindness, O Yahweh, supporteth me.
94:19 In the multitude of my cares within me, Thy consolations, delight my soul.
94:20 Shall the throne that inflicteth ruin, have fellowship with thee, That frameth oppression, by statute?
94:21 They make an attack on the life of the righteous one,—And, innocent blood, they condemn.
94:22 But, Yahweh, hath become for me a high tower, And my God, my rock of refuge.
94:23 Thus hath he brought back on them their iniquity, And, by their own wickedness, will he destroy them, Destroy them, will Yahweh our God.
text.NABRE:
94:1 Lord, avenging God, avenging God, shine forth!
94:2 Rise up, O judge of the earth; give the proud what they deserve!
94:3 II How long, Lord, shall the wicked, how long shall the wicked glory?
94:4 How long will they mouth haughty speeches, go on boasting, all these evildoers?
94:5 They crush your people, Lord, torment your very own.
94:6 They kill the widow and alien; the orphan they murder.
94:7 They say, “The Lord does not see; the God of Jacob takes no notice.”
94:8 III Understand, you stupid people! You fools, when will you be wise?
94:9 Does the one who shaped the ear not hear? The one who formed the eye not see?
94:10 Does the one who guides nations not rebuke? The one who teaches man not have knowledge?
94:11 The Lord knows the plans of man; they are like a fleeting breath.
94:12 IV Blessed the one whom you guide, Lord, whom you teach by your instruction,
94:13 To give rest from evil days, while a pit is being dug for the wicked.
94:14 For the Lord will not forsake his people, nor abandon his inheritance.
94:15 Judgment shall again be just, and all the upright of heart will follow it.
94:16 V Who will rise up for me against the wicked? Who will stand up for me against evildoers?
94:17 If the Lord were not my help, I would long have been silent in the grave.
94:18 When I say, “My foot is slipping,” your mercy, Lord, holds me up.
94:19 When cares increase within me, your comfort gives me joy.
94:20 VI Can unjust judges be your allies, those who create burdens by decree,
94:21 Those who conspire against the just and condemn the innocent to death?
94:22 No, the Lord is my secure height, my God, my rock of refuge,
94:23 Who will turn back their evil upon them and destroy them for their wickedness. Surely the Lord our God will destroy them!
text.DRB:
94:1 Praise of Canticle, to David himself. Come, let us rejoice to our Lord: let us make jubilation to God our saviour.
94:2 Let us prevent his face in confession: and in Psalms let us make jubilation to him.
94:3 Because our Lord is a great God: and a great King above all gods.
94:4 Because in his hand are the ends of the earth: and the heights of the mountains be his.
94:5 Because the sea is his, and he made it: and his hands formed the dry land.
94:6 Come let us adore, and fall down: and weep before our Lord, that made us.
94:7 Because he is the Lord our God, and we the people of his pasture, and the sheep of his hand.
94:8 Today if ye shall hear his voice, harden not your hearts.
94:9 As in the provocation according to the day of the tentation in the desert: where your fathers tempted me, proved me, and saw my works.
94:10 Forty years was I offended with that generation, and said: These always err in heart.
94:11 And these have not known my ways: as I sware in my wrath: if they shall enter into my rest.

text.NRSV:
95:1 O come, let us sing to the Lord; let us make a joyful noise to the rock of our salvation!
95:2 Let us come into his presence with thanksgiving; let us make a joyful noise to him with songs of praise!
95:3 For the Lord is a great God, and a great King above all gods.
95:4 In his hand are the depths of the earth; the heights of the mountains are his also.
95:5 The sea is his, for he made it, and the dry land, which his hands have formed.
95:6 O come, let us worship and bow down, let us kneel before the Lord, our Maker!
95:7 For he is our God, and we are the people of his pasture, and the sheep of his hand. O that today you would listen to his voice!
95:8 Do not harden your hearts, as at Meribah, as on the day at Massah in the wilderness,
95:9 when your ancestors tested me, and put me to the proof, though they had seen my work.
95:10 For forty years I loathed that generation and said,"They are a people whose hearts go astray, and they do not regard my ways."
95:11 Therefore in my anger I swore,"They shall not enter my rest."
text.Coverdale:
95:1 Come, let us sing to the LORD; * let us shout for joy to the Rock of our salvation.
95:2 Let us come before his presence with thanksgiving * and raise a loud shout to him with psalms.
95:3 For the LORD is a great God, * and a great King above all gods.
95:4 In his hand are the caverns of the earth, * and the heights of the hills are his also.
95:5 The sea is his, for he made it, * and his hands have molded the dry land.
95:6 Come, let us bow down, and bend the knee, * and kneel before the LORD our Maker.
95:7 For he is our God, and we are the people of his pasture and the sheep of his hand. * Oh, that today you would hearken to his voice!
95:8 Harden not your hearts, as your forebears did in the wilderness, * at Meribah, and on that day at Massah, when they tempted me.
95:9 They put me to the test, * though they had seen my works.
95:10 Forty years long I detested that generation and said, *"This people are wayward in their hearts; they do not know my ways."
95:11 So I swore in my wrath, *"They shall not enter into my rest."
text.KJV:
95:1 O come, let us sing unto the Lord: let us make a joyful noise to the rock of our salvation.
95:2 Let us come before his presence with thanksgiving, and make a joyful noise unto him with psalms.
95:3 For the Lord is a great God, and a great King above all gods.
95:4 In his hand are the deep places of the earth: the strength of the hills is his also.
95:5 The sea is his, and he made it: and his hands formed the dry land.
95:6 O come, let us worship and bow down: let us kneel before the Lord our maker.
95:7 For he is our God; and we are the people of his pasture, and the sheep of his hand. To day if ye will hear his voice,
95:8 Harden not your heart, as in the provocation, and as in the day of temptation in the wilderness:
95:9 When your fathers tempted me, proved me, and saw my work.
95:10 Forty years long was I grieved with this generation, and said, It is a people that do err in their heart, and they have not known my ways:
95:11 Unto whom I sware in my wrath that they should not enter into my rest.
text.JPS1985:
95:1 Come, let us sing joyously to the LORD, raise a shout for our rock and deliverer;
95:2 let us come into His presence with praise; let us raise a shout for Him in song!
95:3 For the LORD is a great God, the great king of all divine beings.
95:4 In His hand are the depths of the earth; the peaks of the mountains are His.
95:5 His is the sea, He made it; and the land, which His hands fashioned.
95:6 Come, let us bow down and kneel, bend the knee before the LORD our maker,
95:7 for He is our God, and we are the people He tends, the flock in His care. O, if you would but heed His charge this day:
95:8 Do not be stubborn as at Meribah, as on the day of Massah, in the wilderness,
95:9 when your fathers put Me to the test, tried Me, though they had seen My deeds.
95:10 Forty years I was provoked by that generation; I thought, “They are a senseless people; they would not know My ways.”
95:11 Concerning them I swore in anger, “They shall never come to My resting-place! ”
text.Grail1963:

text.Rotherham:
95:1 Come, let us make a joyful noise to Yahweh, Let us shout in triumph, to the rock of our salvation!
95:2 O let us come before his face with thanksgiving, With the sounds of strings, let us shout aloud to him.
95:3 For, a great GOD, is Yahweh, And a great king, above all gods.
95:4 In whose hand, are the hidden recesses of the earth, And, the peaks of the mountains, are his;
95:5 Whose, is the sea, for, he, made it, And, the dry land, his hands, did form.
95:6 Enter! let us bow down, and bend low, Let us kneel, before Yahweh our maker;
95:7 For, he, is our God, And, we, are the people of his pasture, and the flock of his hand? To-day, if, to his voice, ye will hearken,
95:8 Do not harden your heart as at Meribah, As on the day of Massah, in the desert;
95:9 When your fathers, tested me, They proved me, yea they also saw what I could do.
95:10 Forty years, loathed I that generation, So I said—A people going astray in heart, they are, Even they, have not known my ways!
95:11 And I sware in mine anger,—Verily they shall not enter into my rest.
text.NABRE:
95:1 Praise and Obedience I Come, let us sing joyfully to the Lord; cry out to the rock of our salvation.
95:2 Let us come before him with a song of praise, joyfully sing out our psalms.
95:3 For the Lord is the great God, the great king over all gods,
95:4 Whose hand holds the depths of the earth; who owns the tops of the mountains.
95:5 The sea and dry land belong to God, who made them, formed them by hand.
95:6 II Enter, let us bow down in worship; let us kneel before the Lord who made us.
95:7 For he is our God, we are the people he shepherds, the sheep in his hands. III Oh, that today you would hear his voice:
95:8 Do not harden your hearts as at Meribah, as on the day of Massah in the desert.
95:9 There your ancestors tested me; they tried me though they had seen my works.
95:10 Forty years I loathed that generation; I said: “This people’s heart goes astray; they do not know my ways.”
95:11 Therefore I swore in my anger: “They shall never enter my rest.”
text.DRB:
95:1 A Canticle to David himself, when the house was built after the captivity. Sing ye to our Lord a new song: sing to our Lord all the earth.
95:2 Sing ye to our Lord, and bless his name: shew forth his salvation from day to day.
95:3 Shew forth his glory among the Gentiles, his marvelous works in all peoples.
95:4 Because our Lord is great, and exceeding laudable: he is terrible above all gods.
95:5 Because all the gods of the Gentiles are devils, but our Lord made the heavens.
95:6 Confession and beauty in his sight: holiness and magnificence in his sanctification.
95:7 Bring to our Lord ye families of Gentiles, bring ye to our Lord glory and honour:
95:8 bring to our Lord glory unto his name. Take up hosts, and enter into his courts:
95:9 adore ye our Lord in his holy court. Let all the earth be moved before his face:
95:10 Say ye among the Gentiles that our Lord hath reigned. For he hath corrected the round world, which shall not be moved: he will judge peoples in equity.
95:11 Let the heavens be glad, and the earth rejoice, the sea be moved, and the fullness thereof:
95:12 the fields shall be glad, and all things that are in them. Then shall the trees of the woods rejoice
95:13 before the face of our Lord, because he cometh: because he cometh to judge the earth. He will judge the round world in equity, and peoples in his truth.

text.NRSV:
96:1 O sing to the Lord a new song; sing to the Lord, all the earth.
96:2 Sing to the Lord, bless his name; tell of his salvation from day to day.
96:3 Declare his glory among the nations, his marvelous works among all the peoples.
96:4 For great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised; he is to be revered above all gods.
96:5 For all the gods of the peoples are idols, but the Lord made the heavens.
96:6 Honor and majesty are before him; strength and beauty are in his sanctuary.
96:7 Ascribe to the Lord, O families of the peoples, ascribe to the Lord glory and strength.
96:8 Ascribe to the Lord the glory due his name; bring an offering, and come into his courts.
96:9 Worship the Lord in holy splendor; tremble before him, all the earth.
96:10 Say among the nations,"The Lord is king! The world is firmly established; it shall never be moved. He will judge the peoples with equity."
96:11 Let the heavens be glad, and let the earth rejoice; let the sea roar, and all that fills it;
96:12 let the field exult, and everything in it. Then shall all the trees of the forest sing for joy
96:13 before the Lord; for he is coming, for he is coming to judge the earth. He will judge the world with righteousness, and the peoples with his truth.
text.Coverdale:
96:1 Sing to the LORD a new song; * sing to the LORD, all the whole earth.
96:2 Sing to the LORD and bless his Name; * proclaim the good news of his salvation from day to day.
96:3 Declare his glory among the nations * and his wonders among all peoples.
96:4 For great is the LORD and greatly to be praised; * he is more to be feared than all gods.
96:5 As for all the gods of the nations, they are but idols; * but it is the LORD who made the heavens.
96:6 Oh, the majesty and magnificence of his presence! * Oh, the power and the splendor of his sanctuary!
96:7 Ascribe to the LORD, you families of the peoples; * ascribe to the LORD honor and power.
96:8 Ascribe to the LORD the honor due his Name; * bring offerings and come into his courts.
96:9 Worship the LORD in the beauty of holiness; * let the whole earth tremble before him.
96:10 Tell it out among the nations:"The LORD is King! * he has made the world so firm that it cannot be moved; he will judge the peoples with equity."
96:11 Let the heavens rejoice, and let the earth be glad; let the sea thunder and all that is in it; * let the field be joyful and all that is therein.
96:12 Then shall all the trees of the wood shout for joy before the LORD when he comes, * when he comes to judge the earth.
96:13 He will judge the world with righteousness * and the peoples with his truth.
text.KJV:
96:1 O sing unto the Lord a new song: sing unto the Lord, all the earth.
96:2 Sing unto the Lord, bless his name; shew forth his salvation from day to day.
96:3 Declare his glory among the heathen, his wonders among all people.
96:4 For the Lord is great, and greatly to be praised: he is to be feared above all gods.
96:5 For all the gods of the nations are idols: but the Lord made the heavens.
96:6 Honour and majesty are before him: strength and beauty are in his sanctuary.
96:7 Give unto the Lord, O ye kindreds of the people, give unto the Lord glory and strength.
96:8 Give unto the Lord the glory due unto his name: bring an offering, and come into his courts.
96:9 O worship the Lord in the beauty of holiness: fear before him, all the earth.
96:10 Say among the heathen that the Lord reigneth: the world also shall be established that it shall not be moved: he shall judge the people righteously.
96:11 Let the heavens rejoice, and let the earth be glad; let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof.
96:12 Let the field be joyful, and all that is therein: then shall all the trees of the wood rejoice
96:13 Before the Lord: for he cometh, for he cometh to judge the earth: he shall judge the world with righteousness, and the people with his truth.
text.JPS1985:
96:1 Sing to the LORD a new song, sing to the LORD, all the earth.
96:2 Sing to the LORD, bless His name, proclaim His victory day after day.
96:3 Tell of His glory among the nations, His wondrous deeds, among all peoples.
96:4 For the LORD is great and much acclaimed, He is held in awe by all divine beings.
96:5 All the gods of the peoples are mere idols, but the LORD made the heavens.
96:6 Glory and majesty are before Him; strength and splendor are in His temple.
96:7 Ascribe to the LORD, O families of the peoples, ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.
96:8 Ascribe to the LORD the glory of His name, bring tribute and enter His courts.
96:9 Bow down to the LORD majestic in holiness; tremble in His presence, all the earth!
96:10 Declare among the nations, “The LORD is king! ” the world stands firm; it cannot be shaken; He judges the peoples with equity.
96:11 Let the heavens rejoice and the earth exult; let the sea and all within it thunder,
96:12 the fields and everything in them exult; then shall all the trees of the forest shout for joy
96:13 at the presence of the LORD, for He is coming, for He is coming to rule the earth; He will rule the world justly, and its peoples in faithfulness.
text.Grail1963:

sing to the Lord all the earth.

Proclaim his help day by day,
3 tell among the nations his glory
and his wonders among all the peoples.
4 The Lord is great and worthy of praise,
to be feared above all gods;
5 the gods of the heathens are naught.
It was the Lord who made the heavens,
6 his are majesty and state and power

text.DRB:
96:1 To this David, when his land was restored again to him. Our Lord hath reigned, let the earth rejoice: let many Islands be glad.
96:2 Cloud and mist round about him: justice and judgment the correction of his seat.
96:3 Fire shall go before him, and shall inflame his enemies round about.
96:4 His lightnings shined to the round world, the earth saw: and was moved.
96:5 The mountains melted as wax, before the face of our Lord: before the face of our Lord all the earth.
96:6 The heavens have shewed forth his justice: and all peoples have seen his glory.
96:7 Let them all be confounded, that adore sculptiles: and that glory in their idols. Adore him all ye his Angels:
96:8 Sion heard, and was glad. And the daughters of Juda rejoiced, because of thy judgments, o Lord.
96:9 Because thou Lord most high over all the earth: thou art exalted exceedingly above all gods.
96:10 You that love our Lord, hate ye evil, our Lord keepeth the souls of his saints, out of the hand of the sinner he will deliver them.
96:11 Light is risen to the just, and joy to the right of heart.
96:12 Be glad ye just in our Lord: and confess ye to the memory of his sanctification.

text.NRSV:
97:1 The Lord is king! Let the earth rejoice; let the many coastlands be glad!
97:2 Clouds and thick darkness are all around him; righteousness and justice are the foundation of his throne.
97:3 Fire goes before him, and consumes his adversaries on every side.
97:4 His lightnings light up the world; the earth sees and trembles.
97:5 The mountains melt like wax before the Lord, before the Lord of all the earth.
97:6 The heavens proclaim his righteousness; and all the peoples behold his glory.
97:7 All worshipers of images are put to shame, those who make their boast in worthless idols; all gods bow down before him.
97:8 Zion hears and is glad, and the towns of Judah rejoice, because of your judgments, O God.
97:9 For you, O Lord, are most high over all the earth; you are exalted far above all gods.
97:10 The Lord loves those who hate evil; he guards the lives of his faithful; he rescues them from the hand of the wicked.
97:11 Light dawns for the righteous, and joy for the upright in heart.
97:12 Rejoice in the Lord, O you righteous, and give thanks to his holy name!
text.Coverdale:
97:1 The LORD is King; let the earth rejoice; * let the multitude of the isles be glad.
97:2 Clouds and darkness are round about him, * righteousness and justice are the foundations of his throne.
97:3 A fire goes before him * and burns up his enemies on every side.
97:4 His lightnings light up the world; * the earth sees it and is afraid.
97:5 The mountains melt like wax at the presence of the LORD, * at the presence of the Lord of the whole earth.
97:6 The heavens declare his righteousness, * and all the peoples see his glory.
97:7 Confounded be all who worship carved images and delight in false gods! * Bow down before him, all you gods.
97:8 Zion hears and is glad, and the cities of Judah rejoice, * because of your judgments, O LORD.
97:9 For you are the LORD, most high over all the earth; * you are exalted far above all gods.
97:10 The LORD loves those who hate evil; * he preserves the lives of his saints and delivers them from the hand of the wicked.
97:11 Light has sprung up for the righteous, * and joyful gladness for those who are truehearted.
97:12 Rejoice in the LORD, you righteous, * and give thanks to his holy Name.
text.KJV:
97:1 The Lord reigneth; let the earth rejoice; let the multitude of isles be glad thereof.
97:2 Clouds and darkness are round about him: righteousness and judgment are the habitation of his throne.
97:3 A fire goeth before him, and burneth up his enemies round about.
97:4 His lightnings enlightened the world: the earth saw, and trembled.
97:5 The hills melted like wax at the presence of the Lord, at the presence of the Lord of the whole earth.
97:6 The heavens declare his righteousness, and all the people see his glory.
97:7 Confounded be all they that serve graven images, that boast themselves of idols: worship him, all ye gods.
97:8 Zion heard, and was glad; and the daughters of Judah rejoiced because of thy judgments, O Lord.
97:9 For thou, Lord, art high above all the earth: thou art exalted far above all gods.
97:10 Ye that love the Lord, hate evil: he preserveth the souls of his saints; he delivereth them out of the hand of the wicked.
97:11 Light is sown for the righteous, and gladness for the upright in heart.
97:12 Rejoice in the Lord, ye righteous; and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness.
text.JPS1985:
97:1 The LORD is king! Let the earth exult, the many islands rejoice!
97:2 Dense clouds are around Him, righteousness and justice are the base of His throne.
97:3 Fire is His vanguard, burning His foes on every side.
97:4 His lightnings light up the world; the earth is convulsed at the sight;
97:5 mountains melt like wax at the LORD’s presence, at the presence of the Lord of all the earth.
97:6 The heavens proclaim His righteousness and all peoples see His glory.
97:7 All who worship images, who vaunt their idols, are dismayed; all divine beings bow down to Him.
97:8 Zion, hearing it, rejoices, the towns of Judah exult, because of Your judgments, O LORD.
97:9 For You, LORD, are supreme over all the earth; You are exalted high above all divine beings.
97:10 O you who love the LORD, hate evil! He guards the lives of His loyal ones, saving them from the hand of the wicked.
97:11 Light is sown for the righteous, radiance for the upright.
97:12 O you righteous, rejoice in the LORD and acclaim His holy name!

text.Rotherham:
97:1 Yahweh, hath become king, Let the earth exult, Let the multitude of coastlands rejoice.
97:2 Clouds and thick darkness, are round about him, Righteousness and justice, are the establishing of his throne.
97:3 Fire, before him, proceedeth, That it may consume, round about, his adversaries.
97:4 His lightnings, have illumined, the world, The earth, hath seen, and hath trembled;
97:5 The mountains, like wax, have melted, At the presence of Yahweh, At the presence, of the Lord of all the earth.
97:6 The heavens, have declared, his righteousness,—And all the peoples, have seen, his glory.
97:7 Let all who serve an image, be ashamed, They who boast themselves in things of nought, Bow down unto him, all ye gods.
97:8 Zion, hath heard and rejoiced, And the daughters of Judah, have exulted, Because of thy righteous decisions, O Yahweh:
97:9 For, thou, Yahweh, art Most High over all the earth, Greatly hast thou exalted thyself above all gods.
97:10 Ye lovers of Yahweh! be haters of wrong,—He preserveth the lives of his men of lovingkindness, From the hand of the lawless, will he rescue them.
97:11 Light, is sown for the righteous one, And, for the upright in heart, rejoicing:
97:12 Rejoice, ye righteous, in Yahweh, And give ye thanks, at the mention of his holiness.
text.NABRE:
97:1 The Lord is king; let the earth rejoice; let the many islands be glad.
97:2 Cloud and darkness surround him; justice and right are the foundation of his throne.
97:3 Fire goes before him, consuming his foes on every side.
97:4 His lightening illumines the world; the earth sees and trembles.
97:5 The mountains melt like wax before the Lord, before the Lord of all the earth.
97:6 The heavens proclaim his justice; all peoples see his glory.
97:7 II All who serve idols are put to shame, who glory in worthless things; all gods bow down before him.
97:8 Zion hears and is glad, and the daughters of Judah rejoice because of your judgments, O Lord.
97:9 For you, Lord, are the Most High over all the earth, exalted far above all gods.
97:10 You who love the Lord, hate evil, he protects the souls of the faithful, rescues them from the hand of the wicked.
97:11 Light dawns for the just, and gladness for the honest of heart.
97:12 Rejoice in the Lord, you just, and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness.
text.DRB:
97:1 A Psalm to David himself. Sing ye to our Lord a new song: because he hath done marvelous things. His right hand hath wrought salvation to himself: and his arm is holy.
97:2 Our Lord hath made known his salvation: in the sight of the Gentiles he hath revealed his justice.
97:3 He hath remembered his mercy, and his truth to the house of Israel. All the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.
97:4 Make ye jubilation to God all the earth: chant, and rejoice, and sing.
97:5 Sing to our Lord on harp, on harp and voice of Psalm:
97:6 on long drawn trumpets, and voice of cornet of horn. Make jubilation in the sight of the King our Lord:
97:7 let the sea be moved, and the fullness thereof: the round world, and they that dwell therein.
97:8 The rivers shall clap with hand: the mountains together shall rejoice
97:9 at the sight of our Lord: because he cometh to judge the earth. He will judge the round earth in justice, and the peoples in equity.

text.NRSV:
98:1 O sing to the Lord a new song, for he has done marvelous things. His right hand and his holy arm have gotten him victory.
98:2 The Lord has made known his victory; he has revealed his vindication in the sight of the nations.
98:3 He has remembered his steadfast love and faithfulness to the house of Israel. All the ends of the earth have seen the victory of our God.
98:4 Make a joyful noise to the Lord, all the earth; break forth into joyous song and sing praises.
98:5 Sing praises to the Lord with the lyre, with the lyre and the sound of melody.
98:6 With trumpets and the sound of the horn make a joyful noise before the King, the Lord.
98:7 Let the sea roar, and all that fills it; the world and those who live in it.
98:8 Let the floods clap their hands; let the hills sing together for joy
98:9 at the presence of the Lord, for he is coming to judge the earth. He will judge the world with righteousness, and the peoples with equity.
text.Coverdale:
98:1 Sing to the LORD a new song, * for he has done marvelous things.
98:2 With his right hand and his holy arm * has he won for himself the victory.
98:3 The LORD has made known his victory; * his righteousness has he openly shown in the sight of the nations.
98:4 He remembers his mercy and faithfulness to the house of Israel, * and all the ends of the earth have seen the victory of our God.
98:5 Shout with joy to the LORD, all you lands; * lift up your voice, rejoice, and sing.
98:6 Sing to the LORD with the harp, * with the harp and the voice of song.
98:7 With trumpets and the sound of the horn * shout with joy before the King, the LORD.
98:8 Let the sea make a noise and all that is in it, * the lands and those who dwell therein.
98:9 Let the rivers clap their hands, * and let the hills ring out with joy before the LORD, when he comes to judge the earth.
98:10 In righteousness shall he judge the world * and the peoples with equity.
text.KJV:
98:1 A Psalm. O sing unto the Lord a new song; for he hath done marvellous things: his right hand, and his holy arm, hath gotten him the victory.
98:2 The Lord hath made known his salvation: his righteousness hath he openly shewed in the sight of the heathen.
98:3 He hath remembered his mercy and his truth toward the house of Israel: all the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.
98:4 Make a joyful noise unto the Lord, all the earth: make a loud noise, and rejoice, and sing praise.
98:5 Sing unto the Lord with the harp; with the harp, and the voice of a psalm.
98:6 With trumpets and sound of cornet make a joyful noise before the Lord, the King.
98:7 Let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein.
98:8 Let the floods clap their hands: let the hills be joyful together
98:9 Before the Lord; for he cometh to judge the earth: with righteousness shall he judge the world, and the people with equity.
text.JPS1985:
98:1 A psalm. Sing to the LORD a new song, for He has worked wonders; His right hand, His holy arm, has won Him victory.
98:2 The LORD has manifested His victory, has displayed His triumph in the sight of the nations.
98:3 He was mindful of His steadfast love and faithfulness toward the house of Israel; all the ends of the earth beheld the victory of our God.
98:4 Raise a shout to the LORD, all the earth, break into joyous songs of praise!
98:5 Sing praise to the LORD with the lyre, with the lyre and melodious song.
98:6 With trumpets and the blast of the horn raise a shout before the LORD, the King.
98:7 Let the sea and all within it thunder, the world and its inhabitants;
98:8 let the rivers clap their hands, the mountains sing joyously together
98:9 at the presence of the LORD, for He is coming to rule the earth; He will rule the world justly, and its peoples with equity.

text.NABRE:
98:1 A psalm. Sing a new song to the Lord, for he has done marvelous deeds. His right hand and holy arm have won the victory.
98:2 The Lord has made his victory known; has revealed his triumph in the sight of the nations,
98:3 He has remembered his mercy and faithfulness toward the house of Israel. All the ends of the earth have seen the victory of our God.
98:4 II Shout with joy to the Lord, all the earth; break into song; sing praise.
98:5 Sing praise to the Lord with the lyre, with the lyre and melodious song.

text.DRB:
98:1 Apsalm to David himself. Our Lord hath reigned, let peoples be angry: he that sitteth upon the Cherubs, let the earth be moved.
98:2 Our Lord great in Sion: and high above all peoples.
98:3 Let them confess to thy great name: because it is terrible and holy.
98:4 And the honour of the King loveth judgment. Thou hast prepared directions: thou hast done judgment and justice in Jacob.
98:5 Exalt ye the Lord our God, and adore his foot-stool: because it is holy.
98:6 Moyses and Aaron in his Priests: and Samuel among them that invocate his name: They invocated our Lord and he heard them:
98:7 in a pillar of a cloud he spake to them. They kept his testimonies, & the precept which he gave them.
98:8 O Lord our God thou heardest them: God thou wast propitious to them, and taking vengeance upon all their inventions.
98:9 Exalt ye the Lord our God, and adore ye in his holy mount: because the Lord our God is holy.

text.NRSV:
99:1 The Lord is king; let the peoples tremble! He sits enthroned upon the cherubim; let the earth quake!
99:2 The Lord is great in Zion; he is exalted over all the peoples.
99:3 Let them praise your great and awesome name. Holy is he!
99:4 Mighty King, lover of justice, you have established equity; you have executed justice and righteousness in Jacob.
99:5 Extol the Lord our God; worship at his footstool. Holy is he!
99:6 Moses and Aaron were among his priests, Samuel also was among those who called on his name. They cried to the Lord, and he answered them.
99:7 He spoke to them in the pillar of cloud; they kept his decrees, and the statutes that he gave them.
99:8 O Lord our God, you answered them; you were a forgiving God to them, but an avenger of their wrongdoings.
99:9 Extol the Lord our God, and worship at his holy mountain; for the Lord our God is holy.
text.Coverdale:
99:1 The LORD is King; let the people tremble; * he is enthroned upon the cherubim; let the earth shake.
99:2 The LORD is great in Zion; * he is high above all peoples.
99:3 Let them confess his Name, which is great and awesome; * he is the Holy One.
99:4 "O mighty King, lover of justice, you have established equity; * you have executed justice and righteousness in Jacob."
99:5 Proclaim the greatness of the LORD our God and fall down before his footstool; * he is the Holy One.
99:6 Moses and Aaron among his priests, and Samuel among those who call upon his Name, * they called upon the LORD, and he answered them.
99:7 He spoke to them out of the pillar of cloud; * they kept his testimonies and the decree that he gave them.
99:8 "O LORD our God, you answered them indeed; * you were a God who forgave them, yet punished them for their evil deeds."
99:9 Proclaim the greatness of the LORD our God and worship him upon his holy hill; * for the LORD our God is the Holy One.
text.KJV:
99:1 The Lord reigneth; let the people tremble: he sitteth between the cherubims; let the earth be moved.
99:2 The Lord is great in Zion; and he is high above all the people.
99:3 Let them praise thy great and terrible name; for it is holy.
99:4 The king’s strength also loveth judgment; thou dost establish equity, thou executest judgment and righteousness in Jacob.
99:5 Exalt ye the Lord our God, and worship at his footstool; for he is holy.
99:6 Moses and Aaron among his priests, and Samuel among them that call upon his name; they called upon the Lord, and he answered them.
99:7 He spake unto them in the cloudy pillar: they kept his testimonies, and the ordinance that he gave them.
99:8 Thou answeredst them, O Lord our God: thou wast a God that forgavest them, though thou tookest vengeance of their inventions.
99:9 Exalt the Lord our God, and worship at his holy hill; for the Lord our God is holy.
text.JPS1985:
99:1 The LORD, enthroned on cherubim, is king, peoples tremble, the earth quakes.
99:2 The LORD is great in Zion, and exalted above all peoples.
99:3 They praise Your name as great and awesome; He is holy!
99:4 Mighty king who loves justice, it was You who established equity, You who worked righteous judgment in Jacob.
99:5 Exalt the LORD our God and bow down to His footstool; He is holy!
99:6 Moses and Aaron among His priests, Samuel, among those who call on His name— when they called to the LORD, He answered them.
99:7 He spoke to them in a pillar of cloud; they obeyed His decrees, the law He gave them.
99:8 O LORD our God, You answered them; You were a forgiving God for them, but You exacted retribution for their misdeeds.
99:9 Exalt the LORD our God, and bow toward His holy hill, for the LORD our God is holy.
text.Grail1963:
1 The Lord is king; the peoples tremble.
He is throned on the cherubim; the earth quakes.
2 The Lord is great in Zion.
He is supreme over all the peoples.
3 Let them praise his name, so terrible and great,
4 he is holy, full of power.
You are a king who loves what is right;

bow down before Zion, his footstool.

text.Rotherham:
99:1 Yahweh, hath become king, Let the peoples, tremble, He is enthroned on the cherubim, Let the earth, shake.
99:2 Yahweh, in Zion, is great, And, high, is he over all the peoples.
99:3 Let them thank his Name—great and reverend, Holy, is he!
99:4 Yea, with the strength of a king—justice, he loveth,—Thou, hast established equity, Justice and righteousness in Jacob, thou, hast wrought.
99:5 Exalt Yahweh our God, And bow down at his footstool, Holy, is he!
99:6 Moses and Aaron, [were] among his priests, And, Samuel, [was] among them who were calling upon his Name, Who were calling upon Yahweh, and, he, used to answer them:
99:7 In the pillar of cloud, used he to speak unto them, They kept his testimonies, and the statute he gave them.
99:8 O Yahweh our God! thou, answeredst them,—A pardoning GOD, thou becamest to them, Yet one bringing vengeance on their deeds.
99:9 Exalt Yahweh our God, And bow down towards his holy mountain, For, holy, is Yahweh our God.
text.NABRE:
99:1 The Lord is king, the peoples tremble; he is enthroned on the cherubim, the earth quakes.
99:2 Great is the Lord in Zion, exalted above all the peoples.
99:3 Let them praise your great and awesome name: Holy is he!
99:4 II O mighty king, lover of justice, you have established fairness; you have created just rule in Jacob.
99:5 Exalt the Lord, our God; bow down before his footstool; holy is he!
99:6 III Moses and Aaron were among his priests, Samuel among those who called on his name; they called on the Lord, and he answered them.
99:7 From the pillar of cloud he spoke to them; they kept his decrees, the law he had given them.
99:8 O Lord, our God, you answered them; you were a forgiving God to them, though you punished their offenses.
99:9 Exalt the Lord, our God; bow down before his holy mountain; holy is the Lord, our God.
text.DRB:
99:1 A Psalm in confession.
99:2 Make ye jubilation to God all the earth: serve ye our Lord in gladness. Enter ye in before his sight, in exultation.
99:3 Know ye that our Lord he is God: he made us, & not we ourselves. His people, and the sheep of his pasture:
99:4 enter ye into his gates in confession, his courts in hymns, confess ye to him. Praise ye his name:
99:5 because our Lord is sweet, his mercy for ever, and his truth even unto generation and generation.

text.NRSV:
100:1 Make a joyful noise to the Lord, all the earth.
100:2 Worship the Lord with gladness; come into his presence with singing.
100:3 Know that the Lord is God. It is he that made us, and we are his; we are his people, and the sheep of his pasture.
100:4 Enter his gates with thanksgiving, and his courts with praise. Give thanks to him, bless his name.
100:5 For the Lord is good; his steadfast love endures forever, and his faithfulness to all generations.
text.Coverdale:
100:1 Be joyful in the LORD, all you lands; * serve the LORD with gladness and come before his presence with a song.
100:2 Know this: The LORD himself is God; * he himself has made us, and we are his; we are his people and the sheep of his pasture.
100:3 Enter his gates with thanksgiving; go into his courts with praise; * give thanks to him and call upon his Name.
100:4 For the LORD is good; his mercy is everlasting; * and his faithfulness endures from age to age.
text.KJV:
100:1 A Psalm of praise. Make a joyful noise unto the Lord, all ye lands.
100:2 Serve the Lord with gladness: come before his presence with singing.
100:3 Know ye that the Lord he is God: it is he that hath made us, and not we ourselves; we are his people, and the sheep of his pasture.
100:4 Enter into his gates with thanksgiving, and into his courts with praise: be thankful unto him, and bless his name.
100:5 For the Lord is good; his mercy is everlasting; and his truth endureth to all generations.
text.JPS1985:
100:1 A psalm for praise. Raise a shout for the LORD, all the earth;
100:2 worship the LORD in gladness; come into His presence with shouts of joy.
100:3 Acknowledge that the LORD is God; He made us and we are His, His people, the flock He tends.
100:4 Enter His gates with praise, His courts with acclamation. Praise Him! Bless His name!
100:5 For the LORD is good; His steadfast love is eternal; His faithfulness is for all generations.
text.Grail1963:
1 Cry out with joy to the Lord, all the earth.
2 Serve the Lord with gladness.
Come before him, singing for joy.
3 Know that he, the Lord, is God.
He made us, we belong to him,
we are his people, the sheep of his flock.
4 Go within his gates, giving thanks.
Enter his courts with songs of praise.
Give thanks to him and bless his name.
5 Indeed, how good is the Lord,
eternal his merciful love.
He is faithful from age to age.
text.Rotherham:
100:1 Make a joyful noise to Yahweh, all the earth:
100:2 Serve Yahweh with rejoicing, Enter before him, with shouts of triumph.
100:3 Know that, Yahweh, he, is God,—He, made us, and not, we ourselves, His people, and the flock of his pasture.
100:4 Enter ye his gates, with thanksgiving, his courts, with praise, Give ye thanks to him, bless ye his Name;
100:5 For good is Yahweh, Age-abiding is his lovingkindness, And, unto generation after generation, his faithfulness.
text.DRB:
100:1 A Psalm to David himself. Mercy and judgment I will sing to thee, o Lord: I will sing,
100:2 and I shall understand in the immaculate way, when thou shalt come to me. I walked through in the innocency of my heart, in the midst of my house.
100:3 I did not propose before mine eyes any unjust thing: I hated them that do prevarication.
100:4 Aperverse heart hath not cleaved to me: the malignant declining from me I knew not.
100:5 One secretly detracting from his neighbour, him did I persecute. One of a proud eye, and unsatiable heart, with him I did not eat.
100:6 Mine eyes are towards the faithful of the earth that they may sit with me. A man that walketh in the immaculate way he did minister to me.
100:7 He that doth proudly shall not dwell in the midst of my house: he that speaketh unjust things, hath not directed in the sight of mine eyes.
100:8 In the morning did I kill all the sinners of the earth: that I might destroy out of the city of our Lord all those that work iniquity.

text.NRSV:
101:1 I will sing of loyalty and of justice; to you, O Lord, I will sing.
101:2 I will study the way that is blameless. When shall I attain it? I will walk with integrity of heart within my house;
101:3 I will not set before my eyes anything that is base. I hate the work of those who fall away; it shall not cling to me.
101:4 Perverseness of heart shall be far from me; I will know nothing of evil.
101:5 One who secretly slanders a neighbor I will destroy. A haughty look and an arrogant heart I will not tolerate.
101:6 I will look with favor on the faithful in the land, so that they may live with me; whoever walks in the way that is blameless shall minister to me.
101:7 No one who practices deceit shall remain in my house; no one who utters lies shall continue in my presence.
101:8 Morning by morning I will destroy all the wicked in the land, cutting off all evildoers from the city of the Lord.
text.Coverdale:
101:1 I will sing of mercy and justice; * to you, O LORD, will I sing praises.
101:2 I will strive to follow a blameless course; oh, when will you come to me? * I will walk with sincerity of heart within my house.
101:3 I will set no worthless thing before my eyes; * I hate the doers of evil deeds; they shall not remain with me.
101:4 A crooked heart shall be far from me; * I will not know evil.
101:5 Those who in secret slander their neighbors I will destroy; * those who have a haughty look and a proud heart I cannot abide.
101:6 My eyes are upon the faithful in the land, that they may dwell with me, * and only those who lead a blameless life shall be my servants.
101:7 Those who act deceitfully shall not dwell in my house, * and those who tell lies shall not continue in my sight.
101:8 I will soon destroy all the wicked in the land, * that I may root out all evildoers from the city of the LORD.
text.KJV:
101:1 A Psalm of David. I will sing of mercy and judgment: unto thee, O Lord, will I sing.
101:2 I will behave myself wisely in a perfect way. O when wilt thou come unto me? I will walk within my house with a perfect heart.
101:3 I will set no wicked thing before mine eyes: I hate the work of them that turn aside; it shall not cleave to me.
101:4 A froward heart shall depart from me: I will not know a wicked person.
101:5 Whoso privily slandereth his neighbour, him will I cut off: him that hath an high look and a proud heart will not I suffer.
101:6 Mine eyes shall be upon the faithful of the land, that they may dwell with me: he that walketh in a perfect way, he shall serve me.
101:7 He that worketh deceit shall not dwell within my house: he that telleth lies shall not tarry in my sight.
101:8 I will early destroy all the wicked of the land; that I may cut off all wicked doers from the city of the Lord.
text.JPS1985:
101:1 Of David. A psalm. I will sing of faithfulness and justice; I will chant a hymn to You, O LORD.
101:2 I will study the way of the blameless; when shall I attain it? I will live without blame within my house.
101:3 I will not set before my eyes anything base; I hate crooked dealing; I will have none of it.
101:4 Perverse thoughts will be far from me; I will know nothing of evil.
101:5 He who slanders his friend in secret I will destroy; I cannot endure the haughty and proud man.
101:6 My eyes are on the trusty men of the land, to have them at my side. He who follows the way of the blameless shall be in my service.
101:7 He who deals deceitfully shall not live in my house; he who speaks untruth shall not stand before my eyes.
101:8 Each morning I will destroy all the wicked of the land, to rid the city of the LORD of all evildoers.
text.Grail1963:
1 My song is of mercy and justice;
I sing to you, O Lord.
2 I will walk in the way of perfection.
O when, Lord, will you come?
I will walk with blameless heart
within my house;
3 I will not set before my eyes
whatever is base.
I will hate the ways of the crooked;
they shall not be my friends.
4 The falsehearted must keep far away;
the wicked I disown.

5 The man who slanders his neighbor in secret
I will bring to silence.
The man of proud looks and haughty heart
I will never endure.
6 I look to the faithful in the land
that they may dwell with me.
He who walks in the way of perfection
shall be my friend.
7 No man who practices deceit
shall live within my house.
No man who utters lies shall stand
before my eyes.
8 Morning by morning I will silence

all who do evil.
text.NABRE:
101:1 A psalm of David. I sing of mercy and justice; to you, Lord, I sing praise.
101:2 I study the way of integrity; when will you come to me? I act with integrity of heart within my household.
101:3 I do not allow into my presence anything base. I hate wrongdoing; I will have no part of it.
101:4 May the devious heart keep far from me; the wicked I will not acknowledge.
101:5 Whoever slanders a neighbor in secret I will reduce to silence. Haughty eyes and arrogant hearts I cannot endure.
101:6 II I look to the faithful of the land to sit at my side. Whoever follows the way of integrity is the one to enter my service.
101:7 No one who practices deceit can remain within my house. No one who speaks falsely can last in my presence.
101:8 Morning after morning I clear all the wicked from the land, to rid the city of the Lord of all doers of evil.
text.DRB:
101:1 The prayer of the poor, when he shall be anxious, and shall make his petition before our Lord.
101:2 Lord hear my prayer: and let my cry come to thee.
101:3 Turn not away thy face from me: in what day soever I am in tribulation, incline thine ear to me. In what day soever I shall invocate thee, hear me speedily.
101:4 Because my days have vanished as smoke: and my bones are withered as a dry burnt firebrand.
101:5 I am strucken as grass, and my heart is withered: because I have forgotten to eat my bread.
101:6 For the voice of my groaning, my bone hath cleaved to my flesh.
101:7 I am become like a pelican of the wilderness: I am become as a night crow in the house.
101:8 I have watched, and am become as a sparrow solitary in the house-top.
101:9 All the day did mine enemies upbraid me: and they that praised me sware against me.
101:10 Because I did eat ashes as bread, & mingled my drink with weeping.
101:11 At the face of thy wrath and indignation: because lifting me up thou hast thrown me down.
101:12 My days have declined as a shadow: and I am withered as grass.
101:13 But thou o Lord endurest for ever: and thy memorial in generation and generation.
101:14 Thou rising up shalt have mercy on Sion: because it is time to have mercy on it, because the time cometh.
101:15 Because the stones thereof have pleased thy servants: and they shall have pity on the earth thereof.
101:16 And the Gentiles shall fear thy name o Lord, and all the kings of the earth thy glory.
101:17 Because our Lord hath built Sion: and he shall be seen in his glory.
101:18 He hath had respect to the prayer of the humble: and he hath not despised their petition.
101:19 Let these things be written unto another generation: and the people that shall be created shall praise our Lord.
101:20 Because he hath looked forth from his high holy place: our Lord from heaven hath looked upon the earth.
101:21 That he might hear the groanings of the fettered: that he might loose the children of them that are slain:
101:22 That they may shew forth the name of our Lord in Sion: and his praise in Jerusalem.
101:23 In the assembling of the people together in one, and kings to serve our Lord.
101:24 He answered him in the way of his strength: Shew me the fewness of my days.
101:25 Call me not back in the half of my days: thy years are unto generation and generation.
101:26 In the beginning, o Lord, thou didst found the earth: and the heavens are the works of thy hands.
101:27 They shall perish, but thou art permanent: and they shall all wax old as a garment. And as a vesture thou shalt change them, and they shall be changed:
101:28 but thou art the self-same, and thy years shall not fail.
101:29 The children of thy servants shall inhabit: and their seed shall be directed for ever.

text.NRSV:
102:1 Hear my prayer, O Lord; let my cry come to you.
102:2 Do not hide your face from me in the day of my distress. Incline your ear to me; answer me speedily in the day when I call.
102:3 For my days pass away like smoke, and my bones burn like a furnace.
102:4 My heart is stricken and withered like grass; I am too wasted to eat my bread.
102:5 Because of my loud groaning my bones cling to my skin.
102:6 I am like an owl of the wilderness, like a little owl of the waste places.
102:7 I lie awake; I am like a lonely bird on the housetop.
102:8 All day long my enemies taunt me; those who deride me use my name for a curse.
102:9 For I eat ashes like bread, and mingle tears with my drink,
102:10 because of your indignation and anger; for you have lifted me up and thrown me aside.
102:11 My days are like an evening shadow; I wither away like grass.
102:12 But you, O Lord, are enthroned forever; your name endures to all generations.
102:13 You will rise up and have compassion on Zion, for it is time to favor it; the appointed time has come.
102:14 For your servants hold its stones dear, and have pity on its dust.
102:15 The nations will fear the name of the Lord, and all the kings of the earth your glory.
102:16 For the Lord will build up Zion; he will appear in his glory.
102:17 He will regard the prayer of the destitute, and will not despise their prayer.
102:18 Let this be recorded for a generation to come, so that a people yet unborn may praise the Lord:
102:19 that he looked down from his holy height, from heaven the Lord looked at the earth,
102:20 to hear the groans of the prisoners, to set free those who were doomed to die;
102:21 so that the name of the Lord may be declared in Zion, and his praise in Jerusalem,
102:22 when peoples gather together, and kingdoms, to worship the Lord.
102:23 He has broken my strength in midcourse; he has shortened my days.
102:24 "O my God,"I say,"do not take me away at the mid-point of my life, you whose years endure throughout all generations."
102:25 Long ago you laid the foundation of the earth, and the heavens are the work of your hands.
102:26 They will perish, but you endure; they will all wear out like a garment. You change them like clothing, and they pass away;
102:27 but you are the same, and your years have no end.
102:28 The children of your servants shall live secure; their offspring shall be established in your presence.
text.Coverdale:
102:1 LORD, hear my prayer, and let my cry come before you; * hide not your face from me in the day of my trouble.
102:2 Incline your ear to me; * when I call, make haste to answer me,
102:3 For my days drift away like smoke, * and my bones are hot as burning coals.
102:4 My heart is smitten like grass and withered, * so that I forget to eat my bread.
102:5 Because of the voice of my groaning * I am but skin and bones.
102:6 I have become like a vulture in the wilderness, * like an owl among the ruins.
102:7 I lie awake and groan; * I am like a sparrow, lonely on a house-top.
102:8 My enemies revile me all day long, * and those who scoff at me have taken an oath against me.
102:9 For I have eaten ashes for bread * and mingled my drink with weeping.
102:10 Because of your indignation and wrath * you have lifted me up and thrown me away.
102:11 My days pass away like a shadow, * and I wither like the grass.
102:12 But you, O LORD, endure for ever, * and your Name from age to age.
102:13 You will arise and have compassion on Zion, for it is time to have mercy upon her; * indeed, the appointed time has come.
102:14 For your servants love her very rubble, * and are moved to pity even for her dust.
102:15 The nations shall fear your Name, O LORD, * and all the kings of the earth your glory.
102:16 For the LORD will build up Zion, * and his glory will appear.
102:17 He will look with favor on the prayer of the homeless; * he will not despise their plea.
102:18 Let this be written for a future generation, * so that a people yet unborn may praise the LORD.
102:19 For the LORD looked down from his holy place on high; * from the heavens he beheld the earth;
102:20 That he might hear the groan of the captive * and set free those condemned to die;
102:21 That they may declare in Zion the Name of the LORD, * and his praise in Jerusalem;
102:22 When the peoples are gathered together, * and the kingdoms also, to serve the LORD.
102:23 He has brought down my strength before my time; * he has shortened the number of my days;
102:24 And I said,"O my God, do not take me away in the midst of my days; * your years endure throughout all generations.
102:25 In the beginning, O LORD, you laid the foundations of the earth, * and the heavens are the work of your hands;
102:26 They shall perish, but you will endure; they all shall wear out like a garment; * as clothing you will change them, and they shall be changed;
102:27 But you are always the same, * and your years will never end.
102:28 The children of your servants shall continue, * and their offspring shall stand fast in your sight."
text.KJV:
102:1 A Prayer of the afflicted, when he is overwhelmed, and poureth out his complaint before the Lord. Hear my prayer, O Lord, and let my cry come unto thee.
102:2 Hide not thy face from me in the day when I am in trouble; incline thine ear unto me: in the day when I call answer me speedily.
102:3 For my days are consumed like smoke, and my bones are burned as an hearth.
102:4 My heart is smitten, and withered like grass; so that I forget to eat my bread.
102:5 By reason of the voice of my groaning my bones cleave to my skin.
102:6 I am like a pelican of the wilderness: I am like an owl of the desert.
102:7 I watch, and am as a sparrow alone upon the house top.
102:8 Mine enemies reproach me all the day; and they that are mad against me are sworn against me.
102:9 For I have eaten ashes like bread, and mingled my drink with weeping,
102:10 Because of thine indignation and thy wrath: for thou hast lifted me up, and cast me down.
102:11 My days are like a shadow that declineth; and I am withered like grass.
102:12 But thou, O Lord, shalt endure for ever; and thy remembrance unto all generations.
102:13 Thou shalt arise, and have mercy upon Zion: for the time to favour her, yea, the set time, is come.
102:14 For thy servants take pleasure in her stones, and favour the dust thereof.
102:15 So the heathen shall fear the name of the Lord, and all the kings of the earth thy glory.
102:16 When the Lord shall build up Zion, he shall appear in his glory.
102:17 He will regard the prayer of the destitute, and not despise their prayer.
102:18 This shall be written for the generation to come: and the people which shall be created shall praise the Lord.
102:19 For he hath looked down from the height of his sanctuary; from heaven did the Lord behold the earth;
102:20 To hear the groaning of the prisoner; to loose those that are appointed to death;
102:21 To declare the name of the Lord in Zion, and his praise in Jerusalem;
102:22 When the people are gathered together, and the kingdoms, to serve the Lord.
102:23 He weakened my strength in the way; he shortened my days.
102:24 I said, O my God, take me not away in the midst of my days: thy years are throughout all generations.
102:25 Of old hast thou laid the foundation of the earth: and the heavens are the work of thy hands.
102:26 They shall perish, but thou shalt endure: yea, all of them shall wax old like a garment; as a vesture shalt thou change them, and they shall be changed:
102:27 But thou art the same, and thy years shall have no end.
102:28 The children of thy servants shall continue, and their seed shall be established before thee.
text.JPS1985:
102:1 A prayer of the lowly man when he is faint and pours forth his plea before the LORD.
102:2 O LORD, hear my prayer; let my cry come before You.
102:3 Do not hide Your face from me in my time of trouble; turn Your ear to me; when I cry, answer me speedily.
102:4 For my days have vanished like smoke and my bones are charred like a hearth.
102:5 My body is stricken and withered like grass; too wasted to eat my food;
102:6 on account of my vehement groaning my bones show through my skin.
102:7 I am like a great owl in the wilderness, an owl among the ruins.
102:8 I lie awake; I am like a lone bird upon a roof.
102:9 All day long my enemies revile me; my deriders use my name to curse.
102:10 For I have eaten ashes like bread and mixed my drink with tears,
102:11 because of Your wrath and Your fury; for You have cast me far away.
102:12 My days are like a lengthening shadow; I wither like grass.
102:13 But You, O LORD, are enthroned forever; Your fame endures throughout the ages.
102:14 You will surely arise and take pity on Zion, for it is time to be gracious to her; the appointed time has come.
102:15 Your servants take delight in its stones, and cherish its dust.
102:16 The nations will fear the name of the LORD, all the kings of the earth, Your glory.
102:17 For the LORD has built Zion; He has appeared in all His glory.
102:18 He has turned to the prayer of the destitute and has not spurned their prayer.
102:19 May this be written down for a coming generation, that people yet to be created may praise the LORD.
102:20 For He looks down from His holy height; the LORD beholds the earth from heaven
102:21 to hear the groans of the prisoner, to release those condemned to death;
102:22 that the fame of the LORD may be recounted in Zion, His praises in Jerusalem,
102:23 when the nations gather together, the kingdoms, to serve the LORD.
102:24 He drained my strength in mid-course, He shortened my days.
102:25 I say, “O my God, do not take me away in the midst of my days, You whose years go on for generations on end.
102:26 Of old You established the earth; the heavens are the work of Your hands.
102:27 They shall perish, but You shall endure; they shall all wear out like a garment; You change them like clothing and they pass away.
102:28 But You are the same, and Your years never end.
102:29 May the children of Your servants dwell securely and their offspring endure in Your presence.”
text.Grail1963:
.2 O Lord, listen to my prayer
and let my cry for help reach you.
3 Do not hide your face from me
in the day of my distress.
Turn your ear towards me
and answer me quickly when I call.
4 For my days are vanishing like smoke,
my bones burn away like a fire.
5 My heart is withered like the grass.
I forget to eat my bread.
6 I cry with all my strength
and my skin clings to my bones.
7 I have become like a pelican in the wilderness
like an owl in desolate places.
8 I lie awake and I moan
like some lonely bird on a roof.
9 All day long my foes revile me;
those who hate me use my name as a curse.
10 The bread I eat is ashes;
my drink is mingled with tears.
11 In your anger, Lord, and your fury
you have lifted me up and thrown me down.
12 My days are like a passing shadow
and I wither away like the grass.
* * *
13 But you, O Lord, will endure for ever
and your name from age to age.
14 You will arise and have mercy on Zion:

for this is the time to have mercy,
(yes, the time appointed has come)
15 for your servants love her very stones,
are moved with pity even for her dust.
16 The nations shall fear the name of the Lord
and all the earth's kings your glory,
17 when the Lord shall build up Zion again
and appear in all his glory.
18 Then he will turn to the prayers of the helpless;
he will not despise their prayers.
19 Let this be written for ages to come
that a people yet unborn may praise the Lord;
20 for the Lord leaned down from his sanctuary on high.
He looked down from heaven to the earth
21 that he might hear the groans of the prisoners
and free those condemned to die.
29 The sons of your servants shall dwell untroubled
and their race shall endure before you
22 that the name of the Lord may be proclaimed in Zion
and his praise in the heart of Jerusalem,
23 when peoples and kingdoms are gathered together
to pay their homage to the Lord.
* * *
24 He has broken my strength in midcourse;
he has shortened the days of my life.
25 I say to God: "Do not take me away
before my day are complete,
you, whose days last from age to age.
26 Long ago you founded the earth
