text.Rotherham:
108:1 Fixed, is my heart, O God, I will sing and touch the strings, even mine honour.
108:2 Awake, O harp and lyre, I will awaken the dawn!
108:3 I will thank thee among the peoples, O Yahweh, and will sing praise unto thee, among the tribes of men.
108:4 For, great, above the heavens, is thy lovingkindness, and, as far as the skies, thy faithfulness.
108:5 Be thou exalted above the heavens, O God, And, above all the earth, be thy glory.
108:6 To the end thy beloved ones may be delivered, Oh save thou with thy right hand and answer me!
108:7 God, hath spoken in his holiness, I will exult! I will apportion Shechem! And, the Vale of Succoth, will I measure out;
108:8 Mine, is Gilead—mine, Manasseh, but, Ephraim, is the defence of my head, Judah, is my commander’s staff;
108:9 Moab, is my wash-bowl, Upon Edom, will I throw my shoe, Over Philistia, raise a shout of triumph.
108:10 Who will conduct me to a fortified city? Who will lead me as far as Edom?
108:11 Hast not thou, O God, rejected us? and wilt not go forth, O God, with our hosts.
108:12 Grant us help out of distress, for, vain, is the deliverance of man:
108:13 In God, shall we do valiantly, He himself, therefore, shall tread down our adversaries.
text.NABRE:
108:1 A song; a psalm of David.
108:2 I My heart is steadfast, God; my heart is steadfast. Let me sing and chant praise.
108:3 Awake, lyre and harp! I will wake the dawn.
108:4 I will praise you among the peoples, Lord; I will chant your praise among the nations.
108:5 For your mercy is greater than the heavens; your faithfulness, to the skies.
108:6 II Appear on high over the heavens, God; your glory above all the earth.
108:7 Help with your right hand and answer us that your loved ones may escape.
108:8 God speaks in his holiness: “I will exult, I will apportion Shechem; the valley of Succoth I will measure out.
108:9 Gilead is mine, mine is Manasseh; Ephraim is the helmet for my head, Judah, my scepter.
108:10 Moab is my washbowl; upon Edom I cast my sandal; I will shout in triumph over Philistia.”
108:11 Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me into Edom?
108:12 Was it not you who rejected us, God? Do you no longer march with our armies?
108:13 Give us aid against the foe; worthless is human help.
108:14 We will triumph with the help of God, who will trample down our foes.
text.DRB:
108:1 Unto the end, a Psalm of David.
108:2 O God conceal not my praise: because the mouth of the sinner, and the mouth of the deceitful man is open upon me.
108:3 They have spoken against me with deceitful tongue, and with words of hatred they have compassed me: and they have impugned me without cause.
108:4 For that they should love me, they backbited me: but I prayed.
108:5 And they set against me evil things for good: and hatred for my love.
108:6 Appoint a sinner over him: and let the devil stand on his right hand.
108:7 When he is judged, let him come forth condemned: and let his prayer be turned into sin.
108:8 Let his days be made few: and let another take his bishoprick.
108:9 Let his children be made orphans: and his wife a widow.
108:10 Let his children be transported wandering, and let them beg: and let them be cast out of their habitations.
108:11 Let the usurer search all his substance: and let strangers spoil his labours.
108:12 Let there be none to help him: neither let there be any to have pity on his pupils.
108:13 Let his children come to destruction: in one generation let his name be clean put out.
108:14 Let the iniquity of his fathers return to memory in the sight of our Lord: and let not the sin of his mother be blotted out.
108:15 Let them be before our Lord always, and let the memory of them perish out of the earth:
108:16 For that he remembered not to do mercy.
108:17 And he persecuted the poor, and needy man, and the compunct in heart to kill him.
108:18 And he loved cursing, and it shall come to him: and he would not blessing, and it shall be far from him. And he put on cursing as a garment, and it entered as water into his inner parts, and as oil in his bones.
108:19 Be it to him as a garment, wherewith he is covered: and as a girdle, wherewith he is always girded.
108:20 This is the work of them that detract from me before our Lord: and that speak evils against my soul.
108:21 And thou Lord, Lord, do with me for thy name's sake: because thy mercy is sweet. Deliver me
108:22 because I am needy and poor: and my heart is troubled within me.
108:23 As a shadow when it declineth, am I taken away: and I am shaken as locusts.
108:24 My knees are weakened with fasting: and my flesh is changed by reason of oil.
108:25 And I am made a reproach to them: they saw me, and wagged their heads.
108:26 Help me, o Lord my God: save me according to thy mercy.
108:27 And let them know that this is thy hand: & thou, o Lord, hast done it.
108:28 They will curse, and thou shalt bless: let them that rise up against me be confounded: but thy servant shall rejoice.
108:29 Let them that detract from me, be clothed with shame: and let them be covered with their confusion, as with a double patched cloak.
108:30 I will confess to our Lord exceedingly with my mouth: and in the midst of many I will praise him.
108:31 Because he hath stood on the right hand of the poor, that he might save my soul from the persecutors.

text.NRSV:
109:1 Do not be silent, O God of my praise.
109:2 For wicked and deceitful mouths are opened against me, speaking against me with lying tongues.
109:3 They beset me with words of hate, and attack me without cause.
109:4 In return for my love they accuse me, even while I make prayer for them.
109:5 So they reward me evil for good, and hatred for my love.
109:6 They say,"Appoint a wicked man against him; let an accuser stand on his right.
109:7 When he is tried, let him be found guilty; let his prayer be counted as sin.
109:8 May his days be few; may another seize his position.
109:9 May his children be orphans, and his wife a widow.
109:10 May his children wander about and beg; may they be driven out of the ruins they inhabit.
109:11 May the creditor seize all that he has; may strangers plunder the fruits of his toil.
109:12 May there be no one to do him a kindness, nor anyone to pity his orphaned children.
109:13 May his posterity be cut off; may his name be blotted out in the second generation.
109:14 May the iniquity of his father be remembered before the Lord, and do not let the sin of his mother be blotted out.
109:15 Let them be before the Lord continually, and may his memory be cut off from the earth.
109:16 For he did not remember to show kindness, but pursued the poor and needy and the brokenhearted to their death.
109:17 He loved to curse; let curses come on him. He did not like blessing; may it be far from him.
109:18 He clothed himself with cursing as his coat, may it soak into his body like water, like oil into his bones.
109:19 May it be like a garment that he wraps around himself, like a belt that he wears every day."
109:20 May that be the reward of my accusers from the Lord, of those who speak evil against my life.
109:21 But you, O Lord my Lord, act on my behalf for your name's sake; because your steadfast love is good, deliver me.
109:22 For I am poor and needy, and my heart is pierced within me.
109:23 I am gone like a shadow at evening; I am shaken off like a locust.
109:24 My knees are weak through fasting; my body has become gaunt.
109:25 I am an object of scorn to my accusers; when they see me, they shake their heads.
109:26 Help me, O Lord my God! Save me according to your steadfast love.
109:27 Let them know that this is your hand; you, O Lord, have done it.
109:28 Let them curse, but you will bless. Let my assailants be put to shame; may your servant be glad.
109:29 May my accusers be clothed with dishonor; may they be wrapped in their own shame as in a mantle.
109:30 With my mouth I will give great thanks to the Lord; I will praise him in the midst of the throng.
109:31 For he stands at the right hand of the needy, to save them from those who would condemn them to death.
text.Coverdale:
109:1 Hold not your tongue, O God of my praise; * for the mouth of the wicked, the mouth of the deceitful, is opened against me.
109:2 They speak to me with a lying tongue; * they encompass me with hateful words and fight against me without a cause.
109:3 Despite my love, they accuse me; * but as for me, I pray for them.
109:4 They repay evil for good, * and hatred for my love.
109:5 Set a wicked man against him, * and let an accuser stand at his right hand.
109:6 When he is judged, let him be found guilty, * and let his appeal be in vain.
109:7 Let his days be few, * and let another take his office.
109:8 Let his children be fatherless, * and his wife become a widow.
109:9 Let his children be waifs and beggars; * let them be driven from the ruins of their homes.
109:10 Let the creditor seize everything he has; * let strangers plunder his gains.
109:11 Let there be no one to show him kindness, * and none to pity his fatherless children.
109:12 Let his descendants be destroyed, * and his name be blotted out in the next generation.
109:13 Let the wickedness of his fathers be remembered before the LORD, * and his mother's sin not be blotted out;
109:14 Let their sin be always before the LORD; * but let him root out their names from the earth;
109:15 Because he did not remember to show mercy, * but persecuted the poor and needy and sought to kill the brokenhearted.
109:16 He loved cursing, let it come upon him; * he took no delight in blessing, let it depart from him.
109:17 He put on cursing like a garment, * let it soak into his body like water and into his bones like oil;
109:18 Let it be to him like the cloak which he wraps around himself, * and like the belt that he wears continually.
109:19 Let this be the recompense from the LORD to my accusers, * and to those who speak evil against me.
109:20 But you, O Lord my God, oh, deal with me according to your Name; * for your tender mercy's sake, deliver me.
109:21 For I am poor and needy, * and my heart is wounded within me.
109:22 I have faded away like a shadow when it lengthens; * I am shaken off like a locust.
109:23 My knees are weak through fasting, * and my flesh is wasted and gaunt.
109:24 I have become a reproach to them; * they see and shake their heads.
109:25 Help me, O LORD my God; * save me for your mercy's sake.
109:26 Let them know that this is your hand, * that you, O LORD, have done it.
109:27 They may curse, but you will bless; * let those who rise up against me be put to shame, and your servant will rejoice.
109:28 Let my accusers be clothed with disgrace * and wrap themselves in their shame as in a cloak.
109:29 I will give great thanks to the LORD with my mouth; * in the midst of the multitude will I praise him;
109:30 Because he stands at the right hand of the needy, * to save his life from those who would condemn him. Twenty-third Day: Morning Prayer
text.KJV:
109:1 To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. Hold not thy peace, O God of my praise;
109:2 For the mouth of the wicked and the mouth of the deceitful are opened against me: they have spoken against me with a lying tongue.
109:3 They compassed me about also with words of hatred; and fought against me without a cause.
109:4 For my love they are my adversaries: but I give myself unto prayer.
109:5 And they have rewarded me evil for good, and hatred for my love.
109:6 Set thou a wicked man over him: and let Satan stand at his right hand.
109:7 When he shall be judged, let him be condemned: and let his prayer become sin.
109:8 Let his days be few; and let another take his office.
109:9 Let his children be fatherless, and his wife a widow.
109:10 Let his children be continually vagabonds, and beg: let them seek their bread also out of their desolate places.
109:11 Let the extortioner catch all that he hath; and let the strangers spoil his labour.
109:12 Let there be none to extend mercy unto him: neither let there be any to favour his fatherless children.
109:13 Let his posterity be cut off; and in the generation following let their name be blotted out.
109:14 Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered with the Lord; and let not the sin of his mother be blotted out.
109:15 Let them be before the Lord continually, that he may cut off the memory of them from the earth.
109:16 Because that he remembered not to shew mercy, but persecuted the poor and needy man, that he might even slay the broken in heart.
109:17 As he loved cursing, so let it come unto him: as he delighted not in blessing, so let it be far from him.
109:18 As he clothed himself with cursing like as with his garment, so let it come into his bowels like water, and like oil into his bones.
109:19 Let it be unto him as the garment which covereth him, and for a girdle wherewith he is girded continually.
109:20 Let this be the reward of mine adversaries from the Lord, and of them that speak evil against my soul.
109:21 But do thou for me, O God the Lord, for thy name’s sake: because thy mercy is good, deliver thou me.
109:22 For I am poor and needy, and my heart is wounded within me.
109:23 I am gone like the shadow when it declineth: I am tossed up and down as the locust.
109:24 My knees are weak through fasting; and my flesh faileth of fatness.
109:25 I became also a reproach unto them: when they looked upon me they shaked their heads.
109:26 Help me, O Lord my God: O save me according to thy mercy:
109:27 That they may know that this is thy hand; that thou, Lord, hast done it.
109:28 Let them curse, but bless thou: when they arise, let them be ashamed; but let thy servant rejoice.
109:29 Let mine adversaries be clothed with shame, and let them cover themselves with their own confusion, as with a mantle.
109:30 I will greatly praise the Lord with my mouth; yea, I will praise him among the multitude.
109:31 For he shall stand at the right hand of the poor, to save him from those that condemn his soul.
text.JPS1985:
109:1 For the leader. Of David. A psalm. O God of my praise, do not keep aloof,
109:2 for the wicked and the deceitful open their mouth against me; they speak to me with lying tongue.
109:3 They encircle me with words of hate; they attack me without cause.
109:4 They answer my love with accusation and I must stand judgment.
109:5 They repay me with evil for good, with hatred for my love.
109:6 Appoint a wicked man over him; may an accuser stand at his right side;
109:7 may he be tried and convicted; may he be judged and found guilty.
109:8 May his days be few; may another take over his position.
109:9 May his children be orphans, his wife a widow.
109:10 May his children wander from their hovels, begging in search of [bread].
109:11 May his creditor seize all his possessions; may strangers plunder his wealth.
109:12 May no one show him mercy; may none pity his orphans;
109:13 may his posterity be cut off; may their names be blotted out in the next generation.
109:14 May God be ever mindful of his father’s iniquity, and may the sin of his mother not be blotted out.
109:15 May the LORD be aware of them always and cause their names to be cut off from the earth,
109:16 because he was not minded to act kindly, and hounded to death the poor and needy man, one crushed in spirit.
109:17 He loved to curse—may a curse come upon him! He would not bless—may blessing be far from him!
109:18 May he be clothed in a curse like a garment, may it enter his body like water, his bones like oil.
109:19 Let it be like the cloak he wraps around him, like the belt he always wears.
109:20 May the LORD thus repay my accusers, all those who speak evil against me.
109:21 Now You, O God, my Lord, act on my behalf as befits Your name. Good and faithful as You are, save me.
109:22 For I am poor and needy, and my heart is pierced within me.
109:23 I fade away like a lengthening shadow; I am shaken off like locusts.
109:24 My knees give way from fasting; my flesh is lean, has lost its fat.
109:25 I am the object of their scorn; when they see me, they shake their head.
109:26 Help me, O LORD, my God; save me in accord with Your faithfulness,
109:27 that men may know that it is Your hand, that You, O LORD, have done it.
109:28 Let them curse, but You bless; let them rise up, but come to grief, while Your servant rejoices.
109:29 My accusers shall be clothed in shame, wrapped in their disgrace as in a robe.
109:30 My mouth shall sing much praise to the LORD; I will acclaim Him in the midst of a throng,
109:31 because He stands at the right hand of the needy, to save him from those who would condemn him.
text.Grail1963:
1 O God whom I praise, do not be silent,
2 for the mouths of deceit and wickedness
are opened against me.
3 They speak to me with lying tongues;
they beset me with words of hate
and attack me without cause.
4 In return for my love they accuse me
while I pray for them.
5 They repay me evil for good,
hatred for love.
* * *
6 Appoint a wicked man as his judge;
let an accuser stand at his right.
7 When he is judged let him come out condemned;
let his prayer be considered as sin.
8 Let the days of his life be few;
let another man take his office.
9 Let his children be fatherless orphans
and his wife become a widow.
10 Let his children be wanderers and beggars
driven from the ruins of their home.
11 Let the creditor seize all his goods;
let strangers take the fruit of his work.
12 Let no one show him any mercy
nor pity his fatherless children.
13 Let all his sons be destroyed
and with them their names be blotted out.
14 Let his father's guilt be remembered,
his mother's be retained.
15 Let it always stand before the Lord,
that their memory be cut off from the earth.
16 For he did not think of showing mercy
but pursed the poor and the needy,
17 hounding the wretched to death.
He loved cursing; let curses fall upon him.
He scorned blessing; let blessing pass him by.
18 He put on cursing like his coat;
let it sink into his body like water;

let it sink like oil into his bones;
19 let it be like the clothes that cover him,
like a girdle he cannot take off!
* * *
20 Let the Lord thus repay my accusers,
all those who speak evil against me.
21 For your name's sake act in my defense;
in the goodness of your love be my rescuer.
22 For I am poor and needy
and my heart is pierced within me.
23 I fade like an evening shadow;
I am shaken off like a locust.
24 My knees are weak from fasting;
my body is thin and gaunt.
25 I have become an object of scorn,
all who see me toss their heads.
26 Help me, Lord my God;
save me because of your love.
27 Let them know that is your work,
That this is your doing, O Lord.
28 They may curse but you will bless.
Let my attackers be put to shame,
but let your servant rejoice.
29 Let my accusers be clothed with dishonor,
covered with shame as with a cloak.
30 Loud thanks to the Lord are on my lips.
I will praise him in the midst of the throng,
31 for he stands at the poor man's side
to save him from those who condemn him.
text.NABRE:
109:1 For the leader. A psalm of David.
109:2 I O God, whom I praise, do not be silent, for wicked and treacherous mouths attack me. They speak against me with lying tongues;
109:3 with hateful words they surround me, attacking me without cause.
109:4 In return for my love they slander me, even though I prayed for them.
109:5 They repay me evil for good, hatred for my love.
109:6 II Appoint an evil one over him, an accuser to stand at his right hand,
109:7 That he may be judged and found guilty, that his plea may be in vain.
109:8 May his days be few; may another take his office.
109:9 May his children be fatherless, his wife, a widow.
109:10 May his children wander and beg, driven from their hovels.
109:11 May the usurer snare all he owns, strangers plunder all he earns.
109:12 May no one treat him with mercy or pity his fatherless children.
109:13 May his posterity be destroyed, their name rooted out in the next generation.
109:14 May his fathers’ guilt be mentioned to the Lord; his mother’s sin not rooted out.
109:15 May their guilt be always before the Lord, till their memory is banished from the earth,
109:16 For he did not remember to show mercy, but hounded the wretched poor and brought death to the brokenhearted.
109:17 He loved cursing; may it come upon him; he hated blessing; may none come to him.
109:18 May cursing clothe him like a robe; may it enter his belly like water, his bones like oil.
109:19 May it be near as the clothes he wears, as the belt always around him.
109:20 May this be the reward for my accusers from the Lord, for those speaking evil against me.
109:21 III But you, Lord, are my Lord, deal kindly with me for your name’s sake; in your great mercy rescue me.
109:22 For I am poor and needy; my heart is pierced within me.
109:23 Like a lengthening shadow I am gone, I am shaken off like the locust.
109:24 My knees totter from fasting; my flesh has wasted away.
109:25 I have become a mockery to them; when they see me, they shake their heads.
109:26 Help me, Lord, my God; save me in your mercy.
109:27 Make them know this is your hand, that you, Lord, have done this.
109:28 Though they curse, may you bless; arise, shame them, that your servant may rejoice.
109:29 Clothe my accusers with disgrace; make them wear their shame like a mantle.
109:30 I will give fervent thanks to the Lord; before a crowd I will praise him.
109:31 For he stands at the right hand of the poor to save him from those who pass judgment on him.
text.DRB:
109:1 A Psalm of David. Our Lord said to my Lord: Sit on my right hand: till I make thine enemies, the foot-stool of thy feet.
109:2 Our Lord will send forth the rod of thy strength from Sion: rule thou in the midst of thine enemies.
109:3 With thee the beginning in the day of thy strength: in the brightness of holy things: from the womb before the day star I begat thee.
109:4 Our Lord sware, and it shall not repent him: Thou art a Priest for ever according to the order of Melchisedech.
109:5 Our Lord on thy right hand hath broken kings in the day of his wrath.
109:6 He shall judge in nations, he shall fill ruins, he shall crush the heads in the land of many.
109:7 Of the torrent in the way he shall drink: therefore shall he exalt the head.

text.NRSV:
110:1 The Lord says to my lord,"Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies your footstool."
110:2 The Lord sends out from Zion your mighty scepter. Rule in the midst of your foes.
110:3 Your people will offer themselves willingly on the day you lead your forces on the holy mountains. From the womb of the morning, like dew, your youth will come to you.
110:4 The Lord has sworn and will not change his mind,"You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek."
110:5 The Lord is at your right hand; he will shatter kings on the day of his wrath.
110:6 He will execute judgment among the nations, filling them with corpses; he will shatter heads over the wide earth.
110:7 He will drink from the stream by the path; therefore he will lift up his head.
text.Coverdale:
110:1 The LORD said to my Lord,"Sit at my right hand, * until I make your enemies your footstool."
110:2 The LORD will send the scepter of your power out of Zion, * saying,"Rule over your enemies round about you.
110:3 Princely state has been yours from the day of your birth; * in the beauty of holiness have I begotten you, like dew from the womb of the morning."
110:4 The LORD has sworn and he will not recant: *"You are a priest for ever after the order of Melchizedek."
110:5 The Lord who is at your right hand will smite kings in the day of his wrath; * he will rule over the nations.
110:6 He will heap high the corpses; * he will smash heads over the wide earth.
110:7 He will drink from the brook beside the road; * therefore he will lift high his head.
text.KJV:
110:1 A Psalm of David. The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool.
110:2 The Lord shall send the rod of thy strength out of Zion: rule thou in the midst of thine enemies.
110:3 Thy people shall be willing in the day of thy power, in the beauties of holiness from the womb of the morning: thou hast the dew of thy youth.
110:4 The Lord hath sworn, and will not repent, Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchizedek.
110:5 The Lord at thy right hand shall strike through kings in the day of his wrath.
110:6 He shall judge among the heathen, he shall fill the places with the dead bodies; he shall wound the heads over many countries.
110:7 He shall drink of the brook in the way: therefore shall he lift up the head.
text.JPS1985:
110:1 Of David. A psalm. The LORD said to my lord, “Sit at My right hand while I make your enemies your footstool.”
110:2 The LORD will stretch forth from Zion your mighty scepter; hold sway over your enemies!
110:3 Your people come forward willingly on your day of battle. In majestic holiness, from the womb, from the dawn, yours was the dew of youth.
110:4 The LORD has sworn and will not relent, “You are a priest forever, a rightful king by My decree.”
110:5 The Lord is at your right hand. He crushes kings in the day of His anger.
110:6 He works judgment upon the nations, heaping up bodies, crushing heads far and wide.
110:7 He drinks from the stream on his way; therefore he holds his head high.
text.Grail1963:
1 The Lord's revelation to my Master:
"Sit on my right:
your foes I will put beneath your feet."
2 The Lord will wield from Zion
your scepter of power:
rule in the midst of all your foes.
3 A prince from the day of your birth
on the holy mountains;
from the womb before the dawn I begot you.
4 The Lord has sworn an oath he will not change.
"You are a priest for ever,
a priest like Melchizedek of old."

* * *
5 The Master standing at your right hand
will shatter kings in the day of his wrath.
6 He, the judge of the nations
will heap high the bodies;
heads shall be scattered far and wide.
7 He shall drink from the stream by the wayside
and therefore he shall lift up his head.
text.Rotherham:
110:1 The declaration of Yahweh to my Lord—Sit thou at my right hand, Until I make thy foes thy footstool.
110:2 Thy sceptre of strength, will Yahweh extend out of Zion, Tread thou down, in the midst of thy foes.
110:3 Thy people, will freely offer themselves, in the day of thine army,—in the splendours of holiness, out of the womb of the dawn, To thee, [shall spring forth] the dew of thy youth.
110:4 Yahweh, hath sworn—and will not repent, Thou, [shalt be] a priest unto times age-abiding, after the manner of Melchizedek.
110:5 My Lord, on thy right hand,—hath shattered—in the day of his anger—kings;
110:6 He will judge among the nations—full of dead bodies! He hath shattered the head over a land far extended:
110:7 Of the torrent in the way, will he drink;—For this cause, will he lift up [his] head.
text.NABRE:
110:1 A psalm of David. The Lord says to my lord: “Sit at my right hand, while I make your enemies your footstool.”
110:2 The scepter of your might: the Lord extends your strong scepter from Zion. Have dominion over your enemies!
110:3 Yours is princely power from the day of your birth. In holy splendor before the daystar, like dew I begot you.
110:4 The Lord has sworn and will not waver: “You are a priest forever in the manner of Melchizedek.”
110:5 At your right hand is the Lord, who crushes kings on the day of his wrath,
110:6 Who judges nations, heaps up corpses, crushes heads across the wide earth,
110:7 Who drinks from the brook by the wayside and thus holds high his head.
text.DRB:
110:1 Alleluia. I will confess to thee, o Lord, with all my heart: in the counsel of the just, and the congregation.
110:2 The works of our Lord are great: exquisite according to all his wills.
110:3 Confession and magnificence his work: and his justice continueth for ever and ever.
110:4 He hath made a memory of his marvelous works; a merciful & pitiful Lord:
110:5 he hath given meat to them that fear him. He will be mindful for ever of his testament:
110:6 the force of his works he will shew forth to his people:
110:7 To give them the inheritance of the gentiles: the works of his hands truth and judgment.
110:8 All his commandments are faithful: confirmed for ever and ever, made in truth and equity.
110:9 He sent redemption to his people: he commanded his testament for ever. Holy and terrible is his name:
110:10 the fear of our Lord is the beginning of wisdom. Understanding is good to all that do it: his praise remaineth for ever and ever.

text.NRSV:
111:1 Praise the Lord! I will give thanks to the Lord with my whole heart, in the company of the upright, in the congregation.
111:2 Great are the works of the Lord, studied by all who delight in them.
111:3 Full of honor and majesty is his work, and his righteousness endures forever.
111:4 He has gained renown by his wonderful deeds; the Lord is gracious and merciful.
111:5 He provides food for those who fear him; he is ever mindful of his covenant.
111:6 He has shown his people the power of his works, in giving them the heritage of the nations.
111:7 The works of his hands are faithful and just; all his precepts are trustworthy.
111:8 They are established forever and ever, to be performed with faithfulness and uprightness.
111:9 He sent redemption to his people; he has commanded his covenant forever. Holy and awesome is his name.
111:10 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom; all those who practice it have a good understanding. His praise endures forever.
text.Coverdale:
111:1 Hallelujah! I will give thanks to the LORD with my whole heart, * in the assembly of the upright, in the congregation.
111:2 Great are the deeds of the LORD! * they are studied by all who delight in them.
111:3 His work is full of majesty and splendor, * and his righteousness endures for ever.
111:4 He makes his marvelous works to be remembered; * the LORD is gracious and full of compassion.
111:5 He gives food to those who fear him; * he is ever mindful of his covenant.
111:6 He has shown his people the power of his works * in giving them the lands of the nations.
111:7 The works of his hands are faithfulness and justice; * all his commandments are sure.
111:8 They stand fast for ever and ever, * because they are done in truth and equity.
111:9 He sent redemption to his people; he commanded his covenant for ever; * holy and awesome is his Name.
111:10 The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom; * those who act accordingly have a good understanding; his praise endures for ever.
text.KJV:
111:1 Praise ye the Lord. I will praise the Lord with my whole heart, in the assembly of the upright, and in the congregation.
111:2 The works of the Lord are great, sought out of all them that have pleasure therein.
111:3 His work is honourable and glorious: and his righteousness endureth for ever.
111:4 He hath made his wonderful works to be remembered: the Lord is gracious and full of compassion.
111:5 He hath given meat unto them that fear him: he will ever be mindful of his covenant.
111:6 He hath shewed his people the power of his works, that he may give them the heritage of the heathen.
111:7 The works of his hands are verity and judgment; all his commandments are sure.
111:8 They stand fast for ever and ever, and are done in truth and uprightness.
111:9 He sent redemption unto his people: he hath commanded his covenant for ever: holy and reverend is his name.
111:10 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom: a good understanding have all they that do his commandments: his praise endureth for ever.
text.JPS1985:
111:1 Hallelujah. I praise the LORD with all my heart in the assembled congregation of the upright.
111:2 The works of the LORD are great, within reach of all who desire them.
111:3 His deeds are splendid and glorious; His beneficence is everlasting;
111:4 He has won renown for His wonders. The LORD is gracious and compassionate;
111:5 He gives food to those who fear Him; He is ever mindful of His covenant.
111:6 He revealed to His people His powerful works, in giving them the heritage of nations.
111:7 His handiwork is truth and justice; all His precepts are enduring,
111:8 well-founded for all eternity, wrought of truth and equity.
111:9 He sent redemption to His people; He ordained His covenant for all time; His name is holy and awesome.
111:10 The beginning of wisdom is the fear of the LORD; all who practice it gain sound understanding. Praise of Him is everlasting.
text.Grail1963:
1 ALLELUIA!
I will thank the Lord with all my heart
in the meeting of the just and their assembly.
2 Great are the works of the Lord,
to be pondered by all who love them.
3 Majestic and glorious his work,
his justice stands firm for ever.
4 He makes us remember his wonders.
The Lord is compassion and love.
5 He gives food to those who fear him;
keeps his covenant ever in mind.
6 He has shown his might to his people
by giving them the lands of the nations.
7 His works are justice and truth,
his precepts are all of them sure,
8 standing firm for ever and ever;
they are made in uprightness and truth.
9 He has sent deliverance to his people
and established his covenant for ever.
Holy his name, to be feared.
10 To fear the Lord is the first stage of wisdom;
all who do so prove themselves wise.
His praise shall last for ever!
text.Rotherham:
111:1 Praise ye Yah! I will give thanks unto Yahweh, with a whole heart, in the circle of the upright and the assembly.
111:2 Great are the works of Yahweh, sought out, by all who find pleasure therein.
111:3 Honourable and majestic, is his doing, and, his righteousness, standeth for aye.
111:4 A memorial, hath he made by his wonders, Gracious and compassionate, is Yahweh.
111:5 Food, hath he given to them who revere him, He will remember, age-abidingly, his covenant.
111:6 The might of his works, hath he declared to his people, that he may give them the inheritance of the nations.
111:7 The works of his hands, are faithful and just, Firm are all his precepts;
111:8 Upheld to futurity, to times age-abiding, done in faithfulness and equity.
111:9 Ransom, hath he sent to his people, He hath commanded, to times age-abiding, his covenant, Holy and reverend, is his Name.
111:10 The beginning of wisdom, is the reverence of Yahweh, Good discretion, have all that do them, His praise, endureth for aye.
text.NABRE:
111:1 Praise of God for Goodness to Israel Hallelujah! I will praise the Lord with all my heart in the assembled congregation of the upright.
111:2 Great are the works of the Lord, studied by all who delight in them.
111:3 Majestic and glorious is his work, his righteousness endures forever.
111:4 He won renown for his wondrous deeds; gracious and merciful is the Lord.
111:5 He gives food to those who fear him, he remembers his covenant forever.
111:6 He showed his powerful deeds to his people, giving them the inheritance of the nations.
111:7 The works of his hands are true and just, reliable all his decrees,
111:8 Established forever and ever, to be observed with truth and equity.
111:9 He sent release to his people, decreed his covenant forever; holy and fearsome is his name.
111:10 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom; prudent are all who practice it. His praise endures forever.
text.DRB:
111:1 Alleluia. Of the return of Aggeus and Zachary. Blessed is the man that feareth our Lord: he shall have great delight in his commandments.
111:2 His seed shall be mighty in the earth: the generation of the righteous shall be blessed.
111:3 Glory and riches in his house: and his justice abideth for ever and ever.
111:4 Light is risen up in darkness to the righteous: he is merciful, and pitiful, and just.
111:5 Acceptable is the man, that is merciful and lendeth, that shall dispose his words in judgment:
111:6 Because he shall not be moved for ever.
111:7 The just shall be in eternal memory: he shall not fear at the hearing of evil. His heart is ready to hope in our Lord,
111:8 his heart is confirmed: he shall not be moved till he look over his enemies.
111:9 He distributed, he gave to the poor: his justice remaineth for ever and ever, his horn shall be exalted in glory.
111:10 The sinner shall see, and will be angry, he shall gnash his teeth and pine away: the desire of sinners shall perish.

text.NRSV:
112:1 Praise the Lord! Happy are those who fear the Lord, who greatly delight in his commandments.
112:2 Their descendants will be mighty in the land; the generation of the upright will be blessed.
112:3 Wealth and riches are in their houses, and their righteousness endures forever.
112:4 They rise in the darkness as a light for the upright; they are gracious, merciful, and righteous.
112:5 It is well with those who deal generously and lend, who conduct their affairs with justice.
112:6 For the righteous will never be moved; they will be remembered forever.
112:7 They are not afraid of evil tidings; their hearts are firm, secure in the Lord.
112:8 Their hearts are steady, they will not be afraid; in the end they will look in triumph on their foes.
112:9 They have distributed freely, they have given to the poor; their righteousness endures forever; their horn is exalted in honor.
112:10 The wicked see it and are angry; they gnash their teeth and melt away; the desire of the wicked comes to nothing.
text.Coverdale:
112:1 Hallelujah! Happy are they who fear the Lord * and have great delight in his commandments!
112:2 Their descendants will be mighty in the land; * the generation of the upright will be blessed.
112:3 Wealth and riches will be in their house, * and their righteousness will last for ever.
112:4 Light shines in the darkness for the upright; * the righteous are merciful and full of compassion.
112:5 It is good for them to be generous in lending * and to manage their affairs with justice.
112:6 For they will never be shaken; * the righteous will be kept in everlasting remembrance.
112:7 They will not be afraid of any evil rumors; * their heart is right; they put their trust in the Lord.
112:8 Their heart is established and will not shrink, * until they see their desire upon their enemies.
112:9 They have given freely to the poor, * and their righteousness stands fast for ever; they will hold up their head with honor.
112:10 The wicked will see it and be angry; they will gnash their teeth and pine away; * the desires of the wicked will perish.
text.KJV:
112:1 Praise ye the Lord. Blessed is the man that feareth the Lord, that delighteth greatly in his commandments.
112:2 His seed shall be mighty upon earth: the generation of the upright shall be blessed.
112:3 Wealth and riches shall be in his house: and his righteousness endureth for ever.
112:4 Unto the upright there ariseth light in the darkness: he is gracious, and full of compassion, and righteous.
112:5 A good man sheweth favour, and lendeth: he will guide his affairs with discretion.
112:6 Surely he shall not be moved for ever: the righteous shall be in everlasting remembrance.
112:7 He shall not be afraid of evil tidings: his heart is fixed, trusting in the Lord.
112:8 His heart is established, he shall not be afraid, until he see his desire upon his enemies.
112:9 He hath dispersed, he hath given to the poor; his righteousness endureth for ever; his horn shall be exalted with honour.
112:10 The wicked shall see it, and be grieved; he shall gnash with his teeth, and melt away: the desire of the wicked shall perish.
text.JPS1985:
112:1 Hallelujah. Happy is the man who fears the LORD, who is ardently devoted to His commandments.
112:2 His descendants will be mighty in the land, a blessed generation of upright men.
112:3 Wealth and riches are in his house, and his beneficence lasts forever.
112:4 A light shines for the upright in the darkness; he is gracious, compassionate, and beneficent.
112:5 All goes well with the man who lends generously, who conducts his affairs with equity.
112:6 He shall never be shaken; the beneficent man will be remembered forever.
112:7 He is not afraid of evil tidings; his heart is firm, he trusts in the LORD.
112:8 His heart is resolute, he is unafraid; in the end he will see the fall of his foes.
112:9 He gives freely to the poor; his beneficence lasts forever; his horn is exalted in honor.
112:10 The wicked man shall see it and be vexed; he shall gnash his teeth; his courage shall fail. The desire of the wicked shall come to nothing.
text.Grail1963:
1 ALLELUIA!
Happy the man who fears the Lord,
who takes delight in all his commands.
2 His sons shall be powerful on earth;
the children of the upright are blessed.

3 Riches and wealth are in his house;
his justice stands firm for ever.
4 He is a light in the darkness for the upright:
he is generous, merciful and just.
5 The good man takes pity and lends,
he conducts his affairs with honor.
6 The just man will never waver:
he will be remembered for ever.
7 He has no fear of evil news;
with a firm heart he trusts in the Lord.
8 With a steadfast heart he will not fear;
he will see the downfall of his foes.
9 Openhanded, he gives to the poor;
his justice stands firm for ever.
His head will be raised in glory.
10 The wicked man sees and is angry,
grinds his teeth and fades away;
the desire of the wicked leads to doom.
text.NABRE:
112:1 Hallelujah! Blessed the man who fears the Lord, who greatly delights in his commands.
112:2 His descendants shall be mighty in the land, a generation of the upright will be blessed.
112:3 Wealth and riches shall be in his house; his righteousness shall endure forever.
112:4 Light shines through the darkness for the upright; gracious, compassionate, and righteous.
112:5 It is good for the man gracious in lending, who conducts his affairs with justice.
112:6 For he shall never be shaken; the righteous shall be remembered forever.
112:7 He shall not fear an ill report; his heart is steadfast, trusting the Lord.
112:8 His heart is tranquil, without fear, till at last he looks down on his foes.
112:9 Lavishly he gives to the poor; his righteousness shall endure forever; his horn shall be exalted in honor.
112:10 The wicked sees and is angry; gnashes his teeth and wastes away; the desire of the wicked come to nothing.
text.DRB:
112:1 Alleluia. Praise our Lord ye children: praise ye the name of our Lord.
112:2 Be the name of our Lord blessed, from henceforth now and for ever,
112:3 From the rising of the sun unto the going down, the name of our Lord is laudable.
112:4 Our Lord is high above all nations, and his glory above the heavens.
112:5 Who is as the Lord our God, that dwelleth on high,
112:6 and beholdeth the low things in heaven and in earth?
112:7 Raising up the needy from the earth, and lifting up the poor out of the dung:
112:8 To place him with Princes, with the Princes of his people.
112:9 Who maketh the barren woman to dwell in a house, a joyful mother of children.

text.NRSV:
113:1 Praise the Lord! Praise, O servants of the Lord; praise the name of the Lord.
113:2 Blessed be the name of the Lord from this time on and forevermore.
113:3 From the rising of the sun to its setting the name of the Lord is to be praised.
113:4 The Lord is high above all nations, and his glory above the heavens.
113:5 Who is like the Lord our God, who is seated on high,

113:8 to make them sit with princes, with the princes of his people.

text.NABRE:

113:2 Blessed be the name of the Lord both now and forever.
113:3 From the rising of the sun to its setting let the name of the Lord be praised.

text.DRB:
113:1 Alleluia. In the coming forth of Israel out of Ægypt: of the house of Jacob from the barbarous people.
113:2 Jewry was made his sanctification, Israel his dominion.
113:3 The sea saw, and fled: Jordan was turned backward.
113:4 The mountains leaped as rams: and the little hills as the lambs of sheep.
113:5 What aileth thee o sea that thou didst fly: and thou o Jordan, that thou was turned backward?
113:6 Ye mountains leaped as rams, and ye little hills as the lambs of sheep.
113:7 At the face of our Lord was the earth moved, at the face of the God of Jacob.
113:8 Who turned the rock into pools of waters, and stony hill into fountains of waters.
113:9 Not to us O Lord, Not TO US: but to thy name give the glory.
113:10 For thy mercy, and thy truth: lest at any time the Gentiles say: Where is their God?
113:11 But our God is in heaven: he hath done all things what soever he would.
113:12 The idols of the gentiles are silver, and gold, the works of men's hands.
113:13 They have mouth, and shall not speak: they have eyes, an shall not see.
113:14 They have ears, and shall not hear: they have nostrils and shall not smell.
113:15 They have hands, and shall not handle: they have feet, and shall not walk: they shall not cry in their throat.
113:16 Let them that make them become like to them: and all that have confidence in them.
113:17 The house of Israel hath hoped in our Lord: he is their helper and their protector.
113:18 The house of Aaron hath hoped in our Lord: he is their helper and their protector.
113:19 They that fear our Lord have hoped in our Lord; he is their helper and their protector.
113:20 Our Lord hath been mindful of us: and hath blessed us. He hath blessed the house of Israel: he hath blessed the house of Aaron.
113:21 He hath blessed all that fear our Lord, the little with the great.
113:22 Our Lord add upon you: upon you, & upon your children.
113:23 Blessed be you of our Lord, which made heaven and earth.
113:24 The heaven of heaven is to our Lord: but the earth he hath given to the children of men.
113:25 The dead shall not praise thee, o Lord: nor all they that go down into hell:
113:26 but we that live do bless our Lord, from this time, and for ever.

text.NRSV:
114:1 When Israel went out from Egypt, the house of Jacob from a people of strange language,
114:2 Judah became God's sanctuary, Israel his dominion.
114:3 The sea looked and fled; Jordan turned back.
114:4 The mountains skipped like rams, the hills like lambs.
114:5 Why is it, O sea, that you flee? O Jordan, that you turn back?
114:6 O mountains, that you skip like rams? O hills, like lambs?
114:7 Tremble, O earth, at the presence of the Lord, at the presence of the God of Jacob,
114:8 who turns the rock into a pool of water, the flint into a spring of water.

text.NABRE:

text.DRB:
114:1 Alleluia. I have loved, because our Lord will hear the voice of my prayer.
114:2 Because he hath inclined his ear to me: and in my days I will invocate.
114:3 The sorrows of death have compassed me: and the perils of hell have found me. I have found tribulation and sorrow:
114:4 and I invocated the name of our Lord. O Lord deliver my soul:
114:5 our Lord is merciful, and just, and our God doth mercy.
114:6 Our Lord keepeth little ones: I was humbled, and he hath delivered me.
114:7 Turn o my soul into thy rest: because our Lord hath done good to thee.
114:8 Because he hath delivered my soul from death: mine eyes from tears, my feet from sliding.
114:9 I will please our Lord in the country of the living.

text.NRSV:
115:1 Not to us, O Lord, not to us, but to your name give glory, for the sake of your steadfast love and your faithfulness.
115:2 Why should the nations say,"Where is their God?"
115:3 Our God is in the heavens; he does whatever he pleases.
115:4 Their idols are silver and gold, the work of human hands.
115:5 They have mouths, but do not speak; eyes, but do not see.
115:6 They have ears, but do not hear; noses, but do not smell.
115:7 They have hands, but do not feel; feet, but do not walk; they make no sound in their throats.
115:8 Those who make them are like them; so are all who trust in them.
115:9 O Israel, trust in the Lord! He is their help and their shield.
115:10 O house of Aaron, trust in the Lord! He is their help and their shield.
115:11 You who fear the Lord, trust in the Lord! He is their help and their shield.
115:12 The Lord has been mindful of us; he will bless us; he will bless the house of Israel; he will bless the house of Aaron;
115:13 he will bless those who fear the Lord, both small and great.
115:14 May the Lord give you increase, both you and your children.
115:15 May you be blessed by the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
115:16 The heavens are the Lord's heavens, but the earth he has given to human beings.
115:17 The dead do not praise the Lord, nor do any that go down into silence.
115:18 But we will bless the Lord from this time on and forevermore. Praise the Lord!

text.Rotherham:
115:1 Not unto us, O Yahweh, not unto us,—but, unto thine own Name, give glory, concerning thy lovingkindness, concerning thy faithfulness.
115:2 Wherefore should the nations say, Pray where is their God?
115:3 When, our God, is in the heavens, Whatsoever he pleased, hath he done.
115:4 Their idols, are silver and gold, the works of the hands of men,—
115:5 A mouth, have they, but they speak not, Eyes, have they, but they see not;
115:6 Ears, have they, but they hear not, A nose, have they, but they smell not:
115:7 Their hands! but they feel not Their feet! but they walk not, No sound make they in their throat.
115:8 Like unto them, shall be they who make them, Every one who trusteth in them.
115:9 O Israel! trust thou in Yahweh, Their help and their shield, is he!
115:10 O house of Aaron! trust ye in Yahweh, Their help and their shield, is he!
115:11 Ye that revere Yahweh! trust in Yahweh, Their help and their shield, is he!
115:12 Yahweh, hath remembered us, he will bless—He will bless the house of Israel, He will bless the house of Aaron;
115:13 He will bless them who revere Yahweh, the small with the great.
115:14 Yahweh multiply you, You, and your children.
115:15 Blessed are ye of Yahweh, who made the heavens and the earth:
115:16 As for the heavens, the heavens, belong to Yahweh, but the earth, hath he given to the sons of men.
115:17 The dead, cannot praise Yah, nor any that go down into silence;
115:18 But, we, will bless Yah, from henceforth even unto times age-abiding. Praise ye Yah.
text.NABRE:
115:1 God I Not to us, Lord, not to us but to your name give glory because of your mercy and faithfulness.
115:2 Why should the nations say, “Where is their God?”
115:3 Our God is in heaven and does whatever he wills.
115:4 II Their idols are silver and gold, the work of human hands.
115:5 They have mouths but do not speak, eyes but do not see.
115:6 They have ears but do not hear, noses but do not smell.
115:7 They have hands but do not feel, feet but do not walk; they produce no sound from their throats.
115:8 Their makers will be like them, and anyone who trusts in them.
115:9 III The house of Israel trusts in the Lord, who is their help and shield.
115:10 The house of Aaron trusts in the Lord, who is their help and shield.

115:12 The Lord remembers us and will bless us, will bless the house of Israel, will bless the house of Aaron,
115:13 Will bless those who fear the Lord, small and great alike.
115:14 May the Lord increase your number, yours and your descendants.
115:15 May you be blessed by the Lord, maker of heaven and earth.
115:16 The heavens belong to the Lord, but he has given the earth to the children of Adam.

text.DRB:
115:1 Alleluia. I believed, for which cause I spake: but I was humbled exceedingly.
115:2 Isaid in mine excess: Every man is a liar.
115:3 What shall I render to our Lord, for all things that he hath rendered to me?
115:4 I will take the chalice of salvation: and I will invocate the name of our Lord.
115:5 I will render my vows to our Lord before all his people:
115:6 precious in the sight of our Lord is the death of his Saints.
115:7 O Lord because I am thy servant: I am thy servant, and the son of thy handmaid. Thou hast broken my bonds:
115:8 I will sacrifice to thee the host of praise, and I will invocate the name of our Lord.
115:9 I will render my vows to our Lord in the sight of all his people:
115:10 in the courts of the house of our Lord, in the midst of thee, o Jerusalem.

text.NRSV:
116:1 I love the Lord, because he has heard my voice and my supplications.
116:2 Because he inclined his ear to me, therefore I will call on him as long as I live.
116:3 The snares of death encompassed me; the pangs of Sheol laid hold on me; I suffered distress and anguish.
116:4 Then I called on the name of the Lord:"O Lord, I pray, save my life!"
116:5 Gracious is the Lord, and righteous; our God is merciful.
116:6 The Lord protects the simple; when I was brought low, he saved me.
116:7 Return, O my soul, to your rest, for the Lord has dealt bountifully with you.
116:8 For you have delivered my soul from death, my eyes from tears, my feet from stumbling.
116:9 I walk before the Lord in the land of the living.
116:10 I kept my faith, even when I said,"I am greatly afflicted";
116:11 I said in my consternation,"Everyone is a liar."
116:12 What shall I return to the Lord for all his bounty to me?
116:13 I will lift up the cup of salvation and call on the name of the Lord,
116:14 I will pay my vows to the Lord in the presence of all his people.
116:15 Precious in the sight of the Lord is the death of his faithful ones.
116:16 O Lord, I am your servant; I am your servant, the child of your serving girl. You have loosed my bonds.
116:17 I will offer to you a thanksgiving sacrifice and call on the name of the Lord.
116:18 I will pay my vows to the Lord in the presence of all his people,
116:19 in the courts of the house of the Lord, in your midst, O Jerusalem. Praise the Lord!
text.Coverdale:
116:1 I love the LORD, because he has heard the voice of my supplication, * because he has inclined his ear to me whenever I called upon him.
116:2 The cords of death entangled me; the grip of the grave took hold of me; * I came to grief and sorrow.
116:3 Then I called upon the Name of the LORD: *"O LORD, I pray you, save my life."
116:4 Gracious is the LORD and righteous; * our God is full of compassion.
116:5 The LORD watches over the innocent; * I was brought very low, and he helped me.
116:6 Turn again to your rest, O my soul, * for the LORD has treated you well.
116:7 For you have rescued my life from death, * my eyes from tears, and my feet from stumbling.
116:8 I will walk in the presence of the LORD * in the land of the living.
116:9 I believed, even when I said,"I have been brought very low."* In my distress I said,"No one can be trusted."
116:10 How shall I repay the LORD * for all the good things he has done for me?
116:11 I will lift up the cup of salvation * and call upon the Name of the LORD.
116:12 I will fulfill my vows to the LORD * in the presence of all his people.
116:13 Precious in the sight of the LORD * is the death of his servants.
116:14 O LORD, I am your servant; * I am your servant and the child of your handmaid; you have freed me from my bonds.
116:15 I will offer you the sacrifice of thanksgiving * and call upon the Name of the LORD.
116:16 I will fulfill my vows to the LORD * in the presence of all his people,
116:17 In the courts of the LORD'S house, * in the midst of you, O Jerusalem. Hallelujah!
text.KJV:
116:1 I love the Lord, because he hath heard my voice and my supplications.
116:2 Because he hath inclined his ear unto me, therefore will I call upon him as long as I live.
116:3 The sorrows of death compassed me, and the pains of hell gat hold upon me: I found trouble and sorrow.
116:4 Then called I upon the name of the Lord; O Lord, I beseech thee, deliver my soul.
116:5 Gracious is the Lord, and righteous; yea, our God is merciful.
116:6 The Lord preserveth the simple: I was brought low, and he helped me.
116:7 Return unto thy rest, O my soul; for the Lord hath dealt bountifully with thee.
116:8 For thou hast delivered my soul from death, mine eyes from tears, and my feet from falling.
116:9 I will walk before the Lord in the land of the living.
116:10 I believed, therefore have I spoken: I was greatly afflicted:
116:11 I said in my haste, All men are liars.
116:12 What shall I render unto the Lord for all his benefits toward me?
116:13 I will take the cup of salvation, and call upon the name of the Lord.
116:14 I will pay my vows unto the Lord now in the presence of all his people.
116:15 Precious in the sight of the Lord is the death of his saints.
116:16 O Lord, truly I am thy servant; I am thy servant, and the son of thine handmaid: thou hast loosed my bonds.
116:17 I will offer to thee the sacrifice of thanksgiving, and will call upon the name of the Lord.
116:18 I will pay my vows unto the Lord now in the presence of all his people,
116:19 In the courts of the Lord’s house, in the midst of thee, O Jerusalem. Praise ye the Lord.
text.JPS1985:
116:1 I love the LORD for He hears my voice, my pleas;
116:2 for He turns His ear to me whenever I call.
116:3 The bonds of death encompassed me; the torments of Sheol overtook me. I came upon trouble and sorrow
116:4 and I invoked the name of the LORD, “O LORD, save my life! ”
116:5 The LORD is gracious and beneficent; our God is compassionate.
116:6 The LORD protects the simple; I was brought low and He saved me.
116:7 Be at rest, once again, O my soul, for the LORD has been good to you.
116:8 You have delivered me from death, my eyes from tears, my feet from stumbling.
116:9 I shall walk before the LORD in the lands of the living.
116:10 I trust [in the LORD]; out of great suffering I spoke
116:11 and said rashly, “All men are false.”
116:12 How can I repay the LORD for all His bounties to me?
116:13 I raise the cup of deliverance and invoke the name of the LORD.
116:14 I will pay my vows to the LORD in the presence of all His people.
116:15 The death of His faithful ones is grievous in the LORD’s sight.
116:16 O LORD, I am Your servant, Your servant, the son of Your maidservant; You have undone the cords that bound me.
116:17 I will sacrifice a thank offering to You and invoke the name of the LORD.
116:18 I will pay my vows to the LORD in the presence of all His people,
116:19 in the courts of the house of the LORD, in the midst of Jerusalem. Hallelujah.
text.Grail1963:
ALLELUIA!
1 I love the Lord for he has heard
the cry of my appeal;
2 for he turned his ear to me
in the day when I called him.
3 They surrounded me, the snares of death,
with the anguish of the tomb;
they caught me, sorrow and distress.
4 I called on the Lord's name.
O Lord, my God, deliver me!
5 How gracious is the Lord, and just;
our God has compassion.
6 The Lord protects the simple hearts;
I was helpless so he saved me.
7 Turn back, my soul, to your rest
for the Lord has been good;
8 he has kept my soul from death,
(my eyes from tears)
and my feet from stumbling.
9 I will walk in the presence of the Lord
in the land of the living.

10 I trusted, even when I said:
"I am sorely afflicted,"
11 and when I said in my alarm:
"No man can be trusted."
12 How can I repay the Lord
for his goodness to me?

13 The cup of salvation I will raise;
I will call on the Lord's name.
14 My vows to the Lord I will fulfill
before all his people.
15 O precious in the eyes of the Lord
is the death of his faithful.
16 Your servant, Lord, your servant am I;
you have loosened my bonds.
17 A thanksgiving sacrifice I make;
I will call on the Lord's name.
18 My vows to the Lord I will fulfill
before all his people,
19 in the courts of the house of the Lord,
in your midst, O Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham:
116:1 I love Yahweh—because he heareth my voice, my supplications;
116:2 Because he hath bowed down his ear unto me, therefore, throughout my days, will I call.
116:3 The meshes of death encompassed me, and the distresses of hades, came upon me, Peril and sorrow, I found;
116:4 But, on the Name of Yahweh, I called—I beseech thee, Yahweh, deliver my soul.
116:5 Gracious is Yahweh and righteous, and, our God, is full of compassion.
116:6 Yahweh, preserveth the simple, I was brought low, when, to me, he granted salvation.
116:7 Return, O my soul, to thy rest, For, Yahweh, hath dealt bountifully with thee.
116:8 For thou hast rescued my soul from death,—mine eyes from tears, my feet from stumbling.
116:9 I will walk to and fro before Yahweh, in the lands of life.
116:10 I believed that I should speak, I, was greatly depressed.
116:11 I, said in mine alarm, All men, are false!
116:12 How shall I give back to Yahweh, All his benefits unto me?
116:13 The cup of salvation, will I lift, and, on the Name of Yahweh, will I call:
116:14 My vows—to Yahweh, will I pay, Might it be in presence of all his people!
116:15 Costly in the eyes of Yahweh, is, death, for his men of lovingkindness.
116:16 I beseech thee, O Yahweh—for, I, am thy servant,—I, am thy servant, the son of thy handmaid, Thou hast loosened my bonds.
116:17 To thee, will I sacrifice a sacrifice of thanksgiving, and, on the Name of Yahweh, will I call:
116:18 My vows—to Yahweh, will I pay, Might it be in the presence of all his people;—
116:19 In the courts of the house of Yahweh, In the midst of thee, O Jerusalem. Praise ye Yah!
text.NABRE:
116:1 God Who Saves from Death I I love the Lord, who listened to my voice in supplication,
116:2 Who turned an ear to me on the day I called.
116:3 I was caught by the cords of death; the snares of Sheol had seized me; I felt agony and dread.
116:4 Then I called on the name of the Lord, “O Lord, save my life!”
116:5 II Gracious is the Lord and righteous; yes, our God is merciful.
116:6 The Lord protects the simple; I was helpless, but he saved me.
116:7 Return, my soul, to your rest; the Lord has been very good to you.
116:8 For my soul has been freed from death, my eyes from tears, my feet from stumbling.
116:9 I shall walk before the Lord in the land of the living.
116:10 III I kept faith, even when I said, “I am greatly afflicted!”
116:11 I said in my alarm, “All men are liars!”
116:12 How can I repay the Lord for all the great good done for me?
116:13 I will raise the cup of salvation and call on the name of the Lord.
116:14 I will pay my vows to the Lord in the presence of all his people.
116:15 Dear in the eyes of the Lord is the death of his devoted.
116:16 Lord, I am your servant, your servant, the child of your maidservant; you have loosed my bonds.
116:17 I will offer a sacrifice of praise and call on the name of the Lord.
116:18 I will pay my vows to the Lord in the presence of all his people,
116:19 In the courts of the house of the Lord, in your midst, O Jerusalem. Hallelujah!
text.DRB:
116:1 Alleluia. Praise our Lord all ye Gentiles: praise him all ye peoples.
116:2 Because his mercy is confirmed, upon us: and his truth remaineth for ever.

text.NRSV:

117:2 For great is his steadfast love toward us, and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever. Praise the Lord!

1 O praise the Lord, all you nations,
acclaim him all you peoples!
2 Strong is his love for us;
he is faithful for ever.
text.Rotherham:
117:1 Praise Yahweh, all ye nations, Laud him, all ye tribes of men;

text.NABRE:
117:1 Praise Praise the Lord, all you nations! Extol him, all you peoples!
