119:61 Though the bonds of the wicked are coiled round me, I have not neglected Your teaching.
119:62 I arise at midnight to praise You for Your just rules.
119:63 I am a companion to all who fear You, to those who keep Your precepts.
119:64 Your steadfast love, O LORD, fills the earth; teach me Your laws.
119:65 You have treated Your servant well, according to Your word, O LORD.
119:66 Teach me good sense and knowledge, for I have put my trust in Your commandments.
119:67 Before I was humbled I went astray, but now I keep Your word.
119:68 You are good and beneficent; teach me Your laws.
119:69 Though the arrogant have accused me falsely, I observe Your precepts wholeheartedly.
119:70 Their minds are thick like fat; as for me, Your teaching is my delight.
119:71 It was good for me that I was humbled, so that I might learn Your laws.
119:72 I prefer the teaching You proclaimed to thousands of gold and silver pieces.
119:73 Your hands made me and fashioned me; give me understanding that I may learn Your commandments.
119:74 Those who fear You will see me and rejoice, for I have put my hope in Your word.
119:75 I know, O LORD, that Your rulings are just; rightly have You humbled me.
119:76 May Your steadfast love comfort me in accordance with Your promise to Your servant.
119:77 May Your mercy reach me, that I might live, for Your teaching is my delight.
119:78 Let the insolent be dismayed, for they have wronged me without cause; I will study Your precepts.
119:79 May those who fear You, those who know Your decrees, turn again to me.
119:80 May I wholeheartedly follow Your laws so that I do not come to grief.
119:81 I long for Your deliverance; I hope for Your word.
119:82 My eyes pine away for Your promise; I say, “When will You comfort me? ”
119:83 Though I have become like a water-skin dried in smoke, I have not neglected Your laws.
119:84 How long has Your servant to live? when will You bring my persecutors to judgment?
119:85 The insolent have dug pits for me, flouting Your teaching.
119:86 All Your commandments are enduring; I am persecuted without cause; help me!
119:87 Though they almost wiped me off the earth, I did not abandon Your precepts.
119:88 As befits Your steadfast love, preserve me, so that I may keep the decree You proclaimed.
119:89 The LORD exists forever; Your word stands firm in heaven.
119:90 Your faithfulness is for all generations; You have established the earth, and it stands.
119:91 They stand this day to [carry out] Your rulings, for all are Your servants.
119:92 Were not Your teaching my delight I would have perished in my affliction.
119:93 I will never neglect Your precepts, for You have preserved my life through them.
119:94 I am Yours; save me! For I have turned to Your precepts.
119:95 The wicked hope to destroy me, but I ponder Your decrees.
119:96 I have seen that all things have their limit, but Your commandment is broad beyond measure.
119:97 O how I love Your teaching! It is my study all day long.
119:98 Your commandments make me wiser than my enemies; they always stand by me.
119:99 I have gained more insight than all my teachers, for Your decrees are my study.
119:100 I have gained more understanding than my elders, for I observe Your precepts.
119:101 I have avoided every evil way so that I may keep Your word.
119:102 I have not departed from Your rules, for You have instructed me.
119:103 How pleasing is Your word to my palate, sweeter than honey.
119:104 I ponder Your precepts; therefore I hate every false way.
119:105 Your word is a lamp to my feet, a light for my path.
119:106 I have firmly sworn to keep Your just rules.
119:107 I am very much afflicted; O LORD, preserve me in accordance with Your word.
119:108 Accept, O LORD, my freewill offerings; teach me Your rules.
119:109 Though my life is always in danger, I do not neglect Your teaching.
119:110 Though the wicked have set a trap for me, I have not strayed from Your precepts.
119:111 Your decrees are my eternal heritage; they are my heart’s delight.
119:112 I am resolved to follow Your laws to the utmost forever.
119:113 I hate men of divided heart, but I love Your teaching.
119:114 You are my protection and my shield; I hope for Your word.
119:115 Keep away from me, you evildoers, that I may observe the commandments of my God.
119:116 Support me as You promised, so that I may live; do not thwart my expectation.
119:117 Sustain me that I may be saved, and I will always muse upon Your laws.
119:118 You reject all who stray from Your laws, for they are false and deceitful.
119:119 You do away with the wicked as if they were dross; rightly do I love Your decrees.
119:120 My flesh creeps from fear of You; I am in awe of Your rulings.
119:121 I have done what is just and right; do not abandon me to those who would wrong me.
119:122 Guarantee Your servant’s well-being; do not let the arrogant wrong me.
119:123 My eyes pine away for Your deliverance, for Your promise of victory.
119:124 Deal with Your servant as befits Your steadfast love; teach me Your laws.
119:125 I am Your servant; give me understanding, that I might know Your decrees.
119:126 It is a time to act for the LORD, for they have violated Your teaching.
119:127 Rightly do I love Your commandments more than gold, even fine gold.
119:128 Truly by all [Your] precepts I walk straight; I hate every false way.
119:129 Your decrees are wondrous; rightly do I observe them.
119:130 The words You inscribed give light, and grant understanding to the simple.
119:131 I open my mouth wide, I pant, longing for Your commandments.
119:132 Turn to me and be gracious to me, as is Your rule with those who love Your name.
119:133 Make my feet firm through Your promise; do not let iniquity dominate me.
119:134 Redeem me from being wronged by man, that I may keep Your precepts.
119:135 Show favor to Your servant, and teach me Your laws.
119:136 My eyes shed streams of water because men do not obey Your teaching.
119:137 You are righteous, O LORD; Your rulings are just.
119:138 You have ordained righteous decrees; they are firmly enduring.
119:139 I am consumed with rage over my foes’ neglect of Your words.
119:140 Your word is exceedingly pure, and Your servant loves it.
119:141 Though I am belittled and despised, I have not neglected Your precepts.
119:142 Your righteousness is eternal; Your teaching is true.
119:143 Though anguish and distress come upon me, Your commandments are my delight.
119:144 Your righteous decrees are eternal; give me understanding, that I might live.
119:145 I call with all my heart; answer me, O LORD, that I may observe Your laws.
119:146 I call upon You; save me, that I may keep Your decrees.
119:147 I rise before dawn and cry for help; I hope for Your word.
119:148 My eyes greet each watch of the night, as I meditate on Your promise.
119:149 Hear my voice as befits Your steadfast love; O LORD, preserve me, as is Your rule.
119:150 Those who pursue intrigue draw near; they are far from Your teaching.
119:151 You, O LORD, are near, and all Your commandments are true.
119:152 I know from Your decrees of old that You have established them forever.
119:153 See my affliction and rescue me, for I have not neglected Your teaching.
119:154 Champion my cause and redeem me; preserve me according to Your promise.
119:155 Deliverance is far from the wicked, for they have not turned to Your laws.
119:156 Your mercies are great, O LORD; as is Your rule, preserve me.
119:157 Many are my persecutors and foes; I have not swerved from Your decrees.
119:158 I have seen traitors and loathed them, because they did not keep Your word in mind.
119:159 See that I have loved Your precepts; O LORD, preserve me, as befits Your steadfast love.
119:160 Truth is the essence of Your word; Your just rules are eternal.
119:161 Princes have persecuted me without reason; my heart thrills at Your word.
119:162 I rejoice over Your promise as one who obtains great spoil.
119:163 I hate and abhor falsehood; I love Your teaching.
119:164 I praise You seven times each day for Your just rules.
119:165 Those who love Your teaching enjoy well-being; they encounter no adversity.
119:166 I hope for Your deliverance, O LORD; I observe Your commandments.
119:167 I obey Your decrees and love them greatly.
119:168 I obey Your precepts and decrees; all my ways are before You.
119:169 May my plea reach You, O LORD; grant me understanding according to Your word.
119:170 May my petition come before You; save me in accordance with Your promise.
119:171 My lips shall pour forth praise, for You teach me Your laws.
119:172 My tongue shall declare Your promise, for all Your commandments are just.
119:173 Lend Your hand to help me, for I have chosen Your precepts.
119:174 I have longed for Your deliverance, O LORD; Your teaching is my delight.
119:175 Let me live, that I may praise You; may Your rules be my help;
119:176 I have strayed like a lost sheep; search for Your servant, for I have not neglected Your commandments.
text.Grail1963:
*Aleph (A) I
1 They are happy whose life is blameless,
who follow God's law!
2 They are happy who do his will,
seeking him with all their hearts,
3 who never do anything evil
but walk in his ways.
4 You have laid down your precepts
to be obeyed with care.
5 May my footsteps be firm
to obey your statutes.
6 Then I shall not be put to shame
as I heed your commands.
7 I will thank you with an upright heart
as I learn your decrees.
8 I will obey your statutes;
do not forsake me.
*Beth (B) II
9 How shall the young remain sinless?
By obeying your word.
10 I have sought you with all my heart;
let me not stray from your commands.
11 I treasure your promise in my heart
lest I sin against you.
12 Blessed are you, O Lord;
teach me your statutes.
13 With my tongue I have recounted
the decrees of your lips.
14 I rejoiced to do your will
as though all riches were mine.
15 I will ponder all your precepts
and consider your paths.
16 I take delight in your statutes;
I will not forget your word.
*Gimmel (G) III
17 Bless your servant and I shall live
and obey your word.
18 Open my eyes that I may see
the wonders of your law.
19 I am a pilgrim on the earth;
show me your commands.
20 My soul is ever consumed
as I long for your decrees.

21 You threaten the proud, the accursed,
who turn from your commands.
22 Relieve me from scorn and contempt
for I do your will.
23 Though the princes sit plotting against me
I ponder on your statutes.
24 Your will is my delight;
your statutes are my counselors.
*Daleth (D) IV
25 My soul lies in the dust;
by your will revive me.
26 I declared my ways and you answered;
teach me your statutes.
27 Make me grasp the way of your precepts
and I will muse on your wonders.
28 My soul pines away with grief;
by your word raise me up.
29 Keep me from the way of error
and teach me your law.
30 I have chosen the way of truth
with your decrees before me.
31 I bind myself to do your will;
Lord, do not disappoint me.
32 I will run the way of your commands;
you give freedom to my heart.
*He (H) V
33 Teach me the demands of your statutes
and I will keep them to the end.
34 Train me to observe your law,
to keep it with my heart.
35 Guide me in the path of your commands;
for there is my delight.
36 Bend my heart to your will
and not to love of gain.
37 Keep my eyes from what is false;
by your word, give me life.
38 Keep the promise you have made
to the servant who fears you.
39 Keep me from the scorn I dread,
for your decrees are good.
40 See, I long for your precepts;
then in your justice, give me life.
*Vau (V) VI
41 Lord, let your love come upon me,

the saving help of your promise.
42 And I shall answer those who taunt me
for I trust in your word.
43 Do not take the word of truth from my mouth
for I trust in your decrees.
44 I shall always keep your law
for ever and ever.
45 I shall walk in the path of freedom
for I seek your precepts.
46 I will speak of your will before kings
and not be abashed.
47 Your commands have been my delight;
these I have loved.
48 I will worship your commands and love them
and ponder your statutes.
*Zayin (Z) VII
49 Remember your word to your servant
by which you gave me hope.
50 This is my comfort in sorrow:
that your promise gives me life.
51 Though the proud may utterly deride me
I keep to your law.
52 I remember your decrees of old
and these, Lord, console me.
53 I am seized with indignation at the wicked
who forsake your law.
54 Your statutes have become my song
in the land of exile.
55 I think of your name in the nighttime
and I keep your law.
56 This has been my blessing,
the keeping of your precepts.
*Heth (CH) VIII
57 My part, I have resolved, O Lord,
is to obey your word.
58 With all my heart I implore your favor;
show the mercy of your promise.
59 I have pondered over many ways
and returned to your will.
60 I made haste and did not delay
to obey your commands.
61 Though the nets of the wicked ensnared me
I remembered your law.
62 At midnight I will rise and thank you
for your just decrees.

63 I am a friend of all who revere you,
who obey your precepts.
64 Lord, your love fills the earth.
Teach me your statutes.
*Teth (T) IX
65 Lord, you have been good to your servant
according to your word.
66 Teach me discernment and knowledge
for I trust in your commands.
67 Before I was afflicted I strayed
but now I keep your word.
68 You are good and your deeds are good;
teach me your statutes.
69 Though proud men smear me with lies
yet I keep your precepts.
70 Their minds are closed to good
but your law is my delight.
71 It was good for me to be afflicted,
to learn your statutes.
72 The law from your mouth means more to me
than silver and gold.
*Yod (Y) X
73 It was your hands that made me and shaped me:
help me to learn your commands.
74 Your faithful will see me and rejoice
for I trust in your word.
75 Lord, I know that your decrees are right,
that you afflicted me justly.
76 Let your love be ready to console me
by your promise to your servant.
77 Let your love come and I shall live
for your law is my delight.
78 Shame the proud who harm me with lies
while I ponder your precepts.
79 Let your faithful turn to me,
those who know your will.
80 Let my heart be blameless in your statutes
lest I be ashamed.
*Caph (K) XI
81 I yearn for your saving help;
I hope in your word.
82 My eyes yearn to see your promise.
When will you console me

83 Though parched and exhausted with waiting
I remember your statutes.
84 How long must your servant suffer?
When will you judge my foes?
85 For me the proud have dug pitfalls,
against your law.
86 Your commands are all true; then help me
when lies oppress me.
87 They almost made an end of me on earth,
but I kept your precepts.
88 Because of your love give me life
and I will do your will.
*Lamed (L) XII
89 Your word, O Lord, for ever
stands firm in the heavens:
90 your truth lasts from age to age,
like the earth you created.
91 By your decree it endures to this day;
for all things serve you.
92 Had your law not been my delight
I would have died in my affliction.
93 I will never forget your precepts
for with them you give me life.
94 Save me, for I am yours
since I seek your precepts.
95 Though the wicked lie in wait to destroy me
yet I ponder your will.
96 I have seen that all perfection has an end
but your command is boundless.
*Mem (M) XIII
97 Lord, how I love your law!
It is ever in my mind.
98 Your command makes me wiser than my foes;
for it is mine for ever.
99 I have more insight than all who teach me
for I ponder your will.
100 I have more understanding than the old
for I keep your precepts.
101 I turn my feet from evil paths
to obey your word.
102 I have not turned from your decrees;
you yourself have taught me.
103 Your promise is sweeter to my taste
than honey in the mouth.
104 I gain understanding from your precepts

and so I hate false ways.
*Nun (N) XIV
105 Your word is a lamp for my steps
and a light for my path.
106 I have sworn and have made up my mind
to obey your decrees.
107 Lord, I am deeply afflicted;
by your word give me life.
108 Accept, Lord, the homage of my lips
and teach me your decrees.
109 Though I carry my life in my hands,
I remember your law.
110 Though the wicked try to ensnare me,
I do not stray from your precepts.
111 Your will is my heritage for ever,
the joy of my heart.
112 I set myself to carry out your statutes
in fullness, for ever.
*Samech (S) XV
113 I have no love for halfhearted men;
my love is for your law.
114 You are my shelter, my shield;
I hope in your word.
115 Leave me, you who do evil;
I will keep God's command.
116 If you uphold me by your promise I shall live;
let my hopes not be in vain.
117 Sustain me and I shall be saved
and ever observe your statutes.
118 You spurn all who swerve from your statutes;
their cunning is in vain.
119 You throw away the wicked like dross;
so I love your will.
120 I tremble before you in terror;
I fear your decrees.
*Ayin (O) XVI
121 I have done what is right and just:
let me not be oppressed.
122 Vouch for the welfare of your servant
lest the proud oppress me.
123 My eyes yearn for your saving help
and the promise of your justice.
124 Treat your servant with love
and teach me your statutes.

125 I am your servant, give me knowledge;
then I shall know your will.
126 It is time for the Lord to act
for your law has been broken.
127 That is why I love your commands
more than finest gold,
128 why I rule my life by your precepts,
and hate false ways.
*Pe (P) XVII
129 Your will is wonderful indeed;
therefore I obey it.
130 The unfolding of your word gives light
and teaches the simple.
131 I open my mouth and I sigh
as I yearn for your commands.
132 Turn and show me your mercy;
show justice to your friends.
133 Let my steps be guided by your promise;
let no evil rule me.
134 Redeem me from man's oppression
and I will keep your precepts.
135 Let your face shine on your servant
and teach me your decrees.
136 Tears stream from my eyes
because your law is disobeyed.
*Sade (TZ) XVIII
137 Lord, you are just indeed;
your decrees are right.
138 You have imposed your will with justice
and with absolute truth.
139 I am carried away by anger
for my foes forget your word.
140 Your promise is tried in the fire,
the delight of your servant.
141 Although I am weak and despised,
I remember your precepts.
142 Your justice is eternal justice
and your law is truth.
143 Though anguish and distress have seized me,
I delight in your commands.
144 The justice of your will is eternal:
if you teach me I shall live.
*Koph (Q) XIX

145 I call with all my heart; Lord, hear me,
I will keep your statutes.
146 I call upon you, save me
and I will do your will.
147 I rise before dawn and cry for help,
I hope in your word.
148 My eyes watch through the night
to ponder your promise.
149 In your love hear my voice, O Lord;
give me life by your decrees.
150 Those who harm me unjustly draw near;
they are far from your law.
151 But you, O Lord, are close,
your commands are truth.
152 Long have I known that your will
is established for ever.
*Resh (R) XX
153 See my affliction and save me
for I remember your law.
154 Uphold my cause and defend me;
by your promise, give me life.
155 Salvation is far from the wicked
who are heedless of your statutes.
156 Numberless, Lord, are your mercies;
with your decrees give me life.
157 Though my foes and oppressors are countless
I have not swerved from your will.
158 I look at the faithless with disgust;
they ignore your promise.
159 See how I love your precepts;
in your mercy give me life.
160 Your word is founded on truth,
your decrees are eternal.
*Shin (SH) XXI
161 Though princes oppress me without cause
I stand in awe of your word.
162 I take delight in your promise
like one who finds a treasure.
163 Lies I hate and detest
but your law is my love.
164 Seven times a day I praise you
for your just decrees.
165 The lovers of your law have great peace;
they never stumble.
166 I await your saving help,

O Lord, I fulfill your commands.
167 My soul obeys your will
and loves it dearly.
168 I obey your precepts and your will;
all that I do is before you.
*Tau (TH) XXII
169 Lord, let my cry come before you:
teach me by your word.
170 Let my pleading come before you:
save me by your promise.
171 Let my lips proclaim your praise
because you teach me your statutes.
172 Let my tongue sing your promise
for your commands are just.
173 Let your hand be ready to help me,
since I have chosen your precepts.
174 Lord, I long for your saving help
and your law is my delight.
175 Give life to my soul that I may praise you.
Let your decrees give me help.
176 I am lost like a sheep; seek your servant
for I remember your commands.
text.Rotherham:
119:1 [ALEPH.] How happy the men of blameless life, who walk in the law of Yahweh.
119:2 How happy they who observe his testimonies, with a whole heart, they seek him.
119:3 Yea, they have not wrought perversity, In his ways, have they walked.
119:4 Thou, hast commanded thy precepts, that they should be diligently kept.
119:5 Oh would that my ways might be settled! that I might keep thy statutes.
119:6 Then, shall I not be ashamed, when I have respect unto all thy commandments.
119:7 I will thank thee with uprightness of heart, when I have learned thy righteous regulations.
119:8 Thy statutes, will I keep, Do not thou forsake me utterly.
119:9 [BETH.] Wherewithal can a young man keep pure his way? By taking heed, according to thy word.
119:10 With all my heart, have I sought thee, Suffer me not to be led astray from thy commandments.
119:11 In my heart, have I treasured what thou hast said, to the end I may not sin against thee.
119:12 Blessed art thou, O Yahweh—Teach me thy statutes.
119:13 With my lips, have I recounted All the regulations of thy mouth.
119:14 In the way of thy testimonies, have I rejoiced, Like as over all riches.
119:15 In thy precepts, will I meditate, that I may discern thy paths.
119:16 In thy statutes, will I find my dear delight, I will not forget thy word.
119:17 [GIMEL.] Bestow thy bounties upon thy servant—let me live, That I may observe thy word.
119:18 Unveil thou mine eyes, that I may discern Wondrous things out of thy law.
119:19 A sojourner, am, I, in the earth, Do not hide from me, thy commandments.
119:20 My soul is crushed with longing for thy just decisions at all times.
119:21 Thou hast rebuked the proud as accursed, who stray from thy commandments.
119:22 Roll from off me, reproach and contempt, For, thy testimonies, have I observed.
119:23 Even rulers have taken their seat, against me, have talked, Thy servant, will still meditate in thy statutes.
119:24 Yea, thy testimonies, are my dear delight, My counsellors.
119:25 [DALETH.] My soul, cleaveth to the dust, Give me life, according to thy word.
119:26 My ways, I recounted, and thou didst answer me, Teach me thy statutes.
119:27 The way of thy precepts, cause thou me to understand, and I will indeed meditate in thy wonders.
119:28 My soul weepeth itself away, for grief, Confirm thou me, according to thy word.
119:29 The way of falsehood, take thou from me, and, with thy law, O favour me.
119:30 The way of faithfulness, have I chosen, Thy regulations, have I deemed right.
119:31 I have kept close to thy testimonies, O Yahweh! do not put me to shame.
119:32 The way of thy commandments, will I run, for thou wilt enlarge my heart.
119:33 [HE.] Point out to me, O Yahweh, the way of thy statutes, that I may observe it unto the end.
119:34 Give me understanding, that I may observe thy law, that I may keep it with a whole heart.
119:35 Guide me in the path of thy commandments, for, therein, do I find pleasure.
119:36 Incline my heart unto thy testimonies, and not unto unjust gain.
119:37 Turn away mine eyes, from beholding vanity, In thy way, give me life.
119:38 Establish, unto thy servant, thy word, which pertaineth to the reverence of thee.
119:39 Cause to pass away my reproach, that I have feared, for, thy regulations, are good.
119:40 Lo! I have longed for thy precepts, In thy righteousness, give me life.
119:41 [WAW.] And let thy lovingkindness reach me, O Yahweh, thy salvation, according to thy word.
119:42 So shall I have something to answer him that reproacheth me, That I have trusted in thy word.
119:43 And do not snatch away from my mouth the word of truth in any wise, because, for thy regulation, have I waited.
119:44 That I may keep thy law continually, to times age-abiding and beyond.
119:45 That I may walk to and fro in a large place, because, thy precepts, have I sought.
119:46 That I may speak of thy testimonies before kings, and not be ashamed.
119:47 That I may find dear delight in thy commandments, which I have loved.
119:48 That I may lift up my hands unto thy commandments, which I have loved, and may meditate in thy statutes.
119:49 [ZAYIN.] Remember the word unto thy servant, upon which thou hast caused me to hope.
119:50 This, is my comfort in mine affliction, that, thy word, hath given me life.
119:51 Insolent men, have derided me exceedingly, From thy law, have I not swerved.
119:52 I have remembered thy regulations, [which have come down] from age-past times, O Yahweh, and have consoled myself.
119:53 A raging heat, hath seized me, by reason of the lawless, who forsake thy law.
119:54 Songs, have thy statutes become to me, in my house of sojourn.
119:55 I have remembered, in the night, thy Name, O Yahweh, and have kept thy law.
119:56 This, have I had, because, thy precepts, have I observed.
119:57 [HETH.] My portion, is Yahweh, I have promised that I would keep thy words.
119:58 I have sought the smile of thy face with all my heart, Show me favour, according to thy word.
119:59 I have thought upon my ways, and have turned my feet unto thy testimonies.
119:60 I have hastened, and not delayed, to keep thy commandments.
119:61 The meshes of the lawless, have surrounded me, Thy law, have I not forgotten.
119:62 At midnight, I arise to give thanks unto thee, For thy righteous regulations.
119:63 Companion, am I, to all who revere thee, and to them who keep thy precepts.
119:64 Of thy lovingkindness, O Yahweh, the earth, is full, Thy statutes, teach thou me.
119:65 [TETH.] Well, hast thou dealt with thy servant, O Yahweh, according to thy word.
119:66 Good judgment and knowledge, teach thou me, For, in thy commandments, have I trusted.
119:67 Before I was afflicted, I myself was going astray, but, now, thy word, have I kept.
119:68 Good, thou art, and doing good, Teach me thy statutes.
119:69 Insolent men have plastered falsehood over me, I, with a whole heart, will observe thy precepts.
119:70 Gross, like fat, is their heart, I, in thy law, have found dear delight.
119:71 It is, well for me, that I was afflicted, That I might learn thy statutes.
119:72 Better to me, is the law of thy mouth, than thousands of gold and silver.
119:73 [YODH.] Thine own hands, have made me, and formed me. Give me understanding, that I may learn thy commandments.
119:74 They who revere thee, shall see me and rejoice, that, for thy word, I waited.
119:75 I know, O Yahweh, that righteous are thy regulations, and, in faithfulness, didst thou afflict me.
119:76 Let thy lovingkindness, I beseech thee, serve to comfort me, according to thy word to thy servant.
119:77 Let thy compassions reach me, that I may live, for, thy law, is my dear delight.
119:78 Let insolent men, be ashamed, because, by means of falsehood, they have dealt with me perversely, I, will meditate in thy precepts.
119:79 Let them who revere thee, turn unto me, even they who know thy testimonies.
119:80 Let my heart be thorough in thy statutes, that I may not be ashamed.
119:81 [KAPH.] My soul, hath languished for thy salvation, For thy word, have I hoped.
119:82 Mine eyes have failed for thy word, saying, When wilt thou comfort me?
119:83 Though I have been like a wine-skin in the smoke, thy statutes, have I not forgotten.
119:84 How few are the days of thy servant! When wilt thou execute sentence on my persecutors?
119:85 Insolent men digged for me pits, men who are not according to thy law.
119:86 All thy commandments, are faithful, With falsehood, have they persecuted me, O help me!
119:87 A little more, and they had consumed me in the earth, but, I, forsake not thy precepts.
119:88 According to thy lovingkindness, give thou me life, so will I keep the testimonies of thy mouth.
119:89 [LAMED.] Age-abidingly, O Yahweh, hath thy word been set up in the heavens.
119:90 To generation after generation, is thy faithfulness, Thou hast established the earth, and it standeth.
119:91 By thy regulations, do they stand to-day, for, all, are thy servants.
119:92 Had not thy law been my dear delight, then, had I perished in mine affliction.
119:93 Unto times age-abiding, will I not forget thy precepts, For, by them, hast thou given me life.
119:94 Thine, am I,—oh save me! For, thy precepts, have I sought.
119:95 For me, have the lawless waited, to destroy me, Thy testimonies, will I diligently consider.
119:96 To all perfection, have I seen an end, Broad is thy commandment, exceedingly.
119:97 [MEM.] Oh how I love thy law! All the day, is it my meditation.
119:98 Beyond mine enemies, will thy commandment make me wise, for, age-abidingly, shall it be mine.
119:99 Beyond all my teachers, have I shown discretion, for, thy testimonies, are my meditation.
119:100 Beyond the elders, will I show understanding, for, thy precepts, have I observed.
119:101 From every way of wickedness, have I withheld my feet, that I might keep thy word.
119:102 From thy regulations, have I not turned aside, for, thou, hast directed me.
119:103 How smooth to my palate is thy speech, More than honey, to my mouth.
119:104 Out of thy precepts, will I get understanding, For this cause, do I hate every false way.
119:105 [NUN.] A lamp to my feet, is thy word, and a light to my path.
119:106 I sware, and have fulfilled, To keep thy righteous regulations.
119:107 I have been afflicted exceedingly,—O Yahweh, give me life according to thy word.
119:108 The freewill offerings of my mouth, accept, I pray thee, O Yahweh, And, thy regulations, teach thou me.
119:109 My life, is in my hand continually, Yet, thy law, have I not forgotten.
119:110 The lawless have set a snare for me, Yet, from thy precepts, have I not strayed.
119:111 As an inheritance have I taken thy testimonies unto times age-abiding, for, the joy of my heart, they are.
119:112 I have inclined my heart to perform thy statutes, Age-abidingly, to the end.
119:113 [SAMECH.] Half-hearted ones, do I hate, but, thy law, do I love.
119:114 My hiding-place and my buckler, thou art, For thy word, have I waited.
119:115 Depart from me, ye evil-doers,—that I may observe the commandments of my God.
119:116 Uphold me according to thy word, that I may live, and do not shame me out of my hope!
119:117 Sustain me, that I may be saved, and may find dear delight in thy statutes continually.
119:118 Thou hast made light of all who stray from thy statutes, for their fraud is, falsehood.
119:119 Dross, have I accounted all the lawless of the earth, therefore do I love thy testimonies.
119:120 My flesh, bristled up from dread of thee, and, of thy regulations, stand I in fear.
119:121 [AYIN.] I have done justice and righteousness,—Do not leave me to mine oppressors.
119:122 Be thou surety for thy servant for good, Let not insolent men oppress me.
119:123 Mine eyes, have become dim for thy salvation, and for thy righteous word.
119:124 Deal with thy servant according to thy lovingkindness, and, thy statutes, teach thou me.
119:125 Thy servant, I am—give me understanding, so shall I get to know thy testimonies.
119:126 It is time that Yahweh should work, They have frustrated thy law!
119:127 For this cause, do I love thy commandments, More than gold, yea than fine gold!
119:128 For this cause, all thy precepts concerning all things, I deem right, Every way of falsehood, I hate.
119:129 [PE.] Wonderful are thy testimonies, For this cause, hath my soul observed them.
119:130 The opening of thy words, sheddeth light, Giving understanding to the simple.
119:131 My mouth, have I opened wide, and panted, because, for thy commandments, have I longed.
119:132 Turn thyself unto me, and show me favour,—As is befitting, to the lovers of thy Name.
119:133 My steps, direct thou by thy word, and let no iniquity, have dominion over me.
119:134 Set me free from the oppression of man, so will I keep thy precepts.
119:135 Thy face, light thou up on thy servant, and teach me thy statutes.
119:136 Streams of water, have run down mine eyes, because men have not kept thy law.
119:137 [ZADHE.] Righteous art thou, O Yahweh,—and, equitable, are thy regulations.
119:138 Thou hast righteously commanded thy testimonies, yea in great faithfulness.
119:139 My zeal, hath put an end to me, for mine adversaries have forgotten thy words.
119:140 Refined is thy word, to the uttermost, and, thy servant loveth it.
119:141 Small, am I, and despised, Thy precepts, have I not forgotten.
119:142 Thy righteousness, is righteous to times age-abiding, and, thy law, is truth.
119:143 Straitness and distress, have befallen me, Thy commandments, are my dear delights.
119:144 Righteous are thy testimonies, unto times age-abiding, Give me understanding, that I may live.
119:145 [KOPH.] I have cried out with all my heart, answer me, O Yahweh; Thy statutes, will I observe.
119:146 I have cried out unto thee, oh save me, That I may keep thy testimonies.
119:147 I forestalled the twilight, and cried for help, For thy word, I waited.
119:148 Mine eyes forestalled the night-watches, To meditate in thy word.
119:149 My voice, O hear, according to thy lovingkindness, O Yahweh! according to thy wont, give me life.
119:150 They have drawn near, who pursue villainy, From thy law, have they gone far away.
119:151 Near art thou, O Yahweh, and, all thy commandments, are truth.
119:152 Long, have I known, from thy testimonies, That, to times age-abiding, thou didst establish them.
119:153 [RESH.] Behold mine affliction, and rescue me, For, thy law, have I not forgotten.
119:154 Plead my cause, and redeem me, By thy word, give me life
119:155 Far from the lawless, is salvation, For, thy statutes, have they not sought.
119:156 Thy compassions, are great, O Yahweh, According to thy regulations, give me life.
119:157 Many, are my persecutors and mine adversaries, From thy testimonies, have I not swerved.
119:158 I have seen traitors, and felt loathing, Because, thy word, they kept not.
119:159 See thou that, thy precepts, I have loved, O Yahweh, according to thy lovingkindness, give me life.
119:160 The sum of thy word, is truth, and, age-abiding, is every one of thy righteous regulations.
119:161 [SHIN.] Rulers, have persecuted me, without cause, But, of thy word, hath my heart stood in awe.
119:162 Joyful am I over thy word, Like the finder of spoil in abundance.
119:163 Falsehood, I hate and abhor, Thy law, do I love.
119:164 Seven times in the day, have I praised thee, For thy righteous regulations.
119:165 Blessing in abundance, have the lovers of thy law, and nothing to make them stumble.
119:166 I have looked for thy salvation, O Yahweh, and, thy commandments, have I done.
119:167 My soul hath kept thy testimonies, yea I have loved them greatly.
119:168 I have kept thy precepts, and thy testimonies, For, all my ways, are before thee.
119:169 [TAU] Let my shouting come near before thee, O Yahweh, According to thy word, give me understanding.
119:170 Let my supplication come in before thee, According to thy word, deliver me.
119:171 My lips, shall pour out, praise, When thou shalt teach me thy statutes.
119:172 My tongue, shall respond, with thy word, For, all thy commandments, are righteous.
119:173 Be thy hand ready to help me, For, thy statutes, have I chosen.
119:174 I have longed for thy salvation, O Yahweh, and, thy law, is my dear delight.
119:175 Let my soul live, that it may praise thee, So shall thy regulation help me.
119:176 I have strayed like a wandering sheep, O seek thy servant, For, thy commandments, have I not forgotten.
text.NABRE:
119:1 Blessed those whose way is blameless, who walk by the law of the Lord.
119:2 Blessed those who keep his testimonies, who seek him with all their heart.
119:3 They do no wrong; they walk in his ways.
119:4 You have given them the command to observe your precepts with care.
119:5 May my ways be firm in the observance of your statutes!
119:6 Then I will not be ashamed to ponder all your commandments.
119:7 I will praise you with sincere heart as I study your righteous judgments.
119:8 I will observe your statutes; do not leave me all alone.
119:9 Beth How can the young keep his way without fault? Only by observing your words.
119:10 With all my heart I seek you; do not let me stray from your commandments.
119:11 In my heart I treasure your promise, that I may not sin against you.
119:12 Blessed are you, O Lord; teach me your statutes.
119:13 With my lips I recite all the judgments you have spoken.
119:14 I find joy in the way of your testimonies more than in all riches.
119:15 I will ponder your precepts and consider your paths.
119:16 In your statutes I take delight; I will never forget your word.
119:17 Gimel Be kind to your servant that I may live, that I may keep your word.
119:18 Open my eyes to see clearly the wonders of your law.
119:19 I am a sojourner in the land; do not hide your commandments from me.
119:20 At all times my soul is stirred with longing for your judgments.
119:21 With a curse you rebuke the proud who stray from your commandments.
119:22 Free me from disgrace and contempt, for I keep your testimonies.
119:23 Though princes meet and talk against me, your servant meditates on your statutes.
119:24 Your testimonies are my delight; they are my counselors.
119:25 Daleth My soul clings to the dust; give me life in accord with your word.
119:26 I disclosed my ways and you answered me; teach me your statutes.
119:27 Make me understand the way of your precepts; I will ponder your wondrous deeds.
119:28 My soul is depressed; lift me up acccording to your word.
119:29 Lead me from the way of deceit; favor me with your law.
119:30 The way of loyalty I have chosen; I have kept your judgments.
119:31 I cling to your testimonies, Lord; do not let me come to shame.
119:32 I will run the way of your commandments, for you will broaden my heart.
119:33 He Lord, teach me the way of your statutes; I shall keep them with care.
119:34 Give me understanding to keep your law, to observe it with all my heart.
119:35 Lead me in the path of your commandments, for that is my delight.
119:36 Direct my heart toward your testimonies and away from gain.
119:37 Avert my eyes from what is worthless; by your way give me life.
119:38 For your servant, fulfill your promise made to those who fear you.
119:39 Turn away from me the taunts I dread, for your judgments are good.
119:40 See how I long for your precepts; in your righteousness give me life.
119:41 Waw Let your mercy come to me, Lord, salvation in accord with your promise.
119:42 Let me answer my taunters with a word, for I trust in your word.
119:43 Do not take the word of truth from my mouth, for in your judgments is my hope.
119:44 I will keep your law always, for all time and forever.
119:45 I will walk freely in an open space because I cherish your precepts.
119:46 I will speak openly of your testimonies without fear even before kings.
119:47 I delight in your commandments, which I dearly love.
119:48 I lift up my hands to your commandments; I study your statutes, which I love.
119:49 Zayin Remember your word to your servant by which you give me hope.
119:50 This is my comfort in affliction, your promise that gives me life.
119:51 Though the arrogant utterly scorn me, I do not turn from your law.
119:52 When I recite your judgments of old I am comforted, Lord.
119:53 Rage seizes me because of the wicked; they forsake your law.
119:54 Your statutes become my songs wherever I make my home.
119:55 Even at night I remember your name in observance of your law, Lord.
119:56 This is my good fortune, for I have kept your precepts.
119:57 Heth My portion is the Lord; I promise to observe your words.
119:58 I entreat you with all my heart: have mercy on me in accord with your promise.
119:59 I have examined my ways and turned my steps to your testimonies.
119:60 I am prompt, I do not hesitate in observing your commandments.
119:61 Though the snares of the wicked surround me, your law I do not forget.
119:62 At midnight I rise to praise you because of your righteous judgments.
119:63 I am the friend of all who fear you, of all who observe your precepts.
119:64 The earth, Lord, is filled with your mercy; teach me your statutes.
119:65 Teth You have treated your servant well, according to your word, O Lord.
119:66 Teach me wisdom and knowledge, for in your commandments I trust.
119:67 Before I was afflicted I went astray, but now I hold to your promise.
119:68 You are good and do what is good; teach me your statutes.
119:69 The arrogant smear me with lies, but I keep your precepts with all my heart.
119:70 Their hearts are gross and fat; as for me, your law is my delight.
119:71 It was good for me to be afflicted, in order to learn your statutes.
119:72 The law of your mouth is more precious to me than heaps of silver and gold.
119:73 Yodh Your hands made me and fashioned me; give me understanding to learn your commandments.
119:74 Those who fear you rejoice to see me, because I hope in your word.
119:75 I know, Lord, that your judgments are righteous; though you afflict me, you are faithful.
119:76 May your mercy comfort me in accord with your promise to your servant.
119:77 Show me compassion that I may live, for your law is my delight.
119:78 Shame the proud for leading me astray with falsehood, that I may study your testimonies.
119:79 Let those who fear you turn to me, those who acknowledge your testimonies.
119:80 May I be wholehearted toward your statutes, that I may not be put to shame.
119:81 Kaph My soul longs for your salvation; I put my hope in your word.
119:82 My eyes long to see your promise. When will you comfort me?
119:83 I am like a wineskin shriveled by smoke, but I have not forgotten your statutes.
119:84 How long can your servant survive? When will your judgment doom my foes?
119:85 The arrogant have dug pits for me; defying your law.
119:86 All your commandments are steadfast. Help me! I am pursued without cause.
119:87 They have almost put an end to me on earth, but I do not forsake your precepts.
119:88 In your mercy give me life, to observe the testimonies of your mouth.
119:89 Lamedh Your word, Lord, stands forever; it is firm as the heavens.
119:90 Through all generations your truth endures; fixed to stand firm like the earth.
119:91 By your judgments they stand firm to this day, for all things are your servants.
119:92 Had your law not been my delight, I would have perished in my affliction.
119:93 I will never forget your precepts; through them you give me life.
119:94 I am yours; save me, for I cherish your precepts.
119:95 The wicked hope to destroy me, but I seek to understand your testimonies.
119:96 I have seen the limits of all perfection, but your commandment is without bounds.
119:97 Mem How I love your law, Lord! I study it all day long.
119:98 Your commandment makes me wiser than my foes, as it is forever with me.
119:99 I have more insight than all my teachers, because I ponder your testimonies.
119:100 I have more understanding than my elders, because I keep your precepts.
119:101 I keep my steps from every evil path, that I may observe your word.
119:102 From your judgments I do not turn, for you have instructed me.
119:103 How sweet to my tongue is your promise, sweeter than honey to my mouth!
119:104 Through your precepts I gain understanding; therefore I hate all false ways.
119:105 Nun Your word is a lamp for my feet, a light for my path.
119:106 I make a solemn vow to observe your righteous judgments.
119:107 I am very much afflicted, Lord; give me life in accord with your word.
119:108 Accept my freely offered praise; Lord, teach me your judgments.
119:109 My life is always at risk, but I do not forget your law.
119:110 The wicked have set snares for me, but from your precepts I do not stray.
119:111 Your testimonies are my heritage forever; they are the joy of my heart.
119:112 My heart is set on fulfilling your statutes; they are my reward forever.
119:113 Samekh I hate every hypocrite; your law I love.
119:114 You are my refuge and shield; in your word I hope.
119:115 Depart from me, you wicked, that I may keep the commandments of my God.
119:116 Sustain me by your promise that I may live; do not disappoint me in my hope.
119:117 Strengthen me that I may be safe, ever to contemplate your statutes.
119:118 You reject all who stray from your statutes, for vain is their deceit.
119:119 Like dross you regard all the wicked on earth; therefore I love your testimonies.
119:120 My flesh shudders with dread of you; I fear your judgments.
119:121 Ayin I have fulfilled your righteous judgment; do not abandon me to my oppressors.
119:122 Guarantee your servant’s welfare; do not let the arrogant oppress me.
119:123 My eyes long to see your salvation and the promise of your righteousness.
119:124 Act with mercy toward your servant; teach me your statutes.
119:125 I am your servant; give me discernment that I may know your testimonies.
119:126 It is time for the Lord to act; they have disobeyed your law.
119:127 Truly I love your commandments more than gold, more than the finest gold.
119:128 Thus, I follow all your precepts; every wrong way I hate.
119:129 Pe Wonderful are your testimonies; therefore I keep them.
119:130 The revelation of your words sheds light, gives understanding to the simple.
119:131 I sigh with open mouth, yearning for your commandments.
119:132 Turn to me and be gracious, according to your judgment for those who love your name.
119:133 Steady my feet in accord with your promise; do not let iniquity lead me.
119:134 Free me from human oppression, that I may observe your precepts.
119:135 Let your face shine upon your servant; teach me your statutes.
119:136 My eyes shed streams of tears because your law is not observed.
119:137 Sadhe You are righteous, Lord, and just are your judgments.
119:138 You have given your testimonies in righteousness and in surpassing faithfulness.
119:139 I am consumed with rage, because my foes forget your words.
119:140 Your servant loves your promise; it has been proved by fire.
119:141 Though belittled and despised, I do not forget your precepts.
119:142 Your justice is forever right, your law true.
119:143 Though distress and anguish come upon me, your commandments are my delight.
119:144 Your testimonies are forever righteous; give me understanding that I may live.
119:145 I call with all my heart, O Lord; answer me that I may keep your statutes.
119:146 I call to you to save me that I may observe your testimonies.
119:147 I rise before dawn and cry out; I put my hope in your words.
119:148 My eyes greet the night watches as I meditate on your promise.
119:149 Hear my voice in your mercy, O Lord; by your judgment give me life.
119:150 Malicious persecutors draw near me; they are far from your law.
119:151 You are near, O Lord; reliable are all your commandments.
119:152 Long have I known from your testimonies that you have established them forever.
119:153 Resh Look at my affliction and rescue me, for I have not forgotten your law.
119:154 Take up my cause and redeem me; for the sake of your promise give me life.
119:155 Salvation is far from sinners because they do not cherish your statutes.
119:156 Your compassion is great, O Lord; in accord with your judgments, give me life.
119:157 Though my persecutors and foes are many, I do not turn from your testimonies.
119:158 I view the faithless with loathing because they do not heed your promise.
119:159 See how I love your precepts, Lord; in your mercy give me life.
119:160 Your every word is enduring; all your righteous judgments are forever.
119:161 Shin Princes persecute me without reason, but my heart reveres only your word.
119:162 I rejoice at your promise, as one who has found rich spoil.
119:163 Falsehood I hate and abhor; your law I love.
119:164 Seven times a day I praise you because your judgments are righteous.
119:165 Lovers of your law have much peace; for them there is no stumbling block.
119:166 I look for your salvation, Lord, and I fulfill your commandments.
119:167 I observe your testimonies; I love them very much.
119:168 I observe your precepts and testimonies; all my ways are before you.
119:169 Taw Let my cry come before you, Lord; in keeping with your word, give me understanding.
119:170 Let my prayer come before you; rescue me according to your promise.
119:171 May my lips pour forth your praise, because you teach me your statutes.
119:172 May my tongue sing of your promise, for all your commandments are righteous.
119:173 Keep your hand ready to help me, for I have chosen your precepts.
119:174 I long for your salvation, Lord; your law is my delight.
119:175 Let my soul live to praise you; may your judgments give me help.
119:176 I have wandered like a lost sheep; seek out your servant, for I do not forget your commandments.
text.DRB:
119:1 Agradual Canticle. When I was in tribulation I cried to our Lord: and he heard me.
119:2 O Lord deliver my soul from unjust lips, and from a deceitful tongue.
119:3 What may be given thee, or what may be added unto thee to a deceitful tongue?
119:4 The sharp arrows of the mighty, with coals of desolation.
119:5 Woe is to me, that my sojourning is prolonged: I have dwelt with the inhabitants of Cedar:
119:6 My soul hath been long a sojourner.
119:7 With them that hated peace I was peaceable: when I spake to them, they impugned me without cause.

text.NRSV:
120:1 In my distress I cry to the Lord, that he may answer me:
120:2 "Deliver me, O Lord, from lying lips, from a deceitful tongue."
120:3 What shall be given to you? And what more shall be done to you, you deceitful tongue?
120:4 A warrior's sharp arrows, with glowing coals of the broom tree!
120:5 Woe is me, that I am an alien in Meshech, that I must live among the tents of Kedar.
120:6 Too long have I had my dwelling among those who hate peace.
120:7 I am for peace; but when I speak, they are for war.
text.Coverdale:
120:1 When I was in trouble, I called to the LORD; * I called to the LORD, and he answered me.
120:2 Deliver me, O LORD, from lying lips * and from the deceitful tongue.
120:3 What shall be done to you, and what more besides, * O you deceitful tongue?
120:4 The sharpened arrows of a warrior, * along with hot glowing coals.
120:5 How hateful it is that I must lodge in Meshech * and dwell among the tents of Kedar!
120:6 Too long have I had to live * among the enemies of peace.
120:7 I am on the side of peace, * but when I speak of it, they are for war.
text.KJV:
120:1 A Song of degrees. In my distress I cried unto the Lord, and he heard me.
120:2 Deliver my soul, O Lord, from lying lips, and from a deceitful tongue.
120:3 What shall be given unto thee? or what shall be done unto thee, thou false tongue?
120:4 Sharp arrows of the mighty, with coals of juniper.
120:5 Woe is me, that I sojourn in Mesech, that I dwell in the tents of Kedar!
120:6 My soul hath long dwelt with him that hateth peace.
120:7 I am for peace: but when I speak, they are for war.
text.JPS1985:
120:1 A song of ascents. In my distress I called to the LORD and He answered me.
120:2 O LORD, save me from treacherous lips, from a deceitful tongue!
120:3 What can you profit, what can you gain, O deceitful tongue?
120:4 A warrior’s sharp arrows, with hot coals of broom-wood.
120:5 Woe is me, that I live with Meshech, that I dwell among the clans of Kedar.
120:6 Too long have I dwelt with those who hate peace.
120:7 I am all peace; but when I speak, they are for war.
text.Grail1963:
1 To the Lord in the hour of my distress
I call and he answers me.
2 "O Lord, save my soul from lying lips,
from the tongue of the deceitful."
3 What shall he repay you in return,
O treacherous tongue?
4 The warriors arrows sharpened
and coals, redhot, blazing.
5 (Alas, that I abide a stranger in Meshech,
dwell among the tents of Kedar!)
6 Long enough have I been dwelling
with those who hate peace.
7 I am for peace, but when I speak,
they are for fighting.
text.Rotherham:
120:1 Unto Yahweh, in the distress that befell me, I cried—and he answered me.
120:2 O Yahweh! rescue thou my soul—from the false lip, from the deceitful tongue.
120:3 What shall be given to thee, and what shall be added to thee, thou deceitful tongue?
120:4 The arrows of the hero sharpened, with burning coals of broom.
120:5 Woe is me, That I sojourn in Meshek,—That I abide near the tents of Kedar!
120:6 Long, hath my soul had her dwelling with him that hateth peace:
120:7 I, am for peace, and verily I speak, They, are for war!
text.NABRE:
120:1 A song of ascents. The Lord answered me when I called in my distress:
120:2 Lord, deliver my soul from lying lips, from a treacherous tongue.
120:3 What will he inflict on you, O treacherous tongue, and what more besides?
120:4 A warrior’s arrows sharpened with coals of brush wood!
120:5 Alas, I am a foreigner in Meshech, I live among the tents of Kedar!
120:6 Too long do I live among those who hate peace.
120:7 When I speak of peace, they are for war.
text.DRB:
120:1 Agradual Canticle. I have lifted up mine eyes unto the mountains, from whence help shall come to me.
120:2 My help is from our Lord, who made heaven and earth.
120:3 Give he not thy foot to be moved: neither do he slumber that keepeth thee.
120:4 Lo he shall not slumber nor sleep, that keepeth Israel.
120:5 Our Lord keepeth thee, our Lord is thy protection, upon thy right hand.
120:6 By day the sun shall not burn thee: nor the moon by night.
120:7 Our Lord doth keep thee from all evil: our Lord keep thy soul.
120:8 Our Lord keep thy coming in, and thy going out: from hence forth now, and for ever.

text.NRSV:
121:1 I lift up my eyes to the hills— from where will my help come?
121:2 My help comes from the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
121:3 He will not let your foot be moved; he who keeps you will not slumber.
121:4 He who keeps Israel will neither slumber nor sleep.
121:5 The Lord is your keeper; the Lord is your shade at your right hand.
121:6 The sun shall not strike you by day, nor the moon by night.
121:7 The Lord will keep you from all evil; he will keep your life.
121:8 The Lord will keep your going out and your coming in from this time on and forevermore.
text.Coverdale:
121:1 I lift up my eyes to the hills; * from where is my help to come?
121:2 My help comes from the LORD, * the maker of heaven and earth.
121:3 He will not let your foot be moved * and he who watches over you will not fall asleep.
121:4 Behold, he who keeps watch over Israel * shall neither slumber nor sleep;
121:5 The LORD himself watches over you; * the LORD is your shade at your right hand,
121:6 So that the sun shall not strike you by day, * nor the moon by night.
121:7 The LORD shall preserve you from all evil; * it is he who shall keep you safe.
121:8 The LORD shall watch over your going out and your coming in, * from this time forth for evermore.
text.KJV:
121:1 A Song of degrees. I will lift up mine eyes unto the hills, from whence cometh my help.
121:2 My help cometh from the Lord, which made heaven and earth.
121:3 He will not suffer thy foot to be moved: he that keepeth thee will not slumber.
121:4 Behold, he that keepeth Israel shall neither slumber nor sleep.
121:5 The Lord is thy keeper: the Lord is thy shade upon thy right hand.
121:6 The sun shall not smite thee by day, nor the moon by night.
121:7 The Lord shall preserve thee from all evil: he shall preserve thy soul.
121:8 The Lord shall preserve thy going out and thy coming in from this time forth, and even for evermore.
text.JPS1985:
121:1 A song for ascents. I turn my eyes to the mountains; from where will my help come?
121:2 My help comes from the LORD, maker of heaven and earth.
121:3 He will not let your foot give way; your guardian will not slumber;
121:4 See, the guardian of Israel neither slumbers nor sleeps!
121:5 The LORD is your guardian, the LORD is your protection at your right hand.
121:6 By day the sun will not strike you, nor the moon by night.
121:7 The LORD will guard you from all harm; He will guard your life.
121:8 The LORD will guard your going and coming now and forever.
text.Grail1963:
1 I lift up my eyes to the mountains;
from where shall come my help?

2 My help shall come from the Lord
who made heaven and earth.
3 May he never allow you to stumble!
Let him sleep not, your guard.
4 No, he sleeps not nor slumbers,
Israel's guard.
5 The Lord is your guard and your shade;
at your right side he stands.
6 By day the sun shall not smite you
nor the moon in the night.
7 The Lord will guard you from evil,
he will guard your soul.
8 The Lord will guard your going and coming
both now and for ever.
text.Rotherham:
121:1 I will lift up mine eyes, unto the mountains, From whence cometh my help!
121:2 My help, is from Yahweh, who made heavens and earth.
121:3 May he not suffer thy foot, to slip, May thy keeper, not slumber!
121:4 Lo! neither will slumber nor sleep, The keeper of Israel.
121:5 Yahweh, is thy keeper, Yahweh, is thy shade, on thy right hand:
121:6 By day, the sun, shall not smite, nor, the moon, by night.
121:7 Yahweh, will keep thee from all harm, He will keep thy life.
121:8 Yahweh, will keep thy going out and thy coming in, from henceforth, even unto times age-abiding.
text.NABRE:
121:1 A song of ascents. I raise my eyes toward the mountains. From whence shall come my help?
121:2 My help comes from the Lord, the maker of heaven and earth.
121:3 He will not allow your foot to slip; or your guardian to sleep.
121:4 Behold, the guardian of Israel never slumbers nor sleeps.
121:5 The Lord is your guardian; the Lord is your shade at your right hand.
121:6 By day the sun will not strike you, nor the moon by night.
121:7 The Lord will guard you from all evil; he will guard your soul.
121:8 The Lord will guard your coming and going both now and forever.
text.DRB:
121:1 Agradual Canticle. I rejoiced in these things, which were said to me: We shall go into the house of our Lord.
121:2 Our feet were standing, in thy courts, o Jerusalem.
121:3 Jerusalem, which is built as a city: whose participation is together in it-self.
121:4 For thither did the tribes ascend, the tribes of our Lord: the testimony of Israel to confess unto the name of our Lord.
121:5 Because seats sat there in judgment, seats upon the house of David.
121:6 Ask ye the things that are for the peace of Jerusalem: and aboundance to them that love thee.
121:7 Peace be made in thy strength: and aboundance in thy towers.
121:8 For my brethren, and my neighbours' sakes, I spake peace of thee:
121:9 For the house of our Lord God, I have sought good things to thee.

text.NRSV:
122:1 I was glad when they said to me,"Let us go to the house of the Lord!"
122:2 Our feet are standing within your gates, O Jerusalem.
122:3 Jerusalem—built as a city that is bound firmly together.
122:4 To it the tribes go up, the tribes of the Lord, as was decreed for Israel, to give thanks to the name of the Lord.
122:5 For there the thrones for judgment were set up, the thrones of the house of David.
122:6 Pray for the peace of Jerusalem:"May they prosper who love you.
122:7 Peace be within your walls, and security within your towers."
122:8 For the sake of my relatives and friends I will say,"Peace be within you."
122:9 For the sake of the house of the Lord our God, I will seek your good.
text.Coverdale:
122:1 I was glad when they said to me, *"Let us go to the house of the LORD."
122:2 Now our feet are standing * within your gates, O Jerusalem.
122:3 Jerusalem is built as a city * that is at unity with itself;
122:4 To which the tribes go up, the tribes of the LORD, * the assembly of Israel, to praise the Name of the LORD.
122:5 For there are the thrones of judgment, * the thrones of the house of David.
122:6 Pray for the peace of Jerusalem: *"May they prosper who love you.
122:7 Peace be within your walls * and quietness within your towers.
122:8 For my brethren and companions'sake, * I pray for your prosperity.
122:9 Because of the house of the LORD our God, * I will seek to do you good."
text.KJV:
122:1 A Song of degrees of David. I was glad when they said unto me, Let us go into the house of the Lord.
122:2 Our feet shall stand within thy gates, O Jerusalem.
122:3 Jerusalem is builded as a city that is compact together:
122:4 Whither the tribes go up, the tribes of the Lord, unto the testimony of Israel, to give thanks unto the name of the Lord.
122:5 For there are set thrones of judgment, the thrones of the house of David.
122:6 Pray for the peace of Jerusalem: they shall prosper that love thee.
122:7 Peace be within thy walls, and prosperity within thy palaces.
122:8 For my brethren and companions’ sakes, I will now say, Peace be within thee.
122:9 Because of the house of the Lord our God I will seek thy good.
text.JPS1985:
122:1 A song of ascents. Of David. I rejoiced when they said to me, “We are going to the House of the LORD.”
122:2 Our feet stood inside your gates, O Jerusalem,
122:3 Jerusalem built up, a city knit together,
122:4 to which tribes would make pilgrimage, the tribes of the LORD, —as was enjoined upon Israel— to praise the name of the LORD.
122:5 There the thrones of judgment stood, thrones of the house of David.
122:6 Pray for the well-being of Jerusalem; “May those who love you be at peace.
122:7 May there be well-being within your ramparts, peace in your citadels.”
122:8 For the sake of my kin and friends, I pray for your well-being;
122:9 for the sake of the house of the LORD our God, I seek your good.
text.Grail1963:
1 I rejoiced when I heard them say:
"Let us go to God's house."
2 And now our feet are standing
within your gates, O Jerusalem.
3 Jerusalem is built as a city
strongly compact.
4 It is there that the tribes go up,
the tribes of the Lord.
For Israel's law it is,
there to praise the Lord's name.
5 There were set the thrones of judgment
of the house of David.
6 For the peace of Jerusalem pray:
"Peace be to your homes!
7 May peace reign in your walls,
in your palaces, peace!"
8 For love of my brethren and friends
I say: "Peace upon you!"
9 For love of the house of the Lord
I will ask for your good.
text.Rotherham:
122:1 I was glad, when they were saying unto me, Unto the house of Yahweh, let us go!
122:2 Standing are our feet, within thy gates, O Jerusalem!
122:3 Jerusalem! that hath been builded, A true city, all joined together as one:
122:4 Whither have come up the tribes, The tribes of Yah, A testimony to Israel, To give thanks unto the Name of Yahweh:
122:5 For there are set—Thrones for justice, Thrones for the house David.
122:6 Ask ye for the peace of Jerusalem, They shall prosper, who love thee!
122:7 Peace be within thy walls, prosperity within thy palaces:
122:8 For the sake of my brethren and friends, Oh, might I speak [saying], Peace be within thee!
122:9 For the sake of the house of Yahweh our God, will I seek blessing for thee.
text.NABRE:
122:1 A song of ascents. Of David. I rejoiced when they said to me, “Let us go to the house of the Lord.”
122:2 And now our feet are standing within your gates, Jerusalem.
122:3 Jerusalem, built as a city, walled round about.
122:4 There the tribes go up, the tribes of the Lord, As it was decreed for Israel, to give thanks to the name of the Lord.
122:5 There are the thrones of justice, the thrones of the house of David.
122:6 II For the peace of Jerusalem pray: “May those who love you prosper!
122:7 May peace be within your ramparts, prosperity within your towers.”
122:8 For the sake of my brothers and friends I say, “Peace be with you.”
122:9 For the sake of the house of the Lord, our God, I pray for your good.
text.DRB:
122:1 Agradual Canticle. To thee have I lifted up mine eyes, which dwellest in the heavens.
122:2 Behold as the eyes of servants, are on the hands of their masters, As the eyes of the handmaid on the hands of her mistress: so are our eyes to our Lord God until he have mercy on us.
122:3 Have mercy on us, o Lord, have mercy on us: because we are much replenished with contempt.
122:4 Because our soul is much replenished: reproach to them that abound, and contempt to the proud.

text.NRSV:
123:1 To you I lift up my eyes, O you who are enthroned in the heavens!
123:2 As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, as the eyes of a maid to the hand of her mistress, so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until he has mercy upon us.
123:3 Have mercy upon us, O Lord, have mercy upon us, for we have had more than enough of contempt.
123:4 Our soul has had more than its fill of the scorn of those who are at ease, of the contempt of the proud.
text.Coverdale:
123:1 To you I lift up my eyes, * to you enthroned in the heavens.
123:2 As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their masters, * and the eyes of a maid to the hand of her mistress,
123:3 So our eyes look to the LORD our God, * until he show us his mercy.
123:4 Have mercy upon us, O LORD, have mercy, * for we have had more than enough of contempt,
123:5 Too much of the scorn of the indolent rich, * and of the derision of the proud.
text.KJV:
123:1 A Song of degrees. Unto thee lift I up mine eyes, O thou that dwellest in the heavens.
123:2 Behold, as the eyes of servants look unto the hand of their masters, and as the eyes of a maiden unto the hand of her mistress; so our eyes wait upon the Lord our God, until that he have mercy upon us.
123:3 Have mercy upon us, O Lord, have mercy upon us: for we are exceedingly filled with contempt.
123:4 Our soul is exceedingly filled with the scorning of those that are at ease, and with the contempt of the proud.
text.JPS1985:
123:1 A song of ascents. To You, enthroned in heaven, I turn my eyes.
123:2 As the eyes of slaves follow their master’s hand, as the eyes of a slave-girl follow the hand of her mistress, so our eyes are toward the LORD our God, awaiting His favor.
123:3 Show us favor, O LORD, show us favor! We have had more than enough of contempt.
123:4 Long enough have we endured the scorn of the complacent, the contempt of the haughty.
text.Grail1963:
1 To you have I lifted up my eyes,
you who dwell in the heavens;
2 my eyes, like the eyes of slaves
on the hand of their lords.
Like the eyes of a servant
on the hand of her mistress,

3 so our eyes are on the Lord our God
till he show us his mercy.
4 Have mercy on us, Lord, have mercy.
We are filled with contempt.
5 Indeed all too full is our soul
with the scorn of the rich,
(with the proud man's disdain).
text.Rotherham:
123:1 Unto thee, have I lifted up mine eyes, O thou who art enthroned in the heavens.
123:2 Lo! as the eyes of men-servants are unto the hand of their masters, as the eyes of a maid-servant, unto the hand of her mistress, so, are our eyes, unto Yahweh our God, until that he show us favour.
123:3 Show us favour, O Yahweh, show us favour, for, exceedingly, are we sated with contempt:
123:4 Exceeding sated therewith, is our soul,—The scorn of the careless, The contempt of the proud.
text.NABRE:
123:1 A song of ascents. To you I raise my eyes, to you enthroned in heaven.
123:2 Yes, like the eyes of servants on the hand of their masters, Like the eyes of a maid on the hand of her mistress, So our eyes are on the Lord our God, till we are shown favor.
123:3 Show us favor, Lord, show us favor, for we have our fill of contempt.
123:4 Our souls are more than sated with mockery from the insolent, with contempt from the arrogant.
text.DRB:
123:1 Agradual Canticle. But that our Lord was in us, let Israel now say,
123:2 but that our Lord was in us. When men rose up against us,
123:3 perhaps they had swallowed us alive: When their fury was angry against us,
123:4 perhaps water had swallowed us.
123:5 Our soul hath passed through a torrent: perhaps our soul had passed through an intolerable water.
123:6 Blessed be our Lord which hath not given us for a prey to their teeth.
123:7 Our soul as a sparrow is delivered from the snare of the fowlers: The snare is broken, and we are delivered.
123:8 Our help is in the name of our Lord, who made heaven and earth.

text.NRSV:
124:1 If it had not been the Lord who was on our side—let Israel now say—
124:2 if it had not been the Lord who was on our side, when our enemies attacked us,
124:3 then they would have swallowed us up alive, when their anger was kindled against us;
124:4 then the flood would have swept us away, the torrent would have gone over us;
124:5 then over us would have gone the raging waters.
124:6 Blessed be the Lord, who has not given us as prey to their teeth.
124:7 We have escaped like a bird from the snare of the fowlers; the snare is broken, and we have escaped.
124:8 Our help is in the name of the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
text.KJV:
124:1 A Song of degrees of David. If it had not been the Lord who was on our side, now may Israel say;
124:2 If it had not been the Lord who was on our side, when men rose up against us:
124:3 Then they had swallowed us up quick, when their wrath was kindled against us:
124:4 Then the waters had overwhelmed us, the stream had gone over our soul:
124:5 Then the proud waters had gone over our soul.
124:6 Blessed be the Lord, who hath not given us as a prey to their teeth.
124:7 Our soul is escaped as a bird out of the snare of the fowlers: the snare is broken, and we are escaped.
124:8 Our help is in the name of the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
text.JPS1985:
124:1 A song of ascents. Of David. Were it not for the LORD, who was on our side, let Israel now declare,
124:2 were it not for the LORD, who was on our side when men assailed us,
124:3 they would have swallowed us alive in their burning rage against us;
124:4 the waters would have carried us off, the torrent would have swept over us;
124:5 over us would have swept the seething waters.
124:6 Blessed is the LORD, who did not let us be ripped apart by their teeth.
124:7 We are like a bird escaped from the fowler’s trap; the trap broke and we escaped.
124:8 Our help is the name of the LORD, maker of heaven and earth.
text.Grail1963:
1 "If the Lord had not been on our side,"
this is Israel's song.
2 "If the Lord had not been on our side
when men rose up against us,
3 then would they have swallowed us alive
when their anger was kindled.
4 Then would the waters have engulfed us,
the torrent gone over us;
5 over our head would have swept
the raging waters."
6 Blessed be the Lord who did not give us
a prey to their teeth!
7 Our life, like a bird, has escaped
from the snare of the fowler.
Indeed the snare has been broken
and we have escaped.
8 Our help is in the name of the Lord,
who made heaven and earth.
text.Rotherham:
124:1 If it had not been, Yahweh, who was on our side, oh might Israel say:
124:2 If it had not been, Yahweh, who was on our side, when men rose up against us,
124:3 Then, alive, had they swallowed us up, in the glow of their anger against us;
124:4 Then, the waters, had whelmed us, the torrent, gone over our soul;
124:5 Then, had gone over our soul the waters so proud!
124:6 Blessed, be Yahweh, who gave us not as prey to their teeth.
124:7 Our soul, as a bird, hath escaped from the snare of the fowlers, The snare, is broken, and, we, are escaped:
124:8 Our help, is in the Name of Yahweh, who made heaven and earth.
text.NABRE:
124:1 A song of ascents. Of David. Had not the Lord been with us, let Israel say,
124:2 Had not the Lord been with us, when people rose against us,
124:3 Then they would have swallowed us alive, for their fury blazed against us.
124:4 Then the waters would have engulfed us, the torrent overwhelmed us;
124:5 then seething water would have drowned us.
124:6 Blessed is the Lord, who did not leave us to be torn by their teeth.
124:7 We escaped with our lives like a bird from the fowler’s snare; the snare was broken, and we escaped.
124:8 Our help is in the name of the Lord, the maker of heaven and earth.
text.DRB:
124:1 Agradual Canticle. They that trust in our Lord, as mount Sion: he shall not be moved for ever that dwelleth
124:2 in Jerusalem. Mountains round about it: and our Lord round about his people, from hence forth now and for ever.
124:3 Because our Lord will not leave the rod of sinners upon the lot of the just: that the just reach not their hands to iniquity.
124:4 Do well, o Lord, to the good, and right of heart.
124:5 But those that decline into obligations, our Lord will bring with them that work iniquity: peace upon Israel.

text.NRSV:
125:1 Those who trust in the Lord are like Mount Zion, which cannot be moved, but abides forever.
125:2 As the mountains surround Jerusalem, so the Lord surrounds his people, from this time on and forevermore.
125:3 For the scepter of wickedness shall not rest on the land allotted to the righteous, so that the righteous might not stretch out their hands to do wrong.
125:4 Do good, O Lord, to those who are good, and to those who are upright in their hearts.
125:5 But those who turn aside to their own crooked ways the Lord will lead away with evildoers. Peace be upon Israel!
text.Coverdale:
125:1 Those who trust in the LORD are like Mount Zion, * which cannot be moved, but stands fast for ever.
125:2 The hills stand about Jerusalem; * so does the LORD stand round about his people, from this time forth for evermore.
125:3 The scepter of the wicked shall not hold sway over the land allotted to the just, * so that the just shall not put their hands to evil.
125:4 Show your goodness, O LORD, to those who are good * and to those who are true of heart.
125:5 As for those who turn aside to crooked ways, the LORD will lead them away with the evildoers; * but peace be upon Israel. Twenty-seventh Day: Evening Prayer
text.KJV:
125:1 A Song of degrees. They that trust in the Lord shall be as mount Zion, which cannot be removed, but abideth for ever.
125:2 As the mountains are round about Jerusalem, so the Lord is round about his people from henceforth even for ever.
125:3 For the rod of the wicked shall not rest upon the lot of the righteous; lest the righteous put forth their hands unto iniquity.
125:4 Do good, O Lord, unto those that be good, and to them that are upright in their hearts.
125:5 As for such as turn aside unto their crooked ways, the Lord shall lead them forth with the workers of iniquity: but peace shall be upon Israel.
text.JPS1985:
125:1 A song of ascents. Those who trust in the LORD are like Mount Zion that cannot be moved, enduring forever.
125:2 Jerusalem, hills enfold it, and the LORD enfolds His people now and forever.
125:3 The scepter of the wicked shall never rest upon the land allotted to the righteous, that the righteous not set their hand to wrongdoing.
125:4 Do good, O LORD, to the good, to the upright in heart.
125:5 But those who in their crookedness act corruptly, let the LORD make them go the way of evildoers. May it be well with Israel!
text.Grail1963:
1 Those who put their trust in the Lord
are like Mount Zion, that cannot be shaken,
that stands for ever.
2 Jerusalem! The mountains surround her,
so the Lord surrounds his people
both now and for ever.
3 For the scepter of the wicked shall not rest
over the land of the just
for fear that the hands of the just
should turn to evil.
4 Do good, Lord, to those who are good,
to the upright of heart;
5 but the crooked and those who do evil,
drive them away!

On Israel, peace!
text.Rotherham:
125:1 They who trust in Yahweh, [are] like Mount Zion, which shall not be shaken, Age-abidingly, shall it remain.
125:2 Jerusalem! mountains, are round about her; and Yahweh, is round about his people, from henceforth even unto times age-abiding.
125:3 For the sceptre of lawlessness shall not remain over the allotment of the righteous,—lest the righteous put forth—unto perversity—their hands.
125:4 Do good, O Yahweh, unto such as are good, even unto such as are upright in their hearts.
125:5 As for them who turn aside unto their crooked ways, Yahweh, will lead them forth, with the workers of iniquity, Prosperity on Israel!
text.NABRE:
125:1 A song of ascents. Those trusting in the Lord are like Mount Zion, unshakable, forever enduring.
125:2 As mountains surround Jerusalem, the Lord surrounds his people both now and forever.
125:3 The scepter of the wicked will not prevail in the land allotted to the just, Lest the just themselves turn their hands to evil.
125:4 Do good, Lord, to the good, to those who are upright of heart.
125:5 But those who turn aside to crooked ways may the Lord send down with the evildoers. Peace upon Israel!
text.DRB:
125:1 Agradual Canticle. When our Lord turned the captivity of Sion: we were made as men comforted.
125:2 Then was our mouth replenished with joy: and our tongue with exultation. Then shall they say among the Gentiles: Our Lord hath done magnifically with them.
125:3 Our Lord hath done magnifically with us: we are made joyful.
125:4 Turn our captivity, o Lord, as a torrent in the South.
125:5 They that sow in tears, shall reap in joyfulness.
125:6 Going they went and wept, casting their seeds. But coming they shall come with exultation, carrying their sheaves.

text.NRSV:
126:1 When the Lord restored the fortunes of Zion, we were like those who dream.
126:2 Then our mouth was filled with laughter, and our tongue with shouts of joy; then it was said among the nations,"The Lord has done great things for them."
126:3 The Lord has done great things for us, and we rejoiced.
126:4 Restore our fortunes, O Lord, like the watercourses in the Negeb.
126:5 May those who sow in tears reap with shouts of joy.
126:6 Those who go out weeping, bearing the seed for sowing, shall come home with shouts of joy, carrying their sheaves.
text.Coverdale:
126:1 When the LORD restored the fortunes of Zion, * then were we like those who dream.
126:2 Then was our mouth filled with laughter, * and our tongue with shouts of joy.
126:3 Then they said among the nations, *"The LORD has done great things for them."
126:4 The LORD has done great things for us, * and we are glad indeed.
126:5 Restore our fortunes, O LORD, * like the watercourses of the Negev.
126:6 Those who sowed with tears * will reap with songs of joy.
126:7 Those who go out weeping, carrying the seed, * will come again with joy, shouldering their sheaves.
text.KJV:
126:1 A Song of degrees. When the Lord turned again the captivity of Zion, we were like them that dream.
126:2 Then was our mouth filled with laughter, and our tongue with singing: then said they among the heathen, The Lord hath done great things for them.
126:3 The Lord hath done great things for us; whereof we are glad.
126:4 Turn again our captivity, O Lord, as the streams in the south.
126:5 They that sow in tears shall reap in joy.
126:6 He that goeth forth and weepeth, bearing precious seed, shall doubtless come again with rejoicing, bringing his sheaves with him.
text.JPS1985:
126:1 A song of ascents. When the LORD restores the fortunes of Zion — we see it as in a dream —
126:2 our mouths shall be filled with laughter, our tongues, with songs of joy. Then shall they say among the nations, “The LORD has done great things for them! ”
126:3 The LORD will do great things for us and we shall rejoice.
126:4 Restore our fortunes, O LORD, like watercourses in the Negeb.
126:5 They who sow in tears shall reap with songs of joy.
126:6 Though he goes along weeping, carrying the seed-bag, he shall come back with songs of joy, carrying his sheaves.
text.Grail1963:
1 When the Lord delivered Zion from bondage,
it seemed like a dream.
