2 Then was our mouth filled with laughter,
on our lips there were songs.
The heathens themselves said: "What marvels
the Lord worked for them!"
3 What marvels the Lord worked for us!
Indeed we were glad.
4 Deliver us, O Lord, from our bondage
as streams in dry land.
5 Those who are sowing in tears
will sing when they reap.
6 They go out, they go out, full of tears,
carrying seed for the sowing:
they come back, they come back, full of song,
carrying their sheaves.
text.Rotherham:
126:1 When Yahweh brought back the captives of Zion, we were like them who dream:
126:2 Then, was our mouth, filled with laughter, and our tongue with a shout of triumph,—Then, said they among the nations, Yahweh, hath done great things, with these!
126:3 Yahweh, hath done great things with us, we are full of joy!
126:4 Bring thou back, O Yahweh, our captives, like channels in the South.
126:5 They who are sowing with tears, with shouting, shall reap:
126:6 He that, doth indeed go forth, and weep, bearing seed enough to trail along, doth, surely come in, with shouting, bringing his sheaves.
text.NABRE:
126:1 A song of ascents. When the Lord restored the captives of Zion, we thought we were dreaming.
126:2 Then our mouths were filled with laughter; our tongues sang for joy. Then it was said among the nations, “The Lord had done great things for them.”
126:3 The Lord has done great things for us; Oh, how happy we were!
126:4 Restore our captives, Lord, like the dry stream beds of the Negeb.
126:5 II Those who sow in tears will reap with cries of joy.
126:6 Those who go forth weeping, carrying sacks of seed, Will return with cries of joy, carrying their bundled sheaves.
text.DRB:
126:1 Agradual Canticle of Salomon. Unless our Lord build the house, they have laboured in vain that build it. Unless our Lord keep the city, he watcheth in vain that keepeth it.
126:2 It is vain for you to rise before light: rise ye after ye have sitten, which eat the bread of sorrow.
126:3 When he shall give sleep to his beloved: behold the inheritance of our Lord are children: the reward, the fruit of the womb.
126:4 As arrows in the hand of the mighty: so are the children of them that are shaken.
126:5 Blessed is the man that hath filled his desire of them: he shall not be confounded when he shall speak to his enemies in the gate.

text.NRSV:
127:1 Unless the Lord builds the house, those who build it labor in vain. Unless the Lord guards the city, the guard keeps watch in vain.
127:2 It is in vain that you rise up early and go late to rest, eating the bread of anxious toil; for he gives sleep to his beloved.
127:3 Sons are indeed a heritage from the Lord, the fruit of the womb a reward.
127:4 Like arrows in the hand of a warrior are the sons of one's youth.
127:5 Happy is the man who has his quiver full of them. He shall not be put to shame when he speaks with his enemies in the gate.
text.Coverdale:
127:1 Unless the LORD builds the house, * their labor is in vain who build it.
127:2 Unless the LORD watches over the city, * in vain the watchman keeps his vigil.
127:3 It is in vain that you rise so early and go to bed so late; * vain, too, to eat the bread of toil, for he gives to his beloved sleep.
127:4 Children are a heritage from the LORD, * and the fruit of the womb is a gift.
127:5 Like arrows in the hand of a warrior * are the children of one's youth.
127:6 Happy is the man who has his quiver full of them! * he shall not be put to shame when he contends with his enemies in the gate.
text.KJV:
127:1 A Song of degrees for Solomon. Except the Lord build the house, they labour in vain that build it: except the Lord keep the city, the watchman waketh but in vain.
127:2 It is vain for you to rise up early, to sit up late, to eat the bread of sorrows: for so he giveth his beloved sleep.
127:3 Lo, children are an heritage of the Lord: and the fruit of the womb is his reward.
127:4 As arrows are in the hand of a mighty man; so are children of the youth.
127:5 Happy is the man that hath his quiver full of them: they shall not be ashamed, but they shall speak with the enemies in the gate.
text.JPS1985:
127:1 A song of ascents. Of Solomon. Unless the LORD builds the house, its builders labor in vain on it; unless the LORD watches over the city, the watchman keeps vigil in vain.
127:2 In vain do you rise early and stay up late, you who toil for the bread you eat; He provides as much for His loved ones while they sleep.
127:3 Sons are the provision of the LORD; the fruit of the womb, His reward.
127:4 Like arrows in the hand of a warrior are sons born to a man in his youth.
127:5 Happy is the man who fills his quiver with them; they shall not be put to shame when they contend with the enemy in the gate.
text.Grail1963:
1 If the Lord does not build the house,
in vain do its builders labor;
if the Lord does not watch over the city,
in vain does the watchman keep vigil.
2 In vain is your earlier rising,
your going later to rest,
you who toil for the bread you eat,
when he pours gifts on his beloved while they slumber.
3 Truly sons are a gift from the Lord,
a blessing, the fruit of the womb.
4 Indeed the sons of youth
are like arrows in the hand of a warrior.
5 O the happiness of the man
who has filled his quiver with these arrows!
He will have no cause for shame
when he disputes with his foes in the gateways.
text.Rotherham:
127:1 If, Yahweh, build not the house, in vain, have the builders of it toiled thereon, If, Yahweh, watch not the city, in vain, hath the watchman kept awake:
127:2 Vain for you,—to be early in rising, to be late in lying down, to be eating the bread of wearisome toil, So, would he give his beloved one sleep.
127:3 Lo! an inheritance from Yahweh, are children, A reward, the fruit of the womb:
127:4 As arrows in the hand of a warrior, so, are the children of young men.
127:5 How happy the man who hath filled his quiver with them! They will not be ashamed, but will speak with enemies in the gate.
text.NABRE:
127:1 A song of ascents. Of Solomon. Unless the Lord build the house, they labor in vain who build. Unless the Lord guard the city, in vain does the guard keep watch.
127:2 It is vain for you to rise early and put off your rest at night, To eat bread earned by hard toil— all this God gives to his beloved in sleep.
127:3 II Certainly sons are a gift from the Lord, the fruit of the womb, a reward.
127:4 Like arrows in the hand of a warrior are the sons born in one’s youth.
127:5 Blessed is the man who has filled his quiver with them. He will never be shamed for he will destroy his foes at the gate.
text.DRB:
127:1 Agradual Canticle. Blessed are all that fear our Lord, that walk in his ways.
127:2 Because thou shalt eat the labours of thy hands: blessed art thou, and it shall be well with thee.
127:3 Thy wife as a fruitful vine, in the sides of thy house. Thy children as young plants of olive-trees, round about thy table.
127:4 Behold so shall the man be blessed, that feareth our Lord.
127:5 Our Lord out of Sion bless thee: and that thou mayst see the good things of Jerusalem all the days of thy life.
127:6 And that thou mayst see thy children's children, peace upon Israel.

text.NRSV:
128:1 Happy is everyone who fears the Lord, who walks in his ways.
128:2 You shall eat the fruit of the labor of your hands; you shall be happy, and it shall go well with you.
128:3 Your wife will be like a fruitful vine within your house; your children will be like olive shoots around your table.
128:4 Thus shall the man be blessed who fears the Lord.
128:5 The Lord bless you from Zion. May you see the prosperity of Jerusalem all the days of your life.
128:6 May you see your children's children. Peace be upon Israel!
text.Coverdale:
128:1 Happy are they all who fear the LORD, * and who follow in his ways!
128:2 You shall eat the fruit of your labor; * happiness and prosperity shall be yours.
128:3 Your wife shall be like a fruitful vine within your house, * your children like olive shoots round about your table.
128:4 The man who fears the LORD * shall thus indeed be blessed.
128:5 The LORD bless you from Zion, * and may you see the prosperity of Jerusalem all the days of your life.
128:6 May you live to see your children's children; * may peace be upon Israel.
text.KJV:
128:1 A Song of degrees. Blessed is every one that feareth the Lord; that walketh in his ways.
128:2 For thou shalt eat the labour of thine hands: happy shalt thou be, and it shall be well with thee.
128:3 Thy wife shall be as a fruitful vine by the sides of thine house: thy children like olive plants round about thy table.
128:4 Behold, that thus shall the man be blessed that feareth the Lord.
128:5 The Lord shall bless thee out of Zion: and thou shalt see the good of Jerusalem all the days of thy life.
128:6 Yea, thou shalt see thy children’s children, and peace upon Israel.
text.JPS1985:
128:1 A song of ascents. Happy are all who fear the LORD, who follow His ways.
128:2 You shall enjoy the fruit of your labors; you shall be happy and you shall prosper.
128:3 Your wife shall be like a fruitful vine within your house; your sons, like olive saplings around your table.
128:4 So shall the man who fears the LORD be blessed.
128:5 May the LORD bless you from Zion; may you share the prosperity of Jerusalem all the days of your life,
128:6 and live to see your children’s children. May all be well with Israel!
text.Grail1963:
1 O blessed are those who fear the Lord
and walk in his ways!
2 By the labor of your hands you shall eat.

You will be happy and prosper;
3 the wife like a fruitful vine
in the heart of your house;
Your children like shoots of the olive,
around your table.
4 Indeed thus shall be blessed
the man who fears the Lord.
5 May the Lord bless you from Zion
5c all the days of your life!
6 May you see your children's children
5b in a happy Jerusalem!
On Israel, peace!
text.Rotherham:
128:1 How happy is every one that revereth Yahweh, who walketh in his ways!
128:2 The labour of thine own hands, surely thou shalt eat. How happy thou, and well for thine!
128:3 Thy wife, like a fruitful vine, within the recesses of thy house,—Thy children, like plantings of olive-trees, round about thy table.
128:4 Lo! thus, shall be blessed the man who revereth Yahweh.
128:5 Yahweh will bless thee, out of Zion,—and behold thou the welfare of Jerusalem, all the days of thy life!
128:6 And behold thou thy children’s children,—Prosperity on Israel!
text.NABRE:
128:1 A song of ascents. Blessed are all who fear the Lord, and who walk in his ways.
128:2 What your hands provide you will enjoy; you will be blessed and prosper:
128:3 Your wife will be like a fruitful vine within your home, Your children like young olive plants around your table.
128:4 Just so will the man be blessed who fears the Lord.
128:5 II May the Lord bless you from Zion; may you see Jerusalem’s prosperity all the days of your life,
128:6 and live to see your children’s children. Peace upon Israel!
text.DRB:
128:1 Agradual Canticle. Often have they impugned me from my youth, let Israel now say:
128:2 Often have they impugned me from my youth: but they have not prevailed against me.
128:3 Sinners have builded upon my back: they have prolonged their iniquity.
128:4 Our just Lord will cut the necks of sinners:
128:5 let them all be confounded and turned backward, that hate Sion.
128:6 Let them be made as grass in the tops of houses: which is withered before it be plucked up.
128:7 Whereof the reaper hath not filled his hand, and he that gathereth the sheaves his bosom.
128:8 And they said not that passed by: The blessing of our Lord be upon you: we have blessed you in the name of our Lord.

text.NRSV:
129:1 "Often have they attacked me from my youth"—let Israel now say—
129:2 "often have they attacked me from my youth, yet they have not prevailed against me.
129:3 The plowers plowed on my back; they made their furrows long."
129:4 The Lord is righteous; he has cut the cords of the wicked.
129:5 May all who hate Zion be put to shame and turned backward.
129:6 Let them be like the grass on the housetops that withers before it grows up,
129:7 with which reapers do not fill their hands or binders of sheaves their arms,
129:8 while those who pass by do not say,"The blessing of the Lord be upon you! We bless you in the name of the Lord!"
text.KJV:
129:1 A Song of degrees. Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth, may Israel now say:
129:2 Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth: yet they have not prevailed against me.
129:3 The plowers plowed upon my back: they made long their furrows.
129:4 The Lord is righteous: he hath cut asunder the cords of the wicked.
129:5 Let them all be confounded and turned back that hate Zion.
129:6 Let them be as the grass upon the housetops, which withereth afore it groweth up:
129:7 Wherewith the mower filleth not his hand; nor he that bindeth sheaves his bosom.
129:8 Neither do they which go by say, The blessing of the Lord be upon you: we bless you in the name of the Lord.
text.JPS1985:
129:1 A song of ascents. Since my youth they have often assailed me, let Israel now declare,
129:2 since my youth they have often assailed me, but they have never overcome me.
129:3 Plowmen plowed across my back; they made long furrows.
129:4 The LORD, the righteous one, has snapped the cords of the wicked.
129:5 Let all who hate Zion fall back in disgrace.
129:6 Let them be like grass on roofs that fades before it can be pulled up,
129:7 that affords no handful for the reaper, no armful for the gatherer of sheaves,
129:8 no exchange with passersby: “The blessing of the LORD be upon you.” “We bless you by the name of the LORD.”
text.Grail1963:
1 "They have pressed me hard from my youth,"
this is Israel's song.
2 "They have pressed me hard from my youth
but could never destroy me.
3 They plowed my back like plowmen,
drawing long furrows.
4 But the Lord who is just, has destroyed
the yoke of the wicked."
5 Let them be shamed and routed,
those who hate Zion!
6 Let them be like grass on the roof
that withers before it flowers.
7 With that no reaper fill his arms,
no binder makes his sheaves
8 and those passing by will not say:
"On you the Lord's blessing!"
"We bless you in the name of the Lord."
text.Rotherham:
129:1 Many a time, have they harassed me from my youth, well may Israel say:
129:2 Many a time, have they harassed me from youth, yet have they not prevailed against me.
129:3 Upon my back, have ploughmen ploughed, They have lengthened their furrow!
129:4 Yahweh, is righteous, He hath cut asunder the cords of the lawless.
129:5 Let all who hate Zion, be ashamed and shrink back:
129:6 Let them become like the grass of housetops, which, before it is pulled up, hath withered;
129:7 Wherewith no reaper, hath filled his hand, nor binder, his bosom:
129:8 Neither have the passers-by ever said, The blessing of Yahweh, be unto you,—We have blessed you in the Name of Yahweh.
text.NABRE:
129:1 A song of ascents. Viciously have they attacked me from my youth, let Israel say now.
129:2 Viciously have they attacked me from my youth, yet they have not prevailed against me.
129:3 Upon my back the plowers plowed, as they traced their long furrows.
129:4 But the just Lord cut me free from the ropes of the wicked.
129:5 II May they recoil in disgrace, all who hate Zion.
129:6 May they be like grass on the rooftops withered in early growth,
129:7 Never to fill the reaper’s hands, nor the arms of the binders of sheaves,
129:8 And with none passing by to call out: “The blessing of the Lord be upon you! We bless you in the name of the Lord!”
text.DRB:
129:1 Agradual Canticle. From the depths I have cried to thee, o Lord:
129:2 Lord hear my voice: Let thine ears be intent to the voice of my petition.
129:3 If thou shalt observe iniquities, o Lord: Lord who shall sustain it?
129:4 Because with thee there is propitiation: and for thy law I have expected thee, o Lord. My soul hath expected in his word:
129:5 my soul hath hoped in our Lord.
129:6 From the morning watch even until night: let Israel hope in our Lord.
129:7 Because with our Lord there is mercy: and with him plenteous redemption.
129:8 And he shall redeem Israel, from all his iniquities.

text.NRSV:
130:1 Out of the depths I cry to you, O Lord.
130:2 Lord, hear my voice! Let your ears be attentive to the voice of my supplications!
130:3 If you, O Lord, should mark iniquities, Lord, who could stand?
130:4 But there is forgiveness with you, so that you may be revered.
130:5 I wait for the Lord, my soul waits, and in his word I hope;
130:6 my soul waits for the Lord more than those who watch for the morning, more than those who watch for the morning.
130:7 O Israel, hope in the Lord! For with the Lord there is steadfast love, and with him is great power to redeem.
130:8 It is he who will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.
text.Coverdale:
130:1 Out of the depths have I called to you, O LORD; LORD, hear my voice; * let your ears consider well the voice of my supplication.
130:2 If you, LORD, were to note what is done amiss, * O LORD, who could stand?
130:3 For there is forgiveness with you; * therefore you shall be feared.
130:4 I wait for the LORD; my soul waits for him; * in his word is my hope.
130:5 My soul waits for the LORD, more than watchmen for the morning, * more than watchmen for the morning.
130:6 O Israel, wait for the LORD, * for with the LORD there is mercy;
130:7 With him there is plenteous redemption, * and he shall redeem Israel from all their sins.
text.KJV:
130:1 A Song of degrees. Out of the depths have I cried unto thee, O Lord.
130:2 Lord, hear my voice: let thine ears be attentive to the voice of my supplications.
130:3 If thou, Lord, shouldest mark iniquities, O Lord, who shall stand?
130:4 But there is forgiveness with thee, that thou mayest be feared.
130:5 I wait for the Lord, my soul doth wait, and in his word do I hope.
130:6 My soul waiteth for the Lord more than they that watch for the morning: I say, more than they that watch for the morning.
130:7 Let Israel hope in the Lord: for with the Lord there is mercy, and with him is plenteous redemption.
130:8 And he shall redeem Israel from all his iniquities.
text.JPS1985:
130:1 A song of ascents. Out of the depths I call You, O LORD.
130:2 O Lord, listen to my cry; let Your ears be attentive to my plea for mercy.
130:3 If You keep account of sins, O LORD, Lord, who will survive?
130:4 Yours is the power to forgive so that You may be held in awe.
130:5 I look to the LORD; I look to Him; I await His word.
130:6 I am more eager for the Lord than watchmen for the morning, watchmen for the morning.
130:7 O Israel, wait for the LORD; for with the LORD is steadfast love and great power to redeem.
130:8 It is He who will redeem Israel from all their iniquities.
text.Grail1963:
1 Out of the depths I cry to you, O Lord,
2 Lord, hear my voice!
O let your ears be attentive
to the voice of my pleading.
3 If you, O Lord, should mark our guilt,
Lord, who would survive?
4 But with you is found forgiveness:
for this we revere you.
5 My soul is waiting for the Lord.
I count on his word.

6 My soul is longing for the Lord
more than watchman for daybreak.
(Let the watchman count on daybreak
7 and Israel on the Lord.)
Because with the Lord there is mercy
and fullness of redemption,
8 Israel indeed he will redeem
from all its iniquity.
text.Rotherham:
130:1 Out of the depths, have I cried unto thee, O Yahweh.
130:2 O My Lord! hearken thou unto my voice,—Let thine ears be attentive to the voice of my supplications,
130:3 If, iniquities, thou shouldest mark, O Yah, O My Lord, who could stand?
130:4 But, with thee, is forgiveness, that thou mayest be revered.
130:5 I have waited for Yahweh, My soul hath waited for his word;
130:6 I have hoped, O my soul, for My Lord, more than they who watch for the morning, who watch for the morning.
130:7 Wait, O Israel, for Yahweh,—for, with Yahweh, is lovingkindness, and there aboundeth with him—redemption.
130:8 He, therefore, will redeem Israel from all his iniquities.
text.NABRE:
130:1 A song of ascents. Out of the depths I call to you, Lord;
130:2 Lord, hear my cry! May your ears be attentive to my cry for mercy.
130:3 If you, Lord, keep account of sins, Lord, who can stand?
130:4 But with you is forgiveness and so you are revered.
130:5 II I wait for the Lord, my soul waits and I hope for his word.
130:6 My soul looks for the Lord more than sentinels for daybreak. More than sentinels for daybreak,
130:7 let Israel hope in the Lord, For with the Lord is mercy, with him is plenteous redemption,
130:8 And he will redeem Israel from all its sins.
text.DRB:
130:1 Agradual Canticle of David. Lord my heart is not exalted: neither are mine eyes lofty. Neither have I walked in great matters: nor in marvelous things above me.
130:2 If I was not humbly minded: but exalted my soul: As the weaned child is toward his mother, so retribution in my soul.
130:3 Let Israel hope in our Lord, from henceforth now and for ever.

text.NRSV:
131:1 O Lord, my heart is not lifted up, my eyes are not raised too high; I do not occupy myself with things too great and too marvelous for me.
131:2 But I have calmed and quieted my soul, like a weaned child with its mother; my soul is like the weaned child that is with me.
131:3 O Israel, hope in the Lord from this time on and forevermore.
text.Coverdale:
131:1 O LORD, I am not proud; * I have no haughty looks.
131:2 I do not occupy myself with great matters, * or with things that are too hard for me.
131:3 But I still my soul and make it quiet, like a child upon its mother's breast; * my soul is quieted within me.
131:4 O Israel, wait upon the LORD, * from this time forth for evermore. Twenty-eighth Day: Morning Prayer
text.KJV:
131:1 A Song of degrees of David. Lord, my heart is not haughty, nor mine eyes lofty: neither do I exercise myself in great matters, or in things too high for me.
131:2 Surely I have behaved and quieted myself, as a child that is weaned of his mother: my soul is even as a weaned child.
131:3 Let Israel hope in the Lord from henceforth and for ever.
text.Grail1963:
1 O Lord, my heart is not proud
nor haughty my eyes.
I have not gone after things too great
nor marvels beyond me.
2 Truly I have set my soul
in silence and peace.
A weaned child on its mother's breast,
even so is my soul.
3 O Israel, hope in the Lord
both now and forever.
text.Rotherham:
131:1 O Yahweh, my heart, is not haughty, nor are mine eyes, lofty, neither have I moved among great matters, or among affairs too wonderful for me.
131:2 Surely I have soothed and silenced my soul,—like a weaned child, concerning his mother, like a weaned child, concerning myself—mine own soul.
131:3 Wait, O Israel, for Yahweh, from this time forth, even to times age-abiding.
text.NABRE:
131:1 A song of ascents. Of David. Lord, my heart is not proud; nor are my eyes haughty. I do not busy myself with great matters, with things too sublime for me.
131:2 Rather, I have stilled my soul, Like a weaned child to its mother, weaned is my soul.
131:3 Israel, hope in the Lord, now and forever.
text.DRB:
131:1 Agradual Canticle. Remember David, o Lord, and all his meekness:
131:2 As he sware to our Lord, vowed a vow to the God of Jacob.
131:3 If I shall enter into the tabernacle of my house, if I shall ascend into the bed of my couch.
131:4 If I shall give sleep to mine eyes, and slumbering to mine eye lids:
131:5 And rest to my temples: until I find a place for our Lord, a tabernacle for the God of Jacob.
131:6 Behold we have heard of it in Ephrata: we have found it in the fields of the wood.
131:7 We will enter into his tabernacle: we will adore in the place where his feet stood.
131:8 Arise Lord into thy rest, thou, and the ark of thy sanctification.
131:9 Let thy Priests be clothed with justice: & let thy saints rejoice.
131:10 For David thy servant's sake, turn not away the face of thy Christ.
131:11 Our Lord hath sworn truth to David, and he will not disappoint it: Of the fruit of thy womb I will set upon thy seat.
131:12 If thy children shall keep my testament, and these my testimonies which I will teach them: Their children also even for ever, shall sit upon thy seat.
131:13 Because our Lord hath chosen Sion: he hath chosen it for an habitation to himself.
131:14 This is my rest for ever and ever: here will I dwell because I have chosen it.
131:15 Blessing I will bless her widow: her poor I will fill with breads.
131:16 Her Priests I will clothe with salvation: and her saints shall rejoice with joyfulness.
131:17 Thither will I bring forth a horn to David, I have prepared a lamp to my Christ.
131:18 His enemies I will clothe with confusion: but upon him shall my sanctification flourish.

text.NRSV:
132:1 O Lord, remember in David's favor all the hardships he endured;
132:2 how he swore to the Lord and vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob,
132:3 "I will not enter my house or get into my bed;
132:4 I will not give sleep to my eyes or slumber to my eyelids,
132:5 until I find a place for the Lord, a dwelling place for the Mighty One of Jacob."
132:6 We heard of it in Ephrathah; we found it in the fields of Jaar.
132:7 "Let us go to his dwelling place; let us worship at his footstool."
132:8 Rise up, O Lord, and go to your resting place, you and the ark of your might.
132:9 Let your priests be clothed with righteousness, and let your faithful shout for joy.
132:10 For your servant David's sake do not turn away the face of your anointed one.
132:11 The Lord swore to David a sure oath from which he will not turn back:"One of the sons of your body I will set on your throne.
132:12 If your sons keep my covenant and my decrees that I shall teach them, their sons also, forevermore, shall sit on your throne."
132:13 For the Lord has chosen Zion; he has desired it for his habitation:
132:14 "This is my resting place forever; here I will reside, for I have desired it.
132:15 I will abundantly bless its provisions; I will satisfy its poor with bread.
132:16 Its priests I will clothe with salvation, and its faithful will shout for joy.
132:17 There I will cause a horn to sprout up for David; I have prepared a lamp for my anointed one.
132:18 His enemies I will clothe with disgrace, but on him, his crown will gleam."
text.Coverdale:
132:1 LORD, remember David, * and all the hardships he endured;
132:2 How he swore an oath to the LORD * and vowed a vow to the Mighty One of Jacob:
132:3 "I will not come under the roof of my house, * nor climb up into my bed;
132:4 I will not allow my eyes to sleep, * nor let my eyelids slumber;
132:5 Until I find a place for the LORD, * a dwelling for the Mighty One of Jacob."
132:6 "The ark! We heard it was in Ephratah; * we found it in the fields of Jearim.
132:7 Let us go to God's dwelling place; * let us fall upon our knees before his footstool."
132:8 Arise, O LORD, into your resting-place, * you and the ark of your strength.
132:9 Let your priests be clothed with righteousness; * let your faithful people sing with joy.
132:10 For your servant David's sake, * do not turn away the face of your Anointed.
132:11 The LORD has sworn an oath to David; * in truth, he will not break it:
132:12 "A son, the fruit of your body * will I set upon your throne.
132:13 If your children keep my covenant and my testimonies that I shall teach them, * their children will sit upon your throne for evermore."
132:14 For the LORD has chosen Zion; * he has desired her for his habitation:
132:15 "This shall be my resting-place for ever; * here will I dwell, for I delight in her.
132:16 I will surely bless her provisions, * and satisfy her poor with bread.
132:17 I will clothe her priests with salvation, * and her faithful people will rejoice and sing.
132:18 There will I make the horn of David flourish; * I have prepared a lamp for my Anointed.
132:19 As for his enemies, I will clothe them with shame; * but as for him, his crown will shine."
text.KJV:
132:1 A Song of degrees. Lord, remember David, and all his afflictions:
132:2 How he sware unto the Lord, and vowed unto the mighty God of Jacob;
132:3 Surely I will not come into the tabernacle of my house, nor go up into my bed;
132:4 I will not give sleep to mine eyes, or slumber to mine eyelids,
132:5 Until I find out a place for the Lord, an habitation for the mighty God of Jacob.
132:6 Lo, we heard of it at Ephratah: we found it in the fields of the wood.
132:7 We will go into his tabernacles: we will worship at his footstool.
132:8 Arise, O Lord, into thy rest; thou, and the ark of thy strength.
132:9 Let thy priests be clothed with righteousness; and let thy saints shout for joy.
132:10 For thy servant David’s sake turn not away the face of thine anointed.
132:11 The Lord hath sworn in truth unto David; he will not turn from it; Of the fruit of thy body will I set upon thy throne.
132:12 If thy children will keep my covenant and my testimony that I shall teach them, their children shall also sit upon thy throne for evermore.
132:13 For the Lord hath chosen Zion; he hath desired it for his habitation.
132:14 This is my rest for ever: here will I dwell; for I have desired it.
132:15 I will abundantly bless her provision: I will satisfy her poor with bread.
132:16 I will also clothe her priests with salvation: and her saints shall shout aloud for joy.
132:17 There will I make the horn of David to bud: I have ordained a lamp for mine anointed.
132:18 His enemies will I clothe with shame: but upon himself shall his crown flourish.
text.JPS1985:
132:1 A song of ascents. O LORD, remember in David’s favor his extreme self-denial,
132:2 how he swore to the LORD, vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob,
132:3 “I will not enter my house, nor will I mount my bed,
132:4 I will not give sleep to my eyes, or slumber to my eyelids
132:5 until I find a place for the LORD, an abode for the Mighty One of Jacob.”
132:6 We heard it was in Ephrath; we came upon it in the region of Jaar.
132:7 Let us enter His abode, bow at His footstool.
132:8 Advance, O LORD, to Your resting-place, You and Your mighty Ark!
132:9 Your priests are clothed in triumph; Your loyal ones sing for joy.
132:10 For the sake of Your servant David do not reject Your anointed one.
132:11 The LORD swore to David a firm oath that He will not renounce, “One of your own issue I will set upon your throne.
132:12 If your sons keep My covenant and My decrees that I teach them, then their sons also, to the end of time, shall sit upon your throne.”
132:13 For the LORD has chosen Zion; He has desired it for His seat.
132:14 “This is my resting-place for all time; here I will dwell, for I desire it.
132:15 I will amply bless its store of food, give its needy their fill of bread.
132:16 I will clothe its priests in victory, its loyal ones shall sing for joy.
132:17 There I will make a horn sprout for David; I have prepared a lamp for My anointed one.
132:18 I will clothe his enemies in disgrace, while on him his crown shall sparkle.”
text.Grail1963:
1 O Lord, remember David
and all the many hardships he endured,
2 the oath he swore to the Lord,
his vow to the Strong One of Jacob.
3 "I will not enter the house where I live
nor go the bed where I rest.
4 I will give no sleep to my eyes,
to my eyelids I will give no slumber
5 till I find a place for the Lord,
a dwelling for the Strong One of Jacob."
6 At Ephrata we heard of the ark;
we found it in the plains of Yearim.
7 "Let us go to the place of his dwelling;
let us go to kneel at his footstool."
8 Go up, Lord, to the place of your rest,
you and the ark of your strength.
9 Your priests shall be clothed with holiness;
your faithful shall ring out their joy.
10 For the sake of David your servant
do not reject your anointed.
11 The Lord swore an oath to David;
he will not go back on this word:

"A son, the fruit of your body,
will I set upon your throne.
12 If they keep my covenant in truth
and my laws that I have taught them,
their sons too shall rule
on your throne from age to age."
13 For the Lord has chosen Zion;
he has desired it for his dwelling:
14 "This is my restingplace for ever;
here have I chosen to live.
15 I will greatly bless her produce,
I will fill her poor with bread.
16 I will clothe her priests with salvation
and her faithful shall ring out their joy.
17 There David's stock will flower;
I will prepare a lamp for my anointed.
18 I will cover his enemies with shame
but on him my crown shall shine."
text.Rotherham:
132:1 Remember, O Yahweh, unto David, all his humiliations!
132:2 What he Sware to Yahweh,—Vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob:
132:3 Surely I will not enter the home of my own house, nor ascend my curtained couch;
132:4 I will not give sleep to mine eyes, nor, to mine eye-lashes, slumber:
132:5 Until I find a place for Yahweh, habitations for the Mighty One of Jacob.
132:6 Lo! we heard of it at Ephrathah, we found it in the fields of the wood.
132:7 We would enter his habitations, we would bow down at his footstool.
132:8 Arise! O Yahweh, unto thy place of rest, Thou, and the ark of thy strength.
132:9 Thy priests, let them be clothed with righteousness, Thy men of lovingkindness, let them shout for joy!
132:10 For the sake of David thy servant, do not turn away the face of thine Anointed One.
132:11 Yahweh hath sworn unto David, in faithfulness, will he not turn from it,—Of the fruit of thy body, will I seat on thy throne.
132:12 If thy sons keep my covenant, and my testimony which I will teach them, even their sons, unto futurity, shall sit on thy throne.
132:13 For Yahweh hath chosen Zion,—He hath desired it as a dwelling for himself:
132:14 This, is my place of rest unto futurity, Here, will I dwell, for I have desired it;
132:15 Her provision, will I abundantly bless, Her needy ones, will I satisfy with bread;
132:16 And, her priests, will I clothe with salvation, and, her men of lovingkindness, shall, shout aloud, for joy;
132:17 There, will I cause to bud a horn to David, I have prepared a lamp for mine Anointed One;
132:18 His enemies, will I clothe with shame, but, upon himself, shall his crown be resplendent.
text.NABRE:
132:1 A song of ascents. Remember, O Lord, for David all his hardships;
132:2 How he swore an oath to the Lord, vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob:
132:3 “I will not enter the house where I live, nor lie on the couch where I sleep;
132:4 I will give my eyes no sleep, my eyelids no rest,
132:5 Till I find a place for the Lord, a dwelling for the Mighty One of Jacob.”
132:6 “We have heard of it in Ephrathah; we have found it in the fields of Jaar.
132:7 Let us enter his dwelling; let us worship at his footstool.”
132:8 “Arise, Lord, come to your resting place, you and your mighty ark.
132:9 Your priests will be clothed with justice; your devout will shout for joy.”
132:10 For the sake of David your servant, do not reject your anointed.
132:11 II The Lord swore an oath to David in truth, he will never turn back from it: “Your own offspring I will set upon your throne.
132:12 If your sons observe my covenant, and my decrees I shall teach them, Their sons, in turn, shall sit forever on your throne.”
132:13 Yes, the Lord has chosen Zion, desired it for a dwelling:
132:14 “This is my resting place forever; here I will dwell, for I desire it.
132:15 I will bless Zion with provisions; its poor I will fill with bread.
132:16 I will clothe its priests with salvation; its devout shall shout for joy.
132:17 There I will make a horn sprout for David; I will set a lamp for my anointed.
132:18 His foes I will clothe with shame, but on him his crown shall shine.”
text.DRB:
132:1 Agradual Canticle of David. Behold how good and how pleasant a thing it is, for brethren to dwell in one.
132:2 As ointment on the head, which ran down upon the beard, the beard of Aaron, which ran down unto the hem of his garment.
132:3 As the dew of Hermon, which runneth down upon mount Sion. Because there hath our Lord commanded blessing, and life even for ever.

text.NRSV:
133:1 How very good and pleasant it is when kindred live together in unity!
133:2 It is like the precious oil on the head, running down upon the beard, on the beard of Aaron, running down over the collar of his robes.
133:3 It is like the dew of Hermon, which falls on the mountains of Zion. For there the Lord ordained his blessing, life forevermore.
text.Coverdale:
133:1 Oh, how good and pleasant it is, * when brethren live together in unity!
133:2 It is like fine oil upon the head * that runs down upon the beard,
133:3 Upon the beard of Aaron, * and runs down upon the collar of his robe.
133:4 It is like the dew of Hermon * that falls upon the hills of Zion.
133:5 For there the LORD has ordained the blessing: * life for evermore.
text.Grail1963:

running down upon the beard,
running down upon Aaron's beard,
upon the collar of his robes.
3 It is like the dew of Hermon which falls
on the heights of Zion.
For there the Lord gives his blessing,
life for ever.
text.Rotherham:
133:1 Lo! how good and how delightful, for brethren, to dwell together even as one.
133:2 Like the precious oil upon the head, descending upon the beard; the beard of Aaron,—which descended unto the opening of his robe:
133:3 Like the dew of Hermon, which descended upon the mountains of Zion,—for, there, did Yahweh command the blessing, Life, unto times age-abiding?
text.NABRE:
133:1 A song of ascents. Of David. How good and how pleasant it is, when brothers dwell together as one!
133:2 Like fine oil on the head, running down upon the beard, Upon the beard of Aaron, upon the collar of his robe.
133:3 Like dew of Hermon coming down upon the mountains of Zion. There the Lord has decreed a blessing, life for evermore!
text.DRB:
133:1 Agradual Canticle. Lo now bless our Lord, all ye the servants of our Lord: Which stand in the house of our Lord, in the courts of the house of our God.
133:2 In the nights lift up your hands unto the holy places, and bless ye our Lord.
133:3 Our Lord out of Sion bless thee, who made heaven and earth.

text.NRSV:
134:1 Come, bless the Lord, all you servants of the Lord, who stand by night in the house of the Lord!
134:2 Lift up your hands to the holy place, and bless the Lord.
134:3 May the Lord, maker of heaven and earth, bless you from Zion.
text.Coverdale:
134:1 Behold now, bless the LORD, all you servants of the LORD, * you that stand by night in the house of the LORD.
134:2 Lift up your hands in the holy place and bless the LORD; * the LORD who made heaven and earth bless you out of Zion.
text.KJV:
134:1 A Song of degrees. Behold, bless ye the Lord, all ye servants of the Lord, which by night stand in the house of the Lord.
134:2 Lift up your hands in the sanctuary, and bless the Lord.
134:3 The Lord that made heaven and earth bless thee out of Zion.
text.JPS1985:
134:1 A song of ascents. Now bless the LORD, all you servants of the LORD who stand nightly in the house of the LORD.
134:2 Lift your hands toward the sanctuary and bless the LORD.
134:3 May the LORD, maker of heaven and earth, bless you from Zion.
text.Grail1963:

all you who serve the Lord,

in the courts of the house of our God.
2 Lift up your hands to the holy place
and bless the Lord through the night.
3 May the Lord bless you from Zion,

text.Rotherham:
134:1 Lo! bless Yahweh, all ye servants of Yahweh, who stand in the house of Yahweh, by night:
134:2 Lift up your hand in holiness, and bless Yahweh.
134:3 May Yahweh, bless thee, out of Zion, even he that made heaven and earth.
text.NABRE:
134:1 A song of ascents. O come, bless the Lord, all you servants of the Lord You who stand in the house of the Lord throughout the nights.
134:2 Lift up your hands toward the sanctuary, and bless the Lord.
134:3 May the Lord bless you from Zion, the Maker of heaven and earth.
text.DRB:
134:1 Alleluia. Praise ye the name of our Lord, ye servants praise our Lord.
134:2 Ye that stand in the house of our Lord, in the courts of the house of our God.
134:3 Praise ye our Lord, because our Lord is good: sing ye to his name, because it is sweet.
134:4 Because our Lord hath chosen Jacob to himself, Israel for his own possession.
134:5 Because I have known that our Lord is great, and our God above all gods.
134:6 All things whatsoever our Lord would he hath done, in heaven, in earth, in the sea, and in all the depths.
134:7 Bring forth clouds from the uttermost of the earth: lightnings he hath turned into rain. Who bringeth forth the winds out of his treasures:
134:8 who struck the first begotten of Ægypt from man even to beast.
134:9 He sent forth signs and wonders in the midst of thee, o Ægypt: upon Pharao, and upon all his servants.
134:10 Who struck many nations: and slew strong kings:
134:11 Sehon the king of the Amorrheites, & Og the king of Basan, and all the Kingdoms of Chanaan.
134:12 And he gave their land for inheritance, for an inheritance to Israel his people.
134:13 Lord thy name is for ever: Lord thy memorial is unto generation and generation.
134:14 Because our Lord shall judge his people: and will be entreated toward his servants.
134:15 The idols of the Gentiles are silver, and gold, the works of men's hands.
134:16 They have mouth, & shall not speak: they have eyes, and shall not see.
134:17 They have ears, and shall not hear: for neither is there breath in their mouth.
134:18 Let them that make them become like to them: and all that have confidence in them.
134:19 Ye house of Israel bless our Lord: ye house of Aaron bless our Lord.
134:20 Ye house of Levi bless our Lord: you that fear our Lord, bless ye our Lord.
134:21 Blessed be our Lord out of Sion, who dwelleth in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV:
135:1 Praise the Lord! Praise the name of the Lord; give praise, O servants of the Lord,
135:2 you that stand in the house of the Lord, in the courts of the house of our God.
135:3 Praise the Lord, for the Lord is good; sing to his name, for he is gracious.
135:4 For the Lord has chosen Jacob for himself, Israel as his own possession.
135:5 For I know that the Lord is great; our Lord is above all gods.
135:6 Whatever the Lord pleases he does, in heaven and on earth, in the seas and all deeps.
135:7 He it is who makes the clouds rise at the end of the earth; he makes lightnings for the rain and brings out the wind from his storehouses.
135:8 He it was who struck down the firstborn of Egypt, both human beings and animals;
135:9 he sent signs and wonders into your midst, O Egypt, against Pharaoh and all his servants.
135:10 He struck down many nations and killed mighty kings—
135:11 Sihon, king of the Amorites, and Og, king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan—
135:12 and gave their land as a heritage, a heritage to his people Israel.
135:13 Your name, O Lord, endures forever, your renown, O Lord, throughout all ages.
135:14 For the Lord will vindicate his people, and have compassion on his servants.
135:15 The idols of the nations are silver and gold, the work of human hands.
135:16 They have mouths, but they do not speak; they have eyes, but they do not see;
135:17 they have ears, but they do not hear, and there is no breath in their mouths.
135:18 Those who make them and all who trust them shall become like them.
135:19 O house of Israel, bless the Lord! O house of Aaron, bless the Lord!
135:20 O house of Levi, bless the Lord! You that fear the Lord, bless the Lord!
135:21 Blessed be the Lord from Zion, he who resides in Jerusalem. Praise the Lord!
text.Coverdale:
135:1 Hallelujah! Praise the Name of the LORD; * give praise, you servants of the LORD.
135:2 You who stand in the house of the LORD, * in the courts of the house of our God.
135:3 Praise the LORD, for the LORD is good; * sing praises to his Name, for it is lovely.
135:4 For the LORD has chosen Jacob for himself * and Israel for his own possession.
135:5 For I know that the LORD is great, * and that our Lord is above all gods.
135:6 The LORD does whatever pleases him, in heaven and on earth, * in the seas and all the deeps.
135:7 He brings up rain clouds from the ends of the earth; * he sends out lightning with the rain, and brings the winds out of his storehouse.
135:8 It was he who struck down the firstborn of Egypt, * the firstborn both of man and beast.
135:9 He sent signs and wonders into the midst of you, O Egypt, * against Pharaoh and all his servants.
135:10 He overthrew many nations * and put mighty kings to death:
135:11 Sihon, king of the Amorites, and Og, the king of Bashan, * and all the kingdoms of Canaan.
135:12 He gave their land to be an inheritance, * an inheritance for Israel his people.
135:13 O LORD, your Name is everlasting; * your renown, O LORD, endures from age to age.
135:14 For the LORD gives his people justice * and shows compassion to his servants.
135:15 The idols of the heathen are silver and gold, * the work of human hands.
135:16 They have mouths, but they cannot speak; * eyes have they, but they cannot see.
135:17 They have ears, but they cannot hear; * neither is there any breath in their mouth.
135:18 Those who make them are like them, * and so are all who put their trust in them.
135:19 Bless the LORD, O house of Israel; * O house of Aaron, bless the LORD.
135:20 Bless the LORD, O house of Levi; * you who fear the LORD, bless the LORD.
135:21 Blessed be the LORD out of Zion, * who dwells in Jerusalem. Hallelujah! Twenty-eighth Day: Evening Prayer
text.KJV:
135:1 Praise ye the Lord. Praise ye the name of the Lord; praise him, O ye servants of the Lord.
135:2 Ye that stand in the house of the Lord, in the courts of the house of our God,
135:3 Praise the Lord; for the Lord is good: sing praises unto his name; for it is pleasant.
135:4 For the Lord hath chosen Jacob unto himself, and Israel for his peculiar treasure.
135:5 For I know that the Lord is great, and that our Lord is above all gods.
135:6 Whatsoever the Lord pleased, that did he in heaven, and in earth, in the seas, and all deep places.
135:7 He causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth; he maketh lightnings for the rain; he bringeth the wind out of his treasuries.
135:8 Who smote the firstborn of Egypt, both of man and beast.
135:9 Who sent tokens and wonders into the midst of thee, O Egypt, upon Pharaoh, and upon all his servants.
135:10 Who smote great nations, and slew mighty kings;
135:11 Sihon king of the Amorites, and Og king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan:
135:12 And gave their land for an heritage, an heritage unto Israel his people.
135:13 Thy name, O Lord, endureth for ever; and thy memorial, O Lord, throughout all generations.
135:14 For the Lord will judge his people, and he will repent himself concerning his servants.
135:15 The idols of the heathen are silver and gold, the work of men’s hands.
135:16 They have mouths, but they speak not; eyes have they, but they see not;
135:17 They have ears, but they hear not; neither is there any breath in their mouths.
135:18 They that make them are like unto them: so is every one that trusteth in them.
135:19 Bless the Lord, O house of Israel: bless the Lord, O house of Aaron:
135:20 Bless the Lord, O house of Levi: ye that fear the Lord, bless the Lord.
135:21 Blessed be the Lord out of Zion, which dwelleth at Jerusalem. Praise ye the Lord.
text.JPS1985:
135:1 Hallelujah. Praise the name of the LORD; give praise, you servants of the LORD
135:2 who stand in the house of the LORD, in the courts of the house of our God.
135:3 Praise the LORD, for the LORD is good; sing hymns to His name, for it is pleasant.
135:4 For the LORD has chosen Jacob for Himself, Israel, as His treasured possession.
135:5 For I know that the LORD is great, that our Lord is greater than all gods.
135:6 Whatever the LORD desires He does in heaven and earth, in the seas and all the depths.
135:7 He makes clouds rise from the end of the earth; He makes lightning for the rain; He releases the wind from His vaults.
135:8 He struck down the first-born of Egypt, man and beast alike;
135:9 He sent signs and portents against Egypt, against Pharaoh and all his servants;
135:10 He struck down many nations and slew numerous kings—
135:11 Sihon, king of the Amorites, Og, king of Bashan, and all the royalty of Canaan—
135:12 and gave their lands as a heritage, as a heritage to His people Israel.
135:13 O LORD, Your name endures forever, Your fame, O LORD, through all generations;
135:14 for the LORD will champion His people, and obtain satisfaction for His servants.
135:15 The idols of the nations are silver and gold, the work of men’s hands.
135:16 They have mouths, but cannot speak; they have eyes, but cannot see;
135:17 they have ears, but cannot hear, nor is there breath in their mouths.
135:18 Those who fashion them, all who trust in them, shall become like them.
135:19 O house of Israel, bless the LORD; O house of Aaron, bless the LORD;
135:20 O house of Levi, bless the LORD; you who fear the LORD, bless the LORD.
135:21 Blessed is the LORD from Zion, He who dwells in Jerusalem. Hallelujah.
text.Grail1963:
1 ALLELUIA!
Praise the name of the Lord,
praise him, servants of the Lord,
2 who stand in the house of the Lord
in the courts of the house of our God.
3 Praise the Lord for the Lord is good.
Sing a psalm to his name for he is loving.
4 For the Lord has chosen Jacob for himself
and Israel for his own possession.
5 For I know that the Lord is great,
that our Lord is high above all gods.
6 The Lord does whatever he wills,
in heaven, on earth, in the seas.
7 He summons clouds from the ends of the earth;
makes lightning produce the rain;
from his treasuries he sends forth the wind.
8 The firstborn of the Egyptians he smote,
of man and beast alike.
9 Signs and wonders he worked
in the midst of your land, O Egypt,
against Pharaoh and all his servants.
10 Nations in their greatness he struck
and kings in their splendor he slew.
11 Sihon, king of the Amorites,
Og, the king of Bashan,
and all the kingdoms of Canaan.
12 He let Israel inherit their land;
on his people their land he bestowed.
13 Lord, your name stands for ever,
unforgotten from age to age,
14 for the Lord does justice for his people;
the Lord takes pity on his servants.
15 Pagan idols are silver and gold,
the work of human hands.
16 They have mouths but they cannot speak;
they have eyes but they cannot see.
17 They have ears but they cannot hear;
there is never a breath on their lips.
18 Their makers will come to be like them
and so will all who trust in them!
19 Sons of Israel, bless the Lord!
Sons of Aaron, bless the Lord!
20 Sons of Levi, bless the Lord!
You who fear him, bless the Lord!

21 From Zion may the Lord be blessed,
he who dwells in Jerusalem!
text.Rotherham:
135:1 Praise ye Yah, Praise ye the Name of Yahweh, Praise, O ye servants of Yahweh;
135:2 Who stand In the house of Yahweh, In the courts of the house of our God.
135:3 Praise ye Yah, For good is Yahweh, Sing praises to his Name, For it is full of delight;
135:4 For, Jacob, hath Yah chosen for himself, Israel, for his own treasure.
135:5 For, I, know that great is Yahweh, yea, our Lord, is beyond all gods.
135:6 Whatsoever Yahweh hath pleased, he hath done,—in the heavens and on the earth, in the seas, and all resounding deeps:
135:7 Causing vapours to ascend from the end of the earth,—Lightnings for the rain, hath he made, bringing forth wind out of his treasuries.
135:8 Who smote the firstborn of Egypt, both of man, and of beast;
135:9 Sent signs and wonders into thy midst, O Egypt, upon Pharaoh, and upon all his servants.
135:10 Who smote great nations, and slew mighty kings:
135:11 Sihon, king of the Amorites, and Og, king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan;
135:12 And gave their land as an inheritance, an inheritance to Israel his people.
135:13 O Yahweh! thy Name, is age-abiding,—O Yahweh! thy memorial, is to generation after generation.
135:14 For Yahweh will vindicate his people,—and, on his servants, have compassion.
135:15 The idols of the nations, are silver and gold, the work of the hands of men:
135:16 A mouth, have they, but they speak not, Eyes, have they, but they see not;
135:17 Ears, have they, but they hear not, Nose,—there is no breath in their mouth.
135:18 Like unto them, shall be they who make them, every one who trusteth in them.
135:19 O house of Israel! bless Yahweh, O house of Aaron! bless Yahweh;
135:20 O house of Levi! bless Yahweh, Ye that revere Yahweh! bless Yahweh.
135:21 Blessed be Yahweh out of Zion, Who inhabiteth Jerusalem, Praise ye Yah!
text.NABRE:
135:1 Praise of God, the Ruler and Benefactor of Israel Hallelujah! Praise the name of the Lord! Praise, you servants of the Lord,
135:2 Who stand in the house of the Lord, in the courts of the house of our God!
135:3 Praise the Lord, for the Lord is good! Sing to his name, for it brings joy!
135:4 For the Lord has chosen Jacob for himself, Israel as his treasured possession.
135:5 II For I know that the Lord is great, that our Lord is greater than all gods.
135:6 Whatever the Lord desires he does in heaven and on earth, in the seas and all the depths.
135:7 It is he who raises storm clouds from the end of the earth, makes lightning for the rain, and brings forth wind from his storehouse.
135:8 III He struck down Egypt’s firstborn, of human being and beast alike,
135:9 And sent signs and wonders against you, Egypt, against Pharaoh and all his servants.
135:10 It is he who struck down many nations, and slew mighty kings—
135:11 Sihon, king of the Amorites, and Og, king of Bashan, all the kings of Canaan—
135:12 And made their land a heritage, a heritage for Israel his people.
135:13 O Lord, your name is forever, your renown, from generation to generation!
135:14 For the Lord defends his people, shows mercy to his servants.
135:15 IV The idols of the nations are silver and gold, the work of human hands.
135:16 They have mouths but do not speak; they have eyes but do not see;
135:17 They have ears but do not hear; nor is there breath in their mouths.
135:18 Their makers will become like them, and anyone who trusts in them.
135:19 V House of Israel, bless the Lord! House of Aaron, bless the Lord!
135:20 House of Levi, bless the Lord! You who fear the Lord, bless the Lord!
135:21 Blessed be the Lord from Zion, who dwells in Jerusalem! Hallelujah!
text.DRB:
135:1 Alleluia. Confess ye to our Lord because he is good: because his mercy is for ever.
135:2 Confess ye to the God of gods: because his mercy is for ever.
135:3 Confess ye to the Lord of lords: because his mercy is for ever
135:4 Who only doth great marvels: because his mercy is for ever.
135:5 Who made the heaven in understanding: because his mercy is for ever.
135:6 Who established the earth over the waters: because his mercy is for ever.
135:7 Who made the great lights: because his mercy is for ever.
135:8 The sun to rule the day: because his mercy is for ever.
135:9 The moon and stars to rule the night: because his mercy is for ever.
135:10 Who struck Ægypt with their first-begotten: because his mercy is for ever.
135:11 Who brought forth Israel out of the midst of them: because his mercy is for ever.
135:12 In a mighty hand, and lofty arm: because his mercy is for ever.
135:13 Who divided the Red sea into divisions: because his mercy is for ever.
135:14 And brought forth Israel through the midst thereof: because his mercy is for ever.
135:15 And he overthrew Pharao, and his host in the Red sea: because his mercy is for ever.
135:16 Who led his people through the desert: because his mercy is for ever.
135:17 Who struck great kings: because his mercy is for ever.
135:18 And slew strong kings: because his mercy is for ever.
135:19 Sehon the king of the Amorrheites: because his mercy is for ever.
135:20 And Og the king of Basan: because his mercy is for ever:
135:21 And he gave their land for an inheritance: because his mercy is for ever.
135:22 For an inheritance to Israel his servant: because his mercy is for ever.
135:23 For in our humiliation he was mindful of us: because his mercy is for ever.
135:24 And he redeemed us from our enemies: because his mercy is for ever.
135:25 Who giveth food to all flesh: because his mercy is for ever.
135:26 Confess ye to the God of heaven: because his mercy is for ever. Confess ye to the Lord of lords: because his mercy is for ever.

text.KJV:
136:1 O give thanks unto the Lord; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136:2 O give thanks unto the God of gods: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136:3 O give thanks to the Lord of lords: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136:4 To him who alone doeth great wonders: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136:5 To him that by wisdom made the heavens: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136:6 To him that stretched out the earth above the waters: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136:7 To him that made great lights: for his mercy endureth for ever:
136:8 The sun to rule by day: for his mercy endureth for ever:
136:9 The moon and stars to rule by night: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136:10 To him that smote Egypt in their firstborn: for his mercy endureth for ever:
136:11 And brought out Israel from among them: for his mercy endureth for ever:
136:12 With a strong hand, and with a stretched out arm: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136:13 To him which divided the Red sea into parts: for his mercy endureth for ever:
136:14 And made Israel to pass through the midst of it: for his mercy endureth for ever:
136:15 But overthrew Pharaoh and his host in the Red sea: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136:16 To him which led his people through the wilderness: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136:17 To him which smote great kings: for his mercy endureth for ever:
136:18 And slew famous kings: for his mercy endureth for ever:
136:19 Sihon king of the Amorites: for his mercy endureth for ever:
136:20 And Og the king of Bashan: for his mercy endureth for ever:
136:21 And gave their land for an heritage: for his mercy endureth for ever:
136:22 Even an heritage unto Israel his servant: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136:23 Who remembered us in our low estate: for his mercy endureth for ever:
136:24 And hath redeemed us from our enemies: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136:25 Who giveth food to all flesh: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136:26 O give thanks unto the God of heaven: for his mercy endureth for ever.
text.Grail1963:
ALLELUIA!
1 O give thanks to the Lord for he is good,
for his love endures for ever.
2 Give thanks to the God of gods
for his love endures for ever.
3 Give thanks to the Lord of lords,
for his love endures for ever;
4 who alone has wrought marvelous works,
for his love endures for ever;
5 whose wisdom it was made the skies,
for his love endures for ever;
6 who fixed the earth firmly on the seas,
for his love endures for ever.
7 It was he who made the great lights,
for his love endures for ever;
8 the sun to rule in the day,
for his love endures for ever;
9 the moon and the stars in the night,
for his love endures for ever.
10 The firstborn of the Egyptians he smote,
for his love endures for ever.
11 He brought Israel out from the midst,
for his love endures for ever;
12 arm outstretched, with power in his hand,
for his love endures for ever.
13 He divided the Red Sea in two,
for his love endures for ever;
14 he made Israel pass through the midst,
for his love endures for ever;
15 he flung Pharaoh and his force in the sea,
for his love endures for ever.
16 Through the desert his people he led,
for his love endures for ever.
17 Nations in their greatness he struck,
for his love endures for ever.
18 Kings in their splendor he slew,
for his love endures for ever.
19 Sihon, king of the Amorites,
for his love endures for ever;
20 and Og, the king of Bashan,
for his love endures for ever.

for his love endures for ever.

for his love endures for ever.

for his love endures for ever.

for his love endures for ever.

for his love endures for ever.

for his love endures for ever.
text.Rotherham:
136:1 Give ye thanks to Yahweh, for he is good, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:2 Give ye thanks to the God of gods, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:3 Give ye thanks to the Lord of lords, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:4 To him that doeth great wonders, by himself alone, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:5 To him that made the heavens with understanding, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:6 To him that stretched out the earth above the waters, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:7 To him that made great lights, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:8 The sun to rule the day, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:9 The moon and stars to rule the night, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:10 To him that smote Egypt in their firstborn, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:11 And brought forth Israel out of their midst, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:12 With a firm hand, and a stretched-out arm, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:13 To him that divided the Red Sea into division, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:14 And caused Israel to pass through the midst thereof, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:15 And shook off Pharaoh and his army in the Red Sea, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:16 To him that led his people through the desert, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:17 To him that smote great kings, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:18 And slew majestic kings, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:19 Even Sihon, king of the Amorites, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:20 Also Og, king of Bashan, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:21 And gave their land for an inheritance, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:22 An inheritance to Israel his servant, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:23 Who in our low estate, remembered us, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:24 And freed us with force from our adversaries, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:25 Who giveth food to all flesh, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
136:26 Give ye thanks unto the GOD of the heavens, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
text.DRB:
136:1 A Psalm of David for Jeremy. Upon the rivers of Babylon, there we sat and wept: whilst we remembered Sion.
136:2 On the willows in the midst thereof, we hanged up our instruments.
136:3 Because there they that led us captive, demanded of us words of songs. And they that led us away: Sing ye an hymn to us of the songs of Sion.
136:4 How shall we sing the song of our Lord in a strange land?
136:5 If I shall forget thee, o Jerusalem, let my right hand be forgotten.
136:6 Let my tongue cleave to my jaws, if I do not remember thee: If I shall not set Jerusalem in the beginning of my joy.
136:7 Be mindful, o Lord, of the children of Edom, in the day of Jerusalem: That say: Rase it, rase it, even unto the foundation thereof.
136:8 Daughter of Babylon miserable: blessed is he, that shall repay thee thy payment, which thou hast payed us.
136:9 Blessed is he that shall hold, and shall dash thy little ones against the rock.

text.NRSV:
137:1 By the rivers of Babylon— there we sat down and there we wept when we remembered Zion.
137:2 On the willows there we hung up our harps.
137:3 For there our captors asked us for songs, and our tormentors asked for mirth, saying,"Sing us one of the songs of Zion!"
137:4 How could we sing the Lord's song in a foreign land?
137:5 If I forget you, O Jerusalem, let my right hand wither!
137:6 Let my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth, if I do not remember you, if I do not set Jerusalem above my highest joy.
137:7 Remember, O Lord, against the Edomites the day of Jerusalem's fall, how they said,"Tear it down! Tear it down! Down to its foundations!"
137:8 O daughter Babylon, you devastator! Happy shall they be who pay you back what you have done to us!
137:9 Happy shall they be who take your little ones and dash them against the rock!
text.Coverdale:
137:1 By the waters of Babylon we sat down and wept, * when we remembered you, O Zion.
137:2 As for our harps, we hung them up * on the trees in the midst of that land.
137:3 For those who led us away captive asked us for a song, and our oppressors called for mirth: *"Sing us one of the songs of Zion."
137:4 How shall we sing the LORD'S song * upon an alien soil?
137:5 If I forget you, O Jerusalem, * let my right hand forget its skill.
137:6 Let my tongue cleave to the roof of my mouth if I do not remember you, * if I do not set Jerusalem above my highest joy.
137:7 Remember the day of Jerusalem, O LORD, against the people of Edom, * who said,"Down with it! down with it! even to the ground!"
137:8 O Daughter of Babylon, doomed to destruction, * happy the one who pays you back for what you have done to us!
137:9 Happy shall he be who takes your little ones, * and dashes them against the rock!
text.KJV:
137:1 By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down, yea, we wept, when we remembered Zion.
137:2 We hanged our harps upon the willows in the midst thereof.
137:3 For there they that carried us away captive required of us a song; and they that wasted us required of us mirth, saying, Sing us one of the songs of Zion.
137:4 How shall we sing the Lord’s song in a strange land?
137:5 If I forget thee, O Jerusalem, let my right hand forget her cunning.
137:6 If I do not remember thee, let my tongue cleave to the roof of my mouth; if I prefer not Jerusalem above my chief joy.
137:7 Remember, O Lord, the children of Edom in the day of Jerusalem; who said, Rase it, rase it, even to the foundation thereof.
137:8 O daughter of Babylon, who art to be destroyed; happy shall he be, that rewardeth thee as thou hast served us.
137:9 Happy shall he be, that taketh and dasheth thy little ones against the stones.
text.JPS1985:
137:1 By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat, sat and wept, as we thought of Zion.
137:2 There on the poplars we hung up our lyres,
137:3 for our captors asked us there for songs, our tormentors, for amusement: “Sing us one of the songs of Zion.”
137:4 How can we sing a song of the LORD on alien soil?
137:5 If I forget you, O Jerusalem, let my right hand wither;
137:6 let my tongue stick to my palate if I cease to think of you, if I do not keep Jerusalem in memory even at my happiest hour.
137:7 Remember, O LORD, against the Edomites the day of Jerusalem’s fall; how they cried, “Strip her, strip her to her very foundations! ”
137:8 Fair Babylon, you predator, a blessing on him who repays you in kind what you have inflicted on us;
137:9 a blessing on him who seizes your babies and dashes them against the rocks!
